## Knowledge Base

### RiemannRoch subproject ‚ÄĒ known blockers (iter-001 review, 2026-06-20)

- **Degree-1 H¬Ļ headline is NOT axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ it transitively carries `sorryAx` via `injective_flasque`** *(iter-001, first-hand `lean_verify` confirmed; do-not-trust the "out-of-cone" claim).* The prover (H1Vanishing task result) and an in-file comment (`H1Vanishing.lean:781-783`) claim `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` does NOT depend on the open `IsFlasque.injective_flasque` (only the `n=0` base case). **Formally false:** the headline `:= HModule_flasque_eq_zero (‚Ä¶) 1`, whose induction's `succ` case calls `injective_flasque` (a typed sorry, blocked on Mathlib's missing `j_!` extension-by-zero). `lean_verify` on the headline ‚Üí `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. A sorry-free `i=1`-only path is mathematically available but requires **factoring the `n=0` base case out of the induction** (structural refactor) ‚ÄĒ that, not closing `injective_flasque`, is the cheapest unblock for headline #1. `% NOTE:` added to both blueprint proof blocks (`thm:H1_vanishing_flasque`, `lem:H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero_main`).
- **`rationalMap_order_finite_support` is FALSE as typed under `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`** *(iter-001, lean-vs-blueprint-checker + in-code counterexample L807-812; do-not-retry under current signature).* The `.Finite` conclusion fails on a non-quasi-compact integral locally-Noetherian scheme (affine line with infinitely many origins). The `sorry` at L831 fills a false proposition. Blueprint *statement* already carries the correct hyp (Noetherian) and even says the Lean signature should take `[IsNoetherian X]`; the **Lean signature drifted to `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`-only**. Fix = strengthen `principal`'s signature to `[IsNoetherian X]`/`[CompactSpace X]` (derive `CompactSpace C.left` from `IsProper C.hom`) project-wide in one coordinated pass ‚ÄĒ it ripples to `principal`, `principal_apply/_hom`, `LinearEquivalence`, `principal_degree_zero`, `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`, and cross-file callers (`OCofP.lean`, `RationalCurveIso.lean`). Cross-file/protected-shape decision ‚ÄĒ NOT a prover-lane task.
- **`eulerCharacteristic_shortExact_add` is FALSE without a coherence/finiteness hypothesis** *(iter-001, lean-vs-blueprint-checker, explicit counterexample; do-not-retry the 6 finiteness sorries in place).* Stated for an arbitrary `ShortComplex` of `ModuleCat kbar`-sheaves with only `S.ShortExact`; under Mathlib's `finrank = 0` convention Ōá-additivity fails for non-coherent sheaves. 6 of the lemma's 7 sorries are `FiniteDimensional (HModule ‚Ä¶ XŠĶĘ n)` ‚ÄĒ **unprovable as typed** (signal of the wrong signature, NOT routine debt); only the 7th (`Subsingleton (Ext L S.X‚āĀ 2)`, Grothendieck degree-2 vanishing) is honest missing-Mathlib infra. Lemma not frozen; fix = add coherence/finiteness hyp, discharge at the concrete call site `eulerCharacteristic_of_shortExact_skyscraper`. Blueprint `lem:euler_char_shortExact_add` ALSO omits the coherence hyp that its own `def:eulerChar_curve` insists on ‚ÄĒ internal inconsistency, fix both sides.
- **`sheafOf` `D‚Č†0` general construction is done sorry-free except the smooth‚üĻDVR instance** *(iter-001, OcOfD; honest, lean-vs-blueprint-checker 0 must-fix).* The ~430-line subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier/presheaf/sheaf-property construction is sorry-free; the ONLY gap is the localized `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left := ‚ü®fun _ => sorry‚ü©` (every codim-1 stalk of a smooth dim-1 curve over `kŐĄ` is a DVR). This is the project's deliberately-axiomatised hypothesis; closing it as a global instance from `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` (a `mathlib-build` lane) makes the localized sorry vanish. Related: `sheafOf_singlePoint` needs OCofP's `private carrier*` de-privatised OR restatement as iso `‚ČÖ` (strict `Sheaf` equality unreachable); `sheafOf_ses_single_add` needs coherent-sheaf tensor-exactness API absent from Mathlib.
- **`Scheme.Hom.ofFunctionFieldEmbedding` (function-field-determines-curve, Hartshorne I.6.12) has no Mathlib constructor** *(iter-001, WeilDivisor).* Blocks `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` and the residual of `principal_degree_zero` (both bottom out at Hartshorne II.6.9 deg-multiplicativity / ramification-inertia on the finite morphism `ŌÜ : C ‚Üí ‚Ąô¬Ļ`). `mathlib-build`-scale substrate (~several hundred LOC), outside a single prover lane.
- **DVR-stalk Route-C algebra core CLOSED; the two residual blockers are scheme-theory, named** *(iter-007, SmoothStalkDVR.lean ‚ÄĒ NEW file, 3 axiom-clean decls `{propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound}`).* `Algebra.{free_and_finrank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_one, finrank_cotangentSpace_localization_eq_one_of_isStandardSmooth_dim_one, isDiscreteValuationRing_localization_of_isStandardSmooth_dim_one}` abstract over `k S T` with `S` rel-dim-1 standard-smooth and `T = S` localised at `M`; they consume SmoothRegular's Route-C closes. **Use `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map (R S T) (M)` ‚ÄĒ NOT `isLocalizedModule`** (the latter localises the *base*; PROGRESS named the wrong one). Localisation transports freeness via `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule` + rank via `Module.finrank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`; `FormallySmooth k T`/`EssFiniteType k T` assemble by composition (`FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization M` + `.comp`; `EssFiniteType.of_isLocalization`/`.comp`). **Two scheme-pin blockers remain** (each multi-piece, raw material PRESENT): **(B1)** chart-extraction ‚ÄĒ from `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom` + `x`, produce affine `V‚ąčx`, the *non-global* `kŐĄ`-algebra instance on stalk `T = ūĚí™_{C,x}` (kŐĄ not recoverable from the stalk type ‚Üí thread via `letI`/binders), `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 kŐĄ őď(V)` via `IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension.mk` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk` + `Scheme.őďSpecIso`; **(B2)** residue-field rationality `őļ(x)=kŐĄ` (discharges the two `Subsingleton` residue hyps) via Nullstellensatz ‚ÄĒ **no single-call Mathlib lemma**, assemble finite-ext-of-alg-closed-is-trivial.

- **`chart_ringKrullDim_le_one`'s `s ‚Č§ 1` step is a genuine Mathlib gap ‚ÄĒ do NOT re-assign as a plain `prove`** *(iter-012, CurveKrullDim.lean L235; lean-auditor + lvb both confirm genuine, not laundering).* The C lemma (Krull dim of a rel-dim-1 standard-smooth domain over `kŐĄ` is ‚Č§1, Stacks 00P0) lands axiom-clean EXCEPT the isolated `have hs : (s : WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě) ‚Č§ 1`. The Noether-normalization scaffold proves `dim S ‚Č§ s` (`exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg` + `algebraize` + G1 + `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing` + `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field`). The residual is the chain `s = dim S = trdeg(Frac S/kŐĄ) = relDim = rank ő©[S/kŐĄ] = 1`: the links `dim = trdeg` (Tag 00P0) and `trdeg = rank ő©` are **Mathlib-ABSENT (rev v4.30.0-rc2)** ‚ÄĒ confirmed loogle/leansearch (no `ringKrullDim‚ÜĒtrdeg`, no `trdeg = rank ő©`, no `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension ‚Üí ringKrullDim`). The one available fact `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential 1` (`rank ő© = 1`) is in-block but the bridge to `s` is the gap. Informal agent unavailable (no API key). Corrective TYPE = mathlib-build / effort-breaker (decompose into the two named bridge lemmas), NOT `prove`. Write-up: `informal/chart_ringKrullDim_le_one_s_le_one.md`. Closing this makes `krullDim_curve_le_one` (T, own body already sorry-free) axiom-clean and unblocks OcOfD L635 `hdim` wiring. Watch: progress-critic flagged C "amber" ‚Üí STUCK if unclosed by iter-013.
- **OcOfD L635 cotangent-finrank sorry is WIRING-READY** *(iter-012, lean-auditor MJ-2).* `have hfin : ‚Ä¶ CotangentSpace ‚Ä¶ = 1 := sorry` (inside `sheafOf`) feeds `isDiscreteValuationRing_stalk_of_finrank_cotangentSpace_eq_one`; its substrate `finrank_cotangentSpace_stalk_eq_one_of_smooth` (SmoothStalkDVR, iter-011) is axiom-clean. The sorry already exists in-file (plan-validate will not drop it). Verify whether L635 needs `krullDim_curve_le_one`'s `hdim` (blocked on C) or only the cotangent pin ‚ÄĒ if cotangent-only, it can land now. First iter-013 target.

### RiemannRoch ‚ÄĒ proof patterns & dead ends (iter-007)

- **OcOfD skyscraper-cokernel iso `cokernel_carrierSheafHom_iso_skyscraper`: away-from-`P` half PROVED at section level (4 axiom-clean bricks); 3 named gaps remain** *(iter-007, OcOfD.lean ‚ÄĒ sorry 4‚Üí4, the assigned `‚ČÖ` kept as honest typed sorry, NOT a costume skeleton).* New sorry-free private lemmas: `sheafOf.{carrierSet, carrierSubmodule, carrierSubmoduleSheaf}_add_single_eq_of_not_mem` (P.point‚ąČU ‚üĻ the two carrier objects coincide, via `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ext` point-injectivity + `Finsupp.single_apply`/`add_apply`) and `carrierPresheaf_le_hom_app_isIso_of_not_mem` (the section-level away-from-`P` iso). **Remaining gaps:** **(G1)** promote brick-4 to a *stalk* iso for `x‚Č†P.point` ‚ÄĒ needs the geometric fact "`P.point` is a closed point of the curve" (**ABSENT** ‚ÄĒ no `isClosed`/closed-point lemma for `PrimeDivisor`, confirmed by search) + an iso-from-basis colimit-cofinality helper (Mathlib has injective-only `TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`, NO iso/surjective version); **(G2 = deep blocker)** the at-`P` carrier-stalk DVR computation `stalk_P(carrierSheaf D) ‚ČÖ ŌÄ_P^{-n}ūĚí™_{C,P}` then `ŌÄ^{-(n+1)}ūĚí™/ŌÄ^{-n}ūĚí™ ‚ČÖ kŐĄ` ‚ÄĒ no carrier-stalk lemma exists, ~200‚Äď400 LOC separate development; **(G3)** assembly via `stalkSkyscraperSheafAdjunction` + `isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (friction: `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology) (ModuleCat kŐĄ)` vs `TopCat.Sheaf`-phrased API ‚ÄĒ defeq, but `.val`/`.hom` accessors must be matched).
- **DEAD END: `ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective` for an inclusion-of-equal-submodules iso in `ModuleCat`** *(iter-007).* Proving `Function.Bijective` of the app FAILS: the underlying map carries a `ConcreteCategory.hom` wrapper, so `congrArg Subtype.val`/`Subtype.ext_iff.mp` mis-infer which `Subtype.val` (source vs target) and reject the hypothesis. **Robust route:** explicit two-sided inverse `refine ‚ü®ModuleCat.ofHom (Submodule.inclusion (le_of_eq heq.symm)), ?_, ?_‚ü©`, both composites `rfl` after `ModuleCat.hom_ext; LinearMap.ext; rintro ‚ü®x,hx‚ü©`.

### Toolchain / process lessons

- **Re-test a stale Lean-tactical "bomb"/timeout on the current Mathlib before escalating** *(NEW iter-224)*. The `internalHomEval` naturality sorry was diagnosed iter-222 as a `whnf` heartbeat bomb (>200k‚Äď3.2M heartbeats), re-characterized iter-223 as goal-wide/lemma-non-localized, and triggered a full `mathlib-analogist` ts224dual ALIGN escalation (ROUTE A `with_reducible` / ROUTE B `unit`-reshape recipes). At iter-224 the bomb **did not exist** ‚ÄĒ a Mathlib version bump silently removed it; the "bombing" tactic `rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp]` now returns *instantly* ("pattern not found", not a timeout), and the six-step reduction prior provers had already written just compiles. Cost: ~2 iters (222 stub 80‚Üí81, 223 mis-characterization 81‚Üí81) on a phantom. **Rule:** before escalating a multi-iter-old elaboration-cost wall, re-run the plain tactic once on the current toolchain. A version bump can retract such a wall with no other signal.

### Proof patterns (reusable across targets)

- **Carrier-duality recipe: `CommRingCat` scalar `r : R(U)` acting on a `RingCat`-carrier section tensor `A(U)‚äóB(U)`** *(NEW iter-234, the `stalkTensorDescU_smul`/`stalkTensorLinearMap` close ‚ÄĒ d.2 stage iii)*. A `PresheafOfModules (R ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat)` has scalars `r : R.obj (op U)` (CommRingCat coe) but its section-tensor module `A(U) ‚äó B(U)` lives over the *RingCat* carrier `S(U) = (R ‚čô forget‚āā).obj (op U)`. The two carriers are **defeq but NOT syntactic**, so keyed `rw` / instance synthesis fails. **DEAD:** `rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']` (‚Üí `failed to synthesize HSMul ‚ÜĎ(R.obj (op U)) ‚Ä¶`); `set S := ‚Ä¶` + `@`-explicit `smul_tmul'`/`smul_zero` (‚Üí `failed to synthesize Module S ‚ÜĎ(A.obj (op U))` ‚ÄĒ naming the RingCat carrier breaks factor-module instances). **WORKS ‚ÄĒ stay implicit, use defeq/term-mode:** for `tmul` `erw [stalkTensorDescU_tmul, ‚Ä¶, germ_smul]` then `exact (TensorProduct.smul_tmul' _ _ _).symm`; for `add` `erw [smul_add]` + `refine (map_add _ _ _).trans ?_; rw [hp, hq, ‚Üź smul_add]; exact congrArg _ (map_add _ p q).symm`; for `zero` `simp only [‚Ä¶ map_zero, smul_zero]; exact map_zero _` (since `r ‚ÄĘ 0` is defeq `0`). **Rule: never `rw [smul_tmul']` / `map_add` / `map_zero` directly on `ConcreteCategory.hom g` ‚ÄĒ use `erw` (defeq) or term-mode (`refine ‚Ä¶ .trans` / `exact map_zero _`).** Companion: `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` is required for any iso between `restrictScalars`-of-Spec-modules to even typecheck (Mathlib `Tilde.lean` uses it pervasively). Stalk-linearity `map_smul'` then follows the d.1 `stalkLinearMap` pattern: `germ_exist` scalar+vector ‚Üí common nbhd `W=U‚äďV` via `germ_res_apply` ‚Üí chain `‚Üź germ_smul, ‚Ä¶_germ, ‚Ä¶_smul, ‚Üź ‚Ä¶_germ`. Verified axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

- **`PresheafOfModules.Hom` naturality over `Over.map` restriction, codomain at the monoidal unit `ūĚüô_`** *(NEW iter-224, the `internalHomEval` close)*. To prove the naturality square of a presheaf-of-modules morphism whose value is an evaluation/contraction `s ‚äó ŌÜ ‚Ü¶ ŌÜ(s)`: `refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s ŌÜ => ?_)`; split the two `‚Čę` with `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]` then fire the per-section simp-lemma (`internalHomEvalApp_tmul`) ‚ÄĒ `erw`'s defeq matching sees through `restrictScalars` and `Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul` automatically. The `ūĚüô_`-codomain RHS reduces to the bare `evalLin` form **purely by defeq** (the per-section lemma is `:= rfl`), exposed by one `change` ‚ÄĒ **no unit-map rewrite is needed** (`tensorUnit_map` / `unit_map_apply` are *unknown constants*; do not search for them). Then `PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply ŌÜ (Over.homMk f.unop).op s` + `rw [restr_map_homMk]` rewrites further-restrictions to base maps, and `Over.map` pseudofunctor coherence (`Over.mk (ūĚüô Y.unop ‚Čę f.unop) = Over.mk f.unop`, equal source) is bridged by `eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq ‚Ä¶ (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))))`. Verified axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

- **"Locally-iso ‚áí global iso" for `X.Modules` morphisms via the stalkwise criterion** *(NEW iter-226, the `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` B-connector)*. To prove a morphism `ŌÜ : M ‚ü∂ N` of `ūĚí™_X`-modules is `IsIso` from per-point restriction-isos `IsIso ((restrictFunctor (U x).őĻ).map ŌÜ)` (the exact form `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial` supplies via choice): reduce to "every stalk of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf map is iso", then apply `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` and reflect back through `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` (`ReflectsIsomorphisms`) with `CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso`. Per point: pick a preimage `x'` of `x` under the open immersion via `Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp` + `Scheme.Opens.opensRange_őĻ`; the restriction is iso, so the triple-composite functor sends it to an iso; transport across `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).őĻ x'` (restriction commutes with stalks) using `CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff` to land the stalk at `x`. **Gotchas:** (1) `Ab` needs explicit universe `Ab.{u}` everywhere or `stalkFunctor` lands at universe 0 and mismatches `toPresheaf X`'s `TopCat.Presheaf Ab.{u} X`; (2) `Functor.map_isIso` is NOT an instance through a triple composite ‚ÄĒ close `IsIso ((A‚čôB‚čôC).map ŌÜ)` with `dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _`; (3) `TopCat.Sheaf` hom is an induced-category hom with field `.hom` (not `.val`). **No stalk-‚äó ("d.2") is invoked** ‚ÄĒ this is a single-morphism statement. Verified axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

- **Base change along a ring iso commutes with the linear DUAL (`Hom(-,R)`) ‚ÄĒ the C-build H2‚Ä≤ core, the `Hom`-analogue of the H2 tensor equivalence; and the `Scheme.Modules.homMk` wrapper for promoting an `ūĚí™_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module morphism** *(NEW iter-227 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`; `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (~L306, top-level ‚ÄĒ NOT in the `PresheafOfModules` namespace despite the task-result heading) `lean_verify` = `{propext, Quot.sound}` (cleaner than the tensor analogue ‚ÄĒ no `Classical.choice`); `Scheme.Modules.homMk` (~L2034) + `toPresheaf_map_homMk` (~L2042, `@[simp]` `rfl`) `lean_verify` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`; all re-verified first-hand this review, no `sorryAx`; project sorry stays 80, no-sorry infra.)* **(a) `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`:** for `e : R ‚ČÉ+* S` and an `S`-module `M` (given its `R`-structure via `Module.compHom ¬∑ e.toRingHom` placed in the SIGNATURE via `letI`, mirroring `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`), the `R`-linear equivalence `(M ‚Üí‚āó[S] S) ‚ČÉ‚āó[R] (M ‚Üí‚āó[R] R)`, forward `ŌÜ ‚Ü¶ e.symm ‚ąė ŌÜ`, inverse `Ōą ‚Ü¶ e ‚ąė Ōą`. Linearity rests on `hsmulM : r ‚ÄĘ m = e r ‚ÄĘ m` (`rfl`, the `compHom` defeq) + `map_smul`/`map_mul`/`e.symm_apply_apply`/`e.apply_symm_apply`. Gotchas this iter: a naive `change e.symm (e r * ŌÜ m) = ‚Ä¶` fails (not defeq to the `‚ÄĘ` form ‚ÄĒ keep `‚ÄĘ` and rewrite via `hsmulM` first); `map_add'`/`map_smul'` close by `simp [map_add]` / `simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]; rw [hsmulM, ‚Ä¶]`; the outer `map_smul'` needs one `change e.symm ((e r ‚ÄĘ ŌÜ) m) = r * e.symm (ŌÜ m)` then `LinearMap.smul_apply`. This is the concrete `LinearEquiv` that feeds `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv` (the categorical packaging), exactly parallel to how `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` feeds `restrictScalars_isIso_őľ`. **Because `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is an EQUIVALENCE, it commutes with `Hom(-,-)` in BOTH variances ‚ÄĒ so the contravariant dual is d.2-free just as the tensor was.** **(b) `Scheme.Modules.homMk`:** wrap `PresheafOfModules.homMk (M‚āĀ := M.val) (M‚āā := N.val) g hg` in the `SheafOfModules.Hom` constructor `‚ü®¬∑‚ü©`. **The linearity hypothesis MUST be stated over `X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V` / `M.val.obj V` carriers and `g` MUST be `M.val.presheaf ‚ü∂ N.val.presheaf` syntactically** ‚ÄĒ the `Scheme.Modules.presheaf` def does NOT unfold for instance synthesis, and the codomain smul instance is only found on the `.val.presheaf` path. Defining property `toPresheaf_map_homMk : (toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g := rfl`. **A section-level `@[simp]` form `((homMk g hg).val.app V).hom = (g.app V).hom` is FALSE-TYPED** (`PresheafOfModules` `.app` is a `LinearMap`, `g.app V` an `AddMonoidHom`) ‚ÄĒ use the `Ab`-level `toPresheaf` identity instead. This is step (ii) of the `homOfLocalCompat` A-bridge (gluing produces the linear `g`; `homMk` turns it into `M ‚ü∂ N`).

### Soundness Patterns

- **Typed-`sorry` on the *carrier of a definition* silently propagates `sorryAx` through every consumer's typeclass synthesis** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ lean-auditor iter193 finding, dominant project-wide soundness exposure.)* When a `noncomputable def Foo : Carrier := sorry` lands as a file-skeleton placeholder (e.g. `Pic0Scheme`, `PicScheme`, `QuotScheme`, `picSharp`, `divFunctor`, `abelMap`, `PicSharp`, `presheaf`, `PicSharp.etSheaf`), the soundness exposure is qualitatively WORSE than a `theorem foo := sorry`: every typeclass `[Bar (Foo C)]` that fires on `Foo` silently carries `sorryAx`. **Auditor recommendation**: guard the definition behind an existential `Nonempty (ő£' S : Over (Spec k), _)` (or similar) ‚ÄĒ this keeps the *typeclass-synthesis* honest even when the body is sorry-bodied. The `ő£'`-bundle pattern is already used by other file-skeletons in the project (`jacobianWitness`, etc.). A `refactor` subagent (`pic-quot-relpic-carrier-soundness`) holding write_domain across the 4 affected files (`Picard/{IdentityComponent, FGAPicRepresentability, QuotScheme, RelPicFunctor}.lean`) can atomically re-shape all `:= sorry` definition carriers in one pass without changing downstream consumer signatures (since consumers can be re-shaped to `Classical.choice (nonempty_*)` extractions). Reusable pattern: ANY `noncomputable def F := sorry` on a load-bearing definition deserves the existential-bundle pattern, NOT the bare `:= sorry`.

- **Opening an `instance` body's sorry via `refine ‚ü®...‚ü©; sorry` *expands* downstream sorryAx propagation rather than contains it** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ lean-auditor iter195 must-fix on `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:746-772`.)* Iter-195 Lane I advanced the body of `instance instIsRegularInCodimOneProjectiveLineBar` from a bare `:= sorry` to `:= by refine ‚ü®fun Y => ?_‚ü©; sorry` (exposing the per-prime-divisor obligation) AND simultaneously advanced the consumer `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` body's tactic chain (Steps A+B+C). The latter's binder list `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]` means the consumer now silently picks up `sorryAx` through more lines of in-scope tactic state than before ‚ÄĒ the iter is a NET INCREASE in silent propagation surface, not a decrease. **Rule**: when partial structural progress on an instance body is committed (the `refine ‚ü®...‚ü©` step), simultaneously demote `instance` ‚Üí `theorem` and thread `haveI` at consumer sites ‚ÄĒ otherwise the silent-propagation cost grows alongside the structural progress. Reusable for any iter where Lane progress simultaneously advances a sorry-bodied instance AND its consumer's body. (Companion to iter-193 IdentityComponent.lean entry below; the iter-194 IC.lean fix recipe applies mechanically.)

- **`sorry`-derived typeclass instances silently propagate `sorryAx` into every downstream typeclass synthesis automatically** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ lean-auditor iter193 finding, `IdentityComponent.lean:500-507`.)* A `private instance identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` whose body invokes a sorry-bodied helper `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` (L414-420) is qualitatively worse than a sorry-bodied **theorem** because consumers CANNOT opt out ‚ÄĒ instance synthesis fires automatically on every `[GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom]` query. **Auditor recommendation**: either (a) demote to a non-instance lemma until the helper closes; or (b) gate the instance behind a separate `[Hypothesis_GC]` typeclass that callers must explicitly supply. Reusable: NEVER mark `instance` on a typeclass derivation whose body transitively invokes `sorryAx` ‚ÄĒ only `lemma` / `def`.

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
- **Bound `ringKrullDim S ‚Č§ 1` for an f.t.-over-algebraically-closed-`kŐĄ` chart algebra via the EMBEDDING-DIMENSION inequality, NOT trdeg** *(NEW iter-013 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `RiemannRoch/CurveKrullDim.lean`, `chart_ringKrullDim_le_one` CLOSED axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, file 1‚Üí0; `krullDim_curve_le_one` now axiom-clean end-to-end; lean-auditor ts013 + lvb ts013 both 0 must-fix.)* The trdeg route (`s = dim = trdeg = rankő©`) is a DEAD END ‚ÄĒ `dim=trdeg` (Tag 00P0) and `trdeg=rankő©` are Mathlib-ABSENT (v4.30.0-rc2, confirmed iter-012). **Working route:** `Ideal.sup_primeHeight_of_maximal_eq_ringKrullDim` reduces `ringKrullDim S ‚Č§ 1` to `primeHeight ūĚĒ™ ‚Č§ 1` per MAXIMAL `ūĚĒ™` (coercion `WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě ‚ÜĒ ‚Ąē‚ąě` via `WithBot.coe_one` + `WithBot.coe_le_coe` + `iSup_le`); localize `T := Localization.AtPrime ūĚĒ™`, `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height` + `Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight` give `primeHeight ūĚĒ™ = ringKrullDim T`; Krull height theorem `ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal T` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace T` give `ringKrullDim T ‚Č§ finrank őļ(T) (CotangentSpace T)`; close `=1` by REUSING the conormal bridge `Algebra.finrank_cotangentSpace_localization_eq_one_of_isStandardSmooth_dim_one kbar S T ūĚĒ™.primeCompl` (the same bridge the cotangent stalk-pin uses). **GOTCHAS:** (1) **NEVER `letI : Algebra kbar T := (...).toAlgebra` on a `Localization`** ‚ÄĒ it auto-derives `Algebra kbar T` + `IsScalarTower kbar S T` from `[Algebra kbar S]`; a manual instance collides with the OreLocalization SMul (kernel-rejected diamond). (2) The ONLY non-inferred instance is `IsScalarTower kbar S (IsLocalRing.ResidueField T)` ‚ÄĒ build via `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq` + 3 `IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply` rewrites. (3) Noetherianness needed by both height lemmas: `IsNoetherianRing S := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing kbar S`, `IsNoetherianRing T := IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing ūĚĒ™.primeCompl T inferInstance`. (4) Residue rationality (`őļ(T)=kŐĄ`) copied verbatim from the cotangent stalk-pin: `algebraMap_residueField_surjective_of_isLocalization_atPrime` ‚Üí `FiniteType.of_surjective` ‚Üí `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_finiteType` ‚Üí `subsingleton_{kaehlerDifferential,h1Cotangent}_of_algebraMap_bijective` (both root-namespace). (5) Signature needs `[IsAlgClosed kbar]` and `{kbar S : Type u}` (single universe ‚ÄĒ the conormal bridge's `k S T` share `Type u_1`). **Lesson:** when a Mathlib infra gap blocks a route, a cheaper reroute reusing already-built infra (here the conormal bridge) can beat filling the gap.

ALWAYS `lean_verify` a new mathlib-build decl before declaring it axiom-clean. (b) **The ‚äó-substrate now converges to ONE math gap:** both `dual_eval` (‚Üí sub-step 5 `exists_tensorObj_inverse`, the 80‚Üí79 mover) AND the associator are sorry-transitive through the SAME L641 residual = the d.2 stalk-‚äó commutation `(F ‚äóŠĶĖ M)_x ‚ČÖ F_x ‚äó_{R_x} M_x` (Mathlib-absent). `dual_eval` is a ready-to-paste ~10-line axiom-clean add the moment d.2 closes; do NOT dispatch sub-step 5 before d.2 (it would be sorry-transitive). The flat variant `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` is axiom-clean but needs sectionwise flatness, FALSE for line bundles over non-affine opens (iter-212) ‚ÄĒ no substitute.

The `tensor_ext` reduction of naturality to `G: evalLin M Y ((dual M).map f ŌÜ) (M.map f s) = ((ūĚüô_).map f).hom (evalLin M X ŌÜ s)` is verified. **THE remaining wall is pure elaboration cost, NOT math:** instantiating the `rfl`-bridge `restr_map_homMk` at the concrete unit `ūĚüô_` (or the closing `exact key.symm`) triggers a `whnf` explosion of the nested `ofPresheaf‚ąėpushforward‚āÄ‚ąėOver.map‚ąėrestrictScalars‚ąėtensorObj‚ąėunit` machinery ‚ÄĒ `>3.2M` heartbeats, ~exponential. **`maxHeartbeats {1.6M, 3.2M}` ALL time out ‚Üí do NOT bump heartbeats (proven useless); the fix is structural (whnf-free).** Three recorded routes: (1) **generalize the unit** ‚ÄĒ `set U := ūĚüô_ ‚Ä¶` BEFORE `naturality_apply`/`rw ‚Ä¶at key`/`exact`, do the bridge with `U` ABSTRACT (cheap, like `restr_map_homMk M f`), specialize `U := ūĚüô_` only at the very end; (2) use Mathlib `pushforward_obj_map_apply'`/`pushforward_map_app_apply'` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward`, note `pushforward‚āÄ F R = pushforward (ūĚüô (F.op ‚čô R))`) so matching is syntactic; (3) close `G` elementwise via `LinearMap.ext`/`congr 1` avoiding the unit-instantiation form. **Reusable lessons:** (a) isolating a heavy `whnf` defeq into a `private` `rfl` lemma works ONLY when the parameter is abstract ‚ÄĒ it BREAKS (exponential) when instantiated at the concrete unit `ūĚüô_`, whose normal form is enormous; (b) keep eval values at their NATURAL over-ring type (not ascribed to the unit value) to keep the contraction simp lemma `rfl`-provable (the `[[ts-assoc-flatness-gap]]` diamond-bridge trick). **Process note:** iters 219‚Äď221 honoured the build's no-sorry invariant by keeping the morphism ABSENT; iter-222 instead landed it with a stubbed naturality (project counter's first upward move). - **The presheaf internal hom `‚Ąčom(M,N)` (restriction maps + full assembly) is BUILDABLE project-side and axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ `PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.internalHom` via `ofPresheaf`, sub-step 2 of the funded ‚äó-dual block** *(NEW iter-220 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` ¬ß`PresheafOfModules.InternalHom`, 12 axiom-clean decls L996‚Äď1305; `internalHom` `lean_verify` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, no `sorryAx`; INDEPENDENTLY judged genuine by BOTH lean-auditor ts220 (0 must-fix) and lean-vs-blueprint-checker ts220 (4/4 pinned decls faithful).)* Built the restriction maps `(M|_U‚ü∂N|_U)‚Üí(M|_V‚ü∂N|_V)` over `R(g)` (`restrictionMap g ŌÜ := (pushforward‚āÄ (Over.map g) ‚Ä¶).map ŌÜ`), proved functoriality + semilinearity, and ASSEMBLED the presheaf via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`. **Three reusable tricks:** (1) **`hom_app_heq` + `subst` cracks `Over.map` pseudofunctor coherence.** `Over.mapId_eq`/`Over.mapComp_eq` are propositional (NOT defeq), so `restrictionMap`'s `map_id`/`map_comp` are not `rfl`; ~6 attempts failed (dependent-motive `rw`, `eqToHom ‚Ėł rfl`, non-existent `Hom.naturality_apply`). The crack: private `hom_app_heq ŌÜ (h : X = Y) : HEq (ŌÜ.app X) (ŌÜ.app Y) := by subst h; rfl`, then `exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq ŌÜ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))` (the slice restriction depends only on `.left`, identical for reindexed objects ‚áí source/target modules defeq). General device for any `pushforward‚āÄ (Over.map -)` functoriality. (2) **`ofPresheaf` forces a SINGLE-universe base.** It ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to `RingCat.{u}`, but morphism groups `M|_U‚ü∂N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)` for a general base; so `internalHomPresheaf` is universe-polymorphic at `AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC}` but `internalHom` is stated in a fresh section over `{D : Type u}[Category.{u,u} D]{R‚āÄ : DŠĶíŠĶĖ ‚•§ CommRingCat.{u}}` ‚ÄĒ exactly the `Opens X` site (`SmallCategory.{u}`); no generality lost for the project. (3) **CommRingCat/RingCat carrier diamond ‚áí pass the Module instance EXPLICITLY via `@ofPresheaf`.** `internalHomObjModule`'s `Module` is over CommRingCat `R‚āÄ.obj` while `ofPresheaf` wants `(R‚āÄ ‚čô forget‚āā).obj` (RingCat); a `letI` Module binding is KERNEL-REJECTED (diamond). Use `@PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R‚āÄ ‚čô forget‚āā _ _) (internalHomPresheaf M N) (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N) (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)`; `ofPresheaf`'s `respectTransparency false` accepts the defeq carriers. **Minor gotchas:** `AddCommGrpCat.of`/`.ofHom` (NOT `AddCommGrp.of`; `abbrev Ab := AddCommGrpCat`); `Ab`-presheaf `map_id`/`map_comp` need `apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext; refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun ŌÜ => ?_`; semilinearity core `restrictionMap_globalSMul` needs the `Over.w` triangle `(mkIdTerminal.from (Over.map g).obj Y).left = (mkIdTerminal.from Y).left ‚Čę g` + `erw [‚Üź CommRingCat.comp_apply, ‚Üź R.map_comp]`. **`internalHomObjModule` (a `def` of class type `Module`) STILL needs `@[implicit_reducible]`** (lean-auditor ts220 major ‚ÄĒ companion `homModule` has it; without it instance-search for `r ‚ÄĘ m` may silently fail downstream). **Next bricks (sub-steps 3‚Äď5):** `dual M := internalHom M unit`; `internalHomEval`; sheafify to `Scheme.Modules.dual`; then `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. Recipe also in memory `[[ts-assoc-flatness-gap]]`.
- **The slice internal-hom VALUE module of presheaves-of-modules is BUILDABLE project-side and axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ `homModule`/`internalHomObjModule`, the first brick of the funded ‚äó-dual infra block** *(NEW iter-219 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` ¬ß`PresheafOfModules.InternalHom`, 11 axiom-clean decls L996-1129; `internalHomObjModule`/`homModule` `lean_verify` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, no `sorryAx`; INDEPENDENTLY judged genuine by BOTH lean-auditor ts219 (0 must-fix; every module axiom proved through established lemmas, no `rfl` shortcut) and lean-vs-blueprint-checker ts219 (faithful to the blueprint slice formula, no signature mismatch).)* This **refutes the analogist ts219dual pessimism** that the internal hom is "NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL at presheaf, sheaf AND categorical level" ‚ÄĒ at the per-object VALUE level it is now built. **Recipe:** the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M,N)` (for ANY base category `C` with terminal object `T`) is `homModule`, scalar `f ‚ąą R(T)` acting by post-composition with `globalSMul f : N ‚ü∂ N` (multiply each section by the restriction of `f`). The six `Module` axioms reduce to four `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-hom facts (note `globalSMul (f*g) = globalSMul g ‚Čę globalSMul f` ‚ÄĒ composition reverses, required by `mul_smul`) + `Preadditive` bilinearity of composition. The slice value `internalHomObjModule U M N : Module (R(U)) (M|_U ‚ü∂ N|_U)` specialises `homModule` to `T := Over.mk (ūĚüô U)` (terminal of `Over U` via `Over.mkIdTerminal`), with `M|_U = restr U M = pushforward‚āÄ (Over.forget U) M`; the ring-at-terminal is `R(U)` by `rfl`. This is the blueprint-mandated COVARIANT slice formula, NOT the naive contravariant `U ‚Ü¶ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U),N(U))`. **Three recurring gotchas:** (1) **CommRingCat `R.obj Y` vs RingCat `(R ‚čô forget‚āā).obj Y` carrier duality** ‚ÄĒ same type/ops up to defeq but `rw`/`simp` of `map_add`/`add_smul`/`lsmul` fail across them; keep `termRingMap` valued in `R.obj` (CommRingCat) so scalar `f`, `+`/`*`/`1`, and `LinearMap.lsmul` (needs `CommSemiring`) share ONE carrier, bridge to `(Fr R)` semilinearity (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`) with `erw` (defeq) ONLY in the `globalSMul` naturality. (2) **`map_add` is AMBIGUOUS in this file** (`_root_.map_add` vs `Functor.map_add` under `open CategoryTheory`) ‚ÄĒ use `_root_.map_add`. (3) **`ModuleCat.of` of a hom-type module is fragile** ‚ÄĒ `letI := homModule ‚Ä¶; ModuleCat.of _ (M ‚ü∂ N)` fails `Quiver` synthesis; `internalHomObjModule` returns the bare `Module`; when the presheaf needs the `ModuleCat` value, construct with all instances explicit (`@ModuleCat.of ‚Ä¶ AddCommGroup Module`). **Next bricks (precise handoff):** restriction maps `(M|_U‚ü∂N|_U)‚Üí(M|_V‚ü∂N|_V)` over `R(g)` via `Over.map g` precomposition; then `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` assembly (the bulk); then `dual`/`eval`/sheafify. Recipe also in memory `[[ts219-internalhom-value-built]]`. **`homModule` (a `def` of class type `Module`) needs `@[implicit_reducible]`.**
- **The substrate linchpin `tensorObj_restrict_iso` is CLOSED axiom-clean (iter-217, FIRST sorry elimination in 7 iters, project 81‚Üí80) ‚ÄĒ H1 = de-sheafify the SHEAF pushforward API to the PRESHEAF level + close by `leftAdjointUniq`; H2 = build `Functor.Monoidal.őľIso` over the SYNTACTIC `‚čô forget‚āā` base form and `exact` it against the defeq goal** *(NEW iter-217 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`; `tensorObj_restrict_iso` `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, NO `sorryAx`; INDEPENDENTLY axiom-verified + judged genuine by BOTH lean-auditor ts217 and lean-vs-blueprint-checker ts217 ‚ÄĒ the latter confirmed an exact 4-step blueprint match.)* The closed proof is the blueprint's 4-step composite: Step 1 `restrictFunctorIsoPullback f` (Mathlib), Step 2 `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` (Mathlib), Step 3 `sheafification.mapIso` (reduces to the presheaf goal `(pullback ŌÜ).obj (M.val ‚äó‚āö N.val) ‚ČÖ (M.restrict f).val ‚äó‚āö (N.restrict f).val`), Step 4 = H1+H2. **H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient ‚ÄĒ `pushforward ő≤ ‚ČÖ pullback ŌÜ`):** de-sheafify the three sheaf-level decls of `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean` (`pushforwardNatTrans` L154, `pushforwardCongr` L73, `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` L226) to the `PresheafOfModules` level ‚ÄĒ MECHANICAL because every line of the sheaf template already manipulates `.val`/`.val.presheaf` presheaf data; drop the `Sheaf` wrapper + the sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s. Then close H1 in ONE call: `hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction ŌÜR)` ‚ÄĒ **`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` ALREADY EXISTS** (`Presheaf/Pullback.lean:50`); do NOT re-derive it. **GOTCHA 1 (the open-immersion triangle defeq):** discharge the `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` unit/counit `H‚āĀ`/`H‚āā` triangle goals verbatim from Mathlib's sheaf `restrictAdjunction` template via `f.app_appIso_inv` + `congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)` ‚ÄĒ do NOT hand-roll a local `h0` eqToHom lemma (brittle; `congr($h0 x)` fails). **GOTCHA 2 (zeta-transparency ‚ÄĒ load-bearing):** keep `ŌÜR`/`őĪ`/`ő≤` as `let`-bindings and INLINE the open-immersion adjunction `f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction` ‚ÄĒ a `have`/`set` makes `adj.unit` OPAQUE and breaks the `congr` defeq on the triangle goals (matches the inline style of Mathlib's sheaf `restrictAdjunction`); also `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` on the adj def. **H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward ő≤ = pushforward‚āÄ ‚čô restrictScalars ő≤`):** `ő≤` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget‚āā`-image of the open-immersion structure ring ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars ő≤` is STRONG monoidal via the new `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective` (which lifts the file's lax instance through `isIso_of_isIso_app` + the iter-216 `restrictScalars_isIso_{őľ,őĶ}_of_bijective`); `pushforward‚āÄOfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib). **GOTCHA 3 (kernel-rejected diamond ‚ÄĒ the make-or-break technique):** do NOT add local `MonoidalCategory` instances on the `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` base form to build the tensorator there ‚ÄĒ it produces a `‚äó`-vs-`Monoidal.tensorObj` instance DIAMOND the KERNEL REJECTS (20 `declaration type mismatch` errors observed). INSTEAD build `Functor.Monoidal.őľIso` for the composite over the SYNTACTIC `_ ‚čô forget‚āā` base (where the `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` instance is found canonically), then `exact (őľIso ‚Ä¶).symm` against the goal whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only DEFEQ (not syntactic) `X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā`; `(pushforward ő≤).obj M.val = (M.restrict f).val` is definitional so the final `exact` matches by defeq. **5 new reusable axiom-clean decls** (`PresheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr, pushforwardPushforwardAdj, isIso_of_isIso_app, restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}`) ‚ÄĒ the first three are presheaf-level analogues of the sheaf API and are upstream-Mathlib-PR candidates. Recipe also in memory `[[ts-assoc-flatness-gap]]` + `analogies/ts217.md`. **Note:** `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective` (presheaf, bijective form) is DISTINCT from `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv` (module-level, ring-equiv form); a `def` whose type is the `.Monoidal` class needs `@[implicit_reducible]`.
- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring iso is STRONG monoidal ‚ÄĒ the H2 ModuleCat core (`IsIso őľ`/`IsIso őĶ`/`.Monoidal` packaging), CLOSED axiom-clean in-file** *(NEW iter-216 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:197-279`, 6 decls; `restrictScalars_isIso_őľ` `lean_verify` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, no `sorryAx`; lean-auditor ts216 judged all 6 genuine, 0 must-fix.)* Mathlib ships `extendScalars` strong-monoidal but only `restrictScalars` lax; this upgrades `restrictScalars` along `e : R ‚ČÉ+* S`. **Recipe for `IsIso őľ`:** `rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]`, then prove `‚áĎ(Functor.LaxMonoidal.őľ (restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M‚āĀ M‚āā) = ‚áĎ(restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M‚āĀ M‚āā)` by `TensorProduct.induction_on` and conclude bijective via `LinearEquiv.bijective`. **Critical tactic facts (each a failed attempt this iter):** (a) the `tmul` case needs **`erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_őľ_tmul]`** (NOT `rw` ‚ÄĒ universe/instance elaboration) then `exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul e M‚āĀ M‚āā a b).symm`; (b) the `zero` case: `rw [map_zero, map_zero]` FAILS ("Did not find pattern `?f 0`") and `simp only [map_zero]` leaves `‚äĘ 0 = equiv 0` ‚ÄĒ use **`rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm`**; (c) `add` case `rw [map_add, hx, hy]; exact (map_add _ x y).symm`. **`IsIso őĶ`:** `‚áĎőĶ = ‚áĎe` via `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_ő∑`, then `e.bijective`. **Packaging:** `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv := by haveI := restrictScalars_isIso_őĶ e; haveI := fun M‚āĀ M‚āā => restrictScalars_isIso_őľ e M‚āĀ M‚āā; exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _` ‚ÄĒ a `def` whose type is the class `.Monoidal` **must carry `@[implicit_reducible]`** (else "Definition of class type must be marked reducible"). **Bijective-ring-hom forms** (`restrictScalars_isIso_{őľ,őĶ}_of_bijective` for `f : R ‚Üí+* S` + `Bijective f`) reduce via `RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf` + `RingHom.ext (fun _ => rfl)` ‚ÄĒ needed because the presheaf NatIso's componentwise `(őĪ.app X).hom` is bijective but not literally `(_ : R ‚ČÉ+* S).toRingHom`. **Reusable** for any "strong-monoidal restrictScalars along an iso / base-change-along-ring-iso commutes with ‚äó" obligation. **Caveat (lean-auditor BP-1):** `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul` is `@[simp]` but its LHS/RHS live in a `letI` context ‚ÄĒ it likely will NOT fire in real `simp` call-sites; supply it `exact`-style.
- **Base change of modules along a *ring isomorphism* commutes with `‚äó` ‚ÄĒ the H2 "REAL bottom gap" of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, CLOSED axiom-clean in-file** *(NEW iter-215 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:115-193`, `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`, verified `axioms = [propext, Quot.sound]`, NO `sorryAx`; lean-auditor ts215 traced every step "genuine, complete, no hidden sorry/admit", 0 must-fix.)* For `e : R ‚ČÉ+* S` and `S`-modules `A, B` (each given its `R`-structure via `Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom`), the canonical map `a ‚äó‚āú[R] b ‚Ü¶ a ‚äó‚āú[S] b` is an `R`-linear equivalence `A ‚äó[R] B ‚ČÉ‚āó[R] A ‚äó[S] B`. Equivalently `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring iso is *strong* monoidal (its lax tensorator is invertible) ‚ÄĒ the exact Mathlib-absent fact (Mathlib has `extendScalars` strong but `restrictScalars` only `LaxMonoidal`). **THE key trick that unblocks the cross-ring friction** (this is the reusable lesson): build the inverse `bwd : A‚äó[S]B ‚Üí A‚äó[R]B` as an **`AddHom` via `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`**, NOT as an `S`-linear `TensorProduct.lift` into an `e.symm`-base-changed `R`-tensor ‚ÄĒ the latter triggers unsatisfiable `SMulCommClass R S A` / `CompatibleSMul R S A B` synthesis. Then promote `bwd` to `R`-linear by `TensorProduct.induction_on` (the `R`-action on the `S`-tensor is `r ‚ÄĘ x = e r ‚ÄĘ x` by `compHom`, closed with `smul_tmul'` + `rfl`). Round-trips: **right inverse** `fwd‚ąėbwd=id` needs `LinearMap.ext` + `induction_on` (the composite is `R`-linear over the `S`-tensor, so `TensorProduct.ext'` ‚ÄĒ which expects `S`-linearity ‚ÄĒ type-mismatches); **left inverse** `bwd‚ąėfwd=id` uses `TensorProduct.ext'` over `R` + `rfl`. Scalar-swap compatibility on `liftAddHom` uses `s ‚ÄĘ a = e (e.symm s) ‚ÄĘ a` via `e.apply_symm_apply`. Use `change` (not `show`) throughout for the `compHom`-defeq scalar goals. **Reusable** for any "base change along a ring iso / strong-monoidal `restrictScalars`" obligation. **Residual status update**: with H2 closed, the linchpin `tensorObj_restrict_iso` now bottoms out in **H1 alone** ‚ÄĒ a presheaf-level `pushforward ő≤ ‚ČÖ pullback ŌÜ` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`, needing a presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (only the SHEAF version exists; re-confirmed absent via loogle this iter), ~100‚Äď150 LOC ‚ÄĒ plus a ~20-LOC packaging step (`restrictScalars_őľ_isIso` wrapper ‚Üí `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal`). See `informal/tensorObj_restrict_iso.md`.
- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a `PresheafOfModules` morphism (d.1 linearity core) via a germ-chase ‚ÄĒ and the corrected fact that Mathlib HAS the module stalk** *(NEW iter-214 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` ¬ß`StalkLinearMap`, 4 axiom-clean decls `stalkLinearMap`, `stalkLinearMap_germ`, `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`, `stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso`; germ-chase verified honest step-by-step by lean-auditor ts214.)* **(0) FACTUAL CORRECTION to multi-iter premise**: PROGRESS.md / `analogies/ts-monoidalloc214.md` claimed "no `PresheafOfModules` stalk/fiber/point infra (only `Presheaf/ColimitFunctor`)". **FALSE.** `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean` (Andrew Yang, 2026) supplies, for `X : TopCat`, `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, `M : PresheafOfModules (R ‚čô forget‚āā _ _)`: `instance : Module (R.stalk x) ‚ÜĎ(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` + `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`. The module stalk does NOT need building; only the *linearity packaging* of the induced Ab-stalk map was absent. **(1) Recipe for `stalkLinearMap`** (the `R.stalk x`-linear upgrade of `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`): `toFun` = that Ab-stalk map's `ConcreteCategory.hom`; `map_add'` = `map_add`; `map_smul'` = a germ-chase: `obtain ‚ü®U,_,r‚āÄ,rfl‚ü© := germ_exist R x r; obtain ‚ü®V,_,s‚āÄ,rfl‚ü© := germ_exist M.presheaf x s; set W := U ‚äď V`; push both germs to the common refinement via `germ_res_apply`; then `rw [hr, hs, ‚Üź germ_smul M x W, key, map_smul, germ_smul N x W, key]` where `key : stalkFunctor.map g (germ_M_W z) = germ_N_W (g.app z)` is proved from `toPresheaf_map_app_apply` + `stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply`. **Direct `rw` on stalkFunctor-map applications FAILS** (`Did not find pattern ?f (?x+?y)`; `failed to synthesize AddCommMonoid (stalk ?m x)`) due to concrete-category `hom` wrapping ‚ÄĒ the explicit germ-chase is the working pattern. **(2)** `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`: `change Function.Bijective ‚áĎ(ConcreteCategory.hom (...)); exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _`. `stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso := LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (...)`. **Reusable** for any stalk-level `R_x`-linearity on `PresheafOfModules`. **Residual to finish `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`** (still 2 gaps): (d.1-bridge) `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map f) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ x, IsIso ((stalkFunctor Ab x).map ...)` via `WEqualsLocallyBijective` + `locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks` + `app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso`, ~80‚Äď150 LOC; (d.2) `(F ‚äóŠĶĖ M).stalk x ‚ČÖ F_x ‚äó_{R_x} M_x` over a VARYING ring, genuinely Mathlib-absent, ~150‚Äď250 LOC. **MUST specialise the lemma from a general site `C` (no stalks) to `C = Opens X` first** (decl UNPROTECTED; only consumer already runs over `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`).
- **The ‚äó-substrate associator `tensorObj_assoc_iso` is ASSEMBLED via ROUTE (d) (flatness-free, stalkwise) modulo ONE feasibility-confirmed residual** *(NEW iter-213 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`; the 8-iter-stuck associator is now a complete compiling 3-step composite, axioms `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` = `sorryAx` from the lone residual only, no project axioms; verified clean compile + lean-auditor ts213 "mathematically coherent, residual correctly stated".)* Three reusable facts: **(1) Preserve the COMBINED iso, not injectivity alone.** To show `F ‚óĀ g ‚ąą J.W` for an *arbitrary* presheaf-of-modules `F` and `g ‚ąą J.W`, prove local *bijectivity*: surjectivity is free (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness), and bijectivity is stalkwise-iso-stable under `id_F ‚äó ‚ąí` (`(F ‚óĀ g)_x = id_{F_x} ‚äó g_x`, an iso because a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso). This needs NO flatness and NO local triviality ‚ÄĒ `W_whiskerLeft_of_W` / `W_whiskerRight_of_W` (both closed this iter) do exactly this. **This is the root-cause fix for the iters 206‚Äď212 stall**: those split `J.W` into inj+surj and chased *injectivity alone*, which genuinely needs sectionwise flatness (FALSE for invertibles over non-affine opens; attempt 2 this iter re-confirmed the Tor‚āĀ obstruction ‚ÄĒ `ker(id_F‚äóg_U)` is not generated by `F‚äóker(g_U)` without flatness, so the flat route has no section-level swap). **(2) `rfl`-defeq carrier bridge dissolves the iter-212 heartbeat wall.** `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat` holds by `rfl`; transport a missing typeclass instance across it with `letI inst : MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) := inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat)))`. The iter-212 `synthInstance`/`whnf` timeout on `X.ringCatSheaf.val` is GONE. **(3) Composite recipe:** `(asIso hi1).symm ‚Č™‚Čę a.mapIso (presheaf associator) ‚Č™‚Čę asIso hi3`, where `a = sheafification (ūĚüô X.ringCatSheaf.val)`, `hi1/hi3 = isIso_sheafification_map_of_W _ _ (W_whiskerRight/Left_of_W ‚Ä¶)`, and the whiskered unit lies in `J.W` via `toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app` (rfl) + `GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify`. **The associator is strictly STRONGER than the blueprint statement** (arbitrary modules; the `IsLocallyTrivial` hyps `hM hN hP` are dead, retained only to match the pin). See `analogies/ts-monoidal213.md` (the 4-realization map) and `informal/isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W.md` (the residual write-up).
- **Two sheafification-bridge idioms for the ‚äó-group law (the iter-211 "remaining bounded residual" partially discharged, axiom-clean)** *(NEW iter-212 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`; `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (the designated go/no-go gate) + `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`, both axiom-clean `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`, NO `sorryAx`; both confirmed GENUINE CLOSE non-vacuous by lean-auditor ts212.)* (1) **"`J.W f ‚áí IsIso (sheafification.map f)`" is a 3-line rewrite, NOT the plan's 3-step recipe.** The iter-211 KB entry budgeted "toPresheaf reflects isos + AddCommGrpCat-sheafification-is-localization + compatibility iso, ~80‚Äď150 LOC"; the actual route is Mathlib's ready identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` (`J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R‚āÄ) = (isomorphisms (SheafOfModules R)).inverseImage (sheafification őĪ)`, module `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Localization`): read at one morphism `f`, `J.W (toPresheaf.map f)` IS `(J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R‚āÄ)) f`, rewrite to `(isomorphisms _).inverseImage (sheafification őĪ) f` = `IsIso (sheafification.map f)`. **`sheafification` IS the localization** at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R‚āÄ)`. (2) **Braiding-conjugate a left-whisker lemma to a right-whisker one, with EXPLICIT reassociation.** `W_whiskerRight_of_flat` from `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` via `g ‚Ė∑ F = (ő≤_ M F).hom ‚Čę (F ‚óĀ g) ‚Čę (ő≤_ N F).inv`: `braiding_naturality_left` matches a 2-term composite, so `rw [‚Üź braiding_naturality_left g F]` FAILS on the 3-term target ("Did not find an occurrence of `(ő≤_ M F).hom ‚Čę F ‚óĀ g`"); reassociate first ‚ÄĒ `rw [‚Üź Category.assoc, ‚Üź BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]` ‚ÄĒ then `Functor.map_comp` + `J.W.cancel_left/right_of_respectsIso` strip the iso braidings to land on `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg`. **Reusable** for any localizer-class whiskering conjugation. **Caveat (see Known Blockers ‚ÄĒ the iter-211 residual estimate was WRONG):** these two bridges close cleanly, but the iter-211 KB claim that the associator's "single gap" was the `IsIso (a.map f)` bridge + a "cheap `IsInvertible ‚áí sectionwise Module.Flat` derivation" is FALSE ‚ÄĒ the flatness derivation is the real wall, not cheap (it is unprovable; see below).
- **The ‚äó-invertibility group-law gate `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat` is PROVEN axiom-clean in Lean ‚ÄĒ flat-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer with NO `MonoidalClosed`** *(NEW iter-211 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:222-340`, 3 axiom-clean decls `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat`, `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`; blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`; the iter-209/210 pivot's go/no-go GATE, CLEARED ‚ÄĒ reversal trigger did NOT fire.)* For a presheaf of modules `F` sectionwise-flat (`[‚ąÄ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]`) and `g : M ‚ü∂ N` in the sheafification localizer `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)`, the left-whiskered `F ‚óĀ g` is again in `J.W`. **Recipe**: package the two halves via `rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ‚äĘ; exact ‚ü®isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1, isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2‚ü©`. Surjectivity half = pure right-exactness (`TensorProduct.induction_on` element-chase, NO flatness). Injectivity half = where flatness enters: `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)` on the kernel exact sequence `ker(g‚āď) ‚Ü™ M(X) ‚Üí N(X)`, then `TensorProduct.induction_on` on the witness `ő∂` with each simple tensor restricting to 0 on a covering sieve (`Presheaf.equalizerSieve` + `hg.equalizerSieve_mem`). **Three reusable idioms (each cost a session somewhere in the window):** (1) **Localizer universe binder MUST be explicit `Ab.{u}`** ‚ÄĒ the instance binder is `[J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]` (`Ab` = target of `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf`); without the explicit universe, `Ab` defaults to a different universe and synthesis fails. `PresheafOfModules.IsLocallySurjective J g` is *defeq* to `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J ((toPresheaf _).map g)`, so the half-lemmas slot straight into the `W_iff` unfolding. (2) **`tmul_zero` under `restrictScalars`** ‚ÄĒ when a tensor factor elaborates in `(ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f.op)).obj (F.obj X)`, `TensorProduct.tmul_zero`'s `Module` instance is NOT synthesizable on the displayed (wrapped) type; `rw`/`exact`/`simp`/`module`/`abel` ALL fail. Use `erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl` (erw matches up to the restrictScalars defeq; the trailing `0 = 0` needs the explicit `rfl`). (3) **Forcing a monoidal/braided instance through a non-syntactic type** ‚ÄĒ `M.val : PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` is defeq-but-not-*syntactically* `PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat)`, so the `őĽ_`/`ŌĀ_`/`ő≤_` notation FAILS (type ascription is transparent, does not redirect instance resolution). **Force the instance explicitly:** unitors via `(PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val` / `.rightUnitor M.val`; braiding via `BraidedCategory.braiding (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val` (a `BraidedCategory (PresheafOfModules ‚Ä¶)` instance DOES exist in Mathlib). The unitors/braiding/`tensorObj_unit_iso` all close via the same `sheafification.mapIso (coherence) ‚Č™‚Čę (asIso sheafificationAdjunction.counit).app M` pattern, all axiom-clean. **Reusable** for any "lift a sectionwise-flat / coherence iso through PoM sheafification" need. **Remaining bounded residual** (the associator `tensorObj_assoc_iso`, still a typed sorry): the single gap is the bridge `IsIso (a.map f)` from `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f)` (`a` = PoM sheafification) ‚ÄĒ NOT a Mathlib one-liner; build from `toPresheaf` reflecting isos + the underlying `AddCommGrpCat`-sheafification being a localization at `J.W` (`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isIso_map_of_adjunction`) + the `IsInvertible ‚áí sectionwise Module.Flat` derivation + a cheap `W_whiskerRight_of_flat` (conjugate by the braiding). Est. ~80‚Äď150 LOC; the topology `J` is the small-site one of `X.ringCatSheaf`, NOT `Scheme.zariskiTopology`.
- **Strip an outer sheafification from an iso goal via `sheafification.mapIso` ‚ÄĒ reduce a `SheafOfModules`-level iso to a presheaf-level residual** *(NEW iter-208 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:356`, one verified reduction step.)* When both sides of an iso goal in `SheafOfModules R` are `(PresheafOfModules.sheafification őĪ).obj _` applied to presheaves of modules (here after `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `sheafificationCompPullback` deposit the LHS as `sheafification.obj ((pullback ŌÜ).obj (M.val ‚äó‚āö N.val))` and the RHS is `sheafification.obj ((M.restrict f).val ‚äó‚āö (N.restrict f).val)` *by definition*), close the outer layer with `refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (ūĚüô Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_` ‚ÄĒ the residual is the purely presheaf-level comparison, with no sheafification (verify via `lean_goal`). **Reusable** for any "lift a presheaf-of-modules iso through sheafification" reduction. **Caveat**: this exposes ‚ÄĒ does NOT close ‚ÄĒ the presheaf residual; if that residual is an `(PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ).obj (A ‚äó‚āö B) ‚ČÖ ‚Ä¶` base change, you then hit the opaque-abstract-left-adjoint wall (see Known Blockers: the genuine closure is the H1/H2 `pushforward`-bridge route, NOT a sectionwise unfold).
- **Sectionwise lift of a `Functor.LaxMonoidal` to the presheaf-of-modules level** *(NEW iter-207 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:95-176`, 3 axiom-clean decls `restrictScalarsLaxőĶ`, `restrictScalarsLaxőľ`, instance `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`; blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`.)* For a presheaf-of-modules functor that acts *sectionwise* as a known lax-monoidal `ModuleCat` functor (here `PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars őĪ` acting as `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (őĪ.app X).hom`), build the `LaxMonoidal` instance sectionwise: (1) `őĶ`/`őľ` fields take the per-section `Functor.LaxMonoidal.őĶ/őľ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (őĪ.app X).hom)`; (2) every **coherence** field (`őľ_natural_left/right`, `associativity`, `left/right_unitality`) reduces to the already-proven ModuleCat-level law via `intro ‚Ä¶; ext1 Z; exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.<law> (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (őĪ.app Z).hom) ‚Ä¶`; (3) the two **naturality** fields (`őĶ`/`őľ` naturality) need `erw` chains, NOT `simp` (simp stalls under the definitional unfolding) ‚ÄĒ őĶ: `ext r; dsimp; erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_ő∑, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_ő∑]; simp only [map_one]; erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]`; őľ: `refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m‚āĀ m‚āā ‚Ü¶ ?_); dsimp; erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_őľ_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_őľ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]; rfl`. **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` is required** (mirrors Mathlib's own `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal` setup). The monoidal structure requires `CommRingCat`-valued presheaves of rings, so the instance is stated over `R S : CŠĶíŠĶĖ ‚•§ CommRingCat` with `őĪ : R ‚čô forget‚āā ‚Ä¶ ‚ü∂ S ‚čô forget‚āā ‚Ä¶`. **Reusable** for any "lift a sectionwise lax-monoidal module functor to presheaves." **Caveat (see Known Blockers)**: this does NOT by itself supply `(pushforward ŌÜ).LaxMonoidal` for the abstract-adjoint őī-route ‚ÄĒ the scheme ring presheaf is `RingCat`-valued and the CommRingCat-level composition fails on `‚čô`-vs-`forget‚āā` associativity.
- **`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` IS in Mathlib + the "abstract left adjoint has no comparison map" trap** *(NEW iter-206 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`.)* Two reusable facts surfaced while advancing `tensorObj_restrict_iso`: (1) **`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback ŌÜ` exists** (`.../Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackContinuous.lean`): `sheafification ‚čô pullback ŌÜ ‚ČÖ PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ.hom ‚čô sheafification`. This **corrects a multi-iter false premise** that "sheafification does not commute with pullback at the `SheafOfModules` level." Use it to move a `pullback` past `sheafification` for any scheme-`Modules` restrict/pullback. Pair with `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f` (restrict ‚ČÖ pullback along an open immersion). (2) **The abstract-left-adjoint trap**: a functor defined as `(rightAdjoint).leftAdjoint` (e.g. `PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ := (pushforward ŌÜ).leftAdjoint`, `Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44`) has **no sectionwise formula**, hence **no canonical monoidal comparison map to even state** ‚ÄĒ its oplax-monoidal structure (the mate of the right adjoint's lax structure) must be *constructed* before any flat-exactness/bijectivity argument applies. Before planning a flat-exactness closure of `F(A‚äóB) ‚ČÖ FA ‚äó FB`, check whether `F` is concrete (sectionwise) or an abstract adjoint; if the latter, the map-construction is the real work and flatness is downstream of it.
- **Monoidal structure on a localized category via `Localization.Monoidal`, with `IsMonoidal W` reduced to `whiskerLeft` alone** *(NEW iter-205 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:411-452`, 2 axiom-clean `noncomputable def`s reducing the `monoidalCategory`/`tensorObj_restrict_iso` cone to one fact, in compiled Lean.)* To put a `MonoidalCategory` on a localized/sheafified category `SheafOfModules R`: (1) **`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`** ‚ÄĒ `inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategory (LocalizedMonoidal (L := sheafification őĪ) (W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)) (Iso.refl _)))`, i.e. `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.instMonoidalCategoryLocalizedMonoidal` needs the source monoidal structure (the relative `‚äó_R` on `PresheafOfModules R‚āÄ`, exists), the localization instance (`PresheafOfModules.sheafification.IsLocalization W`, exists), `[W.IsMonoidal]` (the gap), and a unit iso (`Iso.refl` since the localization functor is monoidal-on-unit). (2) **`isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`** ‚ÄĒ `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal W` decomposes to the SINGLE hypothesis `whiskerLeft : W g ‚Üí W (F ‚óĀ g)`: `IsMultiplicative W` is a free instance (inverseImage of a localizing property), and `whiskerRight` follows from `whiskerLeft` via the symmetric braiding: `(W.arrow_mk_iso_iff (Arrow.isoMk (ő≤_ F‚āĀ G) (ő≤_ F‚āā G))).2 (hwl hf G)` (requires the source category braided/symmetric, which `PresheafOfModules R‚āÄ` is). **Reusable** for any "monoidal structure descends through a localization" need; the two `def`s are permanent infrastructure that the eventual `whiskerLeft` discharge plugs straight into. **Syntactic-vs-defeq caveat for the scheme-level wiring**: the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal instance keys on `X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat` while the sheafification instances auto-resolve only for `X.ringCatSheaf.val` ‚ÄĒ `rfl`-defeq but synthesised syntactically, so the two forms must be bridged by hand with `inferInstanceAs`.
- **Honest-sorry containment** (iter-203 flag, **RESOLVED iter-206**, TS): a structure you cannot yet prove but must not contaminate consumers should be a `def Foo := sorry`, NEVER an `instance Foo := sorry`. The instance injects `sorryAx` transitively into every downstream consumer; the def localizes the sorry. **Resolution**: the offending `TensorObjSubstrate.monoidalCategory` `noncomputable instance ‚Ä¶ := sorry` (former L150) was **removed entirely** iter-206 ‚ÄĒ together with the off-path `MonoidalClosed`-route supplements (`isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`, `monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) ‚ÄĒ because the full `MonoidalCategory` on all modules is off the relative-Picard critical path (the group law on iso-classes consumes no coherence data; see blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`). lean-auditor iter206 confirmed no dangling references. The principle stands: the remaining `exists_tensorObj_inverse`/`addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` are honest `def`/typed-sorry, not instances.
- **Re-test multi-iter "Mathlib gap" blockers** (iter-203/204, TS): a blocker assumed for several iters can be a stale/incorrect premise (line-bundle sheafification via `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`; `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial` is a genuine predicate, not a sorry). Re-verify a long-standing blocker before budgeting more iters around it.

- **Matsumura "linearly-independent cotangent classes ‚üĻ regular sequence" (Step A1) closure** *(NEW iter-203 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane COE in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` ¬ß3.C, 4 axiom-clean decls; HARD BAR for the 26-iter-stalled Step A1.)* On a regular local Noetherian ring `(A,ūĚĒ™)`, a sequence `rs : Fin n ‚Üí A` whose cotangent classes `(maximalIdeal A).toCotangent ‚ü®rs i,_‚ü©` are `őļ(A)`-linearly independent forms a `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A (List.ofFn rs)`. **Recipe**: induction on `n`, peeling the head via a *ring-quotient* form of Mathlib's `IsRegular.cons'`. Three reusable substrate pieces: (1) **`quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv (r)` : `QuotSMulTop r A ‚ČÉ‚āó[A‚ßłspan{r}] A‚ßłspan{r}`** ‚ÄĒ `QuotSMulTop r A` (= `A‚ßł(r‚ÄĘ‚ä§)`) is NOT defeq to `A‚ßłspan{r}`; build the equiv explicitly from `QuotSMulTop.equivQuotTensor` + `TensorProduct.rid` + `LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective` along surjective `mk`, transport `IsRegular` with `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. (2) **`isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring`** ‚ÄĒ peels a head through that equiv. (3) **`matsumura_descent_cotangent`** (the genuinely hard piece, semilinear over the `őļ(A)‚Üíőļ(A')` residue switch ‚ÄĒ the blueprint's "straightforward `LinearIndependent.map`" was wrong): build `ŌÄ = Ideal.mapCotangent`; prove `ker ŌÄ = A‚ąôtoCotangent(rs 0)` via `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective`; run the descent **directly through `Fintype.linearIndependent_iff`** (avoids constructing quotient őļ-modules / explicit residue-field isos); needs `set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000` (the `linearIndependent_iff` + cotangent-kernel computation are heartbeat-heavy ‚ÄĒ bump is justified, not masking a loop). The head's NZD-ness comes from `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal` + `IsSMulRegular.of_ne_zero`; the quotient stays regular local via `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq`. **The landed signature drops the blueprint's `(hn : ringKrullDim A = n)` hypothesis** ‚ÄĒ the dimension is read off `spanFinrank` internally. **Reusable** for any "linearly-independent cotangent classes descend / form a regular sequence" need. (Step A1 alone does NOT close any top-level COE sorry ‚ÄĒ see Known Blockers re Step A2.)

- **Sheafification of `Scheme.Modules` is NOT a Mathlib gap ‚ÄĒ use `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`** *(NEW iter-203 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane TS in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, `tensorObj` + `tensorObj_functoriality` axiom-clean; corrects a multi-iter false premise.)* The TS lane had been scaffolded for iters on the belief that sheafifying `Scheme.Modules` was a Mathlib gap. It is not. Two facts: (i) `Scheme.Modules X = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`, and `X.ringCatSheaf.val` is *definitionally* `X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat` (`Scheme.ringCatSheaf` is `@[reducible]`), so for `M : X.Modules`, `M.val` is exactly what `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)` consumes ‚ÄĒ no change-of-rings reconciliation; (ii) `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (őĪ : R‚āÄ ‚ü∂ R.val) [...] : PresheafOfModules R‚āÄ ‚•§ SheafOfModules R` exists and is axiom-clean (for `R := X.ringCatSheaf`, `őĪ := ūĚüô ‚Ä¶`, all four instances auto-resolve for the opens site). `tensorObj := (sheafification ‚Ä¶).obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`; functoriality via `(sheafification ‚Ä¶).map (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom f.val g.val)`. **Two reusable gotchas**: (a) **`AddCommGrpCat`, NOT `AddCommGrp`** ‚ÄĒ `HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) AddCommGrpCat.{u}` synthesizes only under the current name; the deprecated alias `AddCommGrp` does NOT carry the instance (cost most of a session). (b) **Ascribe to the unfolded type** `SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`, not `X.Modules`, else `?R` stays a stuck metavariable; for functoriality the `(C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat))` annotation is REQUIRED (so `?R := X.presheaf` unifies) and must be `_root_.`-prefixed inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules`. The remaining `monoidalCategory` instance is a separate monoidal-transport task (sheafification ‚ä£ forget reflective adjunction; do NOT hand-discharge pentagon/triangle) ‚ÄĒ keep as a contained `:= sorry` so no axiom-clean decl synthesizes `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`.

- **Auslander‚ÄďBuchsbaum `_succ_pd` closure via `induction k generalizing M` (syzygy-descent inductive step + matrix-collapse base case)** *(NEW iter-202 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane AB-Path-B-Close in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, the 16+-iter gap CLOSED axiom-clean; 4 new helpers + body.)* The full Auslander‚ÄďBuchsbaum formula `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula` is now axiom-clean. Closure recipe for `_succ_pd`: restructure as `induction k generalizing M`. **Inductive step (`pd M = k+2`)**: minimal surjection `f : R^n ‚Ü† M`; `projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection` squeezes `pd(ker f)=k+1` exactly (`projectiveDimension_le_iff` upper bound + `projectiveDimension_ge_iff` contrapositive lower bound, both off the iter-200 `HasProjectiveDimensionLT` ascent/descent helpers); IH on `ker f`; `Module.depth_ses_ineqs_of_surjection_finite_localRing` packages `depth_of_short_exact` parts (2)(3) (with `depth(R^n)=depth(R)` via `depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty`); `enat_ab_inductive_combine` does the `‚Ąē‚ąě` arithmetic. **Base case (`pd M = 1`)**: consumes the iter-201 matrix-collapse substrate ‚ÄĒ `ker f` free, SES `0‚ÜíR^k‚Üí[A]R^n‚Üí[f]M‚Üí0` with `A`'s entries in `ūĚĒ™=Ann őļ`; `exists_ne_zero_ext_of_depth_eq` gives nonzero `őĪ:Ext^D(őļ,R^k)`, matrix-collapse kills `őĪ.comp(mk‚āÄ(ofHom A))`, `covariant_sequence_exact‚āĀ` transports to `Ext^{D'}(őļ,M)`, `Order.add_one_le_of_lt` finishes. **Stacks 00MF was OBVIATED** by this route (no "what is exact" criterion needed). **Key `‚Ąē‚ąě`/Lean idioms (reusable)**: (a) `‚Ąē‚ąě = WithTop ‚Ąē` has NO `IsStrictOrderedRing` ‚ÄĒ reduce `min`/`sub`/`+` facts to `‚Ąē` disjunctions via `Nat.cast_*` then `omega`; (b) use `by gcongr` (not `add_le_add_right`) for monotone `+1` on `‚Ąē‚ąě`; (c) `ShortComplex.mk ‚Ä¶ (zero proof)` discharges via `rw [‚Üź ModuleCat.ofHom_comp, show f ‚ąė‚āó A = 0 ‚Ä¶]; rfl` (NOT `simp`); (d) free-kernel basis via `(Module.finBasis R K).equivFun.symm`, with `k‚Č•1` proved by subsingleton contradiction; (e) after `induction k generalizing M` the IH takes only the pd hypothesis. **Namespace caveat**: the de-privatised cross-file helpers are `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal` and `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` (NOT bare `RingTheory.‚Ä¶`).

- **`stalkSpecializes`/`germ` naturality: term-mode over `rw`, and `h_compat` discharge by defeq** *(NEW iter-202 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 3 in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:572-621`, 2 axiom-clean decls; HARD BAR.)* For morphism-level naturality of `stalkSpecializes` to a generic point (germ-chase via `TopCat.Presheaf.stalk_hom_ext`): (a) `(F.stalkCongr e).hom = F.stalkSpecializes e.ge` is `rfl` ‚ÄĒ there is NO Mathlib `stalkCongr_hom` lemma; introduce a local `have hcongr := by intros; rfl`. (b) **Plain `rw [TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes]` FAILS** ("pattern not found") on a terminal `germ ‚Čę stalkSpecializes` even when syntactically present; use `simp only [..._assoc]` to peel the composition then close in **term mode** `(germ_stalkSpecializes _ _ _).trans (germ_stalkSpecializes _ _ _).symm` (unifies via expected type). - **Bilinear `Ext` matrix-collapse via `mk‚āÄ_sum` + `mk‚āÄ_smul` + `comp_sum` + `comp_smul`** *(NEW iter-201 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane AB-Stacks-00MF Path B, `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:~1418-1481`, 4 axiom-clean helpers ~120 LOC.)* For a postcomposition `e.comp (mk‚āÄ (ofHom A))` where `A : R^m ‚Üí‚āó R^n` factors through a finite product, the matrix-collapse argument decomposes `A = ‚ąĎ A_{ij} ‚ÄĘ elemMap _ _ i j` (where `elemMap n m i j := toSpanSingleton R (Pi.single i 1) ‚ąė‚āó LinearMap.proj j` is the elementary-matrix linear map) then pushes through `ofHom` via `ModuleCat.hom_sum` + `ofHom_add`, through `mk‚āÄ` via `Ext.mk‚āÄ_sum` + `Ext.mk‚āÄ_smul`, and through `comp` via `Ext.comp_sum` + `Ext.comp_smul`. Each summand reduces to `A_{ij} ‚ÄĘ (e.comp (mk‚āÄ (ofHom (elemMap _ _ i j))))`, closable per-summand via a scalar-annihilator helper (e.g. `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` if all `A_{ij} ‚ąą Ann_R N`). **Critical**: the basis decomposition of the input vector `x = ‚ąĎ_j (x j) ‚ÄĘ Pi.single j 1` must happen BEFORE the outer matrix-decomposition sum reshuffle, not after ‚ÄĒ multiple `lean_run_code` iterations confirmed the reverse ordering breaks the rewrite pattern. **The absence of `ModuleCat.ofHom_smul` does NOT block this** ‚ÄĒ scalars are absorbed into the basis vector before `ofHom`. **Reusable** for ANY postcomposition `e.comp (mk‚āÄ (ofHom A))` argument where matrix entries lie in an annihilator / ideal; the iter-201 `ab-stacks00mf` analogist Path B verdict surfaced the recipe. Path B's binding new substrate for `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` base case `pd M = 1`; iter-202 closure of the body via Nat-induction restructuring will consume this.

Pair with `Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso` (`Scheme.Opens.stalkIso` at the generic point ‚Č™‚Čę `stalkCongr` along `genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion`) for the scheme-side packaging ‚ÄĒ both forced `noncomputable`.

- **`HasProjectiveDimensionLT` SES descent (Mathlib-aligned syzygy descent for AB-style pd/depth arithmetic)** *(NEW iter-200 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane AB-gap1-HasPdLT in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:1290-1404`, 4 axiom-clean helpers ~45 LOC.)* Replaces the literal `ChainComplex ‚Ąē (ModuleCat R)` carrier construction (3-4√ó over budget AND blocked on a termination-of-syzygy-tower lemma absent in Mathlib b80f227) with abstract syzygy descent via `ShortComplex.ShortExact.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_X‚āĀ` / `_X‚āÉ`. **Recipe**: (1) `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq` ‚ÄĒ single-rewrite bridge via `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_lt_iff` from the `Module.projectiveDimension R M = (n : WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě)` carrier to `HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) (n + 1)`; (2) `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection` ‚ÄĒ for a surjection `f : R^n ‚Üí‚āó M` with `HasProjectiveDimensionLT M (k+2)`, the kernel satisfies `HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ker f) (k+1)`, via `LinearMap.shortExact_shortComplexKer` + `ModuleCat.projective_of_free (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n))` + `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_of_ge` + `hS.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_X‚āĀ`; (3) `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker` ‚ÄĒ companion ascent via `_X‚āÉ`; (4) `depth_ker_ge_min_of_surjection_finite_localRing` ‚ÄĒ depth lower bound via `depth_of_short_exact (3)` + `depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty`. **Obviates gap (3)** (snake lemma on minimal resolution) of the AB chapter substrate decomposition entirely. **Residual binding gap**: Stacks 00MF (`pd M > 0 ‚üĻ depth M < depth R`, ~150-200 LOC Mathlib gap candidate) for inductive base case, OR a refined LES analysis proving `depth K ‚Č§ depth M + 1` directly via Ext connecting-map injectivity. **Reusable** for ANY Mathlib-aligned pd/depth arithmetic via SES descent; the recipe was surfaced by mathlib-analogist `ab-natrecursive` Path A (PROCEED verdict, ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB pivot).

- **MvPolynomial maximal-ideal height = n via `finSuccEquiv` + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one` + Jacobson contraction** *(NEW iter-200 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane COE-stage6-iiB Step 2 in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:696-738`, ~25 LOC core induction.)* For Step 2 of the Stacks 00OE Krull-dim formula on smooth k-algebras: every maximal ideal of `MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` (`k` a Field) has height exactly `n`. **Recipe**: induction on `Fin n`. General `[Finite őĻx]` form transports via `MvPolynomial.renameEquiv` + `RingEquiv.height_map`. **Capstone** `ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial` collapses Steps 1+2 into a single direct consumer call. **Dead end (do NOT retry)**: building `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`'s MvPolynomial analogue directly; the cleanest route is induction on `Fin n` (as landed), not on generic `Finite őĻ`. **Reusable** for ANY smooth-algebra Krull-dim formula whose polynomial-ring side needs height computation; the iter-200 mathlib-analogist `coe-stacks00oe` cross-domain-inspiration surfaced the recipe.

- **`Scheme.Opens.stalkIso` thin-wrap pattern for prime-divisor open-immersion descent** *(NEW iter-200 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 1 in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:153-305`, 8 axiom-clean substrate decls ~120 LOC.)* For descent of project-side `Scheme.PrimeDivisor`-bundled regularity / coheight properties across an open immersion `U.toScheme ‚ü∂ X`: thin-wrap Mathlib's `Scheme.Opens.stalkIso` + the iter-183 `CoheightBridge` `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding` substrate. **Recipe (8 decls)**: structure `ext` (cases + `congr`; `coheight` field is `Prop` so collapses); `restrictToOpen : { Y : X.PrimeDivisor // Y.point ‚ąą U } ‚Üí U.toScheme.PrimeDivisor` (thin layer over `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding`; defeq cast on the spec preorder); `ofOpen : U.toScheme.PrimeDivisor ‚Üí X.PrimeDivisor` (symmetric); `equivOpen : { Y // Y.point ‚ąą U } ‚ČÉ U.toScheme.PrimeDivisor` (round-trip; `left_inv`/`right_inv` are `rfl` via Subtype/structure eta); `stalkIso : U.toScheme.presheaf.stalk (restrictToOpen U Y hYU).point ‚ČÖ X.presheaf.stalk Y.point` (direct invocation, must be `noncomputable`); `IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instOpen` (refine on per-prime-divisor obligation; push open-side `YU` to `ofOpen U YU`; harvest `IsDiscreteValuationRing` on ambient-side stalk; transport via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing` along `(Scheme.Opens.stalkIso U YU.point).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv`). **Critical**: `IsLocallyNoetherian U.toScheme` is automatic from typeclass propagation; `IsIntegral U.toScheme` is NOT (open subschemes need non-emptiness ‚ÄĒ threaded explicitly in `instOpen`). **Reusable** for ANY codim-1 regularity descent across open immersion in a project-defined PrimeDivisor bundle; the iter-200 mathlib-analogist `wd-stacks02iz` recipe followed verbatim with one-line `noncomputable` friction.

- **Step 3 additive form for regular-sequence Krull-dim drop (no `HSub` on `WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě`)** *(NEW iter-200 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane COE-stage6-iiB Step 3 in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:790`, ~10 LOC.)* For the Krull-dim formula step `ringKrullDim (R' ‚ßł Ideal.ofList rs) = a - b` (where `a = ringKrullDim R'`, `b = rs.length`, `rs` regular sequence): `WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě` has NO `HSub` instance, so the subtraction form is NOT typeable. **Recipe**: phrase as the additive form `ringKrullDim (R' ‚ßł Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě) = (a : WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě)`, parameterized in `a, b : ‚Ąē` with `hDim : ringKrullDim R' = (a : WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě)` + `hLen : rs.length = b`.
