Pair with the Step 1+2 capstone `ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial` for the polynomial-side direct consumer call.

- **`Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection` retraction-as-iff for Stacks 02JK closed-point cotangent ‚ÜĒ K√§hler iso** *(NEW iter-199 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane COE-stage6-iiA `cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue` + 3 sibling helpers in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:466-628`, ~212 LOC axiom-clean.)* Closes the iter-198 substrate gap on Stacks 02JK in the closed-point case: for a local k-algebra `(S_m, m, őļ)` with `őļ/k` separable (e.g. k = kŐĄ and the point is kŐĄ-rational so `őļ = k`), produce canonical iso `(ker(algMap S‚āė őļ)).Cotangent ‚ČÉ‚āó[S‚āė] őļ ‚äó_S‚āė ő©[S‚āė‚ĀĄk]` axiom-clean. **Recipe (3-step, ~40-50 LOC core)**: (1) **retraction ‚Üí injection** via `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Basic`); this packages exactly the missing ingredient ‚ÄĒ a retraction `l : őļ ‚äó_S‚āė ő©[S‚āė‚ĀĄk] ‚Üí‚āó[S‚āė] m.Cotangent` satisfying `l ‚ąė‚āó kerCotangentToTensor = id` ‚ÄĒ as an iff-equivalent of `FormallySmooth k őļ`. The iter-198 attempts failed because they used only the right half of the conormal sequence. (2) **`Subsingleton ő©[őļ‚ĀĄk]` + `KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` ‚Üí surjection**: the `Subsingleton` typeclass discharges on kŐĄ-rational closed points via `KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective` (`Bijective(algMap k őļ)` ‚üĻ `Surjective(algMap k őļ)`). (3) **`LinearEquiv.ofBijective`** assembles. **Companion helpers** (4 declarations total in COE.lean): maximal-ideal repackaging via `rw [‚Üź hker]` + `IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq` + `IsLocalRing.ker_residue`; őļ-finrank corollary via `LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective` (S‚āė-linear ‚Üí őļ-linear) + iter-198 6.B-RHS substrate; closed-point bundled wrapper via `Bijective (algebraMap R őļ)` single hypothesis discharging both FS-residue + Subsingleton typeclasses through `RingHom.FormallySmooth.of_bijective` + `formallySmooth_algebraMap`. **Reusable** for ANY closed-point cotangent ‚ÜĒ K√§hler iso build where the residue field is formally smooth over the base (typically the kŐĄ-rational case via Nullstellensatz reduction). The iter-199 mathlib-analogist `coe-stacks02jk` report (cross-domain-inspiration mode) surfaced the recipe; consult `analogies/coe-stacks02jk.md` for the porting walkthrough.

- **`Pi.basisFun.constr R m` + `IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis` for per-step minimal-surjection in local-ring Module theory** *(NEW iter-199 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane AB-gap1 `exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing` in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:1198-1296`, ~99 LOC axiom-clean.)* Closes the first step of Stacks `lemma-add-trivial-complex`: for finite `R`-module `M` over local ring `(R, ūĚĒ™)` (Noetherian NOT required), exists `n : ‚Ąē` + surjection `f : (Fin n ‚Üí R) ‚Üí‚āó[R] M` with `n = dim_őļ(őļ ‚äó_R M)` AND `ker f ‚Č§ ūĚĒ™ ‚ÄĘ ‚ä§`. **Recipe (5-step)**: (1) **őļ-basis of residue-tensor** via `Module.finBasis` with `n := finrank őļ (őļ ‚äó_R M)`; (2) **lift basis to `m_i ‚ąą M`** via `TensorProduct.mk_surjective` + `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective` choosing `m_i` with `1 ‚äó m_i = b i`; (3) **define `f := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m`**; evaluation: `f x = ‚ąĎ_i x_i ‚ÄĘ m_i` via `Basis.constr_apply`; (4) **surjectivity** via `LinearMap.range f = span_R(m_i)` (`Basis.constr_range`) + `IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis` (the Nakayama-lift core: span_R(m_i) = ‚ä§ ‚üļ `(1 ‚äó m_i)` is a őļ-basis of őļ ‚äó M); (5) **kernel ‚äÜ ūĚĒ™ ‚ÄĘ ‚ä§** via `1 ‚äó -` applied to `‚ąĎ x_i ‚ÄĘ m_i = 0` + `TensorProduct.tmul_smul` + `Module.Basis.linearIndependent` of őļ-basis (forces each residue x_i = 0) + `IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff` (so each x_i ‚ąą ūĚĒ™) + `Pi.single`-decomposition via `Finset.univ_sum_single` (so x ‚ąą ūĚĒ™ ‚ÄĘ ‚ä§). **Iterates with itself**: each step's kernel is finite under `[IsNoetherianRing R]` (via `Submodule.fg_iff_finite` + `Module.Finite`), so re-applying produces a Nat-recursive minimal finite-free resolution of length `pd_R(M)`. **Reusable** for ANY local-ring minimal generator-set construction; foundation for iter-200+ Nat-recursive `ChainComplex ‚Ąē (ModuleCat R)` resolution construction (gap (1) full iteration), which then feeds the snake-lemma argument (gap (3)) and the AB-formula assembly.

- **Substrate-only iter as canonical response to multi-piece Mathlib infrastructure gaps** *(NEW iter-199 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane WD-A4a in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`, 2 axiom-clean PUSH-BEYOND helpers (`order_neg`, `order_pow_of_ne_zero`) + HARD BAR documented as 3 named Mathlib sub-builds.)* When a HARD BAR closure target depends on multiple Mathlib gaps discovered mid-iter (not all in the planner's recipe; recipe exposes them via failed approaches), the canonical discipline is: (a) commit small axiom-clean ¬ß-substrate lemmas (PUSH-BEYOND) preserving iter velocity; (b) precisely type each Mathlib sub-build as a roadmap entry in the task report; (c) DO NOT inherit `sorry` from an existing weaker-hypothesis declaration (would violate axiom-cleanliness); (d) DO NOT modify a frozen planner signature (would violate role permissions). **Iter-199 instance**: Lane WD-A4a planner directive cited Stacks 02RV + `Ideal.finite_minimalPrimes_of_isNoetherianRing` recipe, but the recipe requires (1) open-immersion stalk-bridge for prime divisors (~150-250 LOC, Stacks 02IZ/005X); (2) `Ring.ordFrac` transport across `IsOpenImmersion.iff_isIso_stalkMap`; (3) coheight ‚ÜĒ height bridge (same as Lane I residual). All 4 closure approaches scoped and rejected. Substrate-only landed 2 helpers. Net-delta is 0 sorries ‚ÄĒ but the iter is forward-compatible with future iters that close one Sub-build at a time. **Anti-pattern**: re-dispatching the same prover lane on the same HARD BAR after a 5-approach exhaustion produces the same exhaustion. Instead: dispatch the Sub-build as an isolated mathlib-build lane. Reusable for ANY iter where the planner's recipe under-estimates the Mathlib substrate gap by 2+ piece-grade levels.

- **Carrier-soundness probe pattern: theorem body axiom-clean via Prop-valued typeclass extraction** *(NEW iter-199 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane FGA-sorry4 `smoothProperQuotient` in `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean:320-391`, theorem body axiom-clean per `lean_verify`.)* Verdict CONFIRMED iter-199 (carrier-soundness probe). For a theorem `theorem T (h‚āĀ h‚āā ... : ...) : Goal := by sorry` whose body has a genuine multi-iter Mathlib gap, the carrier-soundness probe refactor produces an axiom-clean theorem body via: (a) **new Prop-valued typeclass `HasT (h‚āĀ ...)`** with single field `extracted_witness : Goal`; (b) **global default `instance instHasT : HasT h‚āĀ ... := ‚ü®sorry‚ü©`** localising the residual sorry; (c) **rewrite the theorem** to add `[HasT h‚āĀ ...]` hypothesis and extract: `theorem T (h‚āĀ ...) [HasT h‚āĀ ...] : Goal := HasT.extracted_witness (_őĪ := ...)`. **Properties**: (1) **theorem body is axiom-clean** (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` per `lean_verify`); consumers quantifying over `[HasT h‚āĀ ...]` explicitly remain kernel-clean. (2) **Net sorry count unchanged** (free sorry ‚Üí ‚ü®sorry‚ü© instance constructor); but the file's sorries become **structurally homogeneous** ‚ÄĒ all carried by Prop-valued typeclass instance constructors. (3) **sorryAx-via-default-instance**: since the global default instance auto-synthesises, calls to `T` without an explicit typeclass argument inherit `sorryAx` via instance synthesis. This is **intentional** and matches the iter-198 review's reading. (4) **Discipline required**: do NOT close other sorries via tautological `Functor.const (PUnit)` / `0` / `‚ü®0‚ü©` placeholder bodies inside the carrier-soundness pattern ‚ÄĒ that crosses into headline-laundering (iter-198 RPF lesson). Iter-199 Lane FGA correctly REJECTED tautological closures for Sorries 1-3. **Reusable** for ANY theorem whose body has a multi-iter Mathlib gap AND whose statement type is well-defined under existing typeclasses. The pattern's strongest use case is on file-skeletons (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean` 7 sorries now uniformly carrier-bound).

- **`Ring.ordFrac` + `WithZero.log` for axiom-clean order/degree primitive lemmas on Frac(R)** *(NEW iter-198 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane WD-A4a ¬ß2/¬ß4 substrate in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`, 6 axiom-clean helpers (`order_zero`, `order_mul_of_ne_zero`, `order_inv`, `order_units_inv`, `degree_neg`, `degree_sub`) ‚ÄĒ each 3-5 LOC.)* For a `Scheme.RationalMap.order` declared as `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ f)` (where `Ring.ordFrac _ : K ‚Üí*‚āÄ ‚Ą§ŠĶź‚Āį` is the monoid-with-zero hom on the fraction field): `order 0 = 0` discharges via `unfold + map_zero + WithZero.log_zero`; `order (f * g) = order f + order g` (under `f ‚Č† 0`, `g ‚Č† 0`) via `unfold + map_mul + WithZero.log_mul` (the latter needs `map_ne_zero` to verify both factors land in `WithZero.unit_iff`); `order f‚ĀĽ¬Ļ = -order f` via `map_inv‚āÄ + WithZero.log_inv` (works without `f ‚Č† 0` due to junk-zero convention on inverses); units inv form via bridge `((u‚ĀĽ¬Ļ : Kň£) : K) = (u : K)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` + `order_inv`. For `degree_neg` / `degree_sub` on `Scheme.WeilDivisor`: rewrite via `degree_hom_apply` (degree as `AddMonoidHom` evaluation), then `map_neg` / `map_sub` close in 1-2 lines. **Reusable** for any order/degree-bundled-algebra on a fraction field ‚ÄĒ the pattern factors all standard hom-properties through Mathlib's `MonoidWithZeroHom` + `WithZero.log` lemmas.

- **Depth ‚ÜĒ Ext characterization via `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` + LES of `Ext^*(őļ, -)` for depth-drops-by-one** *(NEW iter-198 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane AB `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular` in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:1023-1124`, ~100 LOC axiom-clean.)* Closes Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`: `(R, ūĚĒ™)` Noetherian local, `M` nonzero finite `R`-module, `x ‚ąą ūĚĒ™` `M`-regular ‚üĻ `depth(M/xM) + 1 = depth_ūĚĒ™(M)`. **Recipe (8 steps)**: (1) get `x ‚ąą Ann őļ` from `x ‚ąą ūĚĒ™ = Ann(R/ūĚĒ™)` via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`. (2) `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact` provides the SES `0 ‚Üí M ‚Üí[x] M ‚Üí M/xM ‚Üí 0` in `ModuleCat.smulShortComplex` form. (3) `[x]_*` is zero on `Ext^*(őļ, M)` via the file-local `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` helper (Mathlib's `Ext.linear_smul_zero` of `x ‚ąą Ann őļ`). (4) `covariant_sequence_exact‚āĀ` / `covariant_sequence_exact‚āÉ` extract the short-exact decomposition from the LES: `Ext^i(őļ, M) ‚Ü™ Ext^i(őļ, M/xM) ‚Ü† Ext^{i+1}(őļ, M)`. (5) `postcomp_mk‚āÄ_injective_of_mono` packages the boundary map. (6) `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` translates both depths to ‚Ąē-indexed Ext-vanishing characterizations. (7) `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le` bridges ‚Ąē-indexed Ext to `‚Ąē‚ąě`-valued depth on each direction. (8) `le_antisymm` recovers the equality. **Companion lemma (`exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth`)**: `1 ‚Č§ depth_ūĚĒ™(M)` ‚üĻ exists `M`-regular `x ‚ąą ūĚĒ™` via `if_neg` unfold of `depth` (Nakayama rules out trivial-quotient under `Nontrivial M`) + `lt_sSup_iff` extraction + `isRegular_cons_iff` head readoff. **Reusable** for any depth ‚ÜĒ Ext theorem in Noetherian local ring theory; the (Ann őļ) ‚üĻ zero-on-Ext bridge generalises to any `[x]_*` annihilation argument.

- **`Module.finrank_baseChange` + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq` for residue-field-tensor finrank computation** *(NEW iter-198 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane COE Stage 6.B-RHS substrate in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:373-401`, ~30 LOC axiom-clean.)* For `R` a CommRing, `S‚āė` a nontrivial local CommRing with `[Algebra R S‚āė]`, `[Module.Free S‚āė ő©[S‚āė‚ĀĄR]]`, and `Module.rank S‚āė ő©[S‚āė‚ĀĄR] = n`: `finrank őļ(m‚āė) (őļ(m‚āė) ‚äó_{S‚āė} ő©[S‚āė‚ĀĄR]) = n` discharges in 5 lines via `Module.finrank_baseChange` (free-base-change finrank preservation) + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq` (rank-to-finrank conversion under free). Sibling `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`-form lemma discharges the free + rank hypotheses via Mathlib's `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential` + `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential` and chains into the hypothesis-form. **Reusable** for any base-change finrank computation on K√§hler differentials or similar free-module bundles where the base ring is a standard-smooth algebra.

- **Placeholder closures gated on upstream Mathlib gap ‚ÄĒ `Functor.const` / `0` / `‚ü®0‚ü©` discipline** *(NEW iter-198 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane RPF `PicSharp` / `PicSharp.functorial` / `PicSharp.etSheaf_group_structure` placeholder bodies in `Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`.)* When the math-correct body of a declaration is gated on an upstream Mathlib gap (e.g. `Scheme.Modules` monoidal-structure) but the declaration's type is well-defined and load-bearing for downstream structural elaboration: a signature-preserving placeholder body (the constant `PUnit` functor for a functor-valued def, the zero `AddMonoidHom` for a hom-valued def, the zero natural transformation wrapped in `Nonempty` for an existential witness) unblocks the file-skeleton AND lets downstream sheafification / categorical machinery elaborate kernel-clean. **Trade-offs**: (PRO) source-sorry count drops; consumers compile; the swap to math-correct bodies is signature-preserving once the upstream gap lands. (CON) `\leanok` on the proof block fires (provided `lake env lean` is clean), which can mislead a casual reader; for hom-typed placeholders where `Zero` on the codomain derives from a sorry-bodied instance, `lean_verify` reveals `sorryAx` as a typeclass leak. **Discipline required**: (a) chapter-level `% NOTE: ...` flagging the placeholder; (b) verbatim signature preservation; (c) downstream consumers do NOT depend on the math-correctness of the body (only on the type); (d) the upstream gap is named, dated, and tracked. **Anti-pattern WITHOUT the discipline**: headline laundering ‚ÄĒ sorry count drops, `\leanok` fires, math does not advance. Iter-198 RPF used this pattern; the lean-auditor `iter198` report flags it as "between legitimate progress and headline laundering ‚ÄĒ closer to the laundering end for RelPicFunctor." Use the discipline.

- **`change` (definitional unfolding) sidesteps `Scheme.Hom.app f V`'s dependent-motive blocker on `rw`** *(NEW iter-197 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane E `Proj.awayőĻ_app_basicOpen` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:268`, axiom-clean ~10 LOC.)* Generalises the iter-196 KB entry on the `Scheme.Hom.app` dependent-motive issue: when proving `f.app V = expr` and `f = g` (an equation between morphisms), the standard `rw [f_eq_g]` / `conv_lhs => rw [...]` / `appLE U V e` rewriting paths ALL fail because `f.app V`'s codomain depends on `f` via `f ‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ V`. **Resolution**: use `change` (definitional unfolding) to replace `f` with its equational form via `rfl`; this works precisely when `f_eq_g` is a `rfl`-level equation (definitional). After the `change`, decompose via `Scheme.Hom.comp_app` + `Scheme.Opens.őĻ_app_self` + `Scheme.Hom.app_eq` on the relevant preimage equality, then `aesop_cat` cleans the eqToHom cascade. **Used iter-197 Lane E** to evaluate `Proj.awayőĻ ūĚíú f f_deg hm = Spec.map(basicOpenIsoSpec).inv ‚Čę őĻ` on `.app(basicOpen ūĚíú f)`, sidestepping the iter-196 prescribed `Scheme.invApp` + `Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top` chain which still hit the motive issue. Reusable for ANY `f.app U` evaluation where `f` has a definitional equational form. **Companion KB entry**: pair with the iter-196 "build appTop-level helper" workaround ‚ÄĒ the `change`-route is the strongest sidestep when the equation IS `rfl`-level.

- **Generic-point + IsMax shortcut for codim-1-on-Proj coheight reasoning** *(NEW iter-197 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I `hy_ne_bot` closure in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:916`, ~30 LOC axiom-clean.)* For an open-immersion chart `ūĚíį.f i : Spec(R_i) ‚ü∂ ProjectiveLineBar` and a prime divisor `Y` of the Proj scheme, ruling out `y.asIdeal = ‚ä•` (where `y : Spec(R_i)` is the chart-lift of `Y.point`) ‚ÄĒ needed to apply DVR transport for `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` ‚ÄĒ does NOT require porting topological-coheight preservation under open immersion in general (the documented Stacks 02IZ/005X gap). **Shortcut**: `y.asIdeal = ‚ä•` ‚üĻ `y = genericPoint Spec(R_i)` via `genericPoint_eq_bot_of_affine`; open immersion preserves generic points (`AlgebraicGeometry.genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion`); `(ūĚíį.f i).base y = Y.point` forces `Y.point = genericPoint`; generic points are `IsMax` in the scheme specialisation preorder (`Scheme.le_iff_specializes.mpr` + `genericPoint_specializes`); so `Order.coheight Y.point = 0`, contradicting `Y.coheight = 1`. **Dead-end caveats**: `inferInstance` for `IrreducibleSpace ‚Ü•(ūĚíį.X i)` does NOT unfold through `openCover.X` (need explicit `show`-cast to `Spec R`); `Scheme.le_iff_specializes` endpoints are IMPLICIT args (use `.mpr` no-args); `IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)` does NOT auto-cast from `IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` (manual `letI`-style bridge required); `genericPoint_eq_bot_of_affine R` needs explicit R + `IsDomain ‚ÜĎR` in scope. Reusable for ANY Proj-chart prime-divisor coheight argument where the closed-point alternative gives DVR transport.

- **PUnit IndiscreteTopology + `Opens.eq_bot_or_top` shortcut for `skyscraperSheaf ‚ČÖ constantSheaf`** *(NEW iter-197 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_iso_constantSheaf_punit` in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean:924`, 4 declarations ~150 LOC axiom-clean.)* For PUnit-shaped target spaces with `IndiscreteTopology`, the only open sets are `‚ä•` and `‚ä§` (via `Opens.eq_bot_or_top`). On `‚ä§`, the skyscraper-presheaf value IS the section value (definitionally); on `‚ä•`, both `skyscraperSheaf` and `constantSheaf` value are terminal (via `TopCat.Sheaf.isTerminalOfEqEmpty`). **Construction** (4 decls): (1) `alphaConstToSkyPUnit` ‚ÄĒ `(Functor.const).obj A ‚Üí skyscraperPresheaf PUnit.unit A` pointwise via `eqToHom` (unit-in-U) / `IsTerminal.from` (unit-not-in-U). (2) `betaSkyToConstPUnit` ‚ÄĒ opposite direction via `toSheafify` natural composition. (3) composition lemma via `eqToHom_trans_assoc` (unit-in-U) + `IsTerminal.hom_ext` (‚ä•). (4) iso record `hom = ő≤`, `inv = sheafifyLift őĪ`; `inv_hom_id` via `sheafify_hom_ext`; `hom_inv_id` via `toSheafify_sheafifyLift` (unit-in-U) + `TopCat.Sheaf.isTerminalOfEqEmpty.hom_ext` (‚ä•). **AVOIDS the `Full + Faithful (constantSheaf J D)` Mathlib gap** that the blueprint-described "Route A" relies on. Generalisation to arbitrary irreducible spaces does NOT inherit this shortcut (irreducible spaces have potentially many opens); for that case, use the stalk-based bridge via `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (iter-198 prover recommendation; see anti-pattern below). Reusable for ANY skyscraper-vs-constant-sheaf identification on a PUnit-shaped target.

- **`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv` 11-step recipe for per-chart smooth-of-relative-dim-1 on Proj affine charts** *(NEW iter-197 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane ChartIso `projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_X` helper in `Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean`, ~50 LOC axiom-clean.)* When proving `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n (chart_morphism)` on a Proj-scheme affine chart `Proj.awayőĻ ūĚíú f f_deg hm ‚Čę struct`, the 11-step recipe is: (1) rewrite structure morphism via `Proj.toSpecZero ‚Čę Spec.map(algebraMap)` form (rfl); (2) re-associate via explicit `(Category.assoc _ _ _).symm` (plain `rw [‚Üź Category.assoc]` fails HOU on elided proof args); (3) `Proj.awayőĻ_toSpecZero` collapses `awayőĻ ‚Čę Proj.toSpecZero` to `Spec.map(fromZeroRingHom)`; (4) `Spec.map_comp` backward combines the two `Spec.map`s; (5) `HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @SmoothOfRelativeDimension n)` translates to ring-hom property; (6) `RingHom.locally_of RingHom.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_respectsIso` peels `Locally`; (7) `change` to `algebraMap k (Away ūĚíú f)` form (rfl under project's algebra instance); (8) `RingHom.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_algebraMap.mp` to algebra-property; (9-10) build kbar-algEquiv chain `MvPolynomial (Fin n) kbar ‚ČÉ‚āź[kbar] MvPolynomial Unit kbar ‚ČÉ‚āź[kbar] Away` via `renameEquiv kbar finOneEquiv` + `AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv` upgrading `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` with `_algebraMap`; (11) `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv` with iter-196 `mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` substrate. Wraps in a named-`X i` helper to sidestep dependent f_deg/hm membership-proof issues when the consumer has `(![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 ‚Üí _) i` form; use `fin_cases i` at the outer cover level for the Matrix.cons defeq bridge. Reusable for ANY `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` proof on a `Proj`-chart `(Away ūĚíú X_i, algebraMap kbar)` setting.

- **Named substrate iter ("helper budget = N") discipline** *(NEW iter-197 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A `OCofP.lean` mathlib-build mode, 3 axiom-clean helpers (i)/(ii)/(iii).)* When a top-level sorry encodes a multi-step substrate need (e.g. Stacks 0BCK algebraic Hartogs + őď=kŐĄ), iter-shoehorning a single inline-`exact` is impossible; the discipline is to commit a dedicated "mathlib-build" iter with explicit helper budget `N` (typically 2-4), each helper a precisely-typed `private noncomputable def` / `theorem` with clean signature. Parent body advances to extract the helper outputs before the residual sorry. **Net per-iter delta is 0 closures** but the residual gap is now: (a) precisely typed; (b) testable via `lean_verify` independently; (c) separable from consumer's surrounding proof. Companion to iter-196 KB "named substrate extraction" and iter-195 KB "localParameterAtInfty_uniformiser_witness pattern". The substrate-build iter is the canonical alternative to "wait for Mathlib upstream" when (a) Mathlib gap is genuine and (b) the substrate has a manageable project-side close path. Reusable for ANY substantive multi-step Mathlib gap on the critical path.

- **`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` 0√ó0-Jacobian recipe for `MvPolynomial (Fin n)` standard-smooth** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane BareScheme substrate `mvPolynomialFin_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` in `Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean:165-211`, ~50 LOC axiom-clean.)* Closes the iter-182-flagged Mathlib gap on `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)`. Construction: (1) `mvPolyGenerators` via `Algebra.Generators.ofSurjective val := X` + `MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left` for surjectivity; (2) `mvPolyPresentation` with `ŌÉ := PEmpty` (no relations, ker = ‚ä• via `Algebra.Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val`); (3) `mvPolyPreSubmersivePresentation` with empty `map : PEmpty ‚Üí őĪ`; (4) `mvPolySubmersivePresentation` with `dimension := Nat.card (Fin n) - Nat.card PEmpty = n` and Jacobian = det of 0√ó0 matrix = 1 via `Matrix.det_isEmpty`; (5) instance via `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`. The recipe is pure Mathlib supplement and does not depend on project objects ‚ÄĒ usable wherever `MvPolynomial (Fin n) R` standard-smooth-of-relative-dimension-n is needed. **Mathlib upstream PR candidate**.

- **`IsZariskiLocalAtSource.of_openCover` cover-reduction for smoothness** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane BareScheme `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim` in `Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean:325`, axiom-clean wrapper.)* For any morphism property `P` with `IsZariskiLocalAtSource P`, `P f` follows from `‚ąÄ i, P (ūĚíį.f i ‚Čę f)` for an open cover `ūĚíį : Scheme.OpenCover X`. For `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` (which has `HasRingHomProperty` ‚üĻ `IsZariskiLocalAtSource` per Mathlib `RingHomProperties.lean:375`), this delegates smoothness on `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` to per-chart smoothness on the 2-chart affine cover. Reusable for ANY `SmoothOfRelativeDimension` / `Smooth` / `LocallyOfFiniteType` / `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` proof on a `Proj` scheme via the standard affine cover.

- **`ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.ext` for lifting presheaf equalities to sheaf equalities** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const` outer step in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean:818`, axiom-clean (~10 LOC).)* When a Mathlib lemma proves `P_presheaf = Q_presheaf` for two presheaves and both are also sheaves with the SAME sheaf condition, the equality lifts to a sheaf-level **equality** (not just iso) via `ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.ext`. The `IsSheaf` Prop-valued field is subsingleton-equal, so the only content of a sheaf equality is the presheaf equality. Pattern is reusable wherever a Mathlib `_eq_pushforward` / `_eq_skyscraper` / etc. presheaf identity has a sheafification analogue ‚ÄĒ typically saves the entire iso-construction round-trip.

- **`LocallyQuasiFinite.of_finite_preimage_singleton` as LQF reformulation pattern** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane RCI helper (a) body reformulation in `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:884-943`.)* When the abstract per-fibre LQF goal `LocallyQuasiFinite (ŌÜ.fiberToSpecResidueField x)` is stuck, reformulate via Mathlib's `LocallyQuasiFinite.of_finite_preimage_singleton` (`Morphisms/QuasiFinite.lean:295`). The new obligation is `‚ąÄ x : Y, (ŌÜ.base ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' {x}).Finite` (concrete topological preimage-finiteness) given `[LocallyOfFiniteType ŌÜ]`. The reformulation converts an opaque algebro-geometric obligation into a concrete topological one amenable to case-splits (generic point vs closed points). Companion derivation: `LocallyOfFiniteType ŌÜ` is often in-scope via `[IsProper ŌÜ]` + `IsProper.toLocallyOfFiniteType`. Reusable for ANY scheme-theoretic LQF reformulation where the topological preimage at each point is easier to bound than the per-fibre algebraic statement.

- **Dependent-motive issue on `Scheme.Hom.app f V` BLOCKS direct `rw [f_eq_g]`** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane E `Proj.awayőĻ_app_basicOpen` blocker in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.)* `Scheme.Hom.app f V : őď(Y, V) ‚ü∂ őď(X, f‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ V)` has codomain depending on `f` via the preimage `f‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ V`. Any attempt to `rw [f_eq_g]` or `conv_lhs => rw [...]` on a goal mentioning `f.app V` fails with "motive is not type correct" because the codomain type `őď(X, f‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ V)` changes when `f` changes shape. **Workaround**: build an `appTop`-level helper (or `app U` helper for the specific U), then chain through `Scheme.Hom.comp_app` + `Scheme.Opens.őĻ_app` to evaluate componentwise. Iter-196 Lane E established this for `Proj.awayőĻ ūĚíú f f_deg hm = Spec.map (basicOpenIsoAway _).inv ‚Čę (isAffineOpen_basicOpen _).fromSpec` ‚ÄĒ directly `rw`ing on it under `awayőĻ.app(basicOpen ūĚíú f)` fails; the iter-197 prescription is `Proj.basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_app_top` helper via `Scheme.inv_app` + `Proj.basicOpenToSpec_app_top` then chain. Reusable for ANY equation between `Scheme.Hom`s when downstream goals mention `.app` of the morphism.

- **`Iso.ext` repackaging on `(A ‚Č™‚Čę B).inv` for clean equality between two `Spec.map ... ‚Čę ...` compositions** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane E `Proj.awayőĻ_eq_specMap_fromSpec` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:199`, axiom-clean.)* When proving `g‚āĀ ‚Čę őĻ = g‚āā ‚Čę őĻ` for two compositions through an open immersion `őĻ`, after `congr 1` the remaining `g‚āĀ = g‚āā` may be an iso equation. If both sides factor as `(őĪ ‚Č™‚Čę ő≤).inv` for some `őĪ : X ‚ČÖ Z`, `ő≤ : Z ‚ČÖ Y`, repackage via `change _ = (őĪ ‚Č™‚Čę Spec.mapIso ő≤.op).inv`; then `apply Iso.ext`; reduce to forward direction `Iso.hom = ...` which is typically `rfl` after Mathlib `Iso.trans_hom`, `Functor.mapIso_hom`, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom` simp-rewrites. Used iter-196 to bridge `Proj.awayőĻ` and `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec` via `basicOpenIsoSpec.hom = basicOpenToSpec = toSpecőď ‚Čę Spec.map(awayToSection)`. Reusable for `Proj`-side / affine-open iso bridges that compose multiple Mathlib `_iso_hom` simp lemmas.

- **8-step Route 2 PID-transfer recipe for `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` on `Proj` via affine chart + Spec.stalkIso** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I `isRegularInCodimOneProjectiveLineBar` in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:750`, axiom-clean modulo 1 named topological-to-algebraic residual.)* For a Proj scheme covered by affine charts, prove `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X` via per-prime-divisor DVR by: (1) select chart `i := ūĚíį.idx Y.point`; (2) open-immersion stalk iso `(ūĚíį.f i).stalkMap y : ProjStalk(Y.point) ‚ü∂ ChartStalk(y)` via `IsOpenImmersion.iff_isIso_stalkMap`; (3) `IsDomain` transport via `MulEquiv.isDomain _ hiso.symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.toMulEquiv`; (4) reduce goal to DVR on `Localization.AtPrime y.asIdeal` via `Spec.stalkIso`; (5) `IsDomain`/`IsPrincipalIdealRing`/`IsDedekindDomain` on chart ring `HomogeneousLocalization.Away ūĚíú X_i` via iso chain `Away ūĚíú X_i ‚ČÉ+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ‚ČÉ+* Polynomial kbar` + `Polynomial.instEuclideanDomain` + `EuclideanDomain.to_principal_ideal_domain` + `IsPrincipalIdealRing.isDedekindDomain`; (6) bridge `(![X 0, X 1]) i = X i` defeq via universal-quantifier `‚ąÄ j : Fin 2, ...` + `fin_cases` (defeq does NOT auto-elaborate); (7) `IsLocalization.AtPrime.isDiscreteValuationRing_of_dedekind_domain` with named residual `y.asIdeal ‚Č† ‚ä•` (Stacks 02IZ/005X coheight ‚ÜĒ Krull-dim gap); (8) transport DVR back via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing`. Total ~150 LOC; substantive structural advance reducing a vague PID-transfer-50-80-LOC sorry to a single 5-10 LOC named residual. Caveats: `CommRingCat.of R` does NOT unfold for typeclass synthesis (need explicit `haveI`); `IsDiscreteValuationRing.RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing` is the fully-qualified name (not the unqualified `RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing`). Reusable for ANY `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` on a `Proj` scheme covered by k[t]-like affine charts.

- **`adj.homEquiv` injective decomposition for `toFunctionField`-style injectivity** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A `toFunctionField_injective` in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean:1287`, ~50 LOC axiom-clean.)* For a derived "evaluate-section-to-function-field" map composed from `Subtype.val ‚ąė adj.homEquiv ‚ąė HModule_zero_linearEquiv ‚ąė ...`, prove injectivity by decomposing the chain via `homEquiv_unit`. Pattern: chain `Subtype.val` (injective) ‚Üí `((sheafToPresheaf.map sh).app(op ‚ä§)).hom one_image = ((adj.homEquiv kbar F sh).hom 1)` ‚Üí `LinearMap.ext_ring` + `ModuleCat.hom_ext` for kbar-linear maps from `kbar` ‚Üí `Equiv.injective` on `adj.homEquiv` ‚Üí `LinearEquiv.injective` on the linearEquiv. Reusable for ANY "global-section-to-rational-function" or similar adjunct-chain-based injectivity argument in `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK`-flavoured contexts.

- **Named substrate extraction trades inline sorry for typed-named-helper sorry** *(NEW iter-196 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A `functionField_const_of_complete_curve_of_orderZero` in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean:1390`.)* When an inline `sorry` in the middle of a long proof captures a substantive multi-step Mathlib gap (e.g. Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne I.3.4 algebraic Hartogs + őď = kŐĄ), the canonical discipline is: (a) extract the gap as a typed `private lemma` with clean signature; (b) replace the inline sorry with `exact <helper> ...`; (c) write a docstring on the helper documenting the precise Mathlib infrastructure needed. Net sorry count unchanged (1:1 swap inline ‚Üí named), but the residual is now: clean named-signature target for iter-(N+1); separable from the consumer's surrounding proof; testable via `lean_verify` independently. Companion to iter-195's Lane RCI `localParameterAtInfty_uniformiser_witness` pattern. Reusable for ANY "inline sorry captures a discrete multi-step substrate need".

- **`Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono` packages SES exactness preservation from LEFT-half limit preservation only** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` closure in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, axiom-clean. Closes the iter-192/193/194 STUCK on `SAb.Exact`.)* For proving `(S.map F).Exact` given `S : ShortComplex C` is `ShortExact` and `F : C ‚•§ D` is additive between abelian categories, you do NOT need full `Functor.PreservesHomology` (i.e. BOTH `PreservesFiniteLimits` AND `PreservesFiniteColimits` + `Exact`-preservation). Mathlib's `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono` states `PreservesFiniteLimits F ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ S, S.ShortExact ‚Üí (S.map F).Exact ‚ąß Mono (F.map S.f)`. The `.1` projection delivers exactness directly from LEFT-half limit preservation. **Iter-195 instance**: closed Lane H final residual via 1 new project-local instance `sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits` (~30 LOC, see next entry) instead of the plan-anticipated ~50-100 LOC `PreservesHomology` chain. Reusable for ANY "SES is preserved by a functor between abelian categories" obligation when the inputs are known to be `ShortExact`. **Anti-pattern**: do NOT chase the full `PreservesHomology` substrate when the `ShortExact` packaging is available ‚ÄĒ the LEFT-half iff is qualitatively cheaper.

- **`sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits` (factor through faithful-and-reflects `sheafToPresheaf`)** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H project-local instance in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean` (~L340), axiom-clean.)* For proving `PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose J F)` given `F : A ‚•§ B` between abelian categories with finite limits, `PreservesFiniteLimits F`, and `J.HasSheafCompose F`: factor via the rfl-equality `sheafCompose J F ‚čô sheafToPresheaf J B = sheafToPresheaf J A ‚čô (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F` (by `rfl`); get `PreservesFiniteLimits` on the RHS via composing `whiskeringRight_preservesLimitsOfShape` (Mathlib `CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.FunctorCategory`) with `sheafToPresheaf` finite-limit-preservation (Mathlib `CategoryTheory.Sites.Limits`); transport back via `preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves` (using `sheafToPresheaf J B` fully faithful ‚üĻ reflects isomorphisms). ~30 LOC. Reusable for ANY `sheafCompose`-derived preservation argument when the source `F` is a finite-limit-preserving additive functor (e.g. `forget‚āā ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat`).

- **Generic `Finsupp.sum_max_zero_eq_sum_filter_pos`** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I helper in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:684-709`, kernel-clean.)* For any `D : őĪ ‚Üí‚āÄ ‚Ą§`: `D.sum (fun _ n => max n 0) = ‚ąĎ a ‚ąą D.support.filter (fun a => 0 < D a), D a`. ~10 LOC via `Finsupp.sum` ‚Ü¶ `Finset.sum_filter`-with-`if` + `Finset.sum_congr` to match per-term `max (D a) 0 = if 0 < D a then D a else 0` via `omega`. No scheme content; reusable for any "positive part of an integer-valued Finsupp via support filter" obligation. **Mathlib upstream PR candidate** ‚ÄĒ would live in `Mathlib/Data/Finsupp/Order.lean` or similar.

- **Six-step linear-algebra extraction recipe from `Module.finrank kbar V = 2` to a non-constant section** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A `exists_nonconstant_rational_from_dim_eq_two` body in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean:1323`, 6 axiom-clean substeps.)* Given `_hdim : Module.finrank kbar H‚Āį = 2` and a distinguished constant `s‚āĀ : H‚Āį` with `hs‚āĀ : toFunctionField s‚āĀ = 1`, extract a "different" section `s ‚ąą H‚Āį \ kbar ¬∑ s‚āĀ` in 6 axiom-clean substeps: (1) `htF_zero : toFunctionField 0 = 0` via `simp only [toFunctionField, map_zero, Functor.map_zero]; rfl` ‚ÄĒ `Functor.map_zero` is the load-bearing simp lemma; (2) `htF_smul` + `htF_add` (kbar-linearity of `toFunctionField`, one-liner each via `simp only [..., map_smul]; rfl` and `simp only [..., map_add, Functor.map_add]; rfl`); (3) `hs‚āĀ_ne : s‚āĀ ‚Č† 0` via `htF_zero` + `one_ne_zero`; (4) `Module.Finite kbar H‚Āį` via `Module.finite_of_finrank_pos`; (5) `finrank kbar (Submodule.span kbar {s‚āĀ}) = 1` via `finrank_span_singleton hs‚āĀ_ne`; (6) extract `s : H‚Āį` with `‚ąÄ r ‚Č† 0, r ‚ÄĘ s ‚ąČ Submodule.span kbar {s‚āĀ}` via `Submodule.exists_of_finrank_lt 1 < 2`; specialise to `r = 1` to get `s ‚ąČ Submodule.span kbar {s‚āĀ}`. ~50 LOC end-to-end. Reusable for ANY "extract a vector outside a span" argument from a finrank gap (Hartshorne II.6.7-style genus-0 base-change arguments + RR-induction).

- **Structural lift via file-private named typed-sorry helper preserves no-regression on sorry count when substrate-blocked** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane RCI `localParameterAtInfty_uniformiser_witness` in `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:463`, kernel-only `[propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.)* When an inline `?hyp` typed sorry inside a consumer body is genuinely substrate-blocked (witness construction requires multi-step Mathlib gap fill), the canonical discipline is a 1:1 swap to a file-private typed-sorry helper with documented closure path. Properties of the structural lift: (a) net sorry count unchanged (1:1 swap inline ‚Üí named); (b) consumer body becomes sorry-free at the body level (delegates via `exact <helper> <args>`); (c) substrate need exposed at a single named declaration with a focused iter-(N+1) target + documented recipe; (d) tactic experiments at the consumer (`Classical.arbitrary`, `tauto`, `exact?`) can be definitively closed-out as dead ‚ÄĒ the helper docstring records what was tried. **Anti-pattern**: do NOT revert the upstream signature refactor (e.g. iter-194 `lane-i-localparameter-signature-v2`) when its `?hyp` is substrate-blocked ‚ÄĒ the structural lift preserves the stronger signature for the next iter. Reusable for any "iter-(N-1) refactor introduced a stronger signature whose `?hyp` is inevitably substrate-blocked" pattern.

- **API 529 Overloaded is a real failure mode for high-cost prover lanes; preserve substrate findings as in-file comments at scout-time** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane BareScheme + Lane E ERRORED on API 529 with NO edits committed.)* Iter-195 lost 2 of 8 prover lanes to transient API exhaustion ‚ÄĒ Lane BareScheme after ~25 min / ~140 turns of substantive Mathlib scouting, Lane E after ~3.5 min. Neither committed any edits; ~25 min of substrate exploration was lost as working context. **Mitigation for plan agents**: high-cost lanes (substrate-scouting lanes; lanes whose closure cascade-unblocks 2+ downstream lanes) should carry an explicit "checkpoint commit" instruction: after ‚Č•30 min of substrate scouting with no edit committed, commit a substrate-roadmap comment block to the file at the scaffold sorry sites before continuing. This preserves prover-session value on transient API failure. **Iter-196 implication**: re-dispatch the 2 errored lanes is mandatory ‚ÄĒ neither is a mathematical failure. **Reusable** for any project-side lane facing extended scouting before first edit.

- **`forget‚āā` bridge from `Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)` to `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` for module-valued sheaf SES surjectivity work** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` body in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, structural decomposition axiom-clean modulo a single typeclass goal `(sheafCompose forget‚āā).PreservesHomology`.)* When a SES of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)` needs surjectivity-on-sections proven (Hartshorne II Ex 1.16(b)), DO NOT fight the module-valued category. Lift to `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` via `sheafCompose (forget‚āā ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat)`; apply Mathlib's `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact` to get `Epi (SAb.g.hom.app (op U))`; convert to surjectivity via `AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective`; observe the underlying function is the same as the original `ModuleCat kbar`-level map. **Substrate helpers needed (~35 LOC total, axiom-clean)**: (a) `sheafCompose_additive` instance ‚ÄĒ `F : A ‚•§ B` additive + `J.HasSheafCompose F` ‚üĻ `(sheafCompose J F).Additive`, factoring through `sheafToPresheaf J B`'s faithfulness + `Functor.whiskerRight` componentwise additivity; (b) `sheafCompose_preservesZero` instance derived via `Functor.preservesZeroMorphisms_of_additive`; (c) `Scheme.IsFlasque.toAddCommGrpCat` bridge ‚ÄĒ for each pair `V ‚Č§ U`, the underlying restriction map is the same function after `forget‚āā`, so surjectivity (project predicate) ‚ÜĒ Epi (AddCommGrpCat-level via `AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective`). The remaining residual is a single typeclass goal `(sheafCompose J forget‚āā).PreservesHomology` (or `PreservesRightHomologyOf S`). `Mono SAb.f` proven axiom-clean via `Sheaf.Hom.mono_of_presheaf_mono` + `NatTrans.mono_of_mono_app`; `Epi SAb.g` proven axiom-clean via `Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi'` + `Presheaf.imageSieve_mem`. Reusable for ANY module-valued-sheaf SES reduction needing AddCommGrpCat-level Mathlib API.

- **`Module.depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty` canonical substrate for depth-via-free-rank arguments** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G n=0 branch substrate in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:979`, axiom-clean.)* `depth I (őĻ ‚Üí M) = depth I M` for nonempty finite `őĻ`, ~135 LOC end-to-end kernel-clean. Recipe factor through 4 helpers: (a) `ideal_smul_top_pi_const` ‚ÄĒ `I ‚ÄĘ ‚ä§_{őĻ ‚Üí M} = pi univ (fun _ => I ‚ÄĘ ‚ä§_M)` via `Submodule.smul_induction_on` (‚Č§) + `Pi.single`-decomposition `Finset.univ_sum_single` (‚Č•); (b) `ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff` ‚ÄĒ side condition iff via `Pi.single j m` lift+project; (c) `quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv` ‚ÄĒ `QuotSMulTop r (őĻ ‚Üí M) ‚ČÉ‚āó őĻ ‚Üí QuotSMulTop r M` via `Submodule.quotEquivOfEq` + `Submodule.quotientPi` + `Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul` bridging `r ‚ÄĘ ‚ä§` and `Ideal.span {r} ‚ÄĘ ‚ä§`; (d) `isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty` ‚ÄĒ sequence-level iff by induction on `rs` (generalising `M`); cons-case via `Pi.isSMulRegular_iff` for SMul-regular conjunct + `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` for quotient piece. Assemble the supremum-set equality. Reusable for ANY Auslander‚ÄďBuchsbaum / projective-dimension / flat-module argument identifying depth across `M ‚ČÉ‚āó[R] (Fin k ‚Üí R)` (or analogous `ő£`-products).

- **Closed-points reduction via `JacobsonSpace.closure_closedPoints` converts global topological range-containment into per-closed-point chart-evaluation** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane B `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`, 9 axiom-clean helpers landed.)* When proving `Set.range s_pair ‚äÜ Set.range őĒ` for two scheme morphisms on a Jacobson scheme, reduce to per-closed-point check: (1) derive `JacobsonSpace` on the intersection via `LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace` on a composition chain (LOFT of `cover.f i` via `IsOpenImmersion`; LOFT of `pullback.fst` between open immersions; LOFT of `(PLB ‚äó Gm).hom` via `pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom ‚Čę PLB.hom`; total LOFT compose); `JacobsonSpace (Spec kbar)` via Field ‚Üí artinian ‚Üí jacobson. (2) `Set.range őĒ.base` is closed via `IsClosedImmersion.isClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range`. (3) Closed points are dense via `closure_closedPoints` (Mathlib `Topology/JacobsonSpace.lean`). (4) Assemble: `range s_pair ‚äÜ closure (s_pair '' closedPoints) ‚äÜ closure (range őĒ) = range őĒ` via `Continuous.range_subset_closure_image_dense` + `closure_mono` + `IsClosed.closure_eq`. - **`IsProper.toLocallyOfFiniteType` + `LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian` for proper-over-Noetherian-base regularity prep** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I `instIsLocallyNoetherianProjectiveLineBar` in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`, axiom-clean.)* Direct 4-LOC Mathlib composition: `IsProper X.hom` (via project-side `projectiveLineBar_isProper` from `BareScheme.lean`) ‚Üí `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]` (via `IsProper.toLocallyOfFiniteType` instance at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`) ‚Üí `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left` (via `LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian` at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Noetherian.lean`, given `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec (.of kbar)).left` from `instIsLocallyNoetherianSpecOfIsNoetherianRingCarrier` + Field is Noetherian). - **Instance demotion (`private instance ‚Üí private theorem`) as a clean soundness fix when the body invokes `sorryAx` transitively** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A.3.i `identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean:500-567`, clears the iter-193 lean-auditor must-fix.)* When iter-N landed an `instance` whose body transitively invokes a sorry-bodied helper (e.g. `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`), the silent `sorryAx`-propagation through typeclass synthesis is qualitatively WORSE than a sorry-bodied `theorem` because consumers CANNOT opt out. **Mechanical fix**: keyword swap `instance` ‚Üí `theorem`. **Audit step**: confirm zero `instance`-firing consumers in the file (grep for typeclass-resolution sites needing the predicate). If consumers exist downstream, add `letI := ...` at each site (the descriptor authorises consuming the demoted lemma but only via explicit threading, not silent firing). **Verification**: re-run `lean_verify` on previously-axiom-clean consumers; confirm `sorryAx` is no longer in their axiom dependencies. Reusable for ANY `instance` whose body transitively invokes `sorryAx` ‚ÄĒ only `lemma`/`theorem`/`def` are safe.

- **`LocallyQuasiFinite.of_fiberToSpecResidueField` reduces LQF of a scheme morphism to per-fibre LQF** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane RCI helper (a) body reduction in `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean:866`, axiom-clean modulo per-fibre sorry.)* For `f : X ‚ü∂ Y` between schemes, `LocallyQuasiFinite f` reduces via `LocallyQuasiFinite.of_fiberToSpecResidueField (f := f) (fun x => ?_)` to a per-point fibrewise LQF statement on `f.fiberToSpecResidueField y` (Mathlib `Morphisms/QuasiFinite.lean:210`). Reusable wherever a per-fibre analysis is more tractable than the global statement (e.g. smooth-curve LQF arguments ‚ÄĒ the generic fibre is well-understood; per-fibre is the substantive obligation). Substrate building block for any `IsIso` / `IsAffineHom` / `IsFinite` proof routed through fibrewise reduction. Companion substrate landed iter-194: `algebraMap_bijective_of_finrank_one` (2-line `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_of_finrank_eq_one` + `Algebra.bijective_algebraMap_iff`) + `phi_left_functionField_algEquiv_of_finrank_one` (1-line `RingEquiv.ofBijective` consumer).

- **Bare `sorry` ‚Üí `by`-block + axiom-clean preparatory derivations + precise gap-surface docstring; DO NOT regress sorry count via full structural refactor** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane M‚Üď in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, STUCK PROTOCOL discipline.)* When a body is blocked on a multi-step Mathlib gap (e.g. Stacks 00OE + 02JK + 0AVF), the discipline is: (a) convert bare `:= sorry` to `by` block; (b) derive any axiom-clean preparatory steps inline (e.g. `Y.left.IsSeparated` via `Scheme.isSeparated_iff` + `IsSeparated.of_comp` + `Spec kbar.IsSeparated` from affine); (c) document the residual gap with concrete extraction roadmap + named upstream substrate references. **CRITICAL anti-pattern**: do NOT attempt full structural refactor exposing the gap as a single typed sorry when the refactor breaks the surrounding glue. iter-194 Lane M‚Üď Attempt 2 on `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` tried to expose `f.toPartialMap.domain = ‚ä§` as the sole sorry; the morphism-equation + uniqueness branches each needed extra `subst hdom + simp` chains that did not close cleanly, regressing sorry count 1 ‚Üí 3. Backed off. Rule: prefer "documented bare sorry inside `by` block" over "structural refactor with REGRESSION RISK". Reusable for ANY long-multi-step-Mathlib-gap-blocked body.

- **Carving consumer-facing theorems through named substrate helpers consuming only the substantive hypotheses** *(NEW iter-194 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A OCofP `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` + `exists_nonconstant_genusZero` in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean`.)* When a consumer body has multiple substantive sub-pieces (e.g. Ōá-arithmetic + cohomology vanishing + linear-algebra + Stacks 02P0), carve each into a named substrate helper that consumes ONLY the relevant hypotheses (not the consumer's hypothesis bundle). The consumer body becomes a clean 1-3 line invocation chain (e.g. `have hŌá := h0_sub_h1_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_eq_two P hP hPcoh hg; have hH1 := h1_vanishing_genusZero P hP hPcoh hg; rw [hH1] at hŌá; ... exact_mod_cast`). The carved helpers are independently attackable by future provers without needing to thread the consumer's full hypothesis bundle. **Import-cycle workaround**: when the natural helper invokes a cross-file API that creates a cycle (e.g. Ōá from `RRFormula.lean` consumes from `OcOfD.lean` which consumes from `OCofP.lean`), state the helper at the raw level (e.g. Int-valued `H‚Āį - H¬Ļ = 2` instead of via `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic`). Net mathematical content identical; the bridge is recoverable iter-195+ when the cycle resolves. Reusable for ANY consumer-facing theorem with multiple substantive substrate gaps.

- **`IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq` to bypass opaque `Proj.appIso` / Mathlib-`lift_app` simp loops** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane E `iotaGm_r_1_eq_specMap` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, axiom-clean modulo a separate factorisation sorry. Eliminates a 4-iter STUCK blocker on `Proj.appIso` (iter-188/189/190/191).)* When a goal mentions an abstract `IsOpenImmersion.lift őĻ g h_range` whose unfolding via `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` + `Proj.appIso_inv` causes maximum-recursion `simp` loops, switch routes: (1) define a CONCRETE ring map `R : A ‚Üí+* B` factoring through the open immersion (e.g. `MvPolynomial.eval_at_witness ‚ąė project-side-iso`); (2) prove the Spec-side factorisation `Spec.map R ‚Čę őĻ = g` as a separate (typically axiom-clean or narrowly-scoped) lemma; (3) apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_uniq` to obtain `IsOpenImmersion.lift őĻ g h_range = Spec.map R`. Then downstream rewrites operate on the concrete `Spec.map R`, which has clean Mathlib API (`pullbackSpecIso`, `Algebra.TensorProduct.lid`, etc.) instead of opaque `Proj.appIso_inv` chains. Reusable for any "abstract `lift` produces simp loops because the underlying Mathlib decomposition is opaque" pattern.

- **`simp only` vs `rw` for proof-irrelevant auto-param dependencies inside `pullback.lift` cocycle hypotheses** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane E `iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval` consumer refactor in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.)* When you have a lemma `iotaGm_r_1 = Spec.map kbarChart1Ring` and try to rewrite the conclusion of a `pullback.lift` consumer to substitute the LHS, plain `rw [iotaGm_r_1_eq_specMap]` fails with `motive is not type correct` because `pullback.lift`'s cocycle hypothesis (an auto-generated `_proof_3` argument) has a *type* that mentions `iotaGm_r_1`; rewriting changes the type required for the auto-param. **Use `simp only [iotaGm_r_1_eq_specMap]` instead** ‚ÄĒ `simp only` handles proof-irrelevant dependencies via its built-in `@[congr]` infrastructure and rewrites cleanly through the auto-param. Reusable for any rewrite that crosses a `pullback.lift` / `pullback.map` / `IsOpenImmersion.lift` cocycle boundary.

- **Strong induction with F-generalised quantifier for cohomology vanishing across flasque resolutions** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `HModule_flasque_subsingleton_aux` in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, axiom-clean modulo 2 named substrate Hartshorne II.1.16(b) + III.2.4 sorrys.)* For a goal `‚ąÄ F flasque, Subsingleton (HModule kbar F (n + 1))` requiring strong induction on `n`, generalise the IH over the F-argument so the recursive case can apply to a *derived* flasque quotient `G := cokernel(injectiveSES F).g`. The pattern is reusable for any "preserve property P under quotient" induction in derived-functor cohomology.

- **`Module.depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` substrate for depth-via-free-rank arguments** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G new helper at `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:798`, axiom-clean.)* For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I ‚äÜ R`, and R-modules `M, M'` with R-linear equivalence `e : M ‚ČÉ‚āó[R] M'`, `depth I M = depth I M'`. Reusable for any Auslander‚ÄďBuchsbaum / projective-dimension / flat-module argument that identifies depth across an R-linear iso (e.g. `M ‚ČÉ‚āó[R] (Fin k ‚Üí R)` for finite-free modules).

- **`@IsOpenImmersion.lift X Y Z f g inferInstance H'` explicit form for def-folded-carrier instance synthesis** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A.3.i in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`.)* When `IsOpenImmersion.lift f g H'` fails instance synthesis on `f` despite the `IsOpenImmersion f` instance being readily available (typical when `f`'s source involves a def-folded carrier extraction like `(identityComponentCarrier G).őĻ`), the workaround is the explicit form with ALL three universe-level scheme arguments + explicit `inferInstance`: `@IsOpenImmersion.lift X Y Z f g inferInstance H'`. - **`CategoryTheory.NatTrans.naturality_apply` for pointwise naturality** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `IsFlasque.cokernel_of_shortExact_flasque_flasque` in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, axiom-clean.)* When proving pointwise naturality `S.g.hom.app (op U) (S.g.hom.app (op V) TExt) = ...`, prefer `CategoryTheory.NatTrans.naturality_apply S.g.hom (homOfLE hVU).op TExt` over `NatTrans.naturality` + `FunctionLike.congr_fun`. - **Prime-avoidance via `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite` + IH-rewriting on a fresh witness `x'`** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G `notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ` in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, axiom-clean. Empirically falsifies the iter-191 KB-entry "route (iii) Krull-intersection" sketch ‚ÄĒ see Known Blockers.)* When closing a `(x) ‚ąą minimalPrimes R ‚Üí False` goal inside an induction on `spanFinrank ūĚĒ™ R` with a universally-quantified IH (`hIH : ‚ąÄ R' (...) , IsDomain R'`), do NOT fight the specific `x` from the goal. Pick a *fresh* witness `x' ‚ąą ūĚĒ™ \ (ūĚĒ™¬≤ ‚ą™ ‚čÉ minimalPrimes R)` via `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`. Then: (1) `R/(x')` is regular local of `spanFinrank = k` via `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` (iter-190 helper); (2) `IsDomain (R/(x'))` via `hIH`; (3) `(x')` is prime; (4) pick minimal prime `ūĚĒ≠' ‚äÜ (x')` via `Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le`; `x' ‚ąČ ūĚĒ≠'` by avoidance; (5) for `y ‚ąą ūĚĒ≠' ‚äÜ (x')`: `y = x' ¬∑ z` with `z ‚ąą ūĚĒ≠'` (primality + `x' ‚ąČ ūĚĒ≠'`), so `ūĚĒ≠' ‚äÜ x' ¬∑ ūĚĒ≠' ‚äÜ jacobson R ¬∑ ūĚĒ≠'`; Nakayama (`Submodule.FG.eq_bot_of_le_jacobson_smul`) gives `ūĚĒ≠' = ‚ä•`; (6) `‚ä• ‚ąą minimalPrimes R` is prime, so `IsDomain R`; (7) in a domain `(‚ä•).minimalPrimes = {‚ä•}` (`Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self`), forcing `(x) = ‚ä•` ‚üĻ `x = 0` contradicting `x ‚ąČ ūĚĒ™¬≤`. Key lemmas: `minimalPrimes.finite_of_isNoetherianRing`, `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`, `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`, `Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime`, `Submodule.FG.eq_bot_of_le_jacobson_smul`, `IsLocalRing.ringJacobson_eq_maximalIdeal`, `IsDomain.of_bot_isPrime`. Reusable for any Stacks-00NQ-style "regular local ‚üĻ domain" closure inside an induction where the IH is universally quantified in the ring.

- **Aliasing-`set y` (NOT `let y`) for instance scope + `change`-to-Y-side-action + key identity via `Scheme.Hom.appLE_appIso_inv`** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane F `pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict` in `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`, axiom-clean.)* The canonical recipe for restrict-iso-pullback module isomorphism smul-compatibility on `Scheme.Modules`. Step A: `set y : őď(N, hU.fromSpec ''ŠĶĀ ‚ä§) := ...` (NOT `let y` ‚ÄĒ `set` substitutes the term in the goal, putting the smul instance on `y`'s type in the `őď(Spec _, ‚ä§)`-action defined via the restrict module structure on the carrier; `let` would not propagate the substitution). Step B: `change ((hU.fromSpec.appIso ‚ä§).inv.hom (r ‚ÄĘ _) = ...)` to re-express the smul as the Y-side action via the rfl-unfold of `restrictFunctor`'s smul. Step C: `rw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul]` to migrate the Y-side scalar through the presheaf restriction. Step D: prove the categorical key identity `(őďSpecIso _).inv ‚Čę (hU.fromSpec.appIso ‚ä§).inv ‚Čę Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op = ūĚüô`. **Sub-key identity**: `hU.fromSpec.appLE U ‚ä§ e‚āÄ = (őďSpecIso őď(Y, U)).inv` (where `e‚āÄ : ‚ä§ ‚Č§ hU.fromSpec ‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ U`) closes via `simp [Scheme.Hom.appLE, hU.fromSpec_app_self, ‚Üź Functor.map_comp]` ‚ÄĒ internally collapses the Spec-side `eqToHom hU.fromSpec_preimage_self ‚Čę homOfLE e‚āÄ : ‚ä§ ‚ü∂ ‚ä§` poset arrow through `Functor.map_id`. ~50 LOC total. Reusable for any restrict-iso-pullback module isomorphism whose smul-compatibility is the residual gap.

- **`private` in Lean 4 controls VISIBILITY, not NAMESPACE REGISTRATION ‚ÄĒ collisions with imports possible** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane RRF `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` shadow-removal in `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`.)* Suppose iter-N landed a `private theorem Foo.bar` as a typed-sorry shadow in file A; iter-(N+1) wants to import `Foo.bar` from file B. Naive expectation: `private` scopes the local declaration so the import resolves to the public version. **Wrong**: `private` controls *visibility* (callability from outside the file) but the fully-qualified name `Foo.bar` still registers in the environment. Lean rejects the redeclaration with `a non-private declaration 'Foo.bar' has already been declared` at the import site, NOT silently shadow. **Remediation**: delete the private placeholder entirely; replace with a `/-! sourcing-change note -/` docstring block. Downstream consumers then resolve to the imported public version automatically. **Alternative**: use a distinctive private name (e.g. `Foo.bar_local`) and add an explicit migration TODO. Reusable for any "land file-local shadow iter-N + public iter-(N+1) consumes downstream" pattern.

- **`HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` + `Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚āĀ` + `Abelian.Ext.addEquiv‚āÄ` is the generic abelian-category `Ext^1`-vanishing recipe given SES with injective middle + Hom-surjectivity** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective` in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, axiom-clean. Substrate for the `i = 1` case of `HModule_flasque_eq_zero`.)* For SES `0 ‚Üí S.X‚āĀ ‚Üí S.X‚āā ‚Üí S.X‚āÉ ‚Üí 0` in an abelian category with `S.X‚āā` injective and `Hom(X, S.X‚āā) ‚Üí Hom(X, S.X‚āÉ)` surjective, prove `Ext X S.X‚āĀ 1` is subsingleton via: (1) `HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` ‚üĻ `Ext X S.X‚āā 1` subsingleton ‚üĻ `x‚āĀ.comp (Ext.mk‚āÄ S.f) = 0` for any `x‚āĀ ‚ąą Ext X S.X‚āĀ 1`; (2) `Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚āĀ` lifts `x‚āĀ` to some `x‚āÉ ‚ąą Ext X S.X‚āÉ 0` via the connecting morphism; (3) `Abelian.Ext.addEquiv‚āÄ` identifies `x‚āÉ` with a `Hom(X, S.X‚āÉ)` ‚ÄĒ discharge by the surjectivity hypothesis (lift along `S.g`). Reusable for any Hartshorne III.6.1-style `Ext^1` vanishing on a flasque/injective embedding. Companion lemmas: `Abelian.Ext.mk‚āÄ_comp_mk‚āÄ`, `Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc`, `Abelian.Ext.comp_mk‚āÄ_id`, `Abelian.Ext.comp_zero`, `ShortComplex.ShortExact.comp_extClass_assoc`.

- **`KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` + `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule` for stalk-localised K√§hler differentials freeness** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane M‚Üď Stage 5 in-body chain in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, axiom-clean.)* To deduce `Module.Free (stalk z) ő©[(stalk z)‚ĀĄőď(Spec _, U)]` from `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth őď(Spec _, U) őď(X.left, V)` + `IsLocalization.AtPrime (stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ‚ü®z, hzV‚ü©).asIdeal`, chain: (1) `letI : Algebra őď(Spec (.of kbar), U) (stalk z) := ‚ü®composition‚ü©` to install the algebra structure; (2) `haveI : IsScalarTower őď(Spec _, U) őď(X.left, V) (stalk z) := ‚ü®of_algebraMap_eq, rfl‚ü©`; (3) `haveI : IsLocalizedModule (primeCompl _) (KaehlerDifferential.map _ _ _ _) := KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map _ _ _ _`; (4) `haveI _hFreeStalk : Module.Free (stalk z) ő©[(stalk z)‚ĀĄőď(Spec _, U)] := Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule _ _ _ _ _ _ (KaehlerDifferential.map _ _ _ _)`. Axiom-clean. Residual gap (cotangent ‚ÜĒ K√§hler over a field + smooth dim formula) is narrowly 2-step and may be Mathlib-PR cleaner than project-side. Substrate-level building block for the Smooth ‚üĻ IsRegularLocalRing chain.

- **`Subtype.preconnectedSpace isPreconnected_connectedComponent` + explicit witness ‚üĻ `ConnectedSpace ‚Ü•{p // p ‚ąą connectedComponent x}`** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A.3.i `identityComponentCarrier_connectedSpace` + `identityComponent_connectedSpace` in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`, axiom-clean.)* The clean recipe for `ConnectedSpace` of a connected-component carrier: (1) provide `isPreconnected_connectedComponent` to `Subtype.preconnectedSpace`; (2) provide the explicit witness `x ‚ąą connectedComponent x` (rfl-membership). Transport to the carrier-level scheme `Opens` via `change`-to-defeq + `infer_instance` (instance synthesis fires once the defeq is exposed; the autosynthesis path through the `Opens.toScheme` coercion is not direct without the explicit `change`). Reusable for any "identity component carrier inherits ConnectedSpace" obligation downstream of a `LocallyConnectedSpace` instance.

- **`degree (positivePart D) = D.sum (¬∑ ‚äĒ 0)` via `Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index`** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I `degree_positivePart_eq_sum_max` in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`, axiom-clean.)* Structural reduction for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree (positivePart D)` to the explicit sum form `D.sum (fun _ n => max n 0)`: `unfold positivePart degree; exact Finsupp.sum_mapRange_index (h := fun _ b => b) (by intro _; rfl)`. ~3 LOC. Reusable for any Hartshorne II.6.10 / Riemann-Roch-style "sum-of-positive-orders" computation. Companion: `positivePart` is defined as `Finsupp.mapRange (¬∑ ‚äĒ 0) (by simp)` (the `mapRange` form, avoiding `noncomputable` `Finsupp.semilatticeSup`).

- **Direct ModuleCat-Subsingleton-of-zero route for closed-point skyscraper flasqueness (bypasses planned 2-step composition via constant-sheaf flasque + skyscraper = pushforward)** *(NEW iter-191 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque` in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, axiom-clean.)* When the headline goal is `IsFlasque (skyscraperSheaf p (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar))` on a topological space with decidable membership `[(U : Opens X) ‚Üí Decidable (p ‚ąą U)]`, do NOT route through the planned 2-step composition `skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const + IsFlasque.constant_of_irreducible + IsFlasque.pushforward`. The direct route closes in ~20 LOC by case-splitting `by_cases hV : p ‚ąą V` on the smaller open: (1) `p ‚ąą V`: the restriction map along `V ‚Č§ U` is `eqToHom` (an iso via `instIsIsoEqToHom`), hence surjective via `(ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _).2`; the key Mathlib lemma is `skyscraperPresheaf_map` with `dif_pos hV`. (2) `p ‚ąČ V`: the codomain `(skyscraperPresheaf p A).obj (Opposite.op V)` is the terminal/zero object `‚ä§_ ModuleCat kbar`, hence `Subsingleton` via `ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (terminalIsTerminal.isZero)`; surjectivity is then `intro y; exact ‚ü®0, Subsingleton.elim _ _‚ü©`. Direct route bypasses unproven sheafification-of-constant-presheaf-on-irreducible-space (Mathlib gap at b80f227) and sheaf-level skyscraper-equals-pushforward (Mathlib gap at b80f227) ‚ÄĒ both become OPTIONAL exposition rather than critical-path substrate. Reusable for any flasqueness proof on a sheaf that's locally `eqToHom`-or-`‚ä§_ ModuleCat`-valued.

- **Bypass tensor-IsDomain via open immersion + ambient `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Gm ‚Ü™ ūĚĒł¬Ļ`-type GI proofs** *(NEW iter-191 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane B `gm_geomIrred` in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`, axiom-clean.)* When the goal `GeometricallyIrreducible (Spec.map (algebraMap A B).hom)` for `B = Localization.Away f` requires a heavy `K ‚äó_kbar B` IsDomain (and that tensor-IsDomain is blocked by Mathlib-gap or Mathlib-regression), bypass via open immersion: factor `(Spec.map (algebraMap kbar B)) = Spec.map (algebraMap A B) ‚Čę Spec.map (algebraMap kbar A)` where the first arrow is open immersion (via `isOpenImmersion_SpecMap_localizationAway`) and the second arrow has GI directly via the lighter base change `K ‚äó_kbar A ‚ČÉ ‚Ä¶ (e.g. MvPoly Unit K)` IsDomain. Then apply Mathlib's `[IsOpenImmersion f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g] [Surjective (f ‚Čę g)] ‚Üí GeometricallyIrreducible (f ‚Čę g)` instance. **CRITICAL tooling**: requires `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` to fire the instance ‚ÄĒ without it, the typeclass synthesis stalls. **Surjectivity discharge** via `Subsingleton (Spec K)` for K a field. The bypass replaces a `K ‚äó_kbar Localization.Away f` IsDomain (requires localization synthesis + tensor-IsDomain) with a `K ‚äó_kbar MvPoly Unit kbar ‚ČÉ MvPoly Unit K` IsDomain (pure polynomial-ring base change, ~10 LOC via `Algebra.TensorProduct.comm + MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv + Function.Injective.isDomain`). Reusable for any "GI for an affine open subscheme of an affine GI scheme" obligation.

- **Mathlib b80f227 packages Stacks 00T7 directly off `[Smooth]`: `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth` + auto `[Smooth] : Flat` instance** *(NEW iter-191 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane M‚Üď `Stage 1-2` re-exports in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, axiom-clean.)* When proving regularity of a stalk of a smooth morphism, Stages 1 (smooth ‚üĻ flat at stalk) and 2 (smooth ‚üĻ standard smooth presentation locally) are direct one-liners off the `[Smooth f]` typeclass ‚ÄĒ NO manual affine-cover threading. Stage 1: `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.stalkMap X.hom z` (Mathlib `Morphisms/Flat.lean:94`) ‚ÄĒ `[Smooth f] : Flat f` is a priority-`low` instance at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Smooth.lean:113` that auto-synthesizes. Stage 2: `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z` (Mathlib `Morphisms/Smooth.lean:99`) produces the existential `‚ąÉ U V, IsAffineOpen U, IsAffineOpen V, x ‚ąą V, V ‚Č§ f ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' U, IsStandardSmooth (f.appLE U V e).hom`. Total ~5 LOC for both stages. The remaining Stacks 00TT chain (polynomial generators ‚üĻ regular sequence ‚üĻ regular local ring) is the substantive part (Stages 3-4). Reusable for any "smooth ‚üĻ properties of stalks" obligation.

- **`Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes` + `mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff` + `push Not` extracts a zero-divisor witness from `x ‚ąą minimalPrime ūĚĒ≠` in 5 LOC** *(NEW iter-191 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G `exists_ne_zero_mul_eq_zero_of_mem_minimalPrimes` in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, axiom-clean.)* For any commutative ring `R` and `ūĚĒ≠ ‚ąą minimalPrimes R`, every `x ‚ąą ūĚĒ≠` admits a non-zero `y : R` with `x * y = 0`. Recipe: `(Ideal.disjoint_nonZeroDivisors_of_mem_minimalPrimes hūĚĒ≠).mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_of_mem ‚ü®x, hx‚ü©` gives `¬¨ x ‚ąą nonZeroDivisors R`; `push_neg + mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff` extracts the witness with the equation. This is the canonical Stacks 00NQ substrate for prime-avoidance routes: combined with the `R/(x)` structure under `[IsLocalRing R, IsRegularLocalRing R, x ‚ąą ūĚĒ™ \ ūĚĒ™¬≤]` (gives `IsDomain (R/(x))` by induction), it unlocks the direct prime-avoidance + Krull-intersection route to `IsDomain R` (route (iii) in the docstring of `notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ`). Substrate for iter-192+ via `Ideal.iInf_pow_eq_bot_of_isLocalRing` (Krull intersection). Reusable for any "minimal prime ‚üĻ exists zero-divisor partner" obligation.

- **Mathlib regression watch: `IsLocalization.Away` synthesis inside `set S := TensorProduct ...` bindings stalls at b80f227 ‚ÄĒ workaround is `letI` or explicit type annotations** *(NEW iter-191 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane B `Cross01Substrate.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain` source-compile regression in `Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean`.)* When a proof body uses `set S := TensorProduct R A B with hS` and then needs `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit xS` (or similar instance-driven localization API), Mathlib b80f227 fails to synthesize the `IsLocalization.Away xS ?m.xxx` instance ‚ÄĒ the localization-target metavar isn't resolved through the `set`-binding. **The cached `.olean` from a prior Mathlib state continues to work**, but fresh source re-compilation fails. Mitigation: refactor the body to replace `set S := ...` with explicit `letI` or with explicit type annotations on every use of `S`. Watch: if a future Mathlib bump invalidates the cache, downstream consumers (e.g. `projGm_isReduced`, `gm_geomIrred` via Substrate 2) break silently. This is **NOT** an upstream Mathlib bug to file ‚ÄĒ `set`-bindings have always had instance-synthesis nuances; the regression is just a tightening of the synthesis behaviour. Reusable diagnostic for any IsLocalization-bearing proof body that started failing after a Mathlib bump.

- **Parallel-paired prover dispatch across two files (define-public in A, consume in B) is unsafe ‚ÄĒ serialise across iters or use distinctive private fallback names** *(NEW iter-190 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I positivePart clash in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` + `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean` ‚üĻ integration build RED.)* When a refactor needs a NEW public symbol introduced in file A and consumed in file B in the same iter, the loop's parallel prover dispatch builds each file from its own snapshot ‚ÄĒ file B's prover does NOT see file A's public def. If file B's planned fallback uses the SAME fully-qualified name (even as `private`), Lean rejects the redeclaration at integration time. The `private` modifier does NOT shadow an imported public name ‚ÄĒ Lean's namespace resolution is at the fully-qualified level. Mitigations: (a) **serialise** ‚ÄĒ land the public def in file A iter-(N), consume in file B iter-(N+1); (b) **distinctive private name** ‚ÄĒ mandate the file-B prover to use a clearly-different fallback (e.g. `_positivePartLocal`) with explicit migration scheduled iter-(N+1). The iter-190 plan's instruction "consume in B *after* the dependency lands" is ambiguous: it can mean "serialised across iters" or "ordered within one iter" ‚ÄĒ and the loop's parallel dispatcher defaults to the second reading. Reusable rule: any time a paired prover dispatch involves a NEW public symbol crossing files, the plan agent must explicitly state which iter lands which side, and the prover descriptors should validate against name collisions before integration.

- **Existential vs equational typed-sorry pins: prefer existential when the equation is false-as-stated for free parameters** *(NEW iter-190 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I WeilDivisor `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` in `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`.)* When a downstream equational statement is mathematically false-as-stated for arbitrary values of a free parameter (e.g. `degree (positivePart (principal (algebraMap K K(C) t) halg)) = Module.finrank K K(C)` holds only when `t` is a local parameter at a closed point of Spec K, not for arbitrary `t : K`), prefer an existential signature in the typed-sorry pin: `‚ąÉ (t : K) (halg : ... ‚Č† 0), <equation>`. The existential form is true (witness exists), preserves soundness against typed-sorry exploitation, and is body-able via the standard recipe (Hartshorne II.6.9 + `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`). **Trade-off**: introduces a blueprint‚ÜĒLean mismatch since the blueprint label typically reads as an equation; reviewer flags it for plan-phase resolution (either re-tighten Lean signature with an explicit "local parameter at ‚ąě" hypothesis, or re-phrase blueprint prose to match the existential). Reusable for any "naked equation involving a free generator" obligation whose truth requires the generator to be a specific kind of element.

- **`ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_encard` (Krull's height theorem) sidesteps `IsSMulRegular` requirement for the regular-quotient half of Stacks 00NQ joint induction** *(NEW iter-190 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G2 `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`.)* When proving `R/(x)` is regular local of `spanFinrank = k` for `[IsRegularLocalRing R]` of `spanFinrank ūĚĒ™ = k + 1` and `x ‚ąą ūĚĒ™ \ ūĚĒ™¬≤`, the obvious path via `ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim` requires `IsSMulRegular R x`, which in turn requires `[IsDomain R]` ‚ÄĒ the very thing being proved in the outer Stacks 00NQ induction (circular). Krull's height theorem in the form `ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_encard` does NOT require x to be a non-zero-divisor, just the dim-lower-bound inequality. Combined with cotangent dim drop via `finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ` (iter-188 G1 helper) + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace` for the őļ-finrank ‚ÜĒ spanFinrank translation, the regular-quotient half closes axiom-clean without breaking the outer induction. WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě arithmetic via `WithBot.add_eq_coe` + `WithTop.add_right_cancel`. Reusable for any "regular local of dim k+1 quotient by a ūĚĒ™\ūĚĒ™¬≤ element is regular local of dim k" obligation outside an IsDomain context.

- **Direct irreducibility-based sheaf gluing for ModuleCat-valued carrier presheaves (NOT Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff)** *(NEW iter-189 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A OCofP `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` Case B in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean`.)* When a carrier presheaf is ModuleCat-valued (not Type-valued) and is the project's own design (not a Subfunctor of a Mathlib presheaf), the Subfunctor framework requires an additional Type ‚ÜĒ ModuleCat translation step that doubles the proof length. Cleaner direct argument: extract a non-empty witness index via `iSup_eq_bot` contrapositive; non-empty intersection from preirreducibility via `@nonempty_preirreducible_inter` (the `@`-form is REQUIRED to bridge `IrreducibleSpace ‚Ü•X` (autosynthesised from `IsIntegral X`) vs `Set ‚ÜĎX.toTopCat` (what the lemma operates on) ‚ÄĒ defeq but Lean's instance synthesis does not unfold across the two paths); value uniformity `key_val : ‚ąÄ i, U i ‚Č† ‚ä• ‚Üí (sf i).1 = v` via `congr_arg Subtype.val` on the compat hypothesis + an inline `map_val` helper exploiting `dif_neg hV` to expose the `Submodule.inclusion` underlying value-preservation; membership via `Or.inl hSup` (trivAtBot) + `TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup`; uniqueness via gluing-at-i‚āÄ projected to `.1`. Reusable for any project-defined ModuleCat-valued carrier with `‚äď trivAtBot` empty-fix wrapping (see iter-188 KB entry).

- **Inline 5-LOC ring-kernel-radicality from reduced codomain** *(NEW iter-189 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane B `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` in `Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean`.)* When a Galois-connection chain needs `(RingHom.ker f).IsRadical` and the codomain `S` is reduced, the easy direction "S reduced ‚üĻ `(ker f).IsRadical`" closes in 5 LOC via `intro x ‚ü®n, hxn‚ü©; obtain ‚ü®...‚ü©; exact eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero (h := hxn) ‚ü®n, ...‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ bypasses Mathlib's `RingHom.ker_isRadical_iff_reduced_of_surjective` which requires surjectivity (and is irrelevant when only the easy direction is needed). Reusable for any Galois-connection radical-ideal chain. Iter-189 instance: pushed through `Scheme.Hom.ker_apply` + `Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal` simp lemmas pointwise on affine opens.

- **`letI`-driven őď-module structure on pullback sections** *(NEW iter-189 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane F `pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict` in `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`.)* To set up the `őď(Y, U)`-module structure on a pullback section LHS for a section-level `LinearEquiv` signature, use:
  ```
  letI : Algebra őď(Y, U) őď((Spec őď(Y, U)), ‚ä§) := (Scheme.őďSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Module őď(Y, U) őď((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ‚ä§) :=
    Module.compHom _ (Scheme.őďSpecIso _).inv.hom
  ```
  This installs the typeclass infrastructure that allows `Nonempty (őď(...) ‚ČÉ‚āó[őď(Y, U)] őď(N, U))`-shaped signatures to typecheck. Reusable any time a pullback module-section needs a őď-algebra structure transported via `Scheme.őďSpecIso`.

- **`Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_of_finrank_eq_one` one-liner for function-field iso from finrank** *(NEW iter-189 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I `iso_of_degree_one` Step 1 in `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean`.)* For a finite extension `B/A` with `Module.finrank A B = 1`, the canonical algebra map `A ‚Üí B` is surjective via: `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_of_finrank_eq_one h_finrank` gives `‚ä• = ‚ä§` in the subalgebra lattice; combined with `Algebra.surjective_algebraMap_iff` (membership in `‚ä§` ‚üļ in range), surjectivity is immediate; combined with `RingHom.injective` (for fields) and `RingEquiv.ofBijective`, the iso `B ‚ČÉ+* A` is produced in ~10 LOC. Reusable for any "degree-1 finite extension ‚üĻ iso" obligation. Iter-189 instance: produced the function-field iso `K(C') ‚ČÉ+* K(C)` from `Module.finrank K(C') K(C) = 1` hypothesis on a non-constant morphism of smooth proper curves.

- **Closed-immersion ‚áí QuasiCompact auto-instance chain (no explicit `[QuasiCompact i]` argument needed)** *(NEW iter-189 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane B Substrate 1 signature in `Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean`.)* `IsClosedImmersion ‚Üí IsAffineHom ‚Üí QuasiCompact` are low-prio instance arrows in Mathlib (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Affine.lean:58` + `Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean:149`). When writing a theorem with a `[IsClosedImmersion i]` argument, do NOT also add `[QuasiCompact i]` ‚ÄĒ it synthesizes automatically. Adding the redundant constraint creates downstream brittleness (consumers may not have the typeclass triggered). Reusable for any closed-immersion-bearing signature.

- **Stacks 00NU regularity-propagation via iter-188 G1 cotangent dim-drop bridge** *(NEW iter-189 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G2 `exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` axiom-clean modulo `isDomain_of_regularLocal` (pure Stacks 00NQ), in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`.)* To close the regular-quotient half of joint induction on Krull dimension WITHOUT a direct Mathlib analog of Stacks 00NU, chain: (1) `finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ` (iter-188 G1 helper) gives `dim_őļ (ūĚĒ™(R/(x))/ūĚĒ™¬≤) = k`; (2) `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace` translates őļ-finrank to `spanFinrank`; (3) `ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim` (with `IsSMulRegular R x` precondition) drops Krull dim; (4) `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal` (= ringKrullDim R) + combined arithmetic gives `ringKrullDim (R/(x)) = k`; (5) `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` produces the `[IsRegularLocalRing (R/(x))]` instance. WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě arithmetic via `WithBot.add_eq_coe` + `WithTop.add_right_cancel`. Conditional on `[IsSMulRegular R x]` which requires `[IsDomain R]` (pure Stacks 00NQ ‚ÄĒ separate substrate). Reusable for any "regular local ring quotient by a regular element is regular local of smaller dim" obligation, modulo the Stacks 00NQ substrate.

- **Subagent-licensed structural unbundle: prefer +1/+2 named-pin scaffolding over single-session compositional close** *(NEW iter-189 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane F `_sectionLinearEquiv` unbundle per analogist Decision 4 in `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`.)* When a mathlib-analogist returns "Structural OK, but the residual sorry bundles K ‚Č• 2 independent Mathlib gaps", landing K named typed-sorry pins (each independently targetable) is preferable to attempting full closure in one session ‚ÄĒ even though file sorry count increases by K‚ąí1.
