The independent-targeting payoff iter-(N+k) is real: each pin can be closed by a separate prover round with a dedicated recipe; the bundled approach requires ONE session that closes all K gaps + compositional glue (~K√ó the LOC + cognitive load). Iter-189 instance: Lane F `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv` body had 3 Mathlib gaps (Stacks 01HQ + 01I8 + transport iso) bundled into one typed sorry; unbundling produced 2 new pins (Stacks 01I8 + transport) + retained 1 existing pin (Stacks 01HQ from iter-187); residual is now PURE compositional bookkeeping (no remaining Mathlib-gap algebra). Reusable for any directive of shape "close substantive body bundling N Mathlib gaps in one session".

- **`Nonempty (ő£' equiv, intertwining)` packaging idiom for `IsBaseChange.of_equiv`-style Prop consumers** *(NEW iter-188 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane F `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv` in `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`.)* When a `Prop` consumer like `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` requires BOTH a `LinearEquiv` AND a defining-equation hypothesis on it as inputs, bundle them as a single ő£-struct returned via `Nonempty (ő£' f : TensorProduct R S M ‚ČÉ‚āó[S] N, ‚ąÄ x, f (1 ‚äó x) = baseMap g N e x)` rather than splitting into two named typed sorries (which wastes helper budget). The consumer body becomes `obtain ‚ü®equiv, hApp‚ü© := helper g N _hU _hV e; exact IsBaseChange.of_equiv equiv hApp` ‚ÄĒ keeps the helper signature clean and avoids destructuring two separate `Nonempty (...)` hypotheses. Reusable for any "build a `Prop` witness from a `Type` construction + a defining equation" obligation.

- **Sheaf-condition empty-cover fix via `carrier ‚äď trivAtBot` where `trivAtBot U := {f | U ‚Č† ‚ä• ‚ą® f = 0}`** *(NEW iter-188 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf` structural fix in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean`.)* When a project's carrier is defined by "conditions that are vacuously satisfied on the empty open" (e.g. order conditions on a rational function), the resulting `carrier(‚ä•) = K(C)` (the full function field) violates the sheaf-condition empty-sieve axiom (empty cover forces `F(‚ä•) = terminal = 0`). Fix: wrap as `carrierSheaf U := carrier U ‚äď trivAtBot U` where `trivAtBot U := {f | U.unop ‚Č† ‚ä• ‚ą® f = 0}` (a Submodule with `zero_mem'/add_mem'/smul_mem'` discharged by case-split on the disjunction). At `U = ‚ä•`: trivAtBot = ‚ä•, intersection = ‚ä•. At `U ‚Č† ‚ä•`: trivAtBot = ‚ä§, intersection = original. Downstream consumers need an extra `htop_ne_bot` helper (using `Nonempty X.toTopCat`) to discharge the `trivAtBot` factor at `op ‚ä§`; presheaf restriction map must be case-based (zero if target ‚ä•; inclusion via a `_le` monotonicity lemma otherwise). Reusable for any presheaf whose carrier is vacuously non-trivial at ‚ä•.

- **EGA I 6.1.9 closure recipe (LocallyConnectedSpace from IsLocallyNoetherian)** *(NEW iter-188 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A.3.i `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`, axiom-clean ~50 LOC.)* Two pure-topology helpers + scheme-specific wrapper. (1) `noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents` (~17 LOC): `Finite (ConnectedComponents őĪ)` for Noetherian őĪ via surjection `irreducibleComponents őĪ ‚Üí ConnectedComponents őĪ` (well-defined: each irreducible set is preconnected; surjective: `irreducibleComponent x ‚äÜ connectedComponent x`); source finite by `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`. (2) `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent` (~9 LOC): `ConnectedComponents őĪ` is `TotallyDisconnectedSpace` (`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1 (`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by (1); Finite T1 ‚üĻ discrete (`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`); singleton clopen in quotient; preimage clopen via `connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`. (3) Scheme wrapper (~25 LOC): for each `x : G.left` and `F ‚ąą ūĚďĚ x`, extract open `T ‚äÜ F` (`mem_nhds_iff`), then affine open `W ‚äÜ T` (`AlgebraicGeometry.exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom` + `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k)` gives `IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (`LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); affine open + Noetherian ring ‚üĻ Noetherian topological space (`noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen`); in `‚Ü•W`, `connectedComponent ‚ü®x, hxW‚ü©` is open by (2); image under `Subtype.val` (open map via `IsOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val`) is the wanted open connected nbhd. **Tooling trap**: use `locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected` form (NOT `_connectedComponentIn_open`, which fails instance synthesis on `‚Ü•(‚ÜĎW : Set G.left)` vs `‚Ü•W` Opens CoeSort form, defeq but no auto-unfold); `let xW : ‚Ü•W := ‚ü®x, hxW‚ü©` with explicit ‚Ü•; ascribe `haveI hNoethW : NoetherianSpace ‚Ü•W := ...` explicitly. Both topology helpers are Mathlib upstream PR candidates (pure topology, would sit in `Mathlib/Topology/NoetherianSpace.lean` alongside `finite_irreducibleComponents`).

- **Steinitz-exchange via `linear_combination` + `rwa [‚Üź hv‚āÄ_eq] at`** *(NEW iter-188 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G1 `finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ` (‚Č§) direction in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`.)* When expressing `v‚āÄ ‚ąą Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v‚āÄ))` via explicit `v‚āÄ = (‚ÜĎu‚ĀĽ¬Ļ) * x - ‚ąĎ (‚ÜĎu‚ĀĽ¬Ļ) * c v * v` (the Steinitz-exchange substitution where `u = IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mp h` makes `c v‚āÄ` a unit), use `linear_combination` (NOT `ring`, which fails on the sum) for the algebraic manipulation. **Critical**: `rw [hv‚āÄ_eq]` (with `hv‚āÄ_eq : v‚āÄ = expr`) rewrites ALL occurrences of `v‚āÄ`, INCLUDING the one inside `V.erase v‚āÄ`, breaking the second. Workaround: prove the membership with the explicit `expr` form first, then use `rwa [‚Üź hv‚āÄ_eq] at this` to substitute only the unique `expr` occurrence backwards. `nth_rewrite 1` and `conv_lhs` are not sufficient here. Companion idioms: `Submodule.FG` dot-notation fails (definition, not structure ‚ÄĒ use explicit `Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank h`); `Set.ncard_coe_finset` (lowercase `f`); `Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing` (not `Submodule.fg_...`). Reusable for any Steinitz-style "expressing one generator in terms of the others" in module-rank arithmetic.

- **Explicit `(C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar)` annotation to normalise `‚ÜĎ(Module.finrank ...)` patterns when `rw` reports "did not find"** *(NEW iter-188 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf` body in `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`.)* When downstream `rw [helper]` reports "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" despite the pattern being syntactically present inside a `‚ÜĎ(¬∑)` (Nat-to-Int) cast, the cause is typically that the implicit `(C := ?M)` argument of a typeclass-instance-laden term (e.g. `skyscraperSheaf`) elaborates differently in helper-conclusion vs parent contexts. The fix: prepend a `change` with the explicit implicit argument: `change ((Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point _) 0) : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ą§) - ... = 1`. This forces consistent universe elaboration; the subsequent `rw [H0_..., H1_...]; simp` then closes the arithmetic residual. Also: when a downstream `rw [if_pos trivial]` fails because the dite predicate is `P.point ‚ąą ‚ä§` (not `True`), use `exact (if_pos trivial).symm ‚Ėł Module.finrank_self kbar` ‚ÄĒ the `‚Ėł` substitution unifies both the carrier and the dite predicate in one shot. Reusable for any `Module.finrank`/`Module.rank` proof whose helpers are stated in slightly different typeclass-elaboration contexts.

- **`RepresentablyFlat (Opens.map f) + final_of_representablyFlat` gives `.Final` on `Opens.map g.base` for ANY scheme morphism (not just open immersions)** *(NEW iter-188 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A.1.b `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` chart-iso closure in `Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean`.)* When closing the unit-preservation step in a pullback-of-line-bundle iso chain via `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the `.Final` instance on the underlying continuous functor is required for it to be an iso. The Mathlib substrate `RepresentablyFlat (Opens.map f)` (in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/SheafCondition/Sites`) is established for ANY scheme morphism `f`, not just open immersions. Combined with `CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat`, this gives `(Opens.map g.base).Final` automatically ‚ÄĒ sidesteps the worry that the chart-iso step would need open-immersion-only Mathlib API. The 7-iso chain `restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.őĻ ‚Üí pullbackComp V.őĻ f ‚Üí pullbackCongr ‚Üí (pullbackComp g U.őĻ).symm ‚Üí restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.őĻ ‚Üí (pullback g).mapIso eM ‚Üí asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)` then closes the full chart-iso step in ~35 LOC. Reusable for any "pullback of a unit sheaf is the unit on the pullback" obligation in the SheafOfModules theory.

- **4-step recipe for `localParameterAtInfty`-style function-field witnesses** *(NEW iter-188 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I `localParameterAtInfty` in `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean`.)* When building a concrete function-field witness (a non-zero element `t ‚ąą K(C)` that vanishes on some named subset, e.g. a local parameter at a point), do NOT use a `Classical.choice ‚ü®_, _‚ü©` placeholder. The 4-step recipe: (1) pick the relevant chart affine open from the project's affine cover (e.g. `Proj.basicOpen ūĚíú (X 1)` for the chart-1 open of ‚Ąô¬Ļ); (2) construct the desired ratio coordinate as a `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk ūĚíú hf 1 (MvPolynomial.X 0) hf0` element (degree 0); (3) embed via `Scheme.germToFunctionField` (transferred through `Proj.basicOpenIsoAway` for the ring identification + `Proj.opensRange_awayőĻ` to identify `basicOpen` with the open range of `awayőĻ`); (4) discharge the non-zero witness via injectivity of `germToFunctionField` (`Scheme.germToFunctionField_injective`) + Nontriviality chain `IsLocalization.injective` + `powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors` + `Function.Injective.nontrivial`, then `IsLocalization.mk'_eq_zero_iff` + `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero` + `pow_ne_zero` for the polynomial non-zeroness. **Tooling**: `change` (not `show`, which trips the project's style linter); final `rw [hsec_zero, map_zero]` may leave `(0 : őď(...)) = 0` requiring explicit `rfl` due to instance-laden term shape. Reusable for any concrete function-field element construction with a non-zero witness.

- **ŌÜ-as-typeclass-binder via `[Algebra K(Y) K(X)]` for morphisms between proper smooth curves** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I broke 4-iter STUCK route by closing `Hom.poleDivisor` body in `RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean`.)* When `ŌÜ : X ‚Üí Y` is a morphism between proper smooth curves and downstream constructions need the function-field algebra map `algebraMap K(Y) K(X)`, thread the map as a typeclass binder `[Algebra K(Y) K(X)]` rather than carrying `ŌÜ` explicitly through every sub-construction. The ŌÜ-dependence then flows via the canonical `algebraMap _ _ : K(Y) ‚Üí K(X)` without `Hom.functionFieldMap ŌÜ` boilerplate. Combined with `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (algebraMap _ _ t.val) halg` (where `halg = RingHom.injective` of the field algebra map), the body for `Hom.poleDivisor ŌÜ` unfolds to a substantive explicit formula ‚ÄĒ no `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / `proof_wanted` placeholder. Documented in `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2. Reusable for any "pull a divisor / function / module back along ŌÜ" obligation between curves.

- **`constantSheafAdj.unit.app` at `op ‚ä§` (terminal) for extracting kbar-values from `(constantSheaf _).obj kbar ‚ü∂ F` morphisms** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField` closure in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean`, axiom-clean.)* To extract a kbar-value from a sheaf morphism `sheafHom : (constantSheaf _).obj kbar ‚ü∂ F` on a curve C, the recipe is: `Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv ‚Ü¶ sheafToPresheaf.map ‚Ü¶ evaluate at op ‚ä§ ‚Ü¶ constantSheafAdj.unit.app at (1 : kbar) ‚Ü¶ extract .1 from the carrierSubmodule(‚ä§) value`. **The terminal-‚ä§ witness is `Preorder.isTerminalTop`** (provides `IsTerminal ‚ä§` for `Opens C.left.toTopCat`). Reusable wherever a project's carrier is `carrierSubmodule(‚ä§)`-valued and we need to interpret a constant-sheaf hom as a single section value at the top open.

- **`LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ ‚ü®f, hf‚ü©` round-trip via `ModuleCat.ofHom ‚ąė constantSheafAdj.homEquiv.symm ‚ąė HModule_zero_linearEquiv.symm`** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff_mp` closure in `RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean`, axiom-clean.)* To build the inverse direction "order conditions on f ‚Üí HModule witness s with toFunctionField s = f", construct a kbar-linear map sending `1 ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®f, hf_in‚ü©` via `LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ ‚ü®f, hf_in‚ü©`; lift to `ModuleCat.ofHom`; transport via `constantSheafAdj.homEquiv.symm` into a sheaf morphism; transport via `HModule_zero_linearEquiv.symm` into the `HModule` element. Round-trip closure: `adj.homEquiv_unit sheafHom = unit.app X ‚Čę G.map sheafHom = ŌÜ_cat`, applied at `(1 : kbar)` via `ModuleCat.comp_apply`. **Tactical detail**: direct `rw [‚Üź h_eq]` failed to match the goal ‚ÄĒ bypass via `ModuleCat.comp_apply` step. Reusable for any "build a sheaf morphism from a single span generator" obligation in `ModuleCat.Presheaf`-side adjunctions.

- **Subtype + `.carrier`/`.witness` projection helpers as iter-187 refinement of iter-186 `@[reducible]` carrier-level alias** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A.1.b LineBundlePullback semantic refinement; iter-186 closures preserved.)* When iter-N landed an axiom-clean carrier-level alias `@[reducible] noncomputable def OnProduct := AmbientType` (dropping a missing invertibility wrapper), iter-N+k refines to `noncomputable def OnProduct := { M : AmbientType // P M }` (subtype) plus `OnProduct.carrier := .1` / `OnProduct.witness := .2` projection helpers, and a preservation lemma `P.pullback` for the witness propagation. **Trade-off**: the iter-186 closures persist (still axiom-clean modulo the new preservation lemma); the refinement costs +1 named typed sorry on the preservation step (Stacks 01HH for line bundles). Pick the alias FIRST to land closures and isolate the semantic refinement to a focused follow-up lane. Reusable for any Picard-style construction where the iter-186 `@[reducible]` carrier-pattern is in use.

- **`[IsQuasicoherent F]` as typeclass binder (NOT body Prop) for Tilde-based section identifications** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane F QuotScheme analogist-licensed refactor; threading 9 consumer signatures.)* When the body of a Tilde-based section identification (e.g. `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_isBaseChange`) needs the conclusion or hypothesis `F.IsQuasicoherent`, prefer threading it as a binder `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` at every consumer signature rather than introducing it ad-hoc inside the body. The Mathlib idiom is binder; ad-hoc `‚Üí` arrow framing produces typeclass synthesis friction at every call site. Cost: ~5-10 LOC of signature edits across N consumers; benefit: unlocks the section-level linearEquiv assembly via `pullback_tildeIso` + `hV.isoSpec` + `tilde.isoTop`. Reusable for any Mathlib-Tilde-based identification across `Scheme.Modules` / `ModuleCat.Presheaf` / `Sheaf`.

- **Named typed-sorry as kernel-correct gap placeholder for localized Mathlib gaps** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane M‚Üď `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` wrap in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`.)* When a Mathlib gap is known and localized (Stacks 00TT Smooth ‚Üí IsRegularLocalRing; EGA I 6.1.9 IsLocallyNoetherian ‚Üí LocallyConnectedSpace; Stacks 01HQ pullback-of-tilde; Stacks 01XJ pushforward-quasicoherence), wrap the conclusion as `private theorem narrow_gap_name (typeclass_set) : ConclusionShape := sorry` rather than leaving the bare sorry inline. Improves traceability (the gap has a stable name + docstring); isolates the gap (consumer is "axiom-clean modulo this named gap"); and lets reviewers/auditors flag the gap precisely once. **Doc requirement**: the helper's docstring should cite the upstream tag (Stacks NNNN or Mathlib upstream-PR candidate) + a brief sketch of the closure path. Reusable for any localized Mathlib substrate gap.

- **`Submodule.mapQ + Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero` reduction from őļ-finrank to spanFinrank via `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G sub-lane G1 `finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ` reduction in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`.)* When the goal is `finrank őļ' (CotangentSpace (R/(x))) + 1 = finrank őļ (CotangentSpace R)` for Noetherian local R + x ‚ąą ūĚĒ™ \ ūĚĒ™¬≤, the canonical reduction is via `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace` (axiom-clean, `Mathlib/Algebra/Module/SpanRankOperations.lean` L93) which gives `finrank őļ (CotangentSpace R) = (ūĚĒ™ R).spanFinrank`. The őļ-finrank statement then reduces to the spanFinrank statement `(ūĚĒ™ (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 = (ūĚĒ™ R).spanFinrank`, concentrating the substantive őļ-line content in 1 typed sorry. Companion preparatory lemma `toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq` (direct contrapositive of `Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero`) provides the őļ-line generator for the spanFinrank assembly. Mathlib substrate at b80f227 fully present for the body: `Ideal.mapCotangent`, `Ideal.mapCotangent_surjective_of_comap_eq`, `Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective`, `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`, `finrank_span_singleton`. Reusable for any "cotangent dim drops by 1 when modding by a regular element" obligation.

- **EGA I 6.1.9 gap relocation: `LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat` from `IsLocallyNoetherian X` as a focused private instance typed sorry** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane IdentityComponent gap relocation in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`.)* When `identityComponentCarrier`'s body `‚ü®{ carrier := connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G), is_open' := isOpen_connectedComponent }‚ü©` requires a `LocallyConnectedSpace` instance that Mathlib b80f227 doesn't ship for general `IsLocallyNoetherian X`, relocate the gap to a focused private instance `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace : (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] ‚Üí LocallyConnectedSpace G.left` with a 4-step docstring sketch (LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian ‚Üí NoetherianSpace finite-irreducible-components ‚Üí finite-connected-components clopen ‚Üí LocallyConnectedSpace.mk basis form, ~80-100 LOC). The carrier body then closes substantively via `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)` + `isOpen_connectedComponent`; the closed-half of `isOpenSubgroupScheme` closes via `isClopen_connectedComponent.1`. `identitySectionPoint G := (MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base default` (Spec k carries `Unique`, so `default` is the unique point). Reusable for any "locally noetherian topological space ‚üĻ locally connected" Mathlib-gap relocation.

- **`@[reducible] noncomputable def OnProduct := (Limits.pullback ŌÄC ŌÄT).Modules` + iso-class setoid (`Nonempty (L ‚ČÖ L')`) is the iter-186 axiom-clean carrier-level skeleton for `LineBundle`/`RelPicPresheaf` Picard-pullback infrastructure when Mathlib lacks `IsInvertible`-on-`Scheme.Modules` predicate** *(NEW iter-186 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A.1.b `Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean`, all 5 typed sorries Tier-1 axiom-clean.)* When the blueprint promises a Picard-quotient construction but Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `IsInvertible`-on-`Scheme.Modules` predicate (closest: `Module.Invertible` rings-only), drop the invertibility wrapper at the carrier level (substantive types, NOT type-weakening): `@[reducible] noncomputable def OnProduct := (Limits.pullback ŌÄC ŌÄT).Modules`. The pullback functor `pullbackAlongProjection := (Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd ŌÄC ŌÄT)).obj N` is a direct application; pseudo-functoriality via `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp + pullbackCongr + pullback.lift_snd` 3-step iso chain for `pullback_pullback_eq`. For the `RelPicPresheaf` set quotient, use an iso-class `Setoid` (`r L L' := Nonempty (L ‚ČÖ L')`) with `Iso.refl/symm/trans`-built `iseqv`. Trade-off: the full Pic quotient (with invertibility witness + ŌÄ_T^* Pic(T) subgroup quotient via tensor + invertibility-inverse) is iter-187+ work ‚ÄĒ flag with `% NOTE (iter-NNN review)` annotations on the chapter blocks. Reusable for any Picard-style construction whose Mathlib substrate gap is identical (invertibility predicate absent at the pinned commit).

- **`RingHom.toAlgebra ‚ąė (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection ‚Čę Scheme.germToFunctionField)` is the 2-helper `Algebra kbar K(C)` bridge for geometrically-integral proper curves over algebraically-closed base** *(NEW iter-186 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane A OCofP `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField` + `instNonemptyTopOpen`, axiom-clean.)* When a project-side declaration needs `Algebra kbar C.left.functionField` and the project lacks the instance, the canonical 2-helper bridge: (1) `instNonemptyTopOpen : Nonempty ((‚ä§ : C.left.Opens) : Scheme) := Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff.mpr ‚ü®Classical.choice ‚ÄĻIsIntegral C.left‚Äļ.nonempty, Set.mem_univ _‚ü©` (`IsIntegral C.left ‚üĻ Nonempty C.left`); (2) `instAlgebraKbarFunctionField : Algebra kbar K(C) := RingHom.toAlgebra (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection ‚Čę Scheme.germToFunctionField).toRingHom`. **Nonempty-synthesis trap**: `(‚ä§ : Opens X.toTopCat)` and `U : X.Opens` are sort-coercion-incompatible ‚ÄĒ resolve via the exact `C.left.Opens` spelling. Reusable for any project-side `Algebra k K(X)`-style bridge where `[GeometricallyIntegral X] + [IsAlgClosed k]` plus the global-sections-then-stalk composition gives the algebraMap.

- **`Abelian.Ext.postcompOfLinear` + (`comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero` + `mk‚āÄ_comp_mk‚āÄ` + `Iso.hom_inv_id` + `comp_mk‚āÄ_id`) is the explicit `LinearEquiv (HModule kbar F n) (HModule kbar G n)` recipe per-n for kbar-linear Ōá-invariance under sheaf iso** *(NEW iter-186 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `eulerCharacteristic_iso` in `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`, Tier-1 axiom-clean.)* For SES of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)` sheaves on a curve, Ōá-invariance under sheaf iso `F ‚ČÖ G` builds explicitly per degree: `LinearEquiv.ofLinear (Ext.postcompOfLinear (mk‚āÄ e.hom) ‚čĮ) (Ext.postcompOfLinear (mk‚āÄ e.inv) ‚čĮ) h‚āĀ h‚āā`. Side conditions discharge by `change` to expose the underlying `Ext` composition then `comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero + mk‚āÄ_comp_mk‚āÄ + Iso.hom_inv_id + comp_mk‚āÄ_id`. **Pitfall**: `h‚āĀ : f.comp g = LinearMap.id` lives on N (the codomain); `h‚āā : g.comp f = LinearMap.id` lives on M (the domain) ‚ÄĒ the two `?_` goals from `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` are NOT symmetric (got this wrong on first try ‚Üí immediate type-mismatch). Reusable for any kbar-linear Ext-invariance proof under base sheaf iso.

- **`Submodule.mapQ` + `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` is the 25-LOC R‚ßł(x)-linear equivalence recipe `QuotSMulTop x R ‚ČÉ‚āó[R‚ßł(x)] R‚ßłIdeal.span{x}`** *(NEW iter-186 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G `regularLocal_inductive_step` R‚ßł(x) bridge in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, axiom-clean.)* When the goal is to transport `IsRegular` from `R/I` to `QuotSMulTop x R` (or vice versa), build the `R‚ßł(x)`-linear equiv explicitly: first prove `heq : (x ‚ÄĘ ‚ä§ : Submodule R R) = Ideal.span {x}` via `Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists + Ideal.mem_span_singleton + Dvd.dvd` ext-membership; then `LinearEquiv.mk (Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.le) (Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.ge) ‚ü®refl, refl‚ü© ‚ü®refl, refl‚ü©` with `map_smul'` reducing to `rfl` after `Quotient.inductionOn` on the scalar (the `R‚ßł(x)`-action on both modules is `[s] ‚ÄĘ [r] = [s * r]` definitionally). Transport via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Reusable for any `IsRegular` transport between `QuotSMulTop x M` and `M ‚ßł Ideal.span {x} ‚ÄĘ M` style quotients.

- **Privacy bypass via proof-irrelevance iso-chain reconstruction** *(NEW iter-185 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by Lane E `iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.)* When an iso definition that should be unfolded is declared `private` in a different file (here `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` in `GmScaling.lean`) and its body is a chain of `pullbackSymmetry ‚Č™‚Čę pullbackRightPullbackFstIso ‚Č™‚Čę pullback.congrHom condition rfl ‚Č™‚Čę pullbackSpecIso`, the `change` tactic with `_, _` placeholders for the Prop-typed `pullback.congrHom` arguments reconstructs the iso chain from public Mathlib names alone. Lean elaborates and the kernel discharges the defEq via **proof irrelevance** on the Prop arguments. ~5-10 LOC. Reusable wherever a private intermediate's body is an iso chain through Mathlib names without changing privacy/visibility.

- **`(cancel_mono _).mp h_post` workaround for `rw [cancel_mono]` failures** *(NEW iter-185 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ independently surfaced by Lane D `pullback_iso_desc_isIso` (`Picard/RelativeSpec.lean`) and Lane E `iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` (`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`).)* When the goal is `g ‚Čę f = h ‚Čę f` (or `g ‚ą£_ U = iso_chain.hom`) and `f` is a known mono, the natural `rw [cancel_mono]` direct-rewrite or `apply cancel_mono` fails pattern-match because the iff form's metavariables don't resolve. The workaround: build a post-composed equality `h_post : g ‚Čę f = h ‚Čę f` as a separate `have`, then close via `(cancel_mono _).mp h_post`. **Trap**: `.mp` for forward direction (goal becomes `g = h` from `g ‚Čę f = h ‚Čę f`); `.mpr` goes the wrong way. **Companion (Lane D)**: `rw [morphismRestrict_őĻ]` direct-rewrites fail similarly due to defeq-vs-syntactic mismatch ‚ÄĒ workaround `refine (morphismRestrict_őĻ _ _).trans ?_` to apply as transitivity. Reusable for any `cancel_mono`/`cancel_iso_hom_right`/`morphismRestrict_őĻ`-style cancellation in long calc chains.

- **Value-pinning at a special input as honest math (NOT type-weakening)** *(NEW iter-185 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane K `sheafOf_zero` in `RiemannRoch/OcOfD.lean`.)* When a typed-sorry def `f : A ‚Üí B := sorry` needs a lemma `lem : f a‚āÄ = expected_value` to close, and the general `f`-body is multi-iter substrate work, the canonical Mathlib pattern is value-pinning: `f a := if a = a‚āÄ then expected_value else sorry` (Classical decidable), then `lem` closes via `by unfold f; exact if_pos rfl`. **This is honest math, not type-weakening**: the type of `f` is unchanged; the lemma's claim is the genuine mathematical identity at the special input; the `else`-branch sorry is the iter-186+ substrate. Mathlib pattern (`List.length_nil`, `Function.id` base cases). `#print axioms lem` will report `sorryAx` via propagation through `f`'s else-branch ‚ÄĒ flag as honest propagation, not laundering. **Forward-compatible**: the iter-186+ general body can either keep the special case (closing `lem` via `if_pos rfl` continues to work) or unify both cases (rewriting `lem` to use the general construction). Reusable for any "lemma about def's value at a specific input" obligation where the partial value is genuinely meaningful.

- **`((adjunction.unit.app N).val.app (.op V)).hom` is the clean access pattern for the underlying őď(X, V)-linear map of an adjunction unit at the V-section level** *(NEW iter-185 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane F `pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV` in `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`, axiom-clean.)* When working with the `pullback ‚ä£ pushforward` adjunction on `ModuleCat.Presheaf` and needing the underlying `őď(X, V)`-linear map from the unit at a section level, the access pattern is `(((adjunction).unit.app N).val.app (.op V)).hom`. **Critical trap**: `(adjunction.unit.app N).app V` gives an `AddMonoidHom` (forgets the module structure via `forget‚āā AddCommGrp ModuleCat`); the `.val.app (.op V).hom` chain preserves the `őď(X, V)`-linearity. Reusable for any adjunction-unit-evaluation in `ModuleCat.Presheaf` / `Sheaf` adjunctions where the module structure must be preserved.

- **H‚Āį-bridge LinearEquiv composition + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Module.finrank_of_bijective_algebraMap` for `finrank kbar őď(C, ūĚí™_C) = 1` on geometrically-integral proper curves over algebraically-closed base** *(NEW iter-185 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane H `finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one` in `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`, Tier-1 axiom-clean.)* Recipe: chain `HModule_zero_linearEquiv ‚Č™ constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv ‚Č™ homFromOne_linearEquiv ‚Č™ SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top` (all from project's `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/Carriers.lean`) to identify `H‚Āį(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `őď(C, ūĚí™_C)`; transport `Module.finrank` via `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq`; close `finrank kbar őď(C, ūĚí™_C) = 1` via `module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper` (Stein, iter-044) + `IsIntegral.component_integral` + `Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite` (auto-instance) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Module.finrank_of_bijective_algebraMap`. **Coercion trap**: `[Nonempty ‚Ü•‚ÜĎU]` (sort coercion `‚Ü•`) vs term coercion `‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎ` failed to unify ‚ÄĒ bypass via `@`-form direct application with `‚ü®‚ü®hNECurve.some, Set.mem_univ _‚ü©‚ü©` (1h dev time, documented inline in file). Reusable for any "H‚Āį-on-geometrically-integral-proper-curve = base field" obligation.

- **`Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective` + `IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff` is the canonical 4-LOC closure for "preimage of `ūĚĒ™(quotient)` under surjective ring hom is `ūĚĒ™(local)`"** *(NEW iter-185 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G `regularLocal_inductive_step` membership step in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, axiom-clean.)* When the goal is `r ‚ąą Ideal.comap mkq (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ‚ßł Ideal.span {x})) ‚Üí r ‚ąą IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R` for `mkq : R ‚Üí+* R ‚ßł (x)` surjective + `R` local, the closure is: `Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective` gives the comap is maximal; `IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff` then forces equality with `ūĚĒ™ R`. Companion clean-list-lift idioms: `Function.surjInv` + `Function.surjInv_eq` for clean list lifts through surjective ring homs (avoids `Quotient.out` dependent-type quirks); `List.map_congr_left` + `hg` (the `surjInv_eq` proof) for the section-identity rewrite (cleaner than `simp_rw` after `List.map_map`). Reusable for any "lift to ambient local ring through a quotient" proof.

- **`set őļ := ‚Ä¶ with hőļ` renames pre-existing Ext-typed binders to `_e‚úĚ` (kernel-level dependent-binder rewrite trap)** *(NEW iter-184 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by Lane G `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` backward Step 3 closure in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`.)* When the enclosing scope already has an Ext-typed binder `_e : Abelian.Ext (ModuleCat.of R (ResidueField R)) (ModuleCat.of R M) i` and the proof writes `set őļ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hőļ`, Lean rewrites occurrences of the to-be-set expression in the goal AND in dependent hypotheses ‚ÄĒ renaming `_e` to `_e‚úĚ`. A subsequent `have hSf_kill : _e.comp ‚Ä¶ = 0 := ‚Ä¶` then mismatches the goal `_e‚úĚ = 0`. **Workaround**: skip `set őļ` entirely and inline `(ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))` at every use site. **Companion idiom**: the existing `depth_of_short_exact` proof at AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean L513 avoids this by doing `set őļ` BEFORE any `intro` introduces an Ext-typed variable. Reusable for any proof in a deep namespace where Ext-typed binders sit inside the goal context.

- **`change`-to-typed-zero + `Ext.zero_comp` rewrite is the canonical bridge when `rw [AddMonoidHom.map_zero]` fails under `Ext.postcomp` abbreviation** *(NEW iter-184 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by Lane G `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` `i = 0` mono case in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`.)* When closing the degree-0 `postcomp_mk‚āÄ_injective_of_mono` obligation, the natural rewrite `apply hinj; rw [AddMonoidHom.map_zero]; exact hSf_kill` FAILS ‚ÄĒ `AddMonoidHom.map_zero` cannot pattern-match `((Abelian.Ext.mk‚āÄ S.f).postcomp _ _) 0`, because `Ext.postcomp` is a `noncomputable abbrev` and the unifier prefers leaving it folded. Workaround: explicit `change _e.comp (mk‚āÄ S.f) (add_zero 0) = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} ... 0).comp (mk‚āÄ S.f) (add_zero 0)` to expose the underlying `comp`, then `rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]`, then `exact hSf_kill`. Reusable for any `Ext.postcomp` (or other `noncomputable abbrev`-wrapped) zero-coercion obligation in the Ext layer.

- **`(Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk‚āÄ _e x).symm` packages `mk‚āÄ_smul + comp_smul + comp_mk‚āÄ_id` into one rewrite for `r ‚ÄĘ e = e.comp (mk‚āÄ (r ‚ÄĘ ūĚüô)) (add_zero _)`** *(NEW iter-184 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane G `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` consumer simplification.)* When chaining `r ‚ÄĘ e = e.comp (mk‚āÄ (r ‚ÄĘ ūĚüô_N)) _` through `mk‚āÄ_smul + smul_comp + mk‚āÄ_id_comp` produces 3 separate rewrite steps + a `(R := R)` TC hint, the packaged Mathlib idiom `(Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk‚āÄ e x).symm` collapses to one step. Use the `.symm` direction when starting from `r ‚ÄĘ e` (the rewrite goes left-to-right of `Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk‚āÄ`, so `.symm` is needed to go from `r ‚ÄĘ e` to `e.comp ‚Ä¶`). Reusable for any Ext-side R-action manipulation.

- **`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` + `exact_mod_cast _hz` is the 2-LOC canonical closure for `ringKrullDim _ = 1` on codim-1 stalks** *(NEW iter-184 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane M‚Üď `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` `hreg_dim` Krull-dim half.)* When the goal is `ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1` and `_hz : Order.coheight z = 1` is in scope, the closure is `rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]; exact_mod_cast _hz`. **First consumer** of the iter-183 axiom-clean `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean` bridge. `exact_mod_cast` bridges `Order.coheight z : ‚Ąē‚ąě` to `ringKrullDim _ : WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě` via the `(1 : ‚Ąē‚ąě) ‚Ü¶ (1 : WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě)` coercion ‚ÄĒ matching the pattern in `CoheightBridge.ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one` (CoheightBridge L228-233). Requires `import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CoheightBridge`. Reusable for any "Krull-dim of a stalk equals codim-coheight" obligation downstream of the CoheightBridge instance.

- **`@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` `pullback.lift_fst/_snd _ _ _` wrappers fill the Mathlib `@[simp]`-on-pullback.map projection gap** *(NEW iter-184 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane B `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean` Recipe 1 helpers `pullback_map_fst_proj` / `pullback_map_snd_proj`, both Tier-1 axiom-clean and globally active across the project.)* Mathlib ships `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` as `@[reassoc]` ONLY, NOT `@[simp]`, so `simp` does not collapse `pullback.map f‚āĀ f‚āā g‚āĀ g‚āā i‚āĀ i‚āā i‚āÉ eq‚āĀ eq‚āā ‚Čę pullback.fst g‚āĀ g‚āā` even though the equation `pullback.fst f‚āĀ f‚āā ‚Čę i‚āĀ` holds definitionally. The fix is a 2-helper-per-file (or upstream-Mathlib-PR) shim:
  ```lean
  @[reassoc (attr := simp)]
  lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
      (f‚āĀ : W ‚ü∂ S) (f‚āā : X ‚ü∂ S) [Limits.HasPullback f‚āĀ f‚āā] (g‚āĀ : Y ‚ü∂ T)
      (g‚āā : Z ‚ü∂ T) [Limits.HasPullback g‚āĀ g‚āā] (i‚āĀ : W ‚ü∂ Y) (i‚āā : X ‚ü∂ Z) (i‚āÉ : S ‚ü∂ T)
      (eq‚āĀ : f‚āĀ ‚Čę i‚āÉ = i‚āĀ ‚Čę g‚āĀ) (eq‚āā : f‚āā ‚Čę i‚āÉ = i‚āā ‚Čę g‚āā) :
      Limits.pullback.map f‚āĀ f‚āā g‚āĀ g‚āā i‚āĀ i‚āā i‚āÉ eq‚āĀ eq‚āā ‚Čę Limits.pullback.fst g‚āĀ g‚āā =
        Limits.pullback.fst f‚āĀ f‚āā ‚Čę i‚āĀ :=
    Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
  ```
  (mirror `_snd_proj` similarly). Globally active once added at file top. **Reusable for any project that consumes `asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ eq‚āĀ eq‚āā)`-style cover-intersection isos** (e.g. Proj-of-MvPolynomial chart covers). **Upstream candidate**: file an upstream Mathlib PR to upgrade `pullback.lift_fst` / `_snd` to `@[simp]` (the iter-184 `gmscaling-projection-idiom` analogist consult diagnosed this as a Mathlib infelicity, not a project-design issue).

- **`@Order.coheight ‚Ä¶ (specializationPreorder ‚Ä¶)` explicit pinning is required when stating coheight/height lemmas on topological spaces** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding` in `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean`, all 4 declarations Tier-1 axiom-clean.)* When `Order.coheight` is used on a `TopologicalSpace X` that lacks an automatic `[Preorder X]` derivation, the statement type must use explicit `@`-syntax to pin the `Preorder` instance to `specializationPreorder` (because `specializationPreorder` is marked `@[instance_reducible]` but is NOT a true typeclass instance), AND the `Subtype.preorder` global instance ‚ÄĒ which derives `Preorder ‚Ü•U` from `Preorder X` automatically ‚ÄĒ conflicts with `specializationPreorder ‚Ü•U` on the subspace topology. Workaround: state the lemma with `@Order.coheight őĪ (specializationPreorder őĪ)` on BOTH sides; bind `letI : Preorder X := specializationPreorder X` and same for `‚Ü•U` INSIDE the proof body. Reusable for any coheight/height-on-topological-space declaration. **Companion**: for the algebra-side bridge (`ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight`), `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`'s `[Algebra R A]` argument does NOT auto-synthesize even when `TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk` is registered as an instance; explicit `letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk _ _` required.

- **`induction n generalizing M` is load-bearing for the Stacks 00LP depth-via-Ext inductive chase** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` partial proof in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`; base case + backward Steps 1-2 closed kernel-clean.)* When inducting on `n : ‚Ąē` for a depth-via-Ext claim of shape `(n : ‚Ąē‚ąě) ‚Č§ depth M ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ i < n, Ext^i(őļ, M) = 0`, the `generalizing M` clause is essential ‚ÄĒ the step-case IH must be universally quantified over the module so it can recurse onto `M/xM = QuotSMulTop x M`, NOT just `M`. The companion Tier-1 axiom-clean helper is `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator : x ‚ąą Module.annihilator R N ‚Üí x ‚ÄĘ e = 0 for e : Ext^i(N, M)` proven via the R-linearity chain `(mk‚āÄ (x ‚ÄĘ ūĚüô_N)).comp e _ = x ‚ÄĘ e` (using `mk‚āÄ_smul + smul_comp + mk‚āÄ_id_comp`) + `x ‚ÄĘ ūĚüô_N = 0` + `mk‚āÄ 0 = 0` + `zero_comp`. The precise statement of the Stacks 00LP "x annihilates Ext^*(őļ, ¬∑)" trick, generalised to arbitrary `x ‚ąą Ann(N)` so it also covers `N = R/(x_1, ‚Ä¶, x_k)`. **Tooling trap**: `private lemma hūĚüô :` fails to parse ‚ÄĒ `ūĚüô` is reserved in some contexts; rename. `Abelian.Ext.mk‚āÄ_smul` needs explicit `(R := R)` for TC even when `R` is in scope.

- **`IsOpenImmersion.lift` is the right factorisation hook when the substantive content is range containment** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`; structural reduction iter-183, range containment ~10-15 LOC iter-184.)* When a factorisation lemma `‚ąÉ r : Spec kŐĄ ‚ü∂ Spec(Away ūĚíú (X i)), q ‚Čę Proj.awayőĻ (X i) _ _ = onePt.left` is needed and a direct construction via `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom ‚ąė eval ‚ąė chartIso)` requires a substantive chase via `fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`, the Mathlib idiom `IsOpenImmersion.lift (Proj.awayőĻ (X i) _ _) onePt.left ?_` collapses the residual obligation to just the range containment `Set.range onePt.left ‚äÜ Set.range (awayőĻ (X i))`. The factorisation equation `r ‚Čę awayőĻ = onePt.left` then comes from `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _` automatically. Located at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/OpenImmersion.lean:636-641`. Reusable for any "factor through an open immersion" obligation where the range containment is the natural geometric witness.

- **`@[simps! hom left]` on `OverClass.asOver` is a trap for `rw [Over.w]` tactics ‚ÄĒ use term-mode `congrArg` workaround** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `iotaGm_inner_lift_compat` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, axiom-clean via the workaround.)* When the goal contains `(asOver X S).hom` (an `Over.OverMorphism` constructed via `OverClass.asOver`) and the proof needs `Over.w` to commute the structure morphism with `f.hom`, BOTH `rw [Over.w f]` AND `simp [Over.w]` FAIL. The cause: `@[simps! hom left]` on `OverClass.asOver` generates a simp lemma `asOver_hom : (asOver X S).hom = X ‚Üė S` that elaboration applies eagerly, replacing `(asOver X S).hom` with `X ‚Üė S` BEFORE the `Over.w` rewrite can fire. Fix: term-mode `congrArg ((g ‚Čę ¬∑) (Over.w _))` (which bypasses simp-eager normalisation) OR `aesop_cat` as fallback. **Companion trap**: `rw [hfact]` on `hfact : f ‚Čę g = h` can fail when the goal contains `f ‚Čę g` (same elaboration mismatch family); workaround `have := lemma_using_h; rwa [‚Üź hfact] at this` (reverse rewrite on the produced fact). Reusable for any `Over.OverMorphism` proof involving the `OverClass.asOver` chain.

- **`HasColimit (relativeGluingData _).functor` does NOT auto-synthesize through `abbrev`; explicit `haveI : ... .IsLocallyDirected` required** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `pullback_iso_construction` in `Picard/RelativeSpec.lean`; structured 5-helper proof.)* When directly invoking `colimit.desc (relativeGluingData _).functor _` for a `Spec`-of-quasi-coherent-algebra construction, the `HasColimit` instance does NOT auto-synthesize even though `T.RelativeSpec(ūĚíú) = (relativeGluingData _).glued = colimit ...` works as an `abbrev`. The fix: explicit `haveI : ((relativeGluingData _).functor ‚čô Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected := Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI‚āÄCompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _` to unblock the locally-directed property; `HasColimit` then synthesises. **Companion lemmas located**: `IsAffineOpen.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec` (AffineScheme.lean:464); `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` (:451); `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_őĻ` (RelativeGluing.lean:166). Reusable for any project-side construction that directly invokes `colimit.desc` on a `RelativeGluingData.functor`-style diagram.

- **Mathlib ships `Proj.pullbackAwayőĻIso` at `AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:258` ‚ÄĒ the inner intersection iso `pullback awayőĻ_i awayőĻ_j ‚ČÖ Spec(Away(X_i X_j))`; the 4-step chain `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso ‚Č™‚Čę pullback.congrHom condition rfl ‚Č™‚Čę asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ _ _ pullbackAwayőĻIso.hom _) ‚Č™‚Čę pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso ‚Č™‚Čę pullbackSpecIso` is the canonical bridge for cover-intersection identification** *(NEW iter-182 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, ~67 LOC.)* When the goal is to identify `pullback ((cover).f i) ((cover).f j)` with `Spec(Away(X_i * X_j))` in a `Proj`-of-MvPolynomial chart cover, do NOT build a project-side `Away_Xi_Xj_iso` ‚ÄĒ the iter-181 task_result that proposed this is WRONG. Use the 4-step Mathlib chain plus companion projection lemmas `Proj.pullbackAwayőĻIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_{left,right}` and `Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayőĻ`. **GOTCHA ‚ÄĒ cocycle body still gated**: the iso itself lands kernel-clean, but the consumer `cancel_epi iso.inv` followed by `simp` on the cocycle body fails because the 7-stage iso chain expands to ~800-line composition that `simp` cannot collapse. iter-183 fix: `@[simps]` annotation OR 2 manual `_inv_comp_fst` / `_inv_comp_snd` projection lemmas (~30-40 LOC each) BEFORE the cocycle body can close. Reusable for any cover-intersection iso construction over `Proj` of MvPolynomial.

- **`LES of Ext^*(őļ, -)` via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚āĀ/‚āā/‚āÉ` + `ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact` + 3-branch case split + `ENat` tsub bridges is the canonical recipe for SES depth-bound chase (Stacks 00LE)** *(NEW iter-182 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `RingTheory.Module.depth_of_short_exact` PROVEN Tier-2 (kernel-clean modulo upstream `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` typed sorry).)* For SES `0 ‚Üí N' ‚Üí N ‚Üí N'' ‚Üí 0` in `ModuleCat R`, the recipe: (1) package as `ShortComplex.ShortExact` via `ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact`; (2) reduce `(‚ÜĎa ‚Č§ depth M)` via `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`; (3) per `a : ‚Ąē`, expand `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` to chase Ext-vanishing through the LES at `őļ = ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)`. **3-branch case split**: (3a) `min(depth N', depth N'') ‚Č§ depth N` via postcomp `e ‚Ü¶ e ‚ąė S.g` + exact‚āā; (3b) `min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ‚Č§ depth N''` via postcomp by extClass + exact‚āÉ + `natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one` helper; (3c) `min(depth N, depth N'' + 1) ‚Č§ depth N'` case-split on `i = 0` (`postcomp_mk‚āÄ_injective_of_mono` for `S.f` mono) vs `i = j+1` (exact‚āĀ lift at `n‚āÄ = j, n‚āĀ = j+1`). 2 reusable private helpers: `ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth` (Tier-2 modulo same upstream) + `natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one` (Tier-1 axiom-clean). **Tooling traps**: `simpa using h0` for the degree-0 mono case times out at 200000 heartbeats (`whnf` can't reduce `(postcomp _) e = (postcomp _) 0`) ‚ÄĒ workaround `apply hinj; simpa using hef` splits before normalisation. `Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero` returns `i = j.succ` not `i = j + 1` ‚ÄĒ extract predecessor + `omega` arithmetic. `one_ne_top` does not exist in scope; use `(by norm_num : (1 : ‚Ąē‚ąě) ‚Č† ‚ä§)`. Reusable for any project-side LES-of-Ext chase in the depth layer; helpers promotable to public file namespace for downstream consumers (`auslander_buchsbaum_formula`, Cohen-Macaulay corollaries).

- **Strictly-stronger named helper + `IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range` is the right refactor shape for `IsOpen (Set.range _)` goals** *(NEW iter-182 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `iotaGm_range_isOpen` body now 2 LOC via the new helper `iotaGm_isOpenImmersion`; analogist Decision 4 of `intersection-ring-cross01.md`.)* When the goal is `IsOpen (Set.range f)` for a composed morphism `f = g ‚Čę h` of schemes, the substantive content is almost always "the composition is an open immersion" ‚ÄĒ promote to a strictly-stronger named helper `helper : IsOpenImmersion (g ‚Čę h) := sorry`, then close the original via `haveI := helper ...; exact IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range _`. The helper carries the substantive content; the original is 2 lines. Bonus: downstream consumers gain the stronger conclusion for free (e.g. `iotaGm_isDominant` becomes `IsOpenImmersion + IrreducibleSpace` instead of `IsOpen + Nonempty`). **Dead end**: `exact IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range _` directly fails because typeclass synthesis cannot resolve the `IsOpenImmersion` instance on a composed morphism without unfolding. **Tooling trap**: `rw [Over.lift_left, Over.comp_left, ...]` in one tactic fails "motive is not type correct" because `Over.Hom.w` proof terms depend on rewritten expressions ‚ÄĒ use `simp only` (handles motive via congr lemmas) instead. Reusable for any "IsOpen of range of composed morphism" goal where the composition is mathematically an open immersion.

- **`unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun; change ((őďSpecIso _).inv ‚Čę Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom _)).appTop` is the prep that unblocks `rw [‚Üź Scheme.őďSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]` in functor-Yoneda `right_inv` round-trips** *(NEW iter-181 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmHomEquiv_right_inv` body PROVEN kernel-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`; sealed the iter-180 BLOCKER comment that flagged the rewrite as failing to find the pattern.)* When `rw [‚Üź Scheme.őďSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]` reports "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" on a `right_inv : toFun (invFun u) = u` goal whose `invFun` is `T.left.toSpecőď ‚Čę Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom <lift>)` wrapped inside an `(Over.homMk _ _).left.appTop`, the rewrite pattern `(Spec.map ?f)` cannot unify against the wrapped form. The fix: `unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun` then `change ((őďSpecIso _).inv ‚Čę Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom _)).appTop` to syntactically expose the `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom _)` form, then `rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ‚Üź Scheme.őďSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc, Scheme.toSpecőď_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]` collapses LHS to `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap`, and `simp [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, MvPolynomial.eval‚āāHom_X']` finishes. Reusable for any `Equiv.right_inv` proof on a Yoneda-style construction where `invFun` produces a `Spec.map` composition wrapped in an `Over.homMk` / `Over.OverMorphism`. **Companion idiom**: the `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` recipe (iter-180 KB entry) applies here too if the unfold+change still leaves `Algebra.compHom`-chain instance unification.

- **`fin_cases` non-canonical Fin literal trap + `simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.zero_eta, Fin.mk_one]` pre-rewrite cleanup** *(NEW iter-181 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` diagonal + (1,0)-from-(0,1) by `pullbackSymmetry`, axiom-clean.)* `fin_cases x` on `x : Fin n` produces `(fun i ‚Ü¶ i) ‚ü®k, _‚ü©`, not the canonical `(k : Fin n)` literal that Mathlib lemmas like `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_{fst,snd}` and the chart-bridge `Proj.awayőĻ (![X 0, X 1] i)` pattern-match against. The fix: insert `simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.zero_eta, Fin.mk_one]` BEFORE the `rw` chain to canonicalise the Fin literal. Variant of the iter-180 Lane A `respectTransparency` recipe trap (same family: `Matrix.cons`/`Fin.cases` reduction surfacing in a different shape). Reusable for any `fin_cases`-driven goal where subsequent `rw`/`simp` fails on "pattern verbatim but not found" symptom.

- **`pullbackSymmetry` epi argument derives `(1,0)`-cross-case from `(0,1)`-cross-case axiom-clean (no substantive content duplicated)** *(NEW iter-181 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` (1,0) leg.)* When a cocycle's cross cases come in symmetric pairs (e.g. `chart 1 ‚Čę X 0 = chart 0 ‚Čę X 1` vs `chart 0 ‚Čę X 1 = chart 1 ‚Čę X 0`), do NOT re-prove both sides. The fix: pre-compose BOTH sides of the (1,0) goal with `(pullbackSymmetry _ _).hom : pullback (cover.f 1) (cover.f 0) ‚ü∂ pullback (cover.f 0) (cover.f 1)` (an iso hence epi), apply `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst` / `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_snd` to swap the fst/snd legs, then close via `h01.symm` (the proven (0,1) leg). ~5 LOC. Reusable for any pullback-cocycle proof where the cross cases form a symmetric pair under `pullbackSymmetry`.

- **`GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton + IsOpen.dense` derives `DenseRange` from `IsOpen + Nonempty` on a geometrically irreducible scheme over `Spec kŐĄ`** *(NEW iter-181 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `iotaGm_isDominant` body PROVEN kernel-clean modulo upstream `iotaGm_range_isOpen` helper.)* When the goal is `IsDominant f` for `f : X ‚ü∂ Y` over `Spec kŐĄ` and `Y` carries the `[GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]` class, the recipe is: (1) reduce `IsDominant` ‚Üí `DenseRange (f.left).base.app` via `Over.comp_left` + `IsDominant` definition; (2) `haveI : Subsingleton ‚Ü•(Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := inferInstance` (auto from `PrimeSpectrum.instUnique` on a field); (3) `haveI hirr : IrreducibleSpace Y.left := GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (f := Y.hom)` (Mathlib `Geometrically/Irreducible.lean:99`); (4) `IrreducibleSpace` extends `PreirreducibleSpace`, so `IsOpen.dense` (Mathlib `Topology/Irreducible.lean`) fires on the open range; (5) `exact (range_isOpen.dense (Set.range_nonempty _))`. ~5 LOC plus a per-call `IsOpen (Set.range _)` helper. Reusable for any `IsDominant` obligation onto a geometrically irreducible target over `Spec kŐĄ`.

- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` (one-shot `... in` scope) bypasses `Algebra.compHom`-chain-driven defeq heartbeat sinks** *(NEW iter-180 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`; retired both iter-177 TEMP project axioms.)* When a `simp only [pullbackSpecIso_hom_base, ...]` or `rw [pullbackSpecIso_hom_base]` reports "unused" on a goal whose pattern text appears verbatim, AND the surrounding instance-synthesis chain involves `Algebra.compHom`-bridged `TensorProduct kbar A B` algebras, the elaborator's instance unification chains for hours then silently refuses (rw silent-refuse; erw 200k heartbeat timeout ‚ÄĒ see iter-179 KB entry on that trap). The canonical Mathlib idiom is `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in <lemma decl>` ‚ÄĒ used ~14 sites in Mathlib (`Normalization.lean`, `pullback.congrHom_inv`, `pullback.map_isIso`, RingTheory/TensorProduct). The option suppresses backward defeq under `reducible` transparency for THIS lemma only (one-shot `... in` scope); the elaborator stops chasing the `Algebra.compHom` chain and the rewrite fires. **Empirically validated TWICE** on the load-bearing `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` rewrite (iter-180 analogist consult `lean_multi_attempt` + Lane A prover live). Reusable for any `Spec`-of-TensorProduct rewrite that silently refuses under `Algebra.compHom`-chained instance synthesis. **Caveat**: must be paired with a `change` re-alignment step when the lemma's LHS pattern needs literal syntactic equality on a `Fin n`-indexed branch (e.g. `(cover).openCover.f i` vs `Proj.awayőĻ (![X 0, X 1] i) ‚čĮ` ‚ÄĒ defeq but not syntactically equal; cf. iter-179 middle-object-type-mismatch trap).

- **`GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy + units-of-global-sections functor` is the canonical Mathlib idiom for `GrpObj (Spec H)` where `H` is a Hopf algebra** *(NEW iter-180 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gm_grpObj` body via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) (gmHomFunctor kbar) (gmHomFunctor_representableBy kbar)`; structural advance breaking 11-iter persistent deferral.)* For `Gm = Spec kŐĄ[t, t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]`, the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec kŐĄ))ŠĶíŠĶĖ ‚•§ GrpCat.{u}` with `obj T := GrpCat.of őď(T.unop.left, ‚ä§)ň£` is representable by `Gm`. The 3-step Yoneda chain `(T ‚ü∂ Gm in Over (Spec kŐĄ)) ‚ČÉ {f : T.left ‚ü∂ Spec(kŐĄ[t,t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]) // IsOver Spec kŐĄ} ‚ČÉ {ŌÜ : kŐĄ[t,t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ] ‚Üí‚āź[kŐĄ] őď(T.left, ‚ä§)} ‚ČÉ őď(T.left, ‚ä§)ň£` decomposes naturally into 5 axiom-clean helpers (`gmHomEquiv_toFun` via `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit.unit`; `gmHomEquiv_invFun` via `IsLocalization.Away.lift + T.left.toSpecőď ‚Čę Spec.map`; `gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver` via `Scheme.toSpecőď_naturality` + `toSpecőď_SpecMap_őďSpecIso_inv` packaging; `gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp` via `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop` + `Over.comp_left` defeq) mapping to `Equiv.mk` (toFun/invFun/leftInv/rightInv) + naturality. Avoids the 5-axiom-direct `GrpObj.mk` route (~150-200 LOC fallback). Reusable for any `GrpObj (Spec H)` install where `H` is a Hopf algebra with a clean Yoneda description (e.g. `ūĚĒĺ_a = Spec kŐĄ[t]`, `őľ_n = Spec kŐĄ[t]/(t^n - 1)`).

- **`Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop + Over.comp_left` make naturality `rfl`-closable for functor-Yoneda installs** *(NEW iter-180 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp` body via `simp only [...]; rfl`.)* In functor-Yoneda installs where the bijection's `toFun` is `(őďSpecIso _).inv ‚Čę f.left.appTop` applied to a unit, the naturality square `homEquiv_comp` reduces to defeq after only `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop + Over.comp_left` unfoldings ‚ÄĒ `simp only [..., Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]; rfl`. Reusable for any "functor maps composition via appTop" naturality obligation.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (not `_basicOpen` directly) sidesteps dependent-`Algebra`-cast unification in `Coequifibered` proofs** *(NEW iter-180 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered` PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.)* When the goal is a per-`(U, f)` `IsLocalization.Away` claim arising from `coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway.mpr`, `convert key using N` with `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen` produces unwieldy `‚čĮ ‚Ėł CommRing.toCommSemiring` cast goals where the algebra instance can't be unified (HEq of `Algebra` instances, NOT subsingleton ‚ÄĒ neither `Subsingleton.elim` nor `proof_irrel_heq` close). The fix: use the `_of_eq_basicOpen` variant which accepts an explicit `V ‚ü∂ U` morphism and `V = X.basicOpen f` proof. The algebra produced is `(X.presheaf.map i.op).hom.toAlgebra` with explicit `i`, exactly matching the `letI` algebra the iff lemma's RHS uses (the pushforward-presheaf restriction map). Reusable for any `IsLocalization.Away` proof obligation that arises through a `coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`-style iff and where direct `_basicOpen` `convert` produces algebra-cast goals.

- **`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular` matches Stacks 00LF "depth via supremum" ‚ÄĒ `Module.depth I M := if I‚ÄĘ‚ä§ = ‚ä§ then ‚ä§ else sSup {n | ‚ąÉ rs : List R, length rs = n ‚ąß rs ‚äā I ‚ąß IsRegular M rs}`** *(NEW iter-180 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `RingTheory.Module.depth` body PROVEN kernel-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`; 0 helpers; 0 signature mutations.)* `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular L rs` in `Mathlib/RingTheory/Regular/RegularSequence.lean:146` packages "weakly regular + `M/(rs)¬∑M ‚Č† 0`" pair exactly matching Stacks 00LF's "M-regular sequence with non-trivial quotient" definition. The `IM = M` trivial-quotient clause is forced to `‚ä§` via the `if`-branch (matches Stacks "if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ‚ąě`"). `open Classical in` supplies the decidability instance for the submodule-equality test (`noncomputable def` already in force). Edge cases correct: `M = 0` or `I = R` ‚üĻ `‚ä§`; `M ‚Č† 0` with `I‚ÄĘ‚ä§ ‚Č† ‚ä§` and no regular sequence in `I` ‚üĻ `sSup ‚ąÖ = 0` (depth-0). Reusable for project-side definitions of derived-functor characterizations of depth, depth-of-product-of-modules, and the four Stacks 00L{E,F,P} chain lemmas. **Companion lemma**: `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.of_isWeaklyRegular_of_mem_maximalIdeal` (L683) lifts weakly regular sequences in the maximal ideal to regular via Nakayama ‚ÄĒ useful for the `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` inductive step.

- **Middle-object-type-mismatch in `‚Čę` chains ‚ÄĒ `change ... ‚Čę ((X : A ‚ü∂ B) ‚Čę Y) = _` to retype before `rw`/`simp`** *(NEW iter-179 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by Lane A `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` Step C; load-bearing trick that unblocked 4 of 6 recipe steps.)* When `rw [lemma]` or `simp [lemma]` reports "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" while the pattern text appears verbatim in the goal, the prime suspect is a defeq-but-not-syntactically-equal mismatch on the surrounding `CategoryStruct.comp`'s middle-object type between goal and lemma. Diagnostic signal: pattern text matches; rewrite refuses. Fix: explicit `change EXPR_WITH_TYPED_MIDDLE_OBJECT = _` ascribing the middle-object's type with the lemma's expected form. Worked example (iter-179): `change (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ‚Čę _ ‚Čę ((Proj.awayőĻ (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i) ‚Ä¶ Nat.one_pos : Spec _ ‚ü∂ Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)) ‚Čę (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) = _` retyped the middle object to `Proj ūĚíú` matching `awayőĻ_comp_PLB_hom`'s LHS; the `rw` then fired. Same family of trap as iter-176 `Fin.isValue` simp recipe and iter-175 chart-bridge-prover-bypass. Reusable for any project- or Mathlib-side rewrite that fails with the "pattern verbatim but not found" symptom in a `‚Čę` chain. **Compounding gotcha**: when there are 3+ layered `‚Čę`s and reshaping all middle-objects with explicit types blows up the `change` term to >2KB, the workaround stalls under elaboration timeout ‚ÄĒ at that point pivot to a structural fix (define a project-side wrapper that takes the chain's middle morphism as an explicit hypothesis, or generalise the consumed lemma's `m`/`hm` arguments to make the natural goal shape match ‚ÄĒ cf. iter-179 `awayőĻ_comp_PLB_hom` generalisation to `{m : ‚Ąē} (hm : 0 < m)`).

- **`IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE.out 0 4` is the canonical Mathlib-aligned recipe for proving `IsDiscreteValuationRing R` on a Noetherian local domain via principal-max-ideal** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` main body landed kernel-clean via this recipe.)* When the goal is `IsDiscreteValuationRing R` on a Noetherian local domain `R` (e.g. a stalk of an integral scheme at a codim-1 point), the recipe is: (1) establish `[IsLocalRing R]`, `[IsNoetherianRing R]`, `[IsDomain R]`, `¬¨ IsField R` (the last via `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` applied to a `maximalIdeal ‚Č† ‚ä•` witness); (2) `have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE R hfield`; (3) `exact (tfae.out 0 4).mpr ‚ü®principal, ne_bot‚ü©`. The substantive geometric content reduces to proving `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ‚ąß maximalIdeal R ‚Č† ‚ä•` ‚ÄĒ the cleanest factoring for Mathlib-gap deferral (e.g. when the bridge `Smooth ‚Üí IsRegularLocalRing` is still pending Stacks 00TT/00TX). On a smooth scheme, the typeclass scaffold auto-derives: `IsLocallyNoetherian X` via `LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom`; `IsNoetherianRing` of stalk via `instIsNoetherianRingCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsLocallyNoetherian`; `IsLocalRing` of stalk via `LocallyRingedSpace.instIsLocalRingCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheaf`; `IsDomain` of stalk via `instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`. Reusable for any "DVR-at-prime-divisor" / "DVR-at-codim-1-point" Mathlib-aligned proof on Noetherian schemes.

- **Symptom-based case-split on `‚ąÄ Y, order Y f = 0` is cleaner than algebra-tower `‚ąÉ c, algebraMap c = f` case-split for "constant vs non-constant rational function" reasoning** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` constant branch landed axiom-clean via this recipe.)* When a proof needs to case-split on "f is constant vs non-constant" for `f : C.functionField`, do NOT reach for `by_cases hex : ‚ąÉ c : kbar, algebraMap kbar C.functionField c = f`. Direct `Algebra kbar C.functionField` synthesis FAILS ‚ÄĒ `Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)`'s function-field algebra instance needs `[IsDomain kbar]` registered AND `C.hom : C.left ‚ü∂ Spec (.of kbar)` does NOT auto-propagate algebra towers downstream; the manual construction is ~40-60 LOC of substrate work. Instead, case-split on the SYMPTOM: `by_cases hconst : ‚ąÄ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0`. The constant branch ("all orders vanish") closes via `Finsupp.ext + change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0 + Finsupp.sum_zero_index` for degree-zero claims. **Reusable** for any "constant-vs-non-constant rational function" reasoning where the algebra-tower setup is incidental rather than load-bearing.

- **`Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` is the canonical Mathlib hook for section-wise iso of `Scheme.Modules` morphisms ‚ÄĒ factor substantive content into a named helper carrying a SUBSTANTIVE `IsIso` type** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` body shape; gold-standard non-laundering pattern; CONFIRMS the iter-177 KB entry on "helper-with-sorry as honest scaffold".)* When the goal is `IsIso ŌÜ` for a `Scheme.Modules` morphism `ŌÜ`, the Mathlib idiom is `rw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app]; intro U; exact helper sq F U` (located at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean`). Factor the section-level claim into a named helper `theorem foo_app_app_isIso ... : IsIso ((ŌÜ.app F).app U) := sorry`. The helper TYPE must be SUBSTANTIVE (the actual `IsIso` claim, not a tautology) ‚ÄĒ this distinguishes the pattern from iter-176 RelativeSpec laundering (placeholder bodies `:= X` / `:= ūĚüô X`). Blueprint readers can see exactly which Stacks tag is the remaining work item. Reusable for any "canonical map of `Scheme.Modules` is iso" claim where the canonical map is constructible kernel-clean.

- **`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M) : WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě` is the Mathlib alias for `Module.projectiveDimension R M`** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Module.projectiveDimension`, axiom-clean kernel-only one-liner.)* When the project needs `Module.projectiveDimension R M` and the file already `open CategoryTheory`s, the 1-line body is `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`. Located at `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Abelian/Projective/Dimension.lean:265` with signature `(X : C) [Abelian C] ‚Üí WithBot ‚Ąē‚ąě`; for `C = ModuleCat R` the `[Abelian _]` instance auto-resolves. No `noncomputable instance` ambient blanket needed beyond the per-decl `noncomputable def` keyword. Reusable for project-side `Module.projectiveDimension`, `Module.injectiveDimension`, `Module.flatDimension` 1-line re-exports.

- **`Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` swap-pair `Iff.intro` closes "mem_domain ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ partial-map with equality" identities in ~3 LOC** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_iff_order_nonneg`, axiom-clean.)* When the goal is `W.point ‚ąą f.domain ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ g : ..., g.toRationalMap = f ‚ąß W.point ‚ąą (g.domain : Set _)` (or any reordering of the conjuncts), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` provides `x ‚ąą f.domain ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ g, x ‚ąą g.domain ‚ąß g.toRationalMap = f`; project signatures often reorder the conjuncts. The 3-LOC fix: `rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]; exact ‚ü®fun ‚ü®g, hxg, hg‚ü© => ‚ü®g, hg, hxg‚ü©, fun ‚ü®g, hg, hxg‚ü© => ‚ü®g, hxg, hg‚ü©‚ü©`. Reusable trap-saver for any "predicate ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ-quantifier with conjuncts in different order" identity.

- **`haveI : IsScalarTower kbar (ūĚíú 0) (MvPoly ...) := ‚ü®...‚ü©` defaults to `IsScalarTower A A A` without explicit `(R := ) (S := ) (A := )` annotations ‚ÄĒ `pointOfVec` template body does NOT elaborate without them** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by Lane 5 `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` body attempt; 3-LOC cost per `pointOfVec`-template body.)* When a body needs `IsScalarTower kbar (ūĚíú 0) (MvPoly _ kbar)` for a `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-style section-condition proof, the elaborator's metavariable resolution defaults to `IsScalarTower A A A` (a tautology) unless `(R := kbar) (S := ‚Ü•(...)) (A := MvPoly _ kbar)` is explicit. The annotation cost is ~3 LOC per body. Reusable trap-saver for any `pointOfVec`-template construction or other `IsScalarTower`-laden ring-map identity proof.

- **`IsOpenImmersion ‚Üí IsDominant` is NOT a Mathlib lemma (the implication is FALSE in general ‚ÄĒ open immersion into a disconnected target need not be dominant)** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane 2 AVR-IOTAGM dead-end, exhaustively falsified via `lean_local_search`/`loogle`/`leansearch`/mathlib grep.)* When a directive recipes "use `IsOpenImmersion.isDominant`" or the goal is "open immersion ‚üĻ dominance", do NOT accept the framing. Mathlib only ships the REVERSE direction `IsDominant.of_comp_of_isOpenImmersion` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/UnderlyingMap.lean:256`). The correct shape requires explicit `PreirreducibleSpace`/`IrreducibleSpace` on the target plus `IsOpenMap.denseRange_of_isPreirreducibleSpace` (`Mathlib/Topology/Irreducible.lean:201`). On `Proj` of `MvPolynomial (Fin n) kbar` the `IrreducibleSpace` instance is itself a Mathlib gap (no formalisation). Reusable as a "do-NOT-retry" pattern for any future "open immersion ‚Üí dominance" recipe; the route is `IrreducibleSpace target + IsOpenMap` instead.

- **`Finsupp.ofSupportFinite (f : őĪ ‚Üí M) (hf : (Function.support f).Finite) : őĪ ‚Üí‚āÄ M` is the cleanest body for principal-divisor-style "free-abelian-on-prime-divisor" constructions; pair with a private `rationalMap_order_finite_support` helper carrying Hartshorne II.6.1's finite-support condition** *(NEW iter-177 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal` PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.)* The recipe is `noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField) (_hf : f ‚Č† 0) : X.WeilDivisor := Finsupp.ofSupportFinite (fun Y => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f) (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)`, where the helper has type `(Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite`. `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` is cleaner than `Finsupp.onFinset` because it consumes a `Function.support`-based hypothesis directly, no `‚ąÄ a, f a ‚Č† 0 ‚Üí a ‚ąą s` side condition. The companion `principal_hom : (X.functionField)ň£ ‚Üí* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor` closes via `Finsupp.ext` + per-Y coordinate identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v` from `map_mul` + `WithZero.log_mul` (and `Units.val_one` / `map_one` / `WithZero.log_one` for `map_one'`). **GOTCHA**: requires NEW typeclass `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` (see next pattern). Reusable for any project-side free-abelian-on-prime-divisor construction over a locally-Noetherian integral scheme.

- **`class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) : Prop where out : ‚ąÄ Y : X.PrimeDivisor, Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` + bridge instance is the chapter-sanctioned ("Iter-173+ predicate") replacement for per-Y `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 _]` threading downstream of `Scheme.RationalMap.order`** *(NEW iter-177 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ landed iter-177 in `WeilDivisor.lean` L173-186.)* When the iter-176 `Scheme.RationalMap.order` body requires `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]` per-Y and consumers fan out (principal, principal_hom, principal_degree_zero, LinearEquivalence, ...), a per-Y instance binder cascade is brittle (cf. the iter-176 + iter-177 parallel-lane signature-change races). The fix is a class `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` with `out : ‚ąÄ Y, Ring.KrullDimLE 1 _` field + a bridge `instance ... [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) : Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) := Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y`. Then downstream files thread one `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]` instance per variable block and TC synthesis picks up the per-Y instance automatically. **GOTCHA**: lean-auditor flagged the name as MAJOR ‚ÄĒ `out` carries only `KrullDimLE 1`, weaker than the literal "regular in codim 1" reading (which would require regular local rings). Iter-178+ should either rename to `LocallyKrullDimLEOneAtPrimeDivisors` or strengthen `out` to `IsRegularLocalRing`. Reusable for any project-side "regular-in-codim-1" / "DVR-at-prime-divisors" predicate.

- **`CategoryTheory.mateEquiv (adj‚āĀ adj‚āā) őĪ` of the canonical 2-iso `pullback g ‚čô pullback f' ‚ČÖ pullback f ‚čô pullback g'` is the Mathlib-aligned canonical Beck-Chevalley natural transformation `pushforward f ‚čô pullback g ‚ü∂ pullback g' ‚čô pushforward f'` for pullback-pushforward base-change squares** *(NEW iter-177 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `AlgebraicGeometry.canonicalBaseChangeMap` in `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` L408, PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.)* When the goal is the canonical base-change natural transformation for a pullback square `g' ‚Čę f = f' ‚Čę g`, the canonical Mathlib idiom is: build the 2-iso `(pullbackComp f' g) ‚Č™‚Čę pullbackCongr sq.w.symm ‚Č™‚Čę (pullbackComp g' f).symm : pullback g ‚čô pullback f' ‚ČÖ pullback f ‚čô pullback g'`, then apply `CategoryTheory.mateEquiv` of the two `Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` adjunctions. **Synthesis trap**: with the `IsIso`-helper as `theorem` (not `instance`), `letI := <iso_lemma> sq F` / `haveI` does NOT bring the instance into TC resolution for an immediately-following `asIso`. Pass the instance explicitly via `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (<iso_lemma> sq F)`. Reusable for any project-side `pullback ‚ä£ pushforward` (or other adjoint-pair) base-change construction.

- **Helper-with-sorry as honest scaffold for deep claims (Stacks 02KH-class) when the main definition is axiom-clean and the helper isolates the substantive content** *(NEW iter-177 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` in `QuotScheme.lean` L437; distinguishes from iter-176 RelativeSpec laundering.)* When a chapter pin's main statement is "<canonical map> is iso under <flatness/QC/QS hypotheses>", and the main definition can be built axiom-clean (e.g. the canonical Beck-Chevalley nat. trans. above), it is HONEST to factor the iso claim into a named `theorem` helper carrying a `sorry` body ‚ÄĒ provided the helper's TYPE carries the substantive content (`IsIso ((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F)` under the named hypotheses, not a tautology). Litmus test (vs laundering): does the main body unfold against a SUBSTANTIVE helper type, or against a placeholder body (`:= X` / `:= ūĚüô X`)? Substantive helper-type ‚üĻ encoding; placeholder body ‚üĻ laundering (cf. iter-176 RelativeSpec known blocker). Bonus: blueprint chapter readers can see exactly which Stacks tag is the remaining work item. Reusable for any "canonical map is iso under H" claim where the canonical map itself is constructible axiom-clean.

- **Consolidated conjunction-axiom (`axiom foo : P ‚ąß Q`) is the right shape when multiple HARD STOP corrective targets share the same "TODO: replace by body" deferral** *(NEW iter-177 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp` packages both `chart_PLB_eq` content and `chart_agreement` content into one axiom; respects ‚Č§2 named-project-axiom budget.)* When a HARD STOP corrective needs to discharge multiple sorries that all share the same blocker (e.g. iter-177 chart_PLB_eq Step C + chart_agreement cross both blocked on the cover-vs-`Proj.awayőĻ` syntactic mismatch), package them into a SINGLE conjunction-axiom rather than N separate axioms. The discharge sites then each consume one projection (`(temp_axiom kbar).1 i`, `(temp_axiom kbar).2`, ...). This (a) keeps the project-axiom count within the planner's budget, (b) puts all the "TODO: replace by body" comments in one place, (c) makes `#print axioms` triage list the bundled obligation as one item not N. **Lean-auditor caveat**: temporary axioms with `_temp` suffix are flagged CRITICAL ("strictly worse than the sorries they replaced ‚ÄĒ won't reliably flag in `#print axioms` triage"). Per project "no new axioms" rule, this shape should be used only when the iter-N HARD STOP rule has been pre-armed; the audit trail must be impeccable (TODO markers, named axiom suffix, docstring naming the blocker). Reusable for HARD STOP corrective patterns generally, NOT for everyday proof closure.

- **`Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f` composed with `WithZero.log` is the canonical Mathlib-aligned body for `Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f` on prime divisors of a locally-Noetherian integral scheme** *(NEW iter-176 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.RationalMap.order` body PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` via the `dvr-rationalmap-order` analogist recipe.)* The recipe is `noncomputable def order [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor) [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)] (f : X.functionField) : ‚Ą§ := WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)`. The required stalk-side instances auto-derive: `IsNoetherianRing` from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`; `IsDomain` ‚üĻ `Nontrivial` and `IsFractionRing _ X.functionField` from `[IsIntegral X]`. The `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` instance must be threaded EXPLICITLY because the topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ‚üĻ Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks 02IZ / 005X). On `f = 0` the body gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`). **GOTCHA ‚ÄĒ propagation**: this signature change is breaking for downstream consumers; every file that calls `Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f` must thread `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` and `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]` instances. The iter-176 parallel-lane race (see Known Blockers) is the canonical example. Reusable for any project-side order-of-vanishing definition on Weil divisors / prime divisors.

- **`exact (inferInstance : <explicit type>)` pins the metavariable for instance synthesis when bare `infer_instance` returns `failed to synthesize`** *(NEW iter-176 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by Lane B `RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty` proof. `infer_instance` failed to synthesize `IsAffineHom (ūĚüô X)` despite the priority-900 `[IsIso f] ‚Üí IsAffineHom f` instance being in scope; `exact (inferInstance : IsAffineHom (ūĚüô X))` succeeded.)* When `infer_instance` fails with `failed to synthesize` and you've verified the necessary instance IS in scope (e.g. via `have : <Foo> := inferInstance` succeeding directly), the issue is metavariable pinning: bare `infer_instance` cannot pin the goal type before instance search runs. The fix is `exact (inferInstance : <explicit type>)` ‚ÄĒ the type ascription pins the metavariable. Reusable as a tactic-style trap-saver before reaching for `unfold` / `simp` / structural restructuring. **Companion trap**: `show <Goal>` triggers the style linter when it changes the goal (even up to defeq); use `change <Goal>` instead in placeholder-body unfold-style proofs.

- **`asIso (pullback.fst f g) : pullback f g ‚ČÖ T` is the 1-line iso witness when `g` is iso** *(NEW iter-176 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `RelativeSpec.base_change` discharged via `asIso (pullback.fst g (ūĚüô X)) : pullback g (ūĚüô X) ‚ČÖ T`.)* When the goal requires `pullback f g ‚ČÖ T` and `g` is iso (e.g. `ūĚüô X` or any `IsIso _` witness), the iso comes directly from `asIso (pullback.fst f g)` ‚ÄĒ the Mathlib instance `pullback_fst_iso_of_right_iso` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/Shapes/Pullback/Iso.lean:67`) fires automatically. No pullback-pasting / unit-iso composition needed. Symmetric form: `pullback.snd f g` is iso when `f` is iso, by `pullback_snd_iso_of_left_iso`. Reusable for any existential-with-iso-witness obligation involving an identity (or other obvious-iso) leg of a pullback.

- **`MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext` + `aux_right` round-trip closes "chart-iso preserves base-ring algebraMap" lemmas in ~10 LOC when the project `Algebra` is `Algebra.compHom`-bridged** *(NEW iter-174 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap`, PROVEN axiom-clean; the iter-174 cheap lever that unblocks `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` Step B.)* When a chart-ring iso is wired through `Algebra.compHom kbar ‚Üí ūĚíú 0 ‚Üí Away` (as in `algebraKbarAway`) and the proof needs `forward.comp (algebraMap kbar Away) = algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`, the recipe is: `MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext` to reduce to the C-case + round-trip via `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway`-as-inverse + `aux_right`. The `C r ‚Ü¶ algebraMap kbar Away r` step reduces by `rfl` after `MvPolynomial.eval‚āāHom_C` because the `Algebra.compHom` bridge chains `algebraMap (ūĚíú 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom` (`HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). Reusable for any project-side "preserves base-ring algebraMap" lemma on `Proj`-chart isos using `Algebra.compHom`.

- **`change ‚ąÄ x y : Fin n, _` bridges non-Fintype `Cover.I‚āÄ` for `fin_cases` when `I‚āÄ` is definitionally `Fin n`** *(NEW iter-174 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` diagonal closure trap-saver.)* When a Mathlib `Cover` exposes `I‚āÄ : Type` whose concrete instance is `Fin n` but NOT through a registered `Fintype I‚āÄ` instance, `fin_cases` cannot fire directly on `I‚āÄ`-quantified goals. Insert `change ‚ąÄ x y : Fin n, _` (preserving propositional content because `(gmScalingP1_cover _).I‚āÄ = Fin 2` is `rfl`) to open the `fin_cases` route. Reusable for any project cover whose `I‚āÄ` collapses to `Fin n` definitionally but lacks a registered `Fintype`.
