- **Fin-syntactic-mismatch trap: `MvPolynomial.X (0 : Fin 2)` literal vs `MvPolynomial.X ‚ü®0, _‚ü©` form after `fin_cases` kills `rw`-based bridge identities** *(NEW iter-174 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ iter-174 PARTIAL-low blocker on `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` Step C; documented for iter-175 resolution.)* When a `Fin n`-indexed simp-lemma chain (e.g. `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst`, `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst`, `pullback.lift_fst`) reports all rewrites "unused", the literal-vs-anonymous-mk mismatch is the prime suspect: a `match i with | ‚ü®0, _‚ü© => ... | ‚ü®1, _‚ü© => ...` branch reduces to a goal containing the literal `MvPolynomial.X (0 : Fin 2)`, while `fin_cases i` substitutes to `MvPolynomial.X ‚ü®0, _‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ these are def-eq but `rw` is syntactic. **Workarounds (iter-175 candidates, in roughly ascending invasiveness)**: (1) `change` to explicit form; (2) `Fin.mk_zero'` after `NeZero 2`; (3) **restructure** to define `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso_zero` / `_one` separately, avoid the term-level `match` (analogist Decision 3 alternative); (4) `simp [Fin.isValue]` or `convert ... using 2`.

- **`Cover.hom_ext + őĻ_glueMorphisms_assoc + shared_helper` is the structural Mathlib idiom for "morphism out of a glued cover factors through per-chart certificates"** *(NEW iter-174 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` 3-line body; established by `chart-bridge-shared-helper` analogist as the right shape.)* No `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms_comp` or `hom_ext_of_agreement` exists in Mathlib `b80f227`; the structural idiom IS the 3-line pair `Cover.hom_ext + őĻ_glueMorphisms_assoc + per-chart certificate`. The directed-variant `Cover.glueMorphismsOverOfLocallyDirected` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Directed`) bundles `Over S` coherence but requires `[Cover.LocallyDirected]`, which is overkill for 2-chart covers. Reusable for any "morphism-out-of-a-glued-cover is determined by per-chart certificates" obligation in `Over S`.

- **`CategoryTheory.Limits.fst_eq_snd_of_mono_eq` closes diagonal cocycle cases when cover charts are `pullback.fst _ _` of `IsOpenImmersion`** *(NEW iter-174 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` diagonals.)* When a cocycle `pullback.fst (chart i) (chart j) ‚Čę thing‚āĀ = pullback.snd _ _ ‚Čę thing‚āā` reduces to a diagonal case `i = j`, the chart's `Mono` instance (auto-inherited from `IsOpenImmersion`) lets `fst_eq_snd_of_mono_eq` close in 1‚Äď2 LOC. Cross-cases (`i ‚Č† j`) need substantive content. Reusable for any project cocycle on a `(cover).pullback‚āĀ`-style cover whose charts factor through `IsOpenImmersion`s.

- **Encoding I `structure ... where sheaf; unit` is the right 2-field carrier for sheafified `Under`-object types when `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` infra is gated** *(NEW iter-174 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.QcohAlgebra` PROVEN axiom-clean.)* When a project needs a sheaf-of-algebras-over-a-base-sheaf carrier and the full Mathlib `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` + sheafified-tensor monoidal stack is not yet in place (commit `b80f227` ships only the presheaf form + `IsQuasicoherent` requires `HasSheafCompose` / `HasWeakSheafify` / `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), a 2-field `structure { sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat X.toPresheafedSpace; unit : X.sheaf ‚ü∂ sheaf }` (sheaf-upgrade of `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Ring/Under/Basic.lean:13-18`'s "`Under R = CommAlg R`" idiom) gives a non-tautological carrier downstream consumers can quantify over. The `isQcoh` overlay can be added as a 3rd field LATER without renaming the existing two ‚ÄĒ no semantic regression. Reusable for `RelativeSheafCarrier` / `IsRelativelyAffine` / similar sheafified-Under-object project types.

- **Junk-defined dependent `if`-branch is the honest body for blueprint pins broader than the call-site invariants** *(NEW iter-174 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint`, axiom-clean.)* When a chapter signature is broader than the proof body's context (e.g. accepts arbitrary `Scheme.{u}` with only one weak hypothesis, while the curve-level typeclasses are a call-site invariant), encode the body as `if h : <invariant predicate> then <substantive value> else <zero/junk>`. **Pair with two bridge equation lemmas** `*_eq_<substantive> P hP h` (proves `body = <substantive>` under `h : invariant`) and `*_eq_zero P hP h` (proves `body = 0` when `¬¨h`). Both close by `simp [body, h]` and are kernel-clean. The `unfold body` exposes the named `if-then-else`, immediately revealing the substantive content on the regime hypothesis ‚ÄĒ NOT a tautological body. Reusable for any blueprint pin whose signature is intentionally broader than the call-site invariants.

- **`pullbackSymmetry ‚Čę pullbackRightPullbackFstIso ‚Čę pullback.congrHom ‚Čę pullbackSpecIso` is the 4-step canonical Mathlib chain for `pullback (pullback.fst f g) (Spec.map _) ‚ČÖ Spec (S ‚äó[R] T)`** *(NEW iter-173 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ established this iter via the `chart-bridge173` analogist consult + landed in `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` axiom-clean; the iter-172 prover's PRIMARY 3 blocker, "no single Mathlib lemma for this bridge," is resolved.)* When the goal is to identify a `pullback (pullback.fst _) (Proj.awayőĻ _)` (or other affine projection) with a tensor-product `Spec`, no single Mathlib lemma exists ‚ÄĒ assemble the 4-step chain `pullbackSymmetry ‚Č™‚Čę pullbackRightPullbackFstIso ‚Č™‚Čę pullback.congrHom _ _ ‚Č™‚Čę pullbackSpecIso`. **Mathlib template**: the same pattern is in `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso` at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Cover/Open.lean:160-166`. Citations: `pullbackSymmetry` (`HasPullback.lean:494-496`), `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso` (`Pasting.lean:451-456`), `pullbackSpecIso` (`Pullbacks.lean:702-708`). Reusable for any project-side pullback-of-`Proj.awayőĻ`-vs-`Spec.map` bridge.

- **Pattern-match-on-`Fin n` in term-level `Iso.trans` chains when generic-index reductions are not definitional** *(NEW iter-173 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced when `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` tried generic `i : Fin 2` and hit `(![X 0, X 1]) i = X i` non-definitional.)* When a term-level iso definition needs `(![X_0, X_1]) i = X i` (or similar `Matrix.cons_val`-style reduction) to hold, generic `i : Fin n` does NOT reduce definitionally ‚ÄĒ case-split inside the iso body via `match i with | ‚ü®0, _‚ü© => ... | ‚ü®1, _‚ü© => ...`. Do NOT use `fin_cases i` at the term level: it fails to reduce `Iso.trans` terms in non-Prop positions. The pattern-match approach yields direct `(![X 0, X 1]) ‚ü®0, _‚ü© = X 0` definitional unfolding. Reusable for any explicit-vector-applied-to-`Fin n` reduction in a categorical iso construction.

- **`Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun a ‚Ü¶ h a) : (őĪ ‚Üí‚āÄ M) ‚Üí+ N` is the cleanest universal-property packaging for free-abelian-group AddMonoidHom constructions; beats manual `where`-form by ~5 LOC** *(NEW iter-173 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_hom`, axiom-clean one-liner via `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ‚Ü¶ AddMonoidHom.id ‚Ą§)`.)* When defining a group homomorphism `(őĪ ‚Üí‚āÄ M) ‚Üí+ N` from a family of homomorphisms `őĪ ‚Üí (M ‚Üí+ N)`, reach for `Finsupp.liftAddHom`. The manual `where toFun := ..., map_zero' := ..., map_add' := ...` structure literal works but requires hand-discharging `‚ąÄ a, h a (b‚āĀ + b‚āā) = h a b‚āĀ + h a b‚āā`; `liftAddHom` packages this automatically. Pair with `@[simp]` bridge lemma `Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply` to make the bundled form simp-rewriteable to the unbundled `D.sum (fun _ ‚Ü¶ id)` form for downstream consumers. Reusable for any project-side free-abelian-group ‚Üí AddMonoidHom construction (e.g. `Scheme.WeilDivisor ‚Üí+ ‚Ą§`, `Finsupp K Cycle ‚Üí+ Q`, etc.).

- **`Order.coheight point = 1` is the canonical Mathlib-aligned `codim 1` predicate for prime-divisor structures on `Scheme.carrier`; total without manual `[Preorder]` instance** *(NEW iter-173 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.PrimeDivisor.coheight`, axiom-clean structure refactor; closes the iter-172 lean-auditor must-fix-this-iter on the `True := trivial` placeholder.)* When defining a "codim-1 prime/integral subscheme" structure or predicate on `Scheme.carrier`, use `Order.coheight : őĪ ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚ąě` (from `Mathlib.Order.KrullDimension`) directly with the standard scheme specialisation preorder (`x ‚Č§ y ‚ÜĒ y ‚§≥ x`). NO manual `[Preorder]` instance threading needed ‚ÄĒ `Order.coheight` is total on the carrier. The predicate is `Prop`-valued (`Order.coheight point = 1`) and elaborates cleanly against `‚Ąē‚ąě` literals. Reusable for any project-side "prime/integral codim-1 substructure" definition.

- **Substantive-consequence types are the right scaffold encoding when full Yoneda types are gated; pair with chapter `% NOTE:` annotations naming the iter-N+1 refinement** *(NEW iter-173 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Picard/RelativeSpec.lean` `UniversalProperty`/`affine_base_iff`/`base_change`/`functor` all landed as substantive-consequence weakenings with 4 chapter NOTEs.)* When a chapter pin is a natural bijection `Hom_X(T, F) ‚ČÉ Hom(...)` (or canonical iso, or contravariant `Functor`) but the supporting Hom-set / `őď`-functor / `pullbackQcoh` infrastructure isn't there yet, encode the Lean as a *structural consequence* (`IsAffineHom`, `IsAffine`, `‚ąÉ ūĚíú', Nonempty (... ‚ČÖ ...)`, bare-function-into-`Over`). The encoding **MUST** be non-tautological (no `Iso.refl`, no `True`, no `Classical.choice _`); the litmus test is "unfolding reveals a substantive Prop, not a trivial proposition". Pair each weakened pin with a chapter `% NOTE: iter-N file-skeleton encodes weakened type X; iter-(N+1)+ refines to Y once Z infrastructure lands` annotation (review-agent domain). This iter applied the NOTE pattern to 4 `Picard_RelativeSpec.tex` pins. Strict reviewer reading puts each weakened encoding in `major` severity with hard iter-N+1 refinement obligation; this is preferable to either (a) blocking the file-skeleton landing entirely or (b) silently letting weakened types contaminate downstream consumers.

- **Type-level `:= sorry` on a carrier type is a LEGAL but auditor-flagged scaffold pattern; lean-vs-blueprint-checker classifies as "deliberate scaffold" when documented; lean-auditor (correctly) flags as critical** *(NEW iter-173 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.QcohAlgebra : Type (u+1) := sorry` at `Picard/RelativeSpec.lean:98` ‚ÄĒ every theorem in the file quantifies over `X.QcohAlgebra`.)* When a project decl is a carrier `Type` not yet shipped by Mathlib (e.g. `QcohAlgebra`, `RelativeAlbanese`, etc.), a type-level `:= sorry` is the only honest move at file-skeleton stage ‚ÄĒ but two reviewer subagents will disagree on severity. **Resolution**: treat it as a HARD iter-(N+1) BODY LANE PRIORITY signal, not a "the file should not have landed" rejection. The carrier type IS substantive (downstream theorems quantify over it) ‚ÄĒ it just doesn't have a body yet. Distinguishes from `:= trivial`/`True` placeholders which ARE tautological. Bonus: `Type`-level sorries do NOT contaminate `Prop`-level reasoning kernel-wise. **GOTCHA**: forward-looking `iter-N+1 ...` excuse-comments accumulate around the type-level sorry, get flagged by lean-auditor as aggregate must-fix; they retire when the body lands. - **`MvPolynomial.induction_on` chained with `Subalgebra.{algebraMap_mem, add_mem, mul_mem}` proves `Algebra.adjoin (ūĚíú 0) (Set.range v) = ‚ä§` for `v : Fin n ‚Üí MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`** *(NEW iter-172 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective`, PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, ~140 LOC; closes the iter-171 cancel-surjective body of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left` axiom-clean and retires the iso's `sorryAx` taint.)* When the goal is "polynomial ring is generated over its constant subring by the variables", the canonical recipe is `top_unique; intro p _; refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX` with three cases discharged via `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` / `Subalgebra.add_mem` / `Subalgebra.mul_mem` + `Algebra.subset_adjoin` for the variable case. **GOTCHA**: do NOT use bare `algebraMap_mem` / `add_mem` / `mul_mem` (causes "synthetic hole has already been defined and assigned to value incompatible" elaboration errors); always thread through `Subalgebra.*` variants. Do NOT use `induction p using MvPolynomial.induction_on with | C r => ...` with `let S := ...` binder ‚ÄĒ same synthetic-hole class. The `C`-case bridge from `kbar` to the degree-0 piece via `SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply` + a subtype-equality witness on `MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C` is mechanical. Reusable for any "polynomial-ring generated over its constant subring" claim in a `Proj`-chart context.

- **`HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow` is the dedicated `val (x^n) = x.val^n` ‚ÄĒ `RingHom.map_pow` / `_root_.map_pow` metavariable-fails because `val` is a plain function** *(NEW iter-172 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the `gen_eq_pow` helper of `mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective`.)* When chaining `val`-injection-into-`Localization` with a power, reach for the dedicated `HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow` lemma directly (located at `Mathlib/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:456`); the generic `map_pow` variants fail metavariable resolution because `val` is not bundled as a RingHom. Reusable trap-saver for any `val (x^n) =` rewriting chain in homogeneous-localization proofs.

- **`Fin.ext + omega` substitution is cleaner than `fin_cases i` for explicit-index reduction in `Fin 2` contexts where `otherFin` is involved** *(NEW iter-172 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the `gen_eq_pow` helper's `i : Fin 2` case-split.)* When the proof needs an explicit `i = 0` / `i = 1` substitution to reduce `otherFin i`, `fin_cases i` keeps the `‚ü®0, _‚ü©` / `‚ü®1, _‚ü©` form which doesn't reduce `otherFin` cleanly. Use `have : i.val = 0 ‚ą® i.val = 1 := by omega; rcases ... with hi | hi; have : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi; subst this` for clean substitution. Reusable for any `Fin n`-indexed case-split where `otherFin`-type helpers must reduce against literal values.

- **`Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff` + `Localization.r_iff_exists`** for reducing equality of `Localization.mk` fractions to a `‚ąÉ s ‚ąą S, s ¬∑ numerator_LHS ¬∑ denominator_RHS = s ¬∑ numerator_RHS ¬∑ denominator_LHS` ring identity *(NEW iter-172 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the `gen_eq_pow` helper.)* When the goal is two `Localization.mk` fractions equal in `Localization.Away t`, the canonical rewrite chain is `rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]; refine ‚ü®1, ?_‚ü©; <ring identity>`. The `refine ‚ü®1, ?_‚ü©` discharges the `‚ąÉ s ‚ąą S` quantifier with `s = 1` (always valid for `t = 1` units); the residual ring identity closes by `ring` or explicit `mul_pow` / `pow_add` manipulation. Reusable for any `Localization.Away` equality on monomial-ratio fractions.

- **Second consecutive iter where an Anthropic API 5xx killed a prover lane mid-run with zero file edits (iter-170: 500 / Lane A G0BO; iter-172: 529 / Lane B Picard); KB-recorded "reversal trigger DOES NOT fire under external-API failure" pattern now has 2 data points** *(NEW iter-172 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ extends the iter-170 lone observation into a confirmed failure-mode class.)* When a prover task_result is the harness's `error` status and the log's final event is an `API Error: 5XX` text-block, the lane LITERALLY did not falsify the route ‚ÄĒ it terminated mid-test. The plan reviewer must NOT count this as a CHURNING / STUCK signal; iter-(N+1) plan re-dispatches the lane verbatim (with same HARD GATE state, same objectives, no extra critic consults). Detection rule: log's final `event: text` content matching `^API Error: 5\d\d` + `cache_read_input_tokens > 100000` (= real prover work was happening up to the failure) confirms the diagnosis. Distinguishes from a lane that genuinely failed its substantive goal (which would have file edits + concrete error diagnostics from `lean_diagnostic_messages` or build failures).

- **Body-first attack rescues CHURNING helper-supports chains; force the load-bearing residual into PARTIAL form before any more helper rounds** *(NEW iter-171 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1` body skeleton landed via `Over.homMk + Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` after 5 iters of helper-supports rounds had failed to even start. Generalises the iter-170 progress-critic `routec170` corrective.)* When a chain of helper rounds keeps deferring the load-bearing residual, force the residual into PARTIAL form first ‚ÄĒ write the body as a concrete category-theoretic invocation with internal sorries hoisted to top-level named declarations. The helper shapes are then **typed-constrained** by the residual's signature and stop drifting. iter-171 evidence: 3 prior iters of `aux_left` / iso / chart-ring chasing under iso-route Option A did NOT close the `gmScalingP1` body even with a clean iso to consume. iter-171's body-first Option B wrote `gmScalingP1 := Over.homMk ((gmScalingP1_cover).glueMorphisms (gmScalingP1_chart) (gmScalingP1_chart_agreement)) (gmScalingP1_over_coherence)` directly, and the three named internal sorries each carry a focused, well-typed closure recipe. Detection rule: when a CHURNING route's progress-critic verdict cites "‚Č•3 iters of helper-supports without residual shrinkage", the next plan iter's PRIMARY should be a body-first attack with PARTIAL acceptance ‚ÄĒ NOT another helper round.

- **`Algebra.compHom` is the `kbar ‚Üí ūĚíú 0 ‚Üí A` bridge for `HomogeneousLocalization.Away`-side `Algebra` synthesis** *(NEW iter-171 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `algebraKbarAway`, axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, 3 lines, unblocks downstream tensor-product `CommRing`/`Algebra` synthesis.)* When the project needs `Algebra kbar (HomogeneousLocalization.Away ūĚíú f)`, Mathlib ships `Algebra (ūĚíú 0) (HomogeneousLocalization ūĚíú x)` (via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`) but NOT `Algebra kbar` directly. The 3-line bridge is `noncomputable instance := Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))`. This refutes the iter-169 prover's "TensorProduct (Away _ _) (GmRing kbar) CommRing missing" diagnosis: the missing hop was `Algebra kbar` (not `CommRing`), and `Algebra.TensorProduct.instCommRing` then synthesizes `CommRing (TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) (GmRing kbar))` for free. Reusable for any `Algebra k R`-synthesis on a project ring that already carries `Algebra` along a sub-base-ring (`ūĚíú 0`, `Subring R`, `Algebra.adjoin R _`, etc.).

- **`IsLocalization.Away.invSelf t : Localization.Away t` is the canonical `1/t` factor in `eval‚āāHom`-style ring-map constructions** *(NEW iter-171 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap`, axiom-clean.)* When a ring map needs the `1/t` factor of `Localization.Away t` (e.g. the chart-0 case of `ŌÉ_√ó`: `t ‚Ü¶ t ‚äó őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`), use `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf t : Localization.Away t` directly. Source: `Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/Away/Basic.lean:58`. Do NOT reach for `(IsUnit.unit _).inv` / `(hu.choose)` constructions ‚ÄĒ they require a `IsUnit` witness chain and are more brittle. Reusable for any project ring map into `Localization.Away t` that needs the `t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` factor.

- **`(affineCover).openCover.pullback‚āĀ f` is the canonical "pullback of a cover along a morphism" hook for `Scheme`/`OpenCover`** *(NEW iter-171 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_cover`, axiom-clean.)* When the project needs a cover of `pullback X.hom f` given a cover of `X` and a morphism `f : Y ‚ü∂ X`, the canonical recipe is `(affineCover X).openCover.pullback‚āĀ (pullback.fst X.hom f)`. Implementation lives in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Directed:153` (the `Cover`-level `pullback‚āĀ`) on top of `PreZeroHypercover.pullback‚āĀ` at `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Hypercover.Zero:97`. Reusable for any product-stability or base-change cover construction (e.g. covers of `X √ó Y`, covers of `pullback f g`, etc.).

- **Lean notation trap: `A ‚äó[R] B` in a *type ascription* position parses `[R]` as a list literal when `A` is itself a parametric type** *(NEW iter-171 ‚ÄĒ cost the iter-171 prover one edit cycle: the signature `MvPolynomial Unit kbar ‚äó[kbar] GmRing kbar` parsed as `MvPolynomial Unit (kbar ‚äó GmRing) kbar`.)* When writing a signature involving a `TensorProduct` where the first argument is a parametric type (`MvPolynomial ŌÉ R`, `Polynomial R`, `Module R`, etc.), the `‚äó[R]` infix syntax is ambiguous ‚ÄĒ the `[R]` may parse as a list literal applied to the prior identifier. **Workaround**: use the explicit named form `TensorProduct R A B` in signatures and complex term positions. The `‚äó[R]` notation is fine in body positions where the elaborator has more context. Reusable detection: any "list literal" / "type ascription on list" error message when writing `A ‚äó[R] B`.

- **Three-routes-down-one-Mathlib-gap diagnoses are NOT a route blocker if the gap is mis-named ‚ÄĒ re-run the mathlib-analogist on the *specific* missing instance** *(NEW iter-171 ‚ÄĒ iter-169 prover stalled `gmScalingP1` body for 3 iters citing "TensorProduct (Away _ _) (GmRing kbar) CommRing missing"; iter-170 analogist `tensoraway` correctly identified the missing instance as `Algebra kbar (Away _ _)` (a 3-line `compHom` bridge), unblocking the whole route.)* When a prover lane attempts multiple routes and each fails at a "Mathlib gap", do NOT accept the gap diagnosis at face value ‚ÄĒ the prover may have correctly identified that synthesis fails but mis-named the missing instance. Dispatch a `mathlib-analogist` with the specific error message + the desired final synthesis target and ask: "what is the ACTUAL missing hop, and is it a Mathlib gap or a 3-line bridge we can write in-project?" iter-171 evidence: 3 iters of "Mathlib upstream PR" planning vs ~5min of `compHom` discovery.

- **`lean_run_code` snippets must explicitly annotate `noncomputable` per-decl when mirroring file-context declarations from inside `noncomputable section`** *(NEW iter-170 ‚ÄĒ the iter-170 prover lost 2 of 22 turns to this when verifying the `tensoraway` `Algebra.compHom` recipe before being killed by an external API outage.)* When you reach for `lean_run_code` to sanity-check a recipe that mirrors a file-level declaration sitting inside `noncomputable section`, the snippet does **not** inherit that section's blanket. `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` is `noncomputable`, so the file's `abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading` + `instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing` compile because L65 opens `noncomputable section`; replicate them in a snippet without per-decl `noncomputable` keywords and elaboration fails with "consider marking it as 'noncomputable'". **Tell**: when the file uses `noncomputable section` but its individual decls do NOT carry the keyword, snippet probes need `noncomputable abbrev` / `noncomputable instance` on every dependent decl, or the probe fails for reasons unrelated to the recipe. **Recommendation**: prefer in-file landing of small instance recipes (3-line `Algebra.compHom` bridges, single-LOC ring-hom helpers) directly + `lake build` + `lean_diagnostic_messages` verification over `lean_run_code` standalone snippet pre-verification. The in-file landing is shorter, picks up the section blanket for free, and avoids the snippet-only artifact entirely. Reserve `lean_run_code` for actually-standalone test material.

- **`Proj.map` is the canonical Mathlib hook for morphisms `Proj ‚Ą¨ ‚Üí Proj ūĚíú` from graded ring maps** *(NEW iter-169 ‚ÄĒ `ProjectiveSpectrum/Functor.lean:144` for `Proj.map`, L99 for `sheafedSpaceMap`; identified as the cleanest morphism shape for `ŌÉ_√ó` IF the relative-Proj base-change iso were available.)* When the desired morphism between two `Proj` schemes arises from a graded ring map `f : ūĚíú ‚Üí+*ŠĶć ‚Ą¨` with `‚Ą¨‚āä ‚Č§ ūĚíú‚āä.map f`, `Proj.map f` gives the underlying `Scheme.Hom`. For the `ūĚĒĺ_m`-scaling action `ŌÉ_√ó : ‚Ąô¬Ļ √ó ūĚĒĺ_m ‚ü∂ ‚Ąô¬Ļ`, the natural choice is `ūĚíú = MvPoly (Fin 2) kbar`, `‚Ą¨ = MvPoly (Fin 2) (GmRing kbar)`, both std ‚Ąē-grading, with `X 0 ‚Ü¶ t ¬∑ X 0, X 1 ‚Ü¶ X 1`. The remaining step is identifying `Proj ‚Ą¨ ‚ČÖ ProjectiveLineBar ‚äó Gm`, which requires the **relative-Proj base-change iso** `Proj(MvPoly (Fin n) R) ‚ČÖ Proj(MvPoly (Fin n) k) √ó_{Spec k} Spec R`. **Verified iter-169 ABSENT from Mathlib** (no `Proj.iso_pullback_Spec`/`ProjPolynomial`/`relativeProj`/`Proj.base_change_iso` anywhere under `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/`). Reusable for any future morphism-construction between Proj schemes (when Mathlib gains the relative iso, the route lights up). Also useful as a negative result: do NOT plan a project-side construction that implicitly assumes a relative-Proj formalism without first running this absence-check.

- **"Verify the load-bearing Mathlib decl exists before committing the iter"** *(NEW iter-169 ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the route-(b) functoriality of Proj attempt for `gmScalingP1`; 1-minute `Glob`+`Grep` smoke test prevented a multi-iter route commitment.)* When a critic / analogist / plan-agent suggests a route hinging on a specific Mathlib lemma or formalism, do a quick `Glob`+`Grep` pass across the relevant `Mathlib/*` subtree BEFORE budgeting the iter. iter-169 saved a multi-iter route-(b) commitment by confirming `Proj.iso_pullback_Spec`/`relativeProj`/`ProjPolynomial`/`Proj.base_change_iso` are all genuinely absent across the whole `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/` tree. Pattern: when designing around an external library, validate the cited primitives exist before pencilling the work into the plan. Cheap; complements the lean-auditor "Mathlib gap"-detection rule by catching gap-citations before they congeal into helper-supports churn.

- **Loss of factor through global-sections on a non-affine source ‚ÄĒ morphisms via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from a `Proj √ó Affine` product can only factor through the affine projection** *(NEW iter-169 ‚ÄĒ Attempt 1 of `gmScalingP1` body; semantic dead-end documented to prevent future re-runs.)* When the source `X = Proj ūĚíú √ó Spec R` is non-affine and the target is `Proj ūĚíú'`, applying `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` to a ring map `MvPoly _ k ‚Üí+* őď(X, ‚ä§)` produces a morphism that factors through `Spec (őď(X, ‚ä§))`. By properness of `Proj ūĚíú` over `Spec k`, `őď(Proj ūĚíú, ‚ä§) = k`, so `őď(Proj ūĚíú √ó Spec R, ‚ä§) = k ‚äó_k R = R` ‚ÄĒ the resulting morphism factors through `Spec R = Affine`, LOSING the `Proj ūĚíú` factor. **Tell**: if your construction sends `(x, őĽ) ‚Üí f(őĽ)` with no dependence on `x`, the source's `Proj`-factor is being collapsed; you cannot encode a genuinely bivariate scaling morphism via a single `fromOfGlobalSections` from the global sections of the total space. The correct shape is **chart-glue** (`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` per chart of `Proj ūĚíú` + `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`) OR **functoriality of Proj** (`Proj.map` + base-change iso).

- **`TensorProduct kbar (HomogeneousLocalization.Away ūĚíú _) (GmRing kbar)` lacks `CommRing`/`Algebra` instances under `inferInstance`** *(NEW iter-169 ‚ÄĒ surfaced by Option-B chart-glue Attempt 2 of `gmScalingP1` body; do-not-retry pattern absent project-local instance-build or Mathlib upstream.)* When designing chart-side ring maps for a `Proj`-of-graded-polynomial product, the natural target ring `Away ūĚíú (X i) ‚äó_k GmRing` (or similar `Localization.Away`-of-quotient ‚äó commutative-algebra products) does NOT carry a `CommRing` / `Algebra` instance shipped by Mathlib. The standard `Algebra.TensorProduct` infrastructure handles `R ‚äó_k S` where each is a commutative `k`-algebra, but the `HomogeneousLocalization.Away`-side carries a more specialised structure (quotient of a `Localization` by the degree-zero relation) that the standard `Algebra` instance does not auto-derive. Workarounds: (1) explicit project-local instance build (~30-50 LOC, includes the `CommRing` + `Algebra` + smul-stability); (2) re-encode the chart target as an explicit `Localization.Away` quotient sidestepping `HomogeneousLocalization`; (3) wait for upstream Mathlib `HomogeneousLocalization.AwayTensor`. Pattern: when blueprint-designing a chart-glue construction, first confirm the chart-side ring's tensor with the test-scheme's coordinate ring carries the standard `CommRing`/`Algebra` instances ‚ÄĒ if not, that gap dominates the iter budget.

- **`HomogeneousLocalization.Away` is a domain (no Mathlib gap) ‚ÄĒ `Function.Injective.isDomain` on the algebraMap into `Localization.Away` finishes in <10 LOC, given a domain graded ring and a non-zero localising element** *(NEW iter-168 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`, PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, re-verified this review; the iter-167 docstring claim that this was a Mathlib gap was DISPELLED.)*
  When the goal needs `IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away ūĚíú t)` where `t` is a homogeneous non-zero element of a domain graded ring (e.g. `t = X i : MvPolynomial (Fin n) kŐĄ` for the standard ‚Ąē-grading), the existing `Localization.Away t` is a domain (chain `IsLocalization.isDomain_localization (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (X_ne_zero _))`) and the natural map `algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away ūĚíú t) (Localization.Away t)` is definitionally `.val`, injective by `HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. `Function.Injective.isDomain` finishes. **Reusable for any `Proj`-chart reducedness over a domain graded ring** ‚ÄĒ combine with `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover` (or `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`) to get `IsReduced (Proj ūĚíú).left` from chart-by-chart domain witnesses. **Stale-docstring lesson**: when a docstring claims "Mathlib gap" + describes the very strategy that works, the gap may not exist. Re-audit before accepting as a multi-iter blocker.

- **`Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` `hf` discharge for `MvPolynomial` standard ‚Ąē-grading ‚ÄĒ `MvPolynomial.as_sum` + `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`** *(NEW iter-168 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`, PROVEN axiom-clean; reusable for any `Proj` of `MvPolynomial ŌÉ R` covered by the `X i`s with `m_i = 1`.)*
  To specialise `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to `(őĻ := Fin n, m := ![1, ‚Ä¶, 1], f := ![X 0, ‚Ä¶, X (n-1)])` for the standard ‚Ąē-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin n) R`, the non-trivial obligation is `hf : (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant ūĚíú).toIdeal ‚Č§ Ideal.span (Set.range f)`. **Recipe**: (1) `rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff, GradedRing.proj_apply]`; (2) reduce `(decompose ūĚíú p) 0 = 0` to `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0` via `Subtype.ext (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)`; (3) extract `coeff 0 p = 0` via `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero` + `MvPolynomial.C_injective`; (4) `rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]; refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd => ?_` to reduce to monomials; (5) for each `d ‚ąą p.support`, `d ‚Č† 0` (else `coeff d p = 0` contradicts `mem_support_iff`), so `d i ‚Č† 0` for some `i`, hence `(X i) ‚ą£ monomial d (coeff d p)` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr _)`, hence membership via `Ideal.mul_mem_left` + `Ideal.subset_span ‚ü®i, rfl‚ü©`. **Reusable for any `Proj` of `MvPolynomial ŌÉ R` for the irrelevant-LESpan obligation.**

- **`Localization.awayLift_mk` with explicit `(v := 1) (hv := ...)` shortcut** *(NEW iter-168 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right`, axiom-clean; recurring trap-saver.)*
  When the source ring map sends the localising generator to `1` (a unit), use the explicit `(v := 1) (hv := by simp [...])` parameters to `Localization.awayLift_mk` rather than going through `(IsUnit.unit hu).inv`/`(hu.choose)`. The standard form is `rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i) (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]` ‚ÄĒ removes one indirection layer from the unit witness chain. Reusable for any `awayLift_mk` rewrite when the underlying ring map evaluates the generator to a literal `1`.

- **`RingEquiv.ofRingHom` partial-iso construction ‚ÄĒ partial axiom hygiene is faithfully reported by `lean_verify`** *(NEW iter-168 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`; the iso exists as a definition with one round-trip closed and the other a `sorry`, and `lean_verify` returns `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (sorryAx propagates honestly from `aux_left`); downstream consumers correctly see this partial hygiene.)*
  When staging a ring-iso build across multiple iters, `RingEquiv.ofRingHom forward inverse h_right h_left` is the right shape even when only one of `h_right` / `h_left` is closed. The iso is definable (lets downstream code consume it definitionally / structurally), but `lean_verify` honestly reports the iso depends on `sorryAx` whenever a round-trip is sorry-bodied. **Anti-pattern warning** (iter-168 critical): do NOT pretend "structural setup via `ext`, `Surjective.of_‚Ä¶`, `val_injective`" landed if the body is bare `sorry` ‚ÄĒ the lean-auditor flagged exactly this misrepresentation on `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left`.

- **Stale-"Mathlib gap"-docstring detection: when an excuse-comment cites a "gap" and the proof's actual strategy closes via *existing* Mathlib lemmas, the gap probably never existed** *(NEW iter-168 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` going from "Project-side scaffold sorry (Mathlib does not ship `IsReduced (Proj ūĚíú)` ‚Ä¶ would close via `IsReduced.of_openCover` ‚Ä¶ once `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` is bridged to `IsDomain`)" to closed axiom-clean by building that bridge in <10 LOC.)*
  When auditing scaffold sorries that carry "Mathlib gap" docstrings, run the proof's own described strategy as a smoke test before accepting the claim as a multi-iter blocker. Specifically check whether the cited "missing bridge" can be built from existing Mathlib lemmas using injective-into-a-larger-domain / lift-along-a-`val`-style chains. iter-168 dispelled the iter-167 docstring's claim by closing the body in <10 LOC. **iter-169 corrective**: re-audit the 4 sibling "Mathlib gap" claims in `Genus0BaseObjects.lean` ‚ÄĒ `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`, `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim`, `gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced` ‚ÄĒ each may have a similar <30 LOC closure via existing Mathlib lemmas. **Generally**: any time an "excuse-comment + Mathlib gap" pattern coincides with a downstream consumer that successfully closes via `infer_instance`, the gap is suspect.

- **`(X ‚äó Y).hom = pullback.fst X.hom Y.hom ‚Čę X.hom` is `rfl` in Cartesian-monoidal `Over S` ‚ÄĒ `change` + `infer_instance` reduces every product-stability claim on the monoidal-product `.hom` to the standard composition + base-change instance chain** *(NEW iter-167 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, PROVEN axiom-clean; `gm_irreducibleSpace` uses the same `change` form to swap `Gm.left` for its `Spec _` representation; verified this review by both `lean-auditor-iter167` and `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-g0bo-iter167`.)*
  The `rfl` identity lives in `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Over:62` (`tensorObj_hom : (R ‚äó S).hom = pullback.fst R.hom S.hom ‚Čę R.hom`). To prove a stability instance `P ((X ‚äó Y).hom)` for any `MorphismProperty P` that is both `IsStableUnderComposition` and `IsStableUnderBaseChange` in Mathlib (`LocallyOfFiniteType`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, `Smooth`, ...), the canonical recipe is **`change P (pullback.fst X.hom Y.hom ‚Čę X.hom); infer_instance`** ‚ÄĒ the first step is `rfl`, the second resolves through the base-change instance on `pullback.fst` + the composition instance with `X.hom`. Reusable for every product-stability claim against the monoidal product in `Over S`; this is the right pattern any time the "product of varieties is a variety" infrastructure needs to be re-derived for a specific encoding (no need to wait on a future Mathlib generic "product-stability" lemma). **GOTCHA**: `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` does NOT auto-fire under bare `infer_instance` ‚ÄĒ it needs `exact GeometricallyIrreducible.comp _ _` explicitly (`projGm_geomIrred` cost a redundant first attempt at iter-167). Other properties (LOFT, IsAffineHom) do auto-fire.

- **`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (X_ne_zero _))` ‚ÄĒ "localization of a polynomial-ring domain at the powers of a single variable is a domain"** *(NEW iter-167 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gmRing_isDomain` axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, verified.)*
  When the project encoding needs `IsDomain (Localization.Away t)` for `t = X i : MvPolynomial _ kŐĄ` (or any other ring with `noZeroDivisors` + `X_ne_zero`-style witness), the one-line recipe is:
  ```
  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
  ```
  (after `unfold` of the project alias if needed). Chains `Submonoid.powers t ‚Č§ nonZeroDivisors` (from `noZeroDivisors` of the base ring + `t ‚Č† 0`) + Mathlib's "localization at a submonoid in nonZeroDivisors preserves IsDomain". Reusable for any `Localization.Away`-of-domain claim where `t` is a single non-zero element of a `noZeroDivisors` base. Downstream usage: `gm_irreducibleSpace` chains through `PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`, giving `Spec` of a domain is irreducible.

- **Analogist "FREE 2-3 LOC" verdicts for `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` bridges hide an entire witness-construction layer** *(NEW iter-167 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-167 `ga_grpObj` attempted close. Augments the iter-165 "FREE-from-Mathlib hides a one-step ring-bijectivity gap" pattern.)*
  When a mathlib-analogist consult reports a `GrpObj` instance is "FREE 2-3 LOC" via some Mathlib hook (e.g. `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` for `Ga`, `IsLocalization.Away.lift` for `Gm`), the cited hook covers ONLY the **bijection layer** of the `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` witness. The actual closure also requires: (i) an explicit additive functor `F : (Over S)ŠĶíŠĶĖ ‚•§ AddGrpCat` (or `GrpCat`) with `obj T = AddGrpCat.of (őď(T.left, ‚ä§))` and the morphism action via `Units.map` / `appTop`-comap; (ii) a `(F ‚čô forget _).RepresentableBy G` derivation from the cited hook; (iii) `homEquiv_comp` naturality. **Realistic effort**: 50-100 LOC for the `Ga` case via `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`; 80-150 LOC for the `Gm` case via `IsLocalization.Away.lift` (no `homOverEquiv` shortcut). **Detection tell**: the analogist's claim names ONE Mathlib lemma but the project's `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` expects a full functor + RepresentableBy + naturality bundle. When in doubt, ask the analogist a follow-up scoped to a concrete `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`-witness construction sketch BEFORE budgeting a single-iter close.

- **In-context `infer_instance` for `IsDominant (... ‚Čę f).left` where `f := sorry` fails even though standalone `lean_run_code` smoke tests succeed (backward-defeq quirk)** *(NEW iter-167 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-167 `iotaGm_isDominant` attempted close; do-not-retry pattern.)*
  When a downstream instance synthesis `infer_instance` chain like `GeometricallyIrreducible ‚Üí Surjective ‚Üí IsDominant` (priority-100 instance L226 in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.UnderlyingMap`) is supposed to resolve `IsDominant (lift _ _ ‚Čę f).left` for a morphism `f` whose body is still `sorry`, the synthesis can succeed in a standalone `lean_run_code` smoke test but fail in the actual file context (the in-context synthesis order differs ‚ÄĒ likely a backward-defeq quirk where `f := sorry` doesn't reduce to an obvious composition for the instance resolver). **Do-not-retry rule**: do NOT spin on `infer_instance` variants in this configuration; the discriminator is whether `f` has a concrete body. The correct pattern is to **hoist the `IsDominant` claim to a NAMED top-level `sorry` lemma** with a docstring that explicitly gates it on the eventual concrete `f` body ‚ÄĒ propagation footing is the same, but the helper that consumes the dominance hypothesis stays `sorry`-free. iter-167 instance: `iotaGm_isDominant` (AVR L931‚ÄďL934) gates on Lane A's `gmScalingP1` body. Once `f` lands, the dominance closes via `IsOpenImmersion ‚Üí IsDominant` on the standard `Gm = Spec kŐĄ[t, t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ] ‚Ü™ ‚Ąô¬Ļ` form.

- **The "single project-side helper unlocks N scaffold-sorry exports" pattern: `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` is the cheapest iter-168 lever** *(NEW iter-167 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by iter-167's Lane A discharge analysis; not yet built but identified as the rate-limiting infrastructure.)*
  When multiple parallel scaffold-sorry exports all cite the SAME missing ring-iso bridge (iter-167 case: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover` needs `IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away ūĚíú X_i)` for `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`, AND `projGm_isReduced`'s chart-local branch needs the same iso, AND `gmScalingP1`'s 5-step chartwise glue needs the same iso for step (3)), the right iter-168 lever is to **build the single helper FIRST**, then the multiple downstream scaffold closures fall out. iter-167's identified bridge: `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso : HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i) ‚ČÉ+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` (the "degree-0 of localization at `X_i` = polynomial in `X_{1-i}/X_i`" iso, ~30 LOC). **Three downstream consumers** (the 2 scaffold sorries + 1 sub-step of the deferred body) drop out once it lands. Reusable detection rule: when iterating scaffold-sorry exports, search for **shared missing bridge** citations in docstrings ‚ÄĒ if 2+ scaffold sorries cite the same missing helper, that helper is the next-iter lever, not any of the individual exports.

- **`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` + `Over.homMk` kŐĄ-point constructor (`pointOfVec`) ‚ÄĒ explicit-evaluation-vector recipe for a `kŐĄ`-point of `Proj ūĚíú`** *(NEW iter-166 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `ProjectiveLineBar.{zeroPt, onePt, inftyPt}`, PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, kernel-only, no `sorryAx`, verified this review; both `lean-auditor-iter166` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-g0bo-iter166` 0 must-fix and unit-coordinate convention `[0:1]`/`[1:1]`/`[1:0]` verified consistent with the chapter `x = X‚āÄ/X‚āĀ` description.)*
  To build a `kŐĄ`-rational point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar` (or any `Proj` of a graded
  polynomial ring) from an explicit evaluation vector `v : Fin n ‚Üí kbar` with a `IsUnit (v i)`
  certificate at one coordinate: package the construction into a SHARED helper
  `private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec` that does the four steps below
  uniformly. **(1)** Evaluation ring map: `private noncomputable def evalIntoGlobal (v) :
  MvPolynomial (Fin n) kbar ‚Üí+* őď(Spec kŐĄ, ‚ä§)` via `(Scheme.őďSpecIso _).inv.hom.comp
  (MvPolynomial.eval v)`. **(2)** Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top lemma:
  `private lemma irrelevant_map_eq_top (hi : IsUnit (v i))` using
  `HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)`
  + `Ideal.mem_map_of_mem` + `Ideal.eq_top_iff_one` + the unit-mul-left trick
  `(u‚ĀĽ¬Ļ : őď(Spec kŐĄ, ‚ä§)ň£) * u = 1` for `IsUnit.map (evalIntoGlobal v) hi`. **(3)** The
  `pointOfVec` proper: `Over.homMk (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
  (evalIntoGlobal kbar v) (irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| <section condition>`. **(4)**
  The section condition proof (`fromOfGlobalSections ‚Čę ProjectiveLineBar.hom = ūĚüô`) chases:
  `change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ‚Čę Proj.toSpecZero _ ‚Čę Spec.map _ = _`;
  `rw [‚Üź Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc, ‚Üź Spec.map_comp,
  ‚Üź CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc, ‚Üź IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar,
  MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]` to fold the two algebra maps into `MvPolynomial.C`; then prove
  the CommRingCat-level equation `CommRingCat.ofHom ((evalIntoGlobal v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
  (Scheme.őďSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv` via `CommRingCat.hom_ext + ext r + change + rw
  [MvPolynomial.eval_C]`; finish `calc _ = (Spec _).toSpecőď ‚Čę Spec.map (őďSpecIso _).inv := by
  exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc); _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecőď_SpecMap_őďSpecIso_inv _`.
  **DEAD-END trap (cost an edit this iter):** `rw [heq]` where `heq : (f).comp C = ...` fails
  to match identical-looking patterns in the goal ‚ÄĒ workaround as above (CommRingCat-level
  equation + `congrArg Spec.map hcc`, NOT a direct `rw`). The three iter-166 ‚Ąô¬Ļ-points then
  drop straight out by re-instantiation: `zeroPt := pointOfVec (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else
  1) 1 (by simp)` (= `[0:1]`); `onePt := pointOfVec (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)` (= `[1:1]`);
  `inftyPt := pointOfVec (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)` (= `[1:0]`). Reusable
  for any "explicit `kŐĄ`-rational point on `Proj ūĚíú` from an evaluation vector with a unit
  coordinate" construction.

- **Iso-transport of a rigidity headline through a curve-iso `C ‚ČÖ ‚Ąô¬Ļ` ‚ÄĒ `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`** *(NEW iter-166 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ body landed iter-166 via the recipe below; `lean_verify` axioms `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` ‚ÄĒ `sorryAx` propagates honestly through `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (RR bridge) + `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`'s 5 helper sorries; both review subagents confirm no laundering.)*
  The genus-`0` rigidity headline `f = toUnit C ‚Čę ő∑[A]` lands cleanly from
  `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (`f : ProjectiveLineBar ‚ü∂ A` is constant) via a 4-line
  iso-transport recipe. **Recipe:** `obtain ‚ü®ŌÜ‚ü© := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus`; define
  `g := ŌÜ.inv ‚Čę f : ProjectiveLineBar ‚ü∂ A`; `obtain ‚ü®a‚āÄ, hga‚āÄ‚ü© :=
  morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g`; pin `a‚āÄ = ő∑[A]` by transporting the basepoint `p` along
  `ŌÜ.hom` (build `hpoint : (p ‚Čę ŌÜ.hom) ‚Čę g = ő∑[A]` from the original `_hf : p ‚Čę f = ő∑[A]`
  via `Category.assoc + Iso.hom_inv_id`-style algebra; then `toUnit_unique
  (p ‚Čę ŌÜ.hom ‚Čę toUnit ‚Ąô¬Ļ) (ūĚüô _)` collapses `(p ‚Čę ŌÜ.hom) ‚Čę toUnit ‚Ąô¬Ļ ‚Čę a‚āÄ` to `a‚āÄ`); back-transport
  via `ŌÜ.hom_inv_id` to land `f = ŌÜ.hom ‚Čę g = toUnit C ‚Čę ő∑[A]` (use `toUnit_unique` once more to
  align `ŌÜ.hom ‚Čę toUnit ‚Ąô¬Ļ` with `toUnit C`). Reusable for any "headline-on-target factors
  through iso-substituted source" pattern; the key is that the basepoint hypothesis transports
  along `ŌÜ.hom` cleanly because `toUnit` is unique (anything into `ūĚüô_` is the canonical map).

- **`set őĻ := ‚Ä¶` traps on `őĻ` as a near-reserved token** *(NEW iter-166 ‚ÄĒ recorded inside
  `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`'s prover attempts; trap log preserved in
  `.archon/task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean.md`.)*
  `set őĻ : T := ‚Ä¶` produces `unexpected token 'őĻ'; expected '_' or identifier` in Lean 4 ‚ÄĒ
  `őĻ` is parsed as a token before being treated as an identifier in this context. Rename to a
  regular ASCII identifier (the iter-166 prover landed on `iotaGm`). Trivial trap but blocks
  the build immediately when first encountered.

- **`IsSeparated A.hom` from `[IsProper A.hom]` only ‚ÄĒ `IsProper.toIsSeparated` explicit derivation** *(NEW iter-166 ‚ÄĒ recorded inside `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`; located via `lean_leansearch "IsProper implies IsSeparated"`.)*
  When the chain ahead of you needs `IsSeparated A.hom` but only `[IsProper A.hom]` is in
  scope, bare `‚ÄĻIsSeparated A.hom‚Äļ` FAILS (instance synthesis does not chase
  `IsProper.toIsSeparated` automatically when the goal is `IsSeparated _` and only `IsProper _`
  is in scope). The idiom: `haveI hAsep : IsSeparated A.hom := IsProper.toIsSeparated`. Then
  the downstream `IsSeparated (A.left ‚Üė Spec (.of kbar)) := hAsep` works via `Over` plumbing.
  Reusable for any chain that pulls separatedness out of properness on a target abelian
  variety / proper scheme.

- **Inline `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` when the `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` wrapper is downstream ‚ÄĒ recurring pattern** *(NEW iter-166 trap log addendum ‚ÄĒ `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` had to inline the dominant-globalisation argument; the wrapper `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` lives in `AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity`, which transitively imports any file under `Jacobian.lean`, creating an import cycle when imported into a file upstream of `Jacobian`. Same pattern recurred in iter-158 for `snd_left_isClosedMap`.)*
  When a new upstream file needs the dominant-globalisation argument (`f = g` on schemes given
  agreement on a dense open + separatedness of the target), DON'T `import
  AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity` ‚ÄĒ that file is downstream. Instead replicate `rigidity_core`'s
  inline pattern: locate `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` via
  `lean_leansearch "IsProper implies IsSeparated"` or `lean_hover_info`, then call it directly
  with the `S := Spec (.of kbar)` over-class instance + the dominance + separatedness
  hypotheses. The chain ends `refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_; apply
  ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar)) (X := ‚Ä¶) (Y := A.left) (f := f.left)
  (g := ‚Ä¶) iotaGm.left; ‚Ä¶`. Reusable for any upstream file in the dependency graph of
  `Jacobian.lean`.

- **`Proj ūĚíú` of `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule ŌÉ k` is proper over `Spec k`, via the algebra-map composite ‚ÄĒ `projectiveLineBar_isProper`** *(NEW iter-165 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, re-verified this review by `#print axioms`; both review subagents 0 must-fix.)*
  When `ProjectiveLineBar` (or any `Proj`-of-graded-polynomial scheme) carries `Over (Spec k)` via the COMPOSITION `Proj.toSpecZero ūĚíú ‚Čę Spec.map (algebraMap k ‚Ü•(ūĚíú 0))` (because Mathlib's canonical `Proj.toSpecZero` lands in `Spec ‚Ü•(ūĚíú 0)`, not `Spec k`), the analogist's "FREE from `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero‚Ä¶`" verdict is misleading ‚ÄĒ the composite's properness needs ONE substantive step: prove `algebraMap k ‚Üí ‚Ü•(ūĚíú 0)` bijective, lift to `IsIso (Spec.map _)`, then `infer_instance` on the composite proper-then-iso. **Recipe (axiom-clean, ~25 LOC):** (1) `change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero ūĚíú ‚Čę Spec.map ‚Ä¶)` exposes the composite; (2) install `IsScalarTower k ‚Ü•(ūĚíú 0) (MvPolynomial _ k) := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl` (defeq, no content); (3) install `Algebra.FiniteType ‚Ü•(ūĚíú 0) (MvPolynomial _ k) := Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType k _ _` (descends from finite-type-over-`k`); (4) **prove bijectivity** of `algebraMap k ‚Üí ‚Ü•(ūĚíú 0)` ‚ÄĒ injection via `MvPolynomial.C_injective` + `congrArg Subtype.val`; surjection witness `MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v` for `‚ü®v, hv‚ü©`, using `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)` (degree-`0` component of a homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial = itself) combined with `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero` (degree-`0` component = `C ‚ąė coeff 0`). **DEAD-END (cost ~5 min):** the simp resolver for the bijectivity surjection step is `simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true]` ‚ÄĒ `reduceIte` (more aggressive normalizer) FAILS on the bare `if 0 = 0`. (5) `haveI : IsIso (Spec.map _) := (isIso_SpecMap_iff).mpr hbij`; (6) `infer_instance` closes via Mathlib's `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero‚Ä¶` + composition-with-iso. Reusable for any "`Proj` of a graded ring is proper over the base field" obligation where the project's encoding goes through an algebraMap composition rather than Mathlib's canonical `Proj.toSpecZero` directly.

- **`HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` as the morphism-property ‚ÜĒ ring-hom-property bridge for `Spec.map`-shaped morphisms** *(NEW iter-165 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation`, axiom-clean, verified.)*
  For a `MorphismProperty` `P` of schemes that comes from a `HasRingHomProperty P Q` instance (Mathlib's `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.RingHomProperties`), `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`/`Smooth`/`IsAffine`/etc. on `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A B))` reduce to `Q (algebraMap A B)`. **Recipe:** `(HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := <algebra-target>)).mpr inferInstance)`. The `inferInstance` lands on `Algebra.FinitePresentation k <algebra>` (Mathlib FREE for e.g. `Localization.Away` of a polynomial ring). **GOTCHA:** the bridge needs the `(P := @<Property>)` explicit argument ‚ÄĒ `rw [HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff]` without it fails with "metavariable in pattern". Reusable for any "scheme-property on `Spec.map _` reduces to ring-property" step.

- **`isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine n S).hom` ‚ÄĒ transport `IsReduced` to `AffineSpace n S`** *(NEW iter-165 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `ga_isReduced`, axiom-clean.)*
  Bare `inferInstance` on `IsReduced (AffineSpace n S)` does NOT fire even when the underlying `Spec _` is reduced (Mathlib does not synthesise the iso-transfer). The idiom: `AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine n S : AffineSpace n S ‚ČÖ Spec (.of (MvPolynomial n őď(S, ‚ä§)))` and the iso's `.hom` is open-immersion (any iso is). `isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion` transports `IsReduced` back across the iso (`IsReduced` is local on the target of an open immersion). Spec of MvPolynomial-over-a-domain is reduced (FREE ‚ÄĒ domain ‚üĻ reduced ‚üĻ Spec of reduced ring is reduced). Reusable for any property `P (AffineSpace n S)` where Mathlib provides only `P (Spec _)` and the iso-as-open-immersion direction transports `P`.

- **Analogist "FREE-from-Mathlib" verdicts can hide a one-step ring-bijectivity gap when the project's encoding composes the Mathlib decl with `Spec.map`** *(NEW iter-165 ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-165 `projectiveLineBar_isProper` close; the analogist's `gm-scaling-p1` report listed it FREE.)*
  When a `mathlib-analogist` consult reports an instance `P f` is "FREE from `<MathlibName>`", check whether the project's actually-wired-up morphism is `f` itself or a composition `f ‚Čę Spec.map (algebraMap _ _)` (a common pattern when bridging Mathlib's canonical `_.toSpecZero` to the project's `Over (Spec k)` orientation). If composed, the composition introduces a possibly-non-trivial obligation: prove the algebraMap bijective ‚üĻ lift to `IsIso (Spec.map _)` ‚üĻ `infer_instance` on the composite. **Detection tell:** `inferInstance` directly on the project's `_.hom` fails despite the analogist's "FREE" claim ‚ÄĒ that signals a hidden composition step. **Recipe:** `change` to expose the composite, then run the bijectivity-of-algebraMap chain (see the projectiveLineBar_isProper recipe above). Reusable for every analogist consult that bridges between Mathlib's canonical scheme constructions and the project's `Over S`-oriented encoding.

- **Scaffold-`sorry` handoff to a downstream lane: STATEMENT must match the consumer's `_hf` shape verbatim ‚ÄĒ `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`** *(NEW iter-165 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ both review subagents confirm the statement matches the rigidity consumer's load-bearing antecedent.)*
  When the prover lane is depth-conversion (the iter-165 case: scaffolds the new objects, defers proofs to iter-NNN+1), the *statement* of the load-bearing scaffold companion lemma must match the downstream consumer's hypothesis shape exactly, so iter-NNN+1's consumer can apply the proven Cor 1.5 (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) with no impedance mismatch. **iter-165 instance:** `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` states `lift (toUnit (Gm) ‚Čę ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt) (ūĚüô (Gm)) ‚Čę gmScalingP1 = toUnit (Gm) ‚Čę ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt` ‚ÄĒ the exact `lift _ _ ‚Čę h = toUnit _ ‚Čę _` shape `rigidity_lemma` consumes as `_hf` (with `V = ProjectiveLineBar`, `W = Gm`, base points `zeroPt`, `Gm.onePt`). The PRover for the chartwise glue (iter-166) then closes the body knowing the statement is already-validated by the lemma's role in the rigidity chain. Reusable for every "scaffold the type signature now, prove the body next iter" pattern in a multi-iter arc ‚ÄĒ the type signature MUST be the consumer-validated one, not just "morally right".

- **The `GrpObj`-induced hom-group as the working tool for Milne ¬ßI.1 additivity (Cor 1.5 / Cor 1.2) ‚ÄĒ `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero`** *(NEW iter-163 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ both PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, no `sorryAx`, re-verified this review; both review subagents 0 must-fix, every value-hypothesis load-bearing, no laundering.)*
  For `[GrpObj A]` in a cartesian-monoidal category, Mathlib (`CategoryTheory.MonObj.Hom.group`/`Hom.monoid`, `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Cartesian/Grp_.lean`) gives a `Group` on each hom-set `Hom(X, A)`: **`u * v = lift u v ‚Čę őľ`** (`Hom.mul_def`), **`u‚ĀĽ¬Ļ = u ‚Čę őĻ`**, **`(1 : X ‚ü∂ A) = toUnit X ‚Čę ő∑`** (`Hom.one_def`). Manipulate composites with: `MonObj.comp_mul` (`f ‚Čę (u*v) = (f‚Čęu)*(f‚Čęv)`), `GrpObj.comp_div`, `MonObj.comp_one`, `comp_lift`, `lift_fst_comp_snd_comp` (`lift (fst‚ČęőĪ)(snd‚ČęőĪ) = őĪ ‚äó‚āė őĪ`), and the ordinary group lemmas `div_eq_one`, `div_self'`. **Cor 1.5 recipe:** form `ŌÜ := h / ((p‚Čęf)¬∑(q‚Čęg))`; show `ŌÜ` collapses the complete `V`-axis to `1` (= the `_hf` collapse hyp of `rigidity_lemma`, using `h(v‚āÄ,w‚āÄ)=ő∑[A]`); `rigidity_lemma ŌÜ` factors `ŌÜ = q‚Čęg'`; read `g'=1` off the `{v‚āÄ}√óW` section; `div_eq_one.mp`. **Cor 1.2 recipe:** apply Cor 1.5 to `h := őľ[A]‚ČęőĪ`, `V=W=A`, based at `ő∑[A]`; axis-restrictions reduce to `őĪ` via the monoid unit laws `lift_comp_one_right`/`lift_comp_one_left`; package the result as **`IsMonHom őĪ`** (`one_hom` = the pointed hyp, `mul_hom` from the decomposition + `Hom.mul_def` + `lift_fst_comp_snd_comp`) ‚ÄĒ strictly more reusable than a bare elementwise equation. **DEAD-END trap (cost an edit this iter):** under `open MonObj`, the names **`mul_one`/`one_mul` are AMBIGUOUS** and resolve to the *monoid-object* lemmas `MonObj.mul_one : ő∑ ‚Ė∑ X ‚Čę őľ = ‚Ä¶`, not the ordinary `Group` identities ‚ÄĒ error `rewrite failed: Did not find an occurrence of the pattern ?X ‚óĀ ő∑ ‚Čę őľ in f / (f * 1) = 1`. **FIX:** write `_root_.mul_one` / `_root_.one_mul`. **Product-instance gap:** Cor 1.5/1.2 need `[GeometricallyIrreducible (V‚äóW).hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType (V‚äóW).hom]`, `[IsReduced (V‚äóW).left]` ("V√óW is a variety") ‚ÄĒ true for genuine (abelian) varieties but Mathlib does NOT auto-derive product instances from the factors, so carry them as explicit instance hyps (a future "product of varieties is a variety" instance would let them be dropped). This is the reusable handle for the upcoming Prop 3.9 additive-defect-map argument `Ōą(x,y)=f(x+y)‚ąíf(x)‚ąíf(y)`.

- **Slice-section / retract identities over a monoidal `Over (Spec kŐĄ)` base: do the algebra in `Over`, NOT scheme-level `pullback.hom_ext` ‚ÄĒ `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine` (the Rigidity-Lemma chain's lone deep residual, CLOSED iter-162)** *(NEW iter-162 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ both review subagents 0 must-fix; `lean_verify` on `rigidity_lemma` / the new helper / Step 1 all `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, no `sorryAx`, re-verified this review. The whole chain `rigidity_lemma ‚Ä¶ rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine` is now sorry-free + axiom-clean.)*
  To prove slice identities like `q = aL ‚Čę sL` for a `kŐĄ`-point `q : Spec kŐĄ ‚ü∂ (X‚äóY).left` of a monoidal product: **DEAD END (do NOT repeat)** ‚ÄĒ `pullback.hom_ext` on `(X‚äóY).left` with `Category.assoc`/`simp [Category.assoc]` CANNOT match `(aL ‚Čę sL) ‚Čę pullback.fst` because the composite's middle object is syntactically `(X‚äóY).left` while `pullback.fst`'s domain is `pullback X.hom Y.hom` (defeq but keyed-matching fails); `clear_value` does not help. **FIX:** lift `q` to `qhat := Over.homMk q hqsec : ūĚüô_ ‚ü∂ X‚äóY` (works because `(ūĚüô_).left` defeq `Spec kŐĄ`, `(ūĚüô_).hom` defeq `ūĚüô`), then do ALL slice algebra in `Over (Spec kŐĄ)`: `xq := qhat ‚Čę fst X Y`, `yhat := qhat ‚Čę snd X Y`, `sec := lift (ūĚüô X) (toUnit X ‚Čę yhat)`. Prove `qhat = xq ‚Čę sec` and `qhat ‚Čę retract = x‚āÄ ‚Čę sec` by **`CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext`** (checks after `fst`/`snd`) + `lift_fst`/`lift_snd` + **`toUnit_unique (‚Ä¶ ‚Čę toUnit _) (ūĚüô _)`** for the `_ ‚Čę toUnit = ūĚüô` collapse ‚ÄĒ clean, no pullback-object mismatch. Translate back to the `.left` goal via `simpa only [Over.comp_left, hqhatL] using congrArg Over.Hom.left h‚Ä¶`. The slice‚Üíaffine step is then `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral` (the iter-161 deep-algebra helper) with `IsIntegral X.left` supplied by `isIntegral_of_retract` (below) and `[UniversallyClosed]`/`[LocallyOfFiniteType] X.hom` FREE from `[IsProper X.hom]`. **Tooling traps:** (a) `rw [‚Ä¶ , last_lemma]` can leave a syntactically-refl goal UNSOLVED (defeq-not-syntactic middle object) ‚ÄĒ end with `exact congrArg (¬∑ ‚Čę g) h` instead; (b) a big `calc` failed `Trans Eq Eq ?m` synthesis ‚ÄĒ replace by an explicit `.trans` chain.

- **Integrality descends to a retract ‚ÄĒ `isIntegral_of_retract` (NEW reusable helper iter-162, axiom-clean)** *(both review subagents confirm sound + every hypothesis load-bearing.)*
  `{S T : Scheme} [IsIntegral T] (r : S ‚ü∂ T) (pr : T ‚ü∂ S) (hrp : r ‚Čę pr = ūĚüô S) : IsIntegral S`. Stated generically (any retract), more general than the `X`/`X√óY`/`p‚āĀ` instance it was built for. **Irreducible:** `pr.base` is surjective (section `r.base`), so `(IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ T).image pr.base ‚Ä¶ ` + `hsurj.range_eq` ‚üĻ `irreducibleSpace_def`. **Reduced (PER-STALK, not global sections):** `(r‚Čępr).stalkMap x` is an iso (`Scheme.Hom.stalkMap_congr_hom (r‚Čępr) (ūĚüô S) hrp x` + `stalkMap_id`; the residual `IsIso (stalkCongr.hom ‚Čę ūĚüô)` discharged by **`exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (‚Ä¶ ‚Čę ūĚüô _))`** ‚ÄĒ bare `infer_instance`/`Category.comp_id` both FAIL, the `inferInstanceAs` shape-hint is the fix); `stalkMap_comp` ‚üĻ `pr.stalkMap (r x)` injective; `isReduced_of_injective` into the reduced `T`-stalk. **KEY pin:** `isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk` takes stalk-reducedness as an INSTANCE arg `[‚ąÄ x, IsReduced (stalk x)]` ‚ÄĒ build `haveI hstalk : ‚ąÄ x, ‚Ä¶` FIRST, do NOT `apply ‚Ä¶ ; intro x`.

- **"A global regular function on a proper integral `kŐĄ`-variety is constant", cohomology-free ‚ÄĒ `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral`** *(NEW iter-161 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, re-verified this review via `lean_verify`; both review subagents confirm sound + every hypothesis load-bearing.)*
  For `[IsAlgClosed kbar]`, `W` integral with `wk : W ‚ü∂ Spec (.of kbar)` `[UniversallyClosed wk] [LocallyOfFiniteType wk]`, `g : W ‚ü∂ V` into an affine `V`, and two `kŐĄ`-points (sections `a b` of `wk`, `a ‚Čę wk = ūĚüô`, `b ‚Čę wk = ūĚüô`): `a ‚Čę g = b ‚Čę g`. **Recipe:** `őď(W,‚ä§)` is a field (`isField_of_universallyClosed`); `wk.appTop` is integral (`isIntegral_appTop_of_universallyClosed`), hence ‚ÄĒ `kŐĄ` alg-closed + `őď(W)` a domain ‚ÄĒ **bijective** (`IsAlgClosed.ringHom_bijective_of_isIntegral`), so `F := (őďSpecIso).inv ‚Čę wk.appTop` is `IsIso`, thus `wk.appTop` is iso; both sections left-invert `wk.appTop` on `őď` (`rw [‚Üź Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ha]; simp`), so `a.appTop = b.appTop` (`rw [‚Üź cancel_epi wk.appTop, ‚Ä¶]`); finish `apply ext_of_isAffine`. **Load-bearing (drop-each-fails, auditor-confirmed):** `IsAlgClosed` (collapses the finite extension), `IsIntegral` (else `őď` not a field ‚ÄĒ 2-point ctrex), `UniversallyClosed` (else `őď` not a field ‚ÄĒ `ūĚĒł¬Ļ`), `LocallyOfFiniteType` (finiteness of `őď` over `kŐĄ`), `IsAffine V` (else `ext_of_isAffine` does not pin). The route-B realisation that AVOIDS the relative-Stein / `f_*ūĚí™=ūĚí™` Mathlib gap. Reusable for any "two rational points of a complete variety into an affine agree" step.

- **Closed point of a locally-of-finite-type `kŐĄ`-scheme ‚áí `kŐĄ`-rational point; reduce a residue-field-probe goal to a `kŐĄ`-point goal** *(NEW iter-161 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ inside `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`; reduction confirmed sound by both subagents.)*
  `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AlgClosed/Basic.lean` (Andrew Yang, 2026) packages this: `pointOfClosedPoint wk x hxc : Spec (.of K) ‚ü∂ X` (the `kŐĄ`-point at a closed `x`), `residueFieldIsoBase`, `pointOfClosedPoint_comp` (`pointOfClosedPoint ‚Ä¶ ‚Čę wk = ūĚüô` ‚ÄĒ it is a section), `ext_of_apply_eq`. Available via `import Mathlib`. **Recipe to reduce a goal `U.fromSpecResidueField x ‚Čę ŌÜ = U.fromSpecResidueField x ‚Čę Ōą`:** `set wU := U.őĻ ‚Čę (X‚äóY).hom`; `set px := pointOfClosedPoint wU x hxc`; `rw [‚Üź cancel_epi (Spec.map (residueFieldIsoBase wU x hxc).hom)]` (cancels a genuine iso/epi on the left); `suffices h : px ‚Čę ŌÜ = px ‚Čę Ōą by rw [hpx] at h; simpa only [pointOfClosedPoint, Category.assoc] using h`. Establish `px ‚Čę U.őĻ` is a section via `Category.assoc` + `pointOfClosedPoint_comp`. Reusable to turn any "agree at a closed point after residue-field probe" into the cleaner "agree at the `kŐĄ`-rational point".

- **`JacobsonSpace` of an open subscheme of a locally-of-finite-type `kŐĄ`-scheme** *(NEW iter-161 ‚ÄĒ lane (a) in `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`, axiom-clean.)*
  `haveI : JacobsonSpace (X‚äóY).left := LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace (X‚äóY).hom; exact JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding U.őĻ.isOpenEmbedding`. The `JacobsonSpace (Spec kŐĄ)` premise resolves automatically (field ‚üĻ `IsArtinianRing` ‚üĻ `IsJacobsonRing` ‚üĻ `JacobsonSpace (Spec R)` instance). Supplies the dense-closed-points input that `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints` (the iter-160 globaliser) demands. **Tooling gotcha (iter-161):** `lean_local_search` interpolates the raw query into a ripgrep regex unescaped ‚ÄĒ a multi-word query with a space (`"LocallyOfFiniteType jacobsonSpace"`) errors as a regex parse failure; quote/escape or use single-token queries.

- **Dense-closed-points hom-extensionality: `g‚āĀ = g‚āā` from agreement at every closed point, via a coproduct-of-residue-field-Specs dominant probe** *(NEW iter-160 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`, PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, verified this review; both review subagents confirm sound + standard.)*
  For `{W Z : Scheme} [IsReduced W] [JacobsonSpace W] [Z.IsSeparated]` and `g‚āĀ g‚āā : W ‚ü∂ Z`: if `‚ąÄ x ‚ąą closedPoints W, W.fromSpecResidueField x ‚Čę g‚āĀ = W.fromSpecResidueField x ‚Čę g‚āā` then `g‚āĀ = g‚āā`. This is the connective the iter-159 analogist flagged that Mathlib does NOT package (it ships only the single-morphism `ext_of_isDominant`). **Recipe:** `let F : closedPoints W ‚Üí Scheme := fun x => Spec (W.residueField x.1)`; `let probe : (‚ąź F) ‚ü∂ W := Sigma.desc fun x => W.fromSpecResidueField x.1`; prove `IsDominant probe` via `‚ü®(dense_iff_closure_eq.mpr (closure_closedPoints (X := W))).mono ?_‚ü©` (range ‚äá closed points: for `x ‚ąą closedPoints`, `(Sigma.őĻ F ‚ü®x,hx‚ü©).base pt` maps to `x` since `Sigma.őĻ_desc` rewrites `probe` to `fromSpecResidueField x` whose range is `{x}` ‚ÄĒ `Scheme.range_fromSpecResidueField x = {x}`); finish `refine ext_of_isDominant probe (Sigma.hom_ext _ _ fun x => ?_); rw [‚Üź Category.assoc, ‚Üź Category.assoc, Sigma.őĻ_desc]; exact h x.1 x.2`. **Key API:** `IsDominant f ‚ÜĒ DenseRange f.base` (`‚ü®_‚ü©`/`.denseRange`); `closure_closedPoints : closure (closedPoints X) = univ` needs `[JacobsonSpace X]`; `Type u`-indexed scheme coproducts exist (`HasCoproduct` synthesizes). Reusable for any "morphisms agreeing at dense closed points are equal" obligation; the `[JacobsonSpace W]` is the load-bearing density input (over a field comes from `[LocallyOfFiniteType]`).

- **Absolute separatedness `Z.left.IsSeparated` from a relative separated map `[IsSeparated Z.hom]` over an affine base `Spec kŐĄ`** *(NEW iter-160 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ inside `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`, verified.)*
  `haveI : Z.left.IsSeparated := by rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]; have heq : terminal.from Z.left = Z.hom ‚Čę terminal.from (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := terminal.hom_ext _ _; rw [heq]; infer_instance`. The composite of `Z.hom` (separated by hypothesis) and `terminal.from (Spec kŐĄ)` (separated since `Spec kŐĄ` is affine) is separated; `Scheme.isSeparated_iff` reduces the absolute property to separatedness of the terminal map, and `terminal.hom_ext` rewrites it as the composite. Reusable for transporting any "relative property over an affine base ‚üĻ absolute property" step.

- **LESSON ‚ÄĒ a clean chain signature can hide a hypothesis that only a specific proof route demands; surfacing it is a signature gap to ESCALATE, not a sorry to leave** *(NEW iter-160 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine` route-B globalisation; both review subagents land must-fix even though the gap is honestly documented.)*
  The route-B globalisation (`morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints`) forces `[JacobsonSpace U]`, which over a field needs `(X‚äóY)` locally of finite type ‚ÄĒ a hypothesis the chain signature (`[IsAlgClosed]`, `[IsProper X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible (X‚äóY).hom]`, `[IsReduced (X‚äóY).left]`, `[IsSeparated Z.hom]`) does not carry and cannot recover (`Y` arbitrary `Over (Spec kŐĄ)`). The prover correctly (a) PROVED the route-independent piece axiom-clean, (b) isolated the gap as a documented in-body `:= sorry` + a named Step-1 stub, (c) loudly flagged the exact fix. **This is the OPPOSITE of iter-157 laundering** (sorries visible, `sorryAx` propagates honestly, gap documented) ‚ÄĒ yet still must-fix, because a `:= sorry` propping up an as-typed-unprovable statement lets a clean signature mask an under-hypothesized claim. **The right resolution is a plan-authorized signature change** (add `[LocallyOfFiniteType (X‚äóY).hom]` across the off-limits chain lemmas ‚ÄĒ free downstream since curves/AVs are finite type) **+ a blueprint hypothesis amendment**, NOT another prover round at the current signature. Detection tell: a proof needs an instance `inferInstance` cannot synthesize from the stated hypotheses, AND the instance is not derivable ‚ÄĒ that is a missing antecedent, not a missing lemma.

- **Pullback-fibre over a `kŐĄ`-point via `IsPullback` pasting + the coarse `PullbackCarrier` range lemma ‚ÄĒ `range(slice section) = p‚āā‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(range pt)`** *(NEW iter-159 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `hfib` inside `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`, closed axiom-clean; verified this review.)*
  To prove a slice section `s : X.left ‚ü∂ (X‚äóY).left` (here `s = (lift (ūĚüô X) (toUnit X ‚Čę y‚āÄ)).left`, the map `x ‚Ü¶ (x, y‚āÄ)`) realizes `X` as the fibre of the projection `p‚āā = pullback.snd X.hom Y.hom` over a `kŐĄ`-point `y‚āÄ`: **(1)** build the triangle identities `s ‚Čę p‚āĀ = ūĚüô` (`rw [‚Üź Over.fst_left, ‚Üź Over.comp_left, lift_fst, Over.id_left]`) and `s ‚Čę p‚āā = X.hom ‚Čę y‚āÄ.left`; **(2)** the OUTER square `IsPullback (s ‚Čę p‚āĀ) X.hom X.hom (y‚āÄ.left ‚Čę Y.hom)` is a pullback because both horizontal legs are isos ‚ÄĒ get it from `IsPullback.of_horiz_isIso ‚ü®by simp‚ü©` on the identity square then **rewrite the hypothesis backwards** `rwa [‚Üź hsp1, ‚Üź hsec] at hiso`; **(3)** paste off the canonical right square: `IsPullback.of_right houter hsp2 (IsPullback.of_hasPullback X.hom Y.hom)` recovers `hL : IsPullback s X.hom p‚āā y‚āÄ.left`; **(4)** read the fibre with `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.image_preimage_eq_of_isPullback hL.flip Set.univ` (+ `simpa [Set.image_univ, Set.preimage_univ]`) to get `Set.range s.base = p‚āā.base ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' Set.range y‚āÄ.left.base`. **GOTCHAS (all cost time this iter):** (a) `(toUnit X).left = X.hom` and `y‚āÄ.left ‚Čę Y.hom = ūĚüô _` close by **`by simp`** ‚ÄĒ the explicit `rw [Over.tensorUnit_hom, Category.comp_id]` FAILS (`Category.comp_id` does not match `_ ‚Čę ūĚüô (Spec _)`); (b) `s ‚Čę p‚āā = X.hom ‚Čę y‚āÄ.left` ends with `exact congrArg (¬∑ ‚Čę y‚āÄ.left) htoUnit` ‚ÄĒ a plain `rw [..., htoUnit]` (and `simp only`) leaves a reflexive `a = a` UNSOLVED because the middle object differs syntactically (`(ūĚüô_).left` vs `Spec _`, defeq-not-syntactic); `congrArg` sidesteps it; (c) rewriting under a dependent `IsPullback` goal forward is fragile ("did not find pattern") ‚ÄĒ rewrite the hypothesis backwards instead. **NAMESPACE GOTCHA:** the range lemma is `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.image_preimage_eq_of_isPullback`, NOT `...Scheme.Pullback....` ‚ÄĒ the `Pullback` namespace closes (`PullbackCarrier.lean:376`) BEFORE the lemma (`:414`). Reusable for any "section realizes the fibre of a projection over a rational point" obligation.

- **Extracting a residual `sorry` into a faithfully-stated NAMED top-level helper is GOOD practice (the opposite of laundering) ‚ÄĒ but the helper statement must be TRUE with every hypothesis load-bearing** *(NEW iter-159 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`; both review subagents 0 must-fix.)*
  When a proof reduces to one deep residual obligation, isolating that obligation as a standalone named theorem (precise statement + `sorry` body) makes it explicitly reviewable and is the RIGHT move ‚ÄĒ provided the extraction passes the iter-157 soundness bar. **The validation checklist (apply every time a residual is extracted):** (1) the helper STATEMENT is true-as-stated in the intended generality (here: proper integral slice into an affine ‚üĻ constant in that direction ‚ÄĒ true char-free over alg-closed base); (2) EVERY hypothesis is load-bearing ‚ÄĒ drop each and confirm the conclusion fails (here `_hfU` affine-containment, `_hU‚āÄ` affineness, `_hUV` saturation, `[IsProper]`, `[IsAlgClosed]` are each load-bearing; the helper correctly does NOT carry the upstream collapse hyp `_hf`, which is only needed for non-emptiness, not slice-constancy); (3) the caller discharges all premises with GENUINE (non-trivial) proofs so the helper is applied non-vacuously, not against an unsatisfiable premise set. Contrast with the iter-157 failure (a delegate that DROPPED the conditional hypothesis and was false-as-stated). **Marker-graph consequence (must be paired with a blueprint action):** an extracted helper with a `sorry` body needs its own `\lean{}` block + a `\uses` edge from the consuming proof, ELSE the dependency graph has no edge to the open obligation and the `\leanok`-tagged consumer proofs render as fully proven despite a verified transitive `sorryAx` (a marker-graph laundering vector ‚ÄĒ flagged by `lean-vs-blueprint-checker` this iter; the `\leanok` itself is `sync_leanok`'s call, but the fix is the blueprint block, dispatched to a blueprint-writer).

- **Tooling reliability ‚ÄĒ `mathlib-analogist` recipe citations may carry WRONG namespaces even when stamped "LSP-verified"** *(NEW iter-159 ‚ÄĒ `analogies/rigidity-hfib.md` cited `Scheme.Pullback.image_preimage_eq_of_isPullback`; the real lemma is `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.image_preimage_eq_of_isPullback`.)*
  An analogist consult that claims its cited declarations are "LSP-verified at iter-NNN" is NOT a guarantee of the fully-qualified namespace. Re-verify each cited name (`lean_hover_info` / `lean_run_code` on the FQN) before building a proof around it; a wrong namespace surfaces as a "unknown identifier" / "not found" only at use time. The mathematical content of the recipe was correct this iter ‚ÄĒ only the namespace was off.

- **ANTI-PATTERN ‚ÄĒ laundering a conditional theorem's content into a FALSE unconditional sorry (the "dead hypothesis" tell)** *(NEW iter-157 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ both review subagents `lean-auditor-iter157` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-av-rigidity-iter157` independently caught this on `rigidity_lemma`/`rigidity_core`/`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`.)*
  When a proof of a **conditional** theorem `P ‚Üí Q` (here: `f` collapses a slice `‚üĻ` `f` factors through `snd`) does **not consume its hypothesis `P`** and instead delegates to a helper lemma stating `Q` **unconditionally**, that helper is almost certainly **false**, and the theorem's apparent "proven modulo one sorry" status is **illusory** ‚ÄĒ the residual sorry is unsatisfiable. **Iter-157 instance**: `rigidity_lemma` (Mumford Rigidity Lemma) compiled and reduced to a single `sorry` (`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`), but its proof never used the collapse hypothesis `_hf : f(X√ó{y‚āÄ})={z‚āÄ}`; the delegate lemmas `rigidity_core` (`f = retract ‚Čę f` for arbitrary `f`) and `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` (a non-empty open of agreement, no collapse hyp) are **false** ‚ÄĒ counterexample `f = fst` on `X=Y=Z=‚Ąô¬Ļ` satisfies every stated instance (`IsProper`/`GeometricallyIrreducible`/`IsReduced`/`IsSeparated`) yet `fst ‚Č† (x,y)‚Ü¶x‚āÄ`. **Detection recipe**: (1) spot a load-bearing theorem with a **dead hypothesis** (`_hf`/`y‚āÄ`/`z‚āÄ` unused by the proof ‚ÄĒ `lean_goal` shows them inert; the proof term doesn't reference them); (2) inspect each delegate lemma it routes through for whether it OMITS that hypothesis; (3) **construct a counterexample within the delegate's stated instances** (the projection `fst`/identity `id` are the canonical refutations of "every map is coordinate-independent"). `lean_verify` showing `sorryAx` is NOT enough ‚ÄĒ the axiom check confirms an *open* obligation but does not detect that the obligation is *false*. **Fix**: thread the dropped hypothesis back into the delegates (re-sign), then consume it in the wrapper proof. Reusable for any conditional-theorem formalization where a "geometric heart" is isolated into a helper: verify the isolated helper is still TRUE (carries the conditional data), not just that it type-checks. **RESOLVED iter-158 (both review subagents `lean-auditor-iter158` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-av-rigidity-iter158` independently re-verified):** the fix was executed exactly as prescribed ‚ÄĒ `_hf` re-signed into `rigidity_core` + `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` and threaded through `rigidity_lemma`, then GENUINELY CONSUMED in `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`'s body (`hcomp := congrArg Over.Hom.left _hf` ‚üĻ `hy‚āÄ : y‚āÄpt ‚ąČ Gset` ‚üĻ the non-emptiness witness `s x‚āÄpt`). The `f = fst` counterexample no longer satisfies the antecedent (`_hf` forces `ūĚüô X = toUnit X ‚Čę z‚āÄ`, i.e. `X` collapses to a point). `rigidity_lemma`/`rigidity_core` are now sorry-free in their own bodies; the residual is two TRUE-as-stated sorries inside `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`. **The repair-validation recipe**: after threading, (1) confirm the dropped hyp is referenced by the proof term (not inert), (2) re-run the counterexample against the now-stronger antecedent to confirm it's excluded, (3) confirm `lean_verify` shows only `sorryAx` with NO custom axiom.

- **`Over.snd_left` / `Over.tensorObj_left` bridge monoidal `Over S` projections to scheme pullbacks EXACTLY ‚ÄĒ the idiom for "monoidal projection inherits a scheme-level base-change-stable property"** *(NEW iter-158 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `snd_left_isClosedMap`, axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, verified this review.)*
  In `Over (Spec k)` the cartesian-monoidal `‚äó` IS the chosen pullback, so `CategoryTheory.Over.snd_left : (snd R S).left = Limits.pullback.snd R.hom S.hom` and `Over.tensorObj_left : (R ‚äó S).left = pullback R.hom S.hom` are **exact rewrites** (also `Over.fst_left`, `Over.comp_left`). To transport a base-change-stable scheme property `P f` (here `UniversallyClosed`, ‚üĻ `IsClosedMap` via `Scheme.Hom.isClosedMap`) from `X.hom` to `(snd X Y).left`: `rw [Over.snd_left]` then `<morphismProperty>.IsStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback (IsPullback.of_hasPullback X.hom Y.hom) hp`.
