For properness: `IsProper.toUniversallyClosed` gives `hp`; the stability instance is the *named* `universallyClosed_isStableUnderBaseChange : MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange` (NOT a dotted `UniversallyClosed.isStableUnderBaseChange`, which does not exist). **GOTCHA (cost ~20 min, recurs on any base-change-of-`X.hom` step):** `inferInstance` synthesises `UniversallyClosed (pullback.snd f g)` for *abstract* `f g` with bound `[UniversallyClosed f]`, but FAILS for `X.hom`/`Y.hom` coming from `Over (Spec k)` (whether `UniversallyClosed` or `IsProper` is the bound) ‚ÄĒ you MUST apply `of_isPullback` explicitly. Reusable for any monoidal-`Over`-projection-inherits-a-property step (closed map, separated, finite type, ‚Ä¶).

- **`ext1 <;> simp` closes cartesian-monoidal lift/projection identities uniformly** *(NEW iter-157 ‚ÄĒ `rigidity_snd_lift`, axiom-clean.)*
  In a `CartesianMonoidalCategory` (here `Over (Spec k)`), lift-equalities like `snd X Y ‚Čę lift (toUnit Y ‚Čę x‚āÄ) (ūĚüô Y) = lift (toUnit (X‚äóY) ‚Čę x‚āÄ) (snd X Y)` close by `ext1 <;> simp` ‚ÄĒ no need to name `comp_lift`/`lift_snd` (they resolve through the opened `CartesianMonoidalCategory` namespace). **Gotcha**: `toUnit_unique` is an *equality term*, not a function ‚ÄĒ `exact toUnit_unique _ _` after a manual `congr` over-applies it (`Function expected at toUnit_unique ?m ?m but this term has type ?m = ?m`). **Downstream-import-cycle gotcha** (also iter-157): `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` lives in `AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity`, which imports `Jacobian`, which imports the new upstream `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` ‚ÄĒ importing it creates a CYCLE. Inline the Mathlib argument instead: `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton` ‚Üí `Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_őĻ (IsOpen.dense ‚Ä¶)` ‚Üí `Over.OverMorphism.ext` ‚Üí `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` (`Genus` imports all of Mathlib, so everything is available). **Scaffold-signature caveat**: provisional scaffold signatures of *deep* theorems are NOT pre-validated ‚ÄĒ the iter-156 `[IsProper]`-only scaffold of `rigidity_lemma` was itself mathematically false (disconnected-`X` counterexample); treat them as conjectures the prover must validate.

- **Terminal-object witness skeleton: building a `where`-record whose underlying object is `ūĚüô_ C`, with explicit-term defeq for structural fields and epi-cancellation for uniqueness-out-of-terminal** *(closing the structural fields of `genusZeroWitness`)*
  *(NEW iter-155 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ decomposed the bare `genusZeroWitness` sorry into the blueprint ¬ß C.3 terminal-object skeleton; 6/7 fields + uniqueness closed, verified honest by both review subagents, 0 must-fix.)*
  For a witness `J := ūĚüô_ C` in a cartesian-monoidal category (here `Over (Spec k)`), two gotchas: **(1) `(ūĚüô_).hom` is defeq `ūĚüô (Spec k)` but instance search will NOT unfold it** (defeq but not reducible) ‚ÄĒ bare `inferInstance` / `infer_instance` on a structural field type fails with `failed to synthesize IsProper (ūĚüô_ ‚Ä¶).hom`. The idiom is to supply each field as an **explicit term annotated at the identity**: `proper := (inferInstance : IsProper (ūĚüô (Spec (.of k))))`, `smooth := (inferInstance : Smooth (ūĚüô (Spec (.of k))))`, `smoothGenus := by rw [h]; exact (inferInstance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (ūĚüô (Spec (.of k))))` ‚ÄĒ term-elaboration's defeq check then accepts it. `grpObj := inferInstance` works directly (the trivial GrpObj on the unit object). **(2) Uniqueness of a morphism OUT of the terminal object is NOT free** ‚ÄĒ the terminal property only gives uniqueness of morphisms INTO `ūĚüô`. Close it by cancelling the epimorphism `toUnit C`: `(toUnit C).left = C.hom` (`CategoryTheory.Over.toUnit_left`); `C.hom` is `Flat` (smooth ‚üĻ flat, auto-inferred) + `Surjective` (auto-inferred from the curve binders) hence `Epi` via `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`; lift to `Epi (toUnit C)` via `CategoryTheory.Over.epi_of_epi_left`; then `(cancel_epi (toUnit C)).mp`. Reusable for any witness whose underlying object is the terminal/unit object and whose universal-morphism uniqueness routes through a faithfully-flat-surjective structure morphism. **Note**: the blueprint prose for genus-0 uniqueness ("terminal UP determines a morphism out of `ūĚüô`") is mathematically loose; the epi-cancellation argument above is the correct one (recorded as a `% NOTE` in `Jacobian.tex` for a blueprint-writer to land).

- **Kernel of the universal K√§hler derivation = field of constants, via the single-element / perfect-field / Jacobi‚ÄďZariski `H1Cotangent` route (`D x = 0 ‚üĻ x algebraic over k`, then `[IsAlgClosed k] ‚üĻ x ‚ąą k`)** *(closing `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` ‚ÄĒ KDM)*
  *(NEW iter-154 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ the route that OVERTURNED the iter-153 "genuine Mathlib gap / bright-line STOP" verdict on FT.3; sourced from `analogies/ftthree-kernel-iter154.md` (8 compiling `example` blocks) and verified axiom-clean this review: `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, no `sorryAx`.)*
  For `[IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]` and `B` a finite-type `k`-domain, `D_B b = 0 ‚üĻ b ‚ąą range(algebraMap k B)`. **The decisive move: do NOT try to describe `ker d` directly** (no shipped kernel-of-`d` lemma exists in Mathlib b80f227) ‚ÄĒ instead realize injectivity of the cotangent base-change via the Jacobi‚ÄďZariski / `H1Cotangent` exact sequence + formal smoothness of a perfect-field (char-0) extension. **Three steps**: **(FT.1)** `set K := FractionRing B`; push `D_B b = 0` to `D_K (algebraMap B K b) = 0` via `‚Üź KaehlerDifferential.map_D k k B K b` + `map_zero`; `suffices algebraMap B K b ‚ąą range(algebraMap k K)`, pull back along the injective `B ‚Ü™ K` (`IsFractionRing.injective` + `IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply`). **(FT.2)** reduce to `IsAlgebraic k bK`; `by_contra` ‚üĻ `Transcendental k bK` ‚üĻ `Function.Injective (Polynomial.aeval bK)` (`transcendental_iff_injective`); embed `F := FractionRing (Polynomial k)` into `K` via `letI : Algebra F K := (IsFractionRing.lift hg).toAlgebra` (`hg` = the injectivity), build `IsScalarTower k F K` via `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq` + `IsFractionRing.lift_algebraMap` + `Polynomial.aeval_C`; then `EssFiniteType k K` (`Algebra.EssFiniteType.comp k B (FractionRing B)`) ‚üĻ `EssFiniteType F K` (`.of_comp k F K`); `PerfectField F` (`PerfectField.ofCharZero`) ‚üĻ `FormallySmooth F K` (`Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField`) ‚üĻ `mapBaseChange k F K` injective (via `injective_iff_map_eq_zero` + `Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_őī_mapBaseChange` + `Subsingleton.elim y 0`); the identity `mapBaseChange (1 ‚äó‚āú D_F X_F) = D_K bK` (`mapBaseChange_tmul` + `one_smul` + `map_D`) with RHS `= 0` ‚üĻ `1 ‚äó‚āú D_F X_F = 0` ‚üĻ (`Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff F (ő©[F‚ĀĄk]) _`) `D_F X_F = 0`. **(FT.3)** base case `D_{Frac(k[X])} X ‚Č† 0` (helper `_ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero`: `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` + `IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff` + `polynomialEquiv_D` + `nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero`) contradicts ‚üĻ `bK` algebraic; closer `_algebraic_mem_range` (`IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional` + `Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite` + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral`) lands it in range. **Plumbing gotchas (verified)**: `Algebra F K` / `IsScalarTower k F K` must be supplied explicitly (`letI`/`haveI` + `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq`); `EssFiniteType`/`PerfectField`/`CharZero F` instances resolve by `inferInstance` once stated as `haveI`. **Two analogist-citation corrections**: `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` lives in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Kaehler` (NOT `...Kaehler.Localization`) and needs all FOUR explicit args `R S T M` (a bare `_` does not unify). Required `open scoped IntermediateField TensorProduct` (the `k‚üģb‚üĮ` adjoin + `‚äó‚āú`/`‚äó[F]` notations are scoped). The standard-smooth / relative-dimension hypotheses are NOT used by this route (only `[IsDomain B]` + `[FiniteType k B]` to build `K` and the `EssFiniteType` chain). Reusable for any "`D x = 0` ‚üĻ `x` algebraic over the base field" obligation on a finitely-generated char-0 field extension.

- **Alg-closed constants collapse: `őď(X, O_X) = k` for smooth proper geometrically-irreducible reduced `X` over an algebraically closed `k`, in ~25‚Äď30 LOC** *(closing `constants_integral_over_base_field`)*
  *(NEW iter-153 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ the guaranteed-collapse closure that validated the iter-152 `[IsAlgClosed k]` pivot; axiom-clean, verified `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, no `sorryAx`.)*
  Under `[IsAlgClosed k]`, the goal `RingHom.range ((X ‚Üė Spec (.of k)).appTop.hom) = ‚ä§` closes in three steps with no separability/inseparability factorisation: **(1)** `haveI : IrreducibleSpace X := GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton f` (the `Subsingleton (Spec (.of k))` premise resolves by `inferInstance` ‚ÄĒ a field's spectrum is a singleton, so no manual `haveI` is needed), then `haveI : IsIntegral X := isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X`, then `letI : Field őď(X, ‚ä§) := (isField_of_universallyClosed k f).toField` (properness auto-infers `UniversallyClosed`+`LocallyOfFiniteType`); **(2)** `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k f` plus the iso-transfer pattern below gives `F.hom.Finite`, hence `Module.Finite k őď` and `Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k őď`; **(3)** `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` (`Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.Basic`; needs `[IsDomain őď]` from step 1 + `[Algebra.IsIntegral k őď]` from step 2) gives `algebraMap k őď` bijective; `rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` then `Function.Surjective.of_comp` on the `rfl`-true `F.hom = f.appTop.hom.comp (őďSpecIso).inv.hom`. Reusable for every "global sections of a proper geom-irreducible variety over an alg-closed field = the base field" obligation. **Companion iso-transfer pattern**: to move a `őď(Spec k, ‚ä§)`-side ring-hom property `P` (here `RingHom.Finite`) to the `k`-side, write `let F := (Scheme.őďSpecIso (.of k)).inv ‚Čę f.appTop` and discharge `P F.hom` by `P_respectsIso.2 (e := (Scheme.őďSpecIso (.of k)).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv)` applied to the `appTop`-side witness ‚ÄĒ the exact analogue of the `isIntegral_respectsIso.2` step inside Mathlib's own `isField_of_universallyClosed` proof. `RingHom.finite_respectsIso : RespectsIso @Finite` lives in `RingTheory/RingHom/Finite.lean`; the `.2` direction precomposes by a ring iso. This supersedes the iter-148/149 "PI + Sep" reduction and the `FiniteDimensional k őď` 4-step chain entries below ‚ÄĒ under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the whole separability detour is gone.

- **`lean_verify` axiom-check catches `sorryAx`-laundering consumers (a clean-compiling theorem can silently depend on a `sorry`)**
  *(NEW iter-151 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by `lean-auditor-chartalgebra-iter151` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-chartalgebra-iter151` on `GrpObj.df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`.)*
  A theorem whose body is a one-line delegate to a `sorry`-bearing lemma compiles with **NO `declaration uses 'sorry'` warning of its own**, yet `lean_verify`/`#print axioms` reports `sorryAx` in its axiom set. So a per-file `sorry` count (from `lean_diagnostic_messages` warnings or `sorry_analyzer`) **understates** the unsound surface: every clean-looking downstream consumer of a `sorry`-lemma is also unsound but invisible to the warning-based count. **Recipe**: when a lemma carries a `sorry` (especially a *known-false* one), run `lean_verify <FullyQualified.consumerName>` on each downstream consumer (find them via `grep -n` on the lemma name) to see which ones launder `sorryAx`. **Iter-151 instance**: `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` (`ChartAlgebra.lean:450`) delegates to the false KDM lemma and presents as a closed theorem while depending on `sorryAx`; the project's "9 sorries" headline therefore undercounts the actual unsound dependency set. Reusable whenever a `sorry` lemma has compiled consumers. **Iter-152 update (verified, both review subagents)**: after the alg-closed pivot, `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` (now `ChartAlgebra.lean:411`) STILL carries `sorryAx` transitively (verified via `lean_verify` ‚ÄĒ axiom set `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`), but the laundering is now *honest*: the delegated KDM lemma is mathematically TRUE with a genuine open `sorry`, not a false-statement sorry. So the laundering-risk persists but its character changed from "unsound (false lemma)" to "incomplete (open obligation)". **The blueprint `\leanok` consequence is live**: `sync_leanok` marks df_zero's PROOF block `\leanok` (its own body is sorry-free) even though it transitively depends on the open KDM sorry ‚ÄĒ a `% NOTE (iter-152 review)` was added to `RigidityKbar.tex` (df_zero statement block) to record this so the proof-block `\leanok` is not read as "fully discharged". Same pattern also holds for `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean:249`, transitive on the two witness sorries).

- **Counterexample-driven impossibility detection: when a "kernel-equals-constants" goal won't close, test it on disconnected and non-geometrically-connected algebras before grinding the proof**
  *(NEW iter-151 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ KDM `mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` (C.d) transfer step.)*
  A goal of the shape "`D b = 0 ‚üĻ b ‚ąą range(algebraMap k B)`" (kernel of a derivation = constants) is FALSE for any `B` where `k` is not algebraically closed in `B`, even with `[CharZero k]` + smoothness/standard-smoothness. The two minimal counterexamples that satisfy `[Field k][CharZero k][FiniteType k B][IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]` yet break the conclusion: (CE1) `B = k√ók`, `n=0` (finite √©tale ‚üĻ `ő©=0` ‚üĻ `D=0`; range = diagonal ‚ää B) ‚ÄĒ kills "add connectedness"; (CE2) `k=‚Ąö`, `B=‚Ąö(‚ąö2)`, `n=0` (finite separable ‚üĻ √©tale ‚üĻ `D=0`; range = ‚Ąö ‚ää ‚Ąö(‚ąö2)) ‚ÄĒ kills "add `[IsDomain B]`". `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B` is *exactly* "‚ąÉ submersive presentation of dim n" (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/StandardSmooth.lean` L88) and carries **no** connectedness/geometric-connectedness data. The true hypothesis needed is "`k` algebraically closed in `B`" (‚Č° `B` geometrically integral / `Spec B` geometrically connected over `k`). **Recipe**: for any `ker(derivation) = base` claim on an abstract algebra, before attempting closure, instantiate `B = k√ók` and a non-trivial separable field extension; if either violates the conclusion, the lemma needs a geometric hypothesis and the right move is an architectural signature change, NOT another helper. Reusable for every derivation-kernel / constants-extraction lemma. **Iter-152 resolution**: the architectural signature change this entry recommends was executed ‚ÄĒ JOINT `[IsAlgClosed k]` + `[IsDomain B]` repairs KDM (see the "honest-wrong-signature" anti-pattern entry below for the full resolution). The two counterexamples are now excluded on both Lean and blueprint sides.

- **MvPolynomial pderiv coefficient formula at shifted monomial: `coeff (u - Finsupp.single i 1) (pderiv i f) = coeff u f * (u i : k)` for `u i ‚Č• 1`**
  *(NEW iter-150 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-150 Lane 2 prover lane on `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` (BR.5) FREE-CASE.)*
  Substantive `MvPolynomial`-side lemma NOT in Mathlib b80f227. Closed in ~40 LOC via `MvPolynomial.as_sum` (decomposes `pderiv i f` as a sum over monomials) + `MvPolynomial.pderiv_monomial` (gives the per-monomial derivative formula) + `Finset.sum_eq_single` (singles out the `u`-indexed summand on the LHS) + a finsupp-subtraction uniqueness helper `_finsupp_sub_single_eq_of_one_le` (uses `Nat.lt_one_iff` to absurd a `hxne : x ‚Č† u` from `x - single i 1 = u - single i 1` under `1 ‚Č§ x i ‚ąß 1 ‚Č§ u i`). **Iter-150 instance**: `ChartAlgebra.lean:_mvPoly_coeff_pderiv_at_shifted` (L133‚Äď172); composed with `KaehlerDifferential.mvPolynomialBasis_repr_apply` (which bridges `D f = 0` to `‚ąÄ i, pderiv i f = 0` for the free polynomial algebra) to deliver `_mvPoly_mem_range_C_of_pderiv_eq_zero` (joint-kernel collapse for `MvPolynomial ŌÉ k` in `CharZero`, via support analysis ‚áí `f.support = {0} ‚áí f ‚ąą MvPolynomial.C.range`) + `_mvPoly_mem_range_C_of_D_eq_zero`. Reusable any time a `pderiv` / monomial coefficient identity at a `single i 1`-shifted monomial appears, or any time a "FREE-CASE joint-kernel collapse" closure is needed on a free polynomial algebra in `CharZero`.

- **HYBRID-part-(B) consumer-compatibility blocker (CharZero-collapse signature inflation requires upstream consumer-side propagation first)**
  *(NEW iter-150 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-150 Lane 1 prover lane on `isGeometricallyReduced_Gamma_of_smooth` (S3.sep.1) + `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_isGeometricallyReduced_of_finite` (S3.sep.2).)*
  When a planner directive recommends inflating a signature to add `[CharZero k]` / `[PerfectField k]` for a CharZero-collapse closure (the HYBRID part (B) recipe in `analogies/h1cotangent-vanishing-iter150.md`), verify the **consumer call sites** can supply the new typeclass BEFORE committing the inflation. **Recipe**: (a) identify all consumer call sites via `grep -n` on the lemma name; (b) check whether each consumer's signature carries (or can be augmented with) `[CharZero k]`; (c) if not, propagate `[CharZero k]` through the consumer's signature FIRST as an upstream refactor; (d) THEN inflate the original lemma. **Iter-150 instance**: (S3.sep.1) and (S3.sep.2) inflations broke `constants_integral_over_base_field` at `ChartAlgebra.lean:L431` and `L457` respectively with `failed to synthesize instance of type class PerfectField k`. The clean fix is consumer-side `[CharZero k]` propagation ‚ÄĒ which cascades to `rigidity_over_kbar` and other downstream consumers, a substantial refactor not in iter-150 scope. **Forward-progress infrastructure landed iter-150**: helper lemma `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_finite_of_perfectField` (`ChartAlgebraS3.lean:L435`) ‚ÄĒ the iter-150 HYBRID part (B) CharZero collapse realised as a parallel lemma with stronger hypotheses `[FiniteDimensional k F]` + `[PerfectField k]` ‚ÄĒ pre-stages the iter-151+ rewire. Reusable any time a CharZero-collapse pivot requires consumer-side typeclass propagation.

- **`FiniteDimensional k őď` bridge from `((appTop).hom).Finite` via `Iso.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv` + `RingEquiv.finite` + `RingHom.Finite.comp` + `RingHom.finite_algebraMap.mp`**
  *(NEW iter-149 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-149 Lane 2 prover lane closing the `constants_integral_over_base_field` hSep branch project-internally.)*
  Mathlib b80f227 gives `((X ‚Üė Spec k).appTop.hom).Finite` via `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` for proper `X` over a field `k`; converting this to `FiniteDimensional k őď(X, ‚ä§)` (needed by `Algebra.IsSeparable.of_isGeometricallyReduced_of_finite` and other finite-field-extension consumers) requires a 4-step Mathlib chain. **Recipe**: (1) extract `RingEquiv` from `Scheme.őďSpecIso (.of k)` via `CategoryTheory.Iso.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv` (declared in `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Ring.Basic`); (2) get `((Scheme.őďSpecIso (.of k)).inv.hom).Finite` via `RingEquiv.symm.finite` (declared in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Finiteness.Basic`); (3) compose with `_hAppTopFinite` via `RingHom.Finite.comp` to get `(őĪ.hom).Finite` where `őĪ = (őďSpecIso).inv ‚Čę (appTop)`; (4) bridge `őĪ.hom.Finite ‚ÜĒ (algebraMap k őď).Finite` definitionally via `őĪ.hom.toAlgebra` then close via `RingHom.finite_algebraMap.mp` (declared in `Mathlib.RingTheory.Finiteness.Defs`). **Iter-149 instance**: at `ChartAlgebra.lean:445‚Äď455` the chain produces `Module.Finite k ‚Ü•(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))` from `_hAppTopFinite : ((X ‚Üė Spec(.of k)).appTop.hom).Finite`. Reusable any time a scheme-side appTop-finiteness witness needs to flow to module-side finiteness over the base field. The chain is also expected to be reusable in the hPI branch closure (iter-150+) ‚ÄĒ step (v) of the base-change chain needs `FiniteDimensional k őď` for `(S3.pi.2)`.

- **First-class sub-claim decomposition as a strategy unit (one consolidated sorry ‚Üí multiple named sub-claims with documented Mathlib bridges)**
  *(NEW iter-149 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-149 progress-critic CHURNING corrective + the iter-149 blueprint-writer's `(S3.sep.1) / (S3.sep.2) / (S3.pi.1) / (S3.pi.2)` promotion.)*
  When a sorry has accumulated PARTIAL verdicts across K=3 iters with consistent blocker phrasing, the iter-N progress-critic CHURNING verdict mandates **structural decomposition** as the primary corrective. **Recipe**: (a) blueprint-writer promotes the conjunction-style sub-claims to first-class `\begin{lemma}` blocks with `\label{}`/`\lean{}`/`\uses{}` + proof sketches + Stacks Tag citations; (b) the prover lane scaffolds the new declarations into a new file (or a new section of an existing file) with correct signatures + structured `sorry` bodies + documented closure paths inline; (c) the consumer site is refactored to consume the new named lemmas (in the iter-149 instance, the consolidated `‚ü®hPI, hSep‚ü©` sorry was split into named branches via `refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©`). **Trade-off**: strict sorry count INCREASES (one consolidated wrapper ‚Üí multiple first-class sorries; iter-149: 5 ‚Üí 9), but each new sorry is independently-targetable with named Mathlib bridges; subsequent iters close each separately. **Iter-149 instance**: `constants_integral_over_base_field` substep 3 / KDM bridge ‚áí `(S3.sep.1)` + `(S3.sep.2)` + `(S3.pi.1)` + `(S3.pi.2)` named first-class decls + `(BR.1)`‚Äď`(BR.5)` named sub-steps for the KDM (p2) bridge. The decomposition is planner-deliberate and the strict-count metric should not be read in isolation against it.

- **`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B` is a `Prop` not registered as an instance; dispatch through the named theorem `.isStandardSmooth n` to extract `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k B`**
  *(NEW iter-149 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-149 Lane 2 prover lane on `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` (BR.2).)*
  Mathlib b80f227 ships `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension : ‚Ąē ‚Üí (k : Type u) ‚Üí (B : Type u) ‚Üí [CommRing k] ‚Üí [CommRing B] ‚Üí [Algebra k B] ‚Üí Prop` as a `Prop`, not as a typeclass instance source. Code that needs to consume `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k B` (e.g. `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential` for `Module.Free B (ő©[B‚ĀĄk])`) must dispatch through the named theorem `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n`. **Iter-149 instance** at `ChartAlgebra.lean:138‚Äď139`: `haveI _hStdSm : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k B := Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n`. Also: when declaring `{n : ‚Ąē}` as an explicit binder, downstream call sites must pass `(n := n)` (iter-149 instance at L240 in the `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` consumer; failing to do so produces `typeclass instance problem is stuck ‚ÄĒ Lean will not try to resolve because the first type argument to Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension was a metavariable`). Reusable any time a `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth`-family typeclass needs to be unpacked from a relative-dimension hypothesis.

- **Smart-proof reduction `Function.Surjective (algebraMap k őď) ‚üł IsPurelyInseparable k őď ‚ąß Algebra.IsSeparable k őď` via `IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable`**
  *(NEW iter-148 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-148 prover lane on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` `constants_integral_over_base_field` substep (3).)*
  When the goal is `Function.Surjective (algebraMap k F)` for a finite field extension `F/k` and the route through "explicit algebraic closure base change + flat-base-change-of-őď" hits a Mathlib gap (e.g. proper-őď-flat-base-change in Mathlib b80f227), the **smart-proof reduction** to the conjunction `IsPurelyInseparable k F ‚ąß Algebra.IsSeparable k F` closes via Mathlib's `IsPurelyInseparable.surjective_algebraMap_of_isSeparable` (`Mathlib/FieldTheory/PurelyInseparable/Basic.lean:158`). The reduction **does not eliminate the Mathlib gap**: it relocates the residual into the two sub-claims `IsPurelyInseparable k F` (from `GeometricallyIrreducible` + base change ‚áí unique min prime) and `Algebra.IsSeparable k F` (from smoothness ‚áí geom-reduced ‚áí separable). The advantage is that each sub-claim is independently named at sub-decomposition granularity. **Iter-148 instance** on `constants_integral_over_base_field`: the goal `Function.Surjective ((X ‚Üė Spec(.of k)).appTop.hom)` was reduced via `rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` + the algebraization chain `Scheme.őďSpecIso.inv ‚Čę appTop` + `RingHom.toAlgebra` to `Function.Surjective (algebraMap k őď(X, ‚ä§))` and then to the named conjunction. Reusable any time a `Function.Surjective (algebraMap k F)` claim can be approached via "PI + Sep" (typically: geometric irreducibility provides PI from base change, and smoothness provides Sep from geom-reducedness).

- **`Scheme.őďSpecIso` (NOT bare `őďSpecIso`) ‚ÄĒ fully-qualified namespace required**
  *(NEW iter-148 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-148 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field`.)*
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.őďSpecIso (R : CommRingCat) : őď(Spec R, ‚ä§) ‚ČÖ R` (Mathlib `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean:606`) is inside the `Scheme` namespace; the bare identifier `őďSpecIso` does NOT resolve unless `open Scheme` is in effect. Reusable any time the project needs to algebraize the canonical iso `őď(Spec R, ‚ä§) ‚ČÖ R`. **Iter-148 instance**: the `set őĪ := (Scheme.őďSpecIso (.of k)).inv ‚Čę (X ‚Üė Spec (.of k)).appTop` line at `ChartAlgebra.lean:313` initially failed with `Unknown identifier 'őďSpecIso'` (3 sites); fix is to fully-qualify to `Scheme.őďSpecIso`.

### Anti-Patterns (do not retry)

- **Anti-pattern: ship a `theorem foo : P := by sorry` where the in-source docstring openly admits `P` is mathematically false under stated hypotheses (the "honest-wrong-signature" pattern)**
  *(NEW iter-148 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by `lean-auditor-iter148` flagging `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` at `ChartAlgebra.lean:123` as the "weakened-wrong-definition" anti-pattern in its purest form.)*
  When a Lean declaration's body is `sorry` and the surrounding docstring openly admits the conclusion is FALSE under the stated hypotheses (iter-148 instance: `b ‚ąą (algebraMap k B).range` fails for `B = k[X]`, `b = X^p` in characteristic `p`, with `[Field k] [CommRing B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B] (hDb : D b = 0)`), the declaration cannot be closed honestly without changing the signature. **Honesty about wrongness does not make the claim provable.** The pattern is morally equivalent to an excuse-comment (`-- TODO: replace`, `-- placeholder`) even when the docstring frames the wrongness as "the blueprint-mandated entry-point name" ‚ÄĒ it documents *intentionally shipping a wrong signature with a permanent sorry*. **Rule**: if the docstring would need to admit the conclusion is FALSE under stated hypotheses, the signature must be inflated before the body can be filled ‚ÄĒ OR the blueprint anchor name must migrate to a renamed lemma that is actually provable. The plan agent's "the consumer expects this name" framing is not sufficient ground to ship the wrong signature; the cost is a permanent invisible sorry that no future prover lane can discharge. **Iter-148 corrective path** for the KDM case: inflate to `[CharZero k] + [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension k B]` (and propagate to ő≤-core consumer), or commit to (p1) char-p Cartier chain with `[Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k B]` + Frobenius hypothesis. Documented at `recommendations.md` (iter-148 review) ¬ß "CRITICAL ‚ÄĒ KDM signature". **RESOLVED iter-152**: the iter-149 `[CharZero k]`+std-smooth inflation was confirmed (iter-151) STILL false (counterexamples `B=k√ók`, `‚Ąö(‚ąö2)/‚Ąö`); the architectural fix that actually works is the JOINT addition of `[IsAlgClosed k]` + `[IsDomain B]` to KDM (`[IsDomain B]` kills `B=k√ók`; `[IsAlgClosed k]` kills `‚Ąö(‚ąö2)/‚Ąö`). Neither hypothesis suffices alone ‚ÄĒ the iter-151 "one-at-a-time" reasoning was too pessimistic. Landed via the iter-152 `refactor isalgclosed-signatures`; KDM is now a genuine to-prove `sorry` (the field-of-fractions argument), no longer a false-statement sorry. The general rule stands: a `sorry` whose docstring admits the conclusion is false under stated hypotheses needs a signature change, not a helper round.

- **Anti-pattern: carry decorative typeclasses on a consumer that does not invoke its callee with them (the "consumer-with-unused-typeclasses" pattern)**
  *(NEW iter-148 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by `lean-auditor-iter148` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-chartalgebra-iter148` on `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` at `ChartAlgebra.lean:196`.)*
  When a consumer carries four `C`-side scheme typeclasses (`[Smooth (C ‚Üė Spec k)]`, `[IsProper]`, `[IsReduced]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible]`) and the body is a one-line wrapper around a helper that accepts none of them, the typeclasses are **decorative**. The signature *looks* like it has more chart-of-proper-curve context, but typeclass synthesis cannot bridge them through the helper call. Either (a) prune the unused typeclasses to align the signature with the actual body's commitment, or (b) inflate the helper's signature to consume them substantively. Maintaining the decorative typeclasses is the visual analog of the wrong-signature anti-pattern above ‚ÄĒ the consumer reads as if it does more than its body actually does. **Rule**: every typeclass / hypothesis in a signature should be consumed somewhere in the body; if none of the body's term-level identifiers consume the typeclass, prune it or strengthen the body. **Iter-148 instance**: `ChartAlgebra.lean:196 GrpObj.df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` carries `[Smooth (C ‚Üė Spec k)] [IsProper] [IsReduced] [GeometricallyIrreducible]` on `C` but the body is `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero hDb` which consumes nothing from `C`. The iter-148 review-phase `% NOTE:` block on the blueprint side authorises the iter-148 thin-wrapper disposition pending iter-149+ substantive refinement; the Lean-side fix is queued as REC-3-bis. Reusable as a flag for any "consumer-of-named-helper" closure where the consumer's signature is wider than the helper's.

- **Signature-refinement-via-delegate: closing a `: True := sorry` placeholder by routing to a separately-named helper that itself carries the substantive sorry**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` ő≤-core (`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`).)*
  When the iter-N blueprint disposition concentrates the substantive content at a separately-named helper (the iter-146 B.preferred disposition for the chart-algebra (ő≤) chain: chart-of-proper-curve hypothesis pushed into `constants_in_chart_of_proper_curve`, leaving the consumer `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` as a one-line delegate), the iter-N+k prover can close the consumer as **sorry-free** by replacing its `: True := sorry` placeholder with the real signature and a body that delegates to the named helper. Requirements: (a) the consumer's hypotheses are exactly the standing premises the helper consumes internally; (b) the helper exists by name (even if its body is `sorry`); (c) namespace ordering lets the consumer reference the helper (iter-147 instance required re-ordering KDM to land **before** the second `namespace GrpObj` reopening so ő≤-core could reference `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`). The consumer's body becomes a one-line `:= helper_name args`; net per-iter cost is ~10‚Äď20 LOC. Reusable for any consumer-of-named-helper situation. Caveat: the helper's `sorry` is **not** discharged by this pattern ‚ÄĒ the substantive content is just relocated to the helper. Track the helper sorry-count separately.

- **`rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` exposes `Function.Surjective ‚áĎf` as the next goal**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field` substep (3).)*
  When the goal is `f.range = ‚ä§` for a ring hom `f` and the natural continuation constructs a surjectivity witness (rather than chasing the `Subalgebra` / `range` layer), `rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` rewrites the goal to `Function.Surjective ‚áĎf`. This composes cleanly with Mathlib bijectivity lemmas ‚ÄĒ iter-147 instance: post-rewrite, the algclose-base-change chain consumes `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` (`Mathlib/FieldTheory/IsAlgClosed/Basic.lean`) to get the bijectivity of `algebraMap k őď(X_{\bar k}, ‚ä§)`, which is the natural continuation. Without the rewrite, the chain has to fight the `Subalgebra`/`range` layer at every step (the rewrite eliminates the chain-internal `RingHom.range`/`AlgHom.range`/`Subalgebra` impedance mismatches). Reusable any time a `range = ‚ä§` claim has a natural surjectivity-style witness.

- **Multi-step closure chain pattern: in-source comments naming each substep's Mathlib lemma + one residual `sorry` concentrated at the substantive gap**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field` substep (3) 7-step closure chain.)*
  When a closure chain has many substeps but only one is the substantive Mathlib gap (most others have catalogued Mathlib lemmas), commit the chain as in-source comments with each substep's target lemma named, place the residual `sorry` at the chain's exit (NOT at each substep), and document the named Mathlib gap immediately above the sorry. This preserves the iter-N+k continuation point without requiring the prover to chase every substep before committing. Iter-147 instance: substep (3) decomposed into 7 steps (a)‚Äď(g) at `ChartAlgebra.lean:243‚Äď293`; 6 steps name Mathlib lemmas (`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral`, `Module.finrank_baseChange`, `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_iff_finrank_eq_one`, `IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible`, `isField_of_universallyClosed`, `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`); step (e) (flat base change of `őď` for proper scheme; Stacks 02KH) is the residual gap, concentrated at the chain-exit `sorry`. The pattern reads honestly (no over-claim) and lets iter-148+ work the wrapper precisely at the named gap.

- **`Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` is a `local instance` inside `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`; downstream files must re-enable it at file scope to use the canonical `Algebra.IsPushout k B‚āĀ B‚āā (TensorProduct k B‚āĀ B‚āā)` instance**
  *(NEW iter-146 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-146 prover lane on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` `algebra_isPushout_of_affine_product`.)*
  Mathlib's `TensorProduct.isPushout` instance (`Mathlib/RingTheory/IsTensorProduct.lean:630`) discharges `Algebra.IsPushout k B‚āĀ B‚āā (B‚āĀ ‚äó[k] B‚āā)` directly ‚ÄĒ but it requires both the left algebra instance on `TensorProduct k B‚āĀ B‚āā` (global) AND the right algebra instance (Mathlib ships as `local instance` inside `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`). Downstream files trying to use `inferInstance` will fail with `failed to synthesize instance of type class Algebra B‚āā (TensorProduct k B‚āĀ B‚āā)`. The unlock is `attribute [local instance] Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` at file scope. Documented in-file at `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean:70‚Äď74`. **Iter-146 instance**: the chart-algebra (őĪ) helper collapsed from a planned ~80‚Äď150 LOC three-step Mathlib chain (`pullbackSpecIso` ‚Üí `isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout` ‚Üí `CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout`) to a 5-LOC `inferInstance` body once the unlock landed. The three-step chain is still the honest scheme-level identification; the algebra-level instance is what the chart-algebra consumers (ő≤-core + KDM + lift) actually need. **Caveat**: file-scope re-enablement widens the instance-search surface for every importer; if a narrower-scope option suffices (`local instance` inside a `section`), prefer that.

- **`isField_of_universallyClosed` + `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` chain for "őď(X, ‚ä§) is a finite-dim field extension of k" under `IsProper` + `[IsReduced X]` + `[GeometricallyIrreducible (X ‚Üė Spec k)]`**
  *(NEW iter-146 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-146 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field`.)*
  For `X : Scheme` smooth proper geometrically irreducible over a field `k`, the cleanest Mathlib chain to show `őď(X, ‚ä§)` is a finite-dim field extension of `k`:
  1. `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X ‚Üė Spec k)` ‚Üí `IrreducibleSpace X` (uses singleton-base `Spec k`).
  2. `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X` ‚Üí `IsIntegral X` (needs `[IsReduced X]` ‚ÄĒ see Knowledge Base entry on `Smooth ‚áí IsReduced` gap).
  3. `[IsProper (X ‚Üė Spec k)]` extends `UniversallyClosed` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` automatically.
  4. `isField_of_universallyClosed k (X ‚Üė Spec k)` ‚Üí `IsField őď(X, ‚ä§)`.
  5. `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k (X ‚Üė Spec k)` ‚Üí `appTop.hom` is finite.
  Combined: `őď(X, ‚ä§)` is a finite field extension of `k`. The geom-irr-forces-dim_k=1 step (substep 3) is then the iter-147+ closure path via `IsBaseChange` + `Module.finrank_baseChange`. Reusable for any chart-algebra-style argument that needs to reduce a `őď`-type claim to a dimension claim over the base field.

- **Named-concept-vs-body mismatch as iter-146 review-phase pattern (signature-reduction blueprint NOTE discipline)**
  *(NEW iter-146 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-146 prover lane on `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` + paired auditor / lean-vs-blueprint-checker must-fix findings.)*
  When a prover refines a `: True := sorry` placeholder by delegating to a renamed-but-existing lemma (iter-146 instance: `ext_of_diff_zero` body is a one-line delegate to the iter-125 `ext_of_eqOnOpen` packaging, dropping the planner-spec'd `df = dg` hypothesis), the closure is sorry-free Lean but the blueprint statement and Lean signature drift out of sync. **Rule**: the blueprint MUST receive a `% NOTE: (iter-N review)` annotation flagging the deliberate signature reduction, naming what was dropped, and scheduling the iter-N+k re-refinement. Otherwise the next prover attempt at the load-bearing form will be confused about whether the lemma is already closed. **Honest-on-the-Lean-side alone is insufficient** ‚ÄĒ both sides need to acknowledge the reduction. **Iter-146 instance**: `ChartAlgebra.lean:196‚Äď203` docstring documents the reduction; `RigidityKbar.tex:lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero` received the `% NOTE: (iter-146 review)` annotation this review-phase pairing the call-out.

- **Mathlib `b80f227` lacks general `Smooth (X ‚Üí Spec k) ‚áí IsReduced X` over a field; project carries `[IsReduced X]` explicitly at every call site**
  *(NEW iter-146 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-146 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field`. Now documented at three sites: `Rigidity.lean`'s "Hypothesis history" (pre-existing), `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean:140‚Äď143` (iter-146 add), `RigidityKbar.tex:lem:constants_integral_over_base_field` `% NOTE: (iter-146 review)` (iter-146 review-phase add).)*
  Mathlib snapshot `b80f227` ships `Smooth ‚áí IsReduced` only for perfect-base via `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField`. For a general base field `k`, no such direct lemma exists. The project's discipline is to explicitly carry `[IsReduced X]` at every call site that needs it. Once the Mathlib gap closes, the explicit-`IsReduced` hypothesis becomes derivable from `[Smooth (X ‚Üí Spec k)]` and may be dropped at every site in tandem. **Do NOT** try to drop `[IsReduced X]` from a declaration relying on the chain "smooth ‚áí reduced ‚áí integral (under geom-irr)" until the Mathlib gap is closed. **Iter-146 instance**: `ChartAlgebra.lean:148` adds `[IsReduced X]` alongside `[Smooth (X ‚Üė Spec (.of k))]`; the `IsIntegral X` instance synthesis at L156 chains through this explicit-`IsReduced` rather than through `Smooth`.

- **In-tree excise is the iter-141-discipline-consistent response to strategy-critic Q7 ("auditable record" of descoped route) findings**
  *(NEW iter-145 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by `strategy-critic-iter145` Q7 + `refactor-chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-excise-iter145` execution.)*
  When a strategy-critic flags ~hundreds-of-LOC of sorry-bodied scaffolding as "preserved in-tree as auditable record" of a now-descoped route, the iter-141 anti-pattern (preservation-of-bundled-framing-as-silent-option) extends from prose to in-tree disposition. **Deletion is the disciplined response, not preservation.** Iter-145 instance: 3 named targets + 2 cascade-excised consumers totalling 272 LOC removed from `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`; project-side audit record is preserved via (a) git history (`git log` / `git show`) for the deleted bodies, (b) `analogies/` design notes for design context (`analogies/chart-algebra-vs-bundled-iter144.md`, `analogies/d-app-d-map-recipe-shape.md`, etc.), (c) `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` sidecars for the per-iter trajectory. **Rule**: a refactor that ONLY changes disposition (not signatures) on sorry-bodied descoped declarations is the iter-141-discipline response. If the planner believes "auditable record" is the right disposition despite the Q7 challenge, it must write down a concrete falsification criterion ("We keep the scaffolding because [named future use]"); if no such use exists, deletion is correct.

- **`Mathlib.RingTheory.IsPushout` does NOT exist; use `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct` for the `Algebra.IsPushout` square API**
  *(NEW iter-145 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-145 refactor on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`.)*
  The natural import name `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsPushout` is not in Mathlib snapshot `b80f227`. The canonical anchor for the `Algebra.IsPushout k A B C` square API is `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`. Documented in-file at `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean:10‚Äď16` for future refactor / writer reference. Generally reusable when scaffolding chart-algebra or `Algebra.IsPushout`-based files.

- **Project-authorised `: True := sorry` placeholder + lean-auditor must-fix classification (subagent-semantics tension)**
  *(NEW iter-145 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by `lean-auditor-review145` flagging the 5 chart-algebra placeholders as must-fix despite the iter-145 refactor directive explicitly authorising them.)*
  When a plan-agent directive authorises `: True := sorry` placeholder signatures (per the iter-128 ‚Üí iter-131 cautionary-tale "safer than committing wrong signatures" rule), the lean-auditor descriptor's "no strategic bias" stance mandates classifying them as must-fix (triple-hit: suspect-body + weakened-wrong-definition + excuse-comment). Both correct in their domain: the directive's authorisation is project-strategic; the auditor's classification is project-bias-blind by descriptor. **Future plan-agent directives that authorise `: True` placeholders should note the auditor's expected must-fix classification in `summary.md` / `recommendations.md` so review-phase findings are read in context.** The review-agent honors the project authorisation when triaging recommendations ‚ÄĒ must-fix-this-iter from the auditor on a directive-authorised placeholder does NOT block downstream prover work if the placeholder is the iter-N+1 refinement target.

- **Pre-committed conditional-fallback chart-algebra pivot (mechanical-gate-honored rule)**
  *(NEW iter-144 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-144
  mandatory chart-algebra-vs-bundled re-evaluation gate firing with
  PIVOT_TO_CHART_ALGEBRA per
  `mathlib-analogist-chart-algebra-iter144` + persistent file
  `analogies/chart-algebra-vs-bundled-iter144.md`)*:
  When an iter-N `mathlib-analogist` surfaces a chart-algebra (or
  more generally: alternative-closure-path) alternative with a
  CONTINUE_BUNDLED criterion of the form "pivot unless ‚Č•M
  sub-sorries closed iter-N+k" (or "pivot unless ‚Č•M of K sub-sorries
  closed across iter-N+1..N+k"), and the bundled route does NOT
  mechanically meet the criterion in the budgeted K iters, the pivot
  must be honored even if an intervening iter-N+j strategy-critic
  has rejected a related-but-different "route-pivot" question. The
  CONTINUE_BUNDLED gate is the **authoritative trigger**;
  strategy-critic verdicts on adjacent route-pivot questions (e.g.,
  base-change) do NOT preclude honoring the mechanical gate on a
  closure-path-change pivot. **Iter-144 instance**: iter-140
  chart-algebra analogist set CONTINUE_BUNDLED at "‚Č•2 sub-sorries
  closed iter-140"; iter-140 closed 0/3, cumulative iter-140/142/143
  closed 1/3 (d_map only); iter-141 strategy-critic rejected an
  over-k vs over-`kŐĄ` base-change pivot as wrong-question
  (piece (i.b) Step 2 is base-independent) ‚ÄĒ but the iter-144 pivot
  answers a closure-path question, not a base-change question. The
  mechanical gate fired cleanly; iter-144 PIVOT honored. **Rule**:
  pre-committed conditional fallbacks must be enforced at the
  scheduled gate, regardless of intervening strategy-critic
  framing ‚ÄĒ the strategy-critic's territory is sunk-cost / soundness
  / coherence, NOT mechanical-gate enforcement. **Documented at
  `iter/iter-144/plan.md`, `iter/iter-144/review.md`, and
  `analogies/chart-algebra-vs-bundled-iter144.md`.**

- **`Over.w` chain for `Over`-categorical-product identities**
  *(NEW iter-143 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-143 prover lane on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`'s d_app
  Step 3.a at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:637‚Äď638`)*:
  When an Over-categorical identity of the form
  `(fst G G).left ‚Čę G.hom = (snd G G).left ‚Čę G.hom` is needed under
  `CartesianMonoidalCategory.fst`/`snd` notation, the cleanest closure is
  `by rw [(fst G G).w, (snd G G).w]` (~3 LOC). Both `.w` equalities equate
  the composite with `(G ‚äó G).hom` in `Over (Spec k)`, so chaining them
  via `rw` gives the identity directly. **Avoids** the `Over.comp_left`
  detour (which would unwrap the structure and re-introduce mismatched
  `Over.Hom.left`/`Hom.left` syntax). Reusable any time an Over-categorical
  equality is needed between morphisms involving categorical-product
  projections. Verified standalone via `lean_multi_attempt` returning
  `goals: []`. Caveat (auditor-flagged iter-143): if the resulting
  hypothesis `hw` is not consumed before a downstream `sorry`, the
  pattern reads as dead-load. Consume `hw` in the same iter (e.g. via
  `PresheafedSpace.comp_c_app` Step 3.b lift) or defer the `have` until
  the chase can proceed.

- **Anti-pattern: blind `simp only [Adjunction.homEquiv_*_symm]` does NOT
  close adjunction-transpose goals where the inner equation is a
  c-component equality**
  *(NEW iter-143 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-143 prover lane on
  the d_app sub-sorry, attempt 4)*:
  `simp only [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, Adjunction.homEquiv_counit,
  Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm,
  Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm]` applied to a raw
  `(KD _).d (Ōą.app X .hom (ŌÜ_G.app X .hom a)) = 0` shape leaves the goal
  structurally identical. The transpose lemmas need explicit instantiation
  at the right adjunction (`G.hom.base` vs `(fst G G).left.base`) AND
  applied to a lifted c-equality (obtained via `PresheafedSpace.comp_c_app`),
  not as blind rewrites. The lifted equation must be available in the
  context before the transpose lemmas can fire. **Working pattern**: lift
  the categorical `hw` to c-component level first (`PresheafedSpace.comp_c_app`
  twice, once per `Over` morphism), then explicitly invoke
  `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm` at the named adjunction with
  the lifted equation as argument.

- **Anti-pattern: `refine (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D _).d_map ?_`
  fails when the goal carries `Ōą ‚ąė ŌÜ_G` (not literally `ŌÜ_LHS ‚ąė h`)**
  *(NEW iter-143 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-143 prover lane on
  the d_app sub-sorry, attempt 2)*:
  `d_map` produces a metavariable-typed sub-goal (with `?m.161/?m.162/?m.163`
  unresolved on parameter types) when the factoring witness `h` is not
  derivable from the surface goal shape. The witness must be constructed
  **before** `d_map` can apply. For the
  `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation` d_app: build the witness via
  the 4-step recipe (3.a `Over.w` chain + 3.b `PresheafedSpace.comp_c_app`
  lift + 3.c `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm` transpose +
  3.d `d_map` discharge) per `analogies/d-app-d-map-recipe-shape.md`
  Decision 2 (NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL). `cat_disch`/`aesop_cat` also do
  NOT synthesize this witness (iter-143 confirmed).

- **`pushforward‚āÄ` `whnf`-opacity extends to `_=0`-shape goals when the LHS crosses adjunction-transpose terms ‚ÄĒ fully-explicit `change` blocks (no `_` placeholders) are mandatory whenever ANY side of the equation crosses `pushforward‚āÄ`-annotated definitions, regardless of the RHS shape**
  *(NEW iter-142 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-142 prover lane regression test on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`'s d_app sub-sorry
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:637`)*:
  Iter-140 + iter-141 codified the `change`-avoidance rule for `_=_`-shape
  goals carrying `((pushforward Ōą).obj LHS).map f` on the RHS (whnf
  descends into `pushforward‚āÄ` which is `set_option
  backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`-annotated at
  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:37, 55`).
  Iter-142 strengthens this: even `change KD.d _ = 0` (the d_app
  shape, RHS literal `0`) deterministically times out at 200k
  heartbeats when the `_` LHS placeholder elides
  `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.homEquiv.symm`-transposed terms ‚ÄĒ
  whnf is forced to elaborate the `_` and descends through
  `pushforward‚āÄ`.
  **Working pattern (codified iter-142)** for any
  derivation-construction proof obligation that crosses
  `pushforward‚āÄ`-annotated definitions:
  - Write `change` BLOCKS literally, spelling out BOTH the LHS and the
    RHS in full (no `_` anywhere on a side that touches a
    `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` term or a `(pushforward _).obj`
    term).
  - Goal-state inspection up-front (via `lean_goal`) to know exactly
    what canonical form to spell out ‚ÄĒ the prover spent ~3 attempts
    iter-140 + iter-142 discovering this via trial-and-error.
  - Only invoke `_` placeholders on a side where the elided expression
    is `0` or a single beta-redex unrelated to `pushforward‚āÄ`.
  **Bright-line diagnostic generalised**: when ANY `change` invocation
  carries `_` placeholders AND the goal contains the substring
  `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` OR `(pushforward _).obj`, the
  invocation will deterministically hit the 200k whnf cap. Pre-empt
  by writing fully-explicit `change` blocks from the start.

- **`rw [show <explicit `.hom`-form equality> from NatTrans.naturality_apply Ōą f x]` packaging ‚ÄĒ required when the post-`change` goal carries `RingCat.Hom.hom`/`CommRingCat.Hom.hom`-form terms but `NatTrans.naturality_apply` produces `ConcreteCategory.hom`-form equalities**
  *(NEW iter-142 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-142 prover
  lane d_map closure on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:643`)*:
  `NatTrans.naturality_apply` (at
  `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/ConcreteCategory/Basic.lean:221`) produces
  an equality of the shape
  `(ConcreteCategory.hom (ŌÜ.app Y)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (F.map f)) x) =
   (ConcreteCategory.hom (G.map f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (ŌÜ.app X)) x)`,
  where the outer projection is `ConcreteCategory.hom`. After a
  fully-explicit `change` block (per the iter-140/141/142
  `pushforward‚āÄ`-opacity rule), the goal carries `RingCat.Hom.hom` /
  `CommRingCat.Hom.hom`-form terms with the `.hom` projection
  inlined. The two are definitionally equal but `rw` matches
  syntactically, so a bare `rw [NatTrans.naturality_apply ŌÜ f x]`
  fails with "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern".
  **Working pattern (codified iter-142)**:
  ```lean
  rw [show ((ŌÜ.app Y).hom) ((F.map f).hom x) =
            ((G.map f).hom) (((ŌÜ.app X).hom) x) from
        NatTrans.naturality_apply ŌÜ f x]
  ```
  The `show ... from ...` wrapper retypes the equality into the goal's
  `.hom`-form, so `rw`'s syntactic match succeeds. The two forms are
  definitionally equal; the wrapper is purely syntactic alignment.
  **Generalisation**: whenever a `rw` against a `ConcreteCategory`-API
  lemma fails with a "Did not find an occurrence" error on a goal
  whose terms use `.hom` field-projection notation, package the lemma
  in `rw [show <explicit `.hom`-form goal pattern> from <lemma>]`.

- **The right Mathlib lemma for unfolding `((pushforward F).obj M).map f` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply'` ‚ÄĒ use `simp only`, NEVER `change`**
  *(NEW iter-141 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-141
  `mathlib-analogist-d-app-d-map-iter141` dispatch on the iter-140
  deterministic `whnf` timeout)*:
  The unfolding lemma exists in Mathlib as
  `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply'` at
  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:99‚Äď106`
  (`@[simp]`-tagged, equation
  `(((pushforward F).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m`).
  Use `simp only [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply']` to
  unfold; do **not** use `change` because `pushforward‚āÄ` and
  `pushforward‚āÄ_obj` are annotated with `set_option
  backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` at
  `Pushforward.lean:37, 55`, which **explicitly disables**
  `isDefEq`/`whnf`-based unfolding. The iter-140 d_map `change`
  attempt hit a deterministic timeout at `maxHeartbeats=200000` for
  exactly this reason.
  **Working 3-step ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB d_map chase**:
  1. `simp only [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply']` (NOT `change`).
  2. `NatTrans.naturality` for the relevant compatibility Ōą.
  3. `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'_map_d`
     for the cross-open `KaehlerDifferential.D` identity.
  **Generalisation**: `set_option
  backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` annotations on
  Mathlib `@[simps]` definitions are the bright-line diagnostic for
  `change`-avoidance ‚ÄĒ when a `whnf` invocation deterministically
  times out on a goal whose terms involve such an annotated
  definition, the right action is to find the explicit `@[simp]`
  unfolding lemma the Mathlib authors provided as the intended API
  (`apply'`-suffixed names are the convention; e.g.
  `pushforward_obj_map_apply'`).

- **`change` succeeds on `d_app`-shape (`_ = 0`) but fails on `d_map`-shape (matched-functor-map) inside `ModuleCat.Derivation.mk` / `PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk` lambdas ‚ÄĒ use `simp only` with explicit lemma names for the latter**
  *(NEW iter-140 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-140 PARTIAL
  prover lane on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:573`)*:
  Two qualitatively different shapes of derivation-internal naturality
  goals interact very differently with `change`. The **d_app shape**
  (LHS = some KaehlerDifferential.D expression, RHS = `0`) only needs
  `whnf` on the LHS; `change (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D _).d _
  = 0` succeeds and beta-reduces the inner lambda cleanly. The
  **d_map shape** (LHS = `(d_Y).d (presheaf.map f ...)`, RHS =
  `(pushforward Ōą).obj LHS .map f .hom (d_X.d ...)`) needs `whnf`
  on the **RHS** which carries `((pushforward Ōą).obj LHS).map f` ‚ÄĒ
  this is opaque (`pushforward = pushforward‚āÄ ‚ąė restrictScalars`
  is `@[simps]` but not reducible; file
  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:39,86`).
  `change` triggers `whnf` on this term and hits 200 k heartbeats
  before unfolding (deterministic timeout).
  **Working pattern (codified iter-140)** for d_map-shaped goals
  under a `Derivation'.mk`/`ModuleCat.Derivation.mk` construction:
  - Do NOT use `change` to reshape the goal; the `whnf` invocation
    blows up.
  - Use `simp only` with explicit lemma names ‚ÄĒ e.g.
    `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map`,
    `restrictScalars_map_apply` ‚ÄĒ to rewrite the
    `(pushforward _).obj _ .map f` expression into its `simp`-normal
    form without invoking `whnf`.
  - Then chain `NatTrans.naturality` for Ōą + Mathlib's
    `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'_map_d`
    for the `KaehlerDifferential.D` cross-open identity.
  **Generalisation**: whenever a derivation-construction proof
  obligation has matched nested-functor terms on both sides, the
  `change`-then-`rw` pattern that worked for the iter-138 `d_add`/
  `d_mul` laws and the iter-140 `d_app` law (all of which have one
  side a syntactic `0` or a single beta-redex) does NOT extend. The
  presence of `(pushforward F).map f`-style terms is the bright-line
  diagnostic for `change`-avoidance.

- **`PresheafOfModules`-morphism iso check via `isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R)` + `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`** *(NEW iter-140 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-140 prover lane structural refactor on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:688`)*:
  The standard way to check that a morphism `f : M ‚ü∂ N` of
  presheaves of modules over a presheaf of rings `R` is an
  isomorphism is to chain two Mathlib facts: (i)
  `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R` is a `ReflectsIsomorphisms`-functor
  (needs `import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced`
  for typeclass synthesis ‚ÄĒ the file is `Balanced` + `Faithful` ‚Üí
  `ReflectsIsomorphisms`); (ii) `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
  reduces "iso of natural transformation" to "iso of each app".
  Combined, this reduces a `PresheafOfModules`-morphism iso check
  to a per-open `IsIso (f.app X)` check inside `ModuleCat (R.obj X)`.
  **5-LOC helper** (codified as
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.isIso_of_app_iso_module`, private,
  upstream-PR candidate; mirrors the existing
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` for
  the `SheafOfModules` variant in
  `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:132`):
  ```lean
  private theorem isIso_of_app_iso_module {C : Type*} [Category C]
      {R : CŠĶíŠĶĖ ‚•§ RingCat} {M N : PresheafOfModules R}
      (f : M ‚ü∂ N) (h : ‚ąÄ X, IsIso (f.app X)) : IsIso f := by
    rw [‚Üź isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R),
        NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
    intro X
    exact Functor.map_isIso (forget‚āā (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat) (f.app X)
  ```
  **Critical caveat**: without
  `import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced` (or a
  transitive import that pulls it in), the
  `(PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).ReflectsIsomorphisms` typeclass
  does not synthesise. The chain
  `Balanced + Faithful ‚Üí ReflectsIsomorphisms` is the load-bearing
  Mathlib infrastructure.
  **Use case**: any `PresheafOfModules`-level iso construction
  that defers the categorical work to per-open ModuleCat-level
  iso checks (e.g., chart-localised arguments using
  `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv.symm` per affine open).

- **`simp` does NOT fire inside `ModuleCat.Derivation.mk` / `PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk` lambdas ‚ÄĒ use `have / change / rw / exact`**
  *(NEW iter-138 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-138 PARTIAL
  prover lane on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:547`; codified in
  `debug_feedback.md` and `task_results/Cotangent_GrpObj.lean.md` ¬ß
  "Negative lessons")*:
  When constructing a derivation via `ModuleCat.Derivation.mk
  (fun b => f.d (ŌÜ b)) _ _ _` (or the presheaf variant
  `PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk`), the additive (`d_add`) and
  multiplicative (`d_mul`) proof obligations are stated with the inner
  function appearing as a **beta-redex** in the goal. `simp` with the
  obvious lemma names (`map_add`, `map_mul`, `ModuleCat.Derivation.d_add`,
  `ModuleCat.Derivation.d_mul`) does NOT fire because the lemma
  patterns expect `f (a + b)` or `f.d (a + b)` applied syntactically,
  but the goal carries `(fun b ‚Ü¶ f.d (ŌÜ b)) (a + b)` and `simp` does
  not beta-reduce through the lambda before applying the simp set.
  **Working pattern** (validated iter-138 by closing the `d_add` /
  `d_mul` laws of `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`):
  ```lean
  fun a b => by
    have h : ((Ōą.app X).hom (a + b)) =
             ((Ōą.app X).hom a) + ((Ōą.app X).hom b) :=
      RingHom.map_add _ _ _
    change (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D _).d _ = _
    rw [h]
    exact ModuleCat.Derivation.d_add _ _ _
  ```
  Four steps: (1) extract the additivity/multiplicativity identity
  for the inner function `ŌÜ` into a `have h`; (2) `change` to reshape
  the outer goal so the `KaehlerDifferential.D` (or other derivation
  carrier) is exposed for rewriting; (3) `rw [h]` to apply the
  extracted identity; (4) `exact` the explicit derivation law. The
  same pattern works for any `ModuleCat.Derivation.mk` /
  `PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk` construction whose inner function
  is non-trivial (composed of multiple ring/algebra maps).
  **Generalisation**: for derivation constructions with multi-step
  inner functions, the `have / change / rw / exact` pattern is
  preferable to `simp` even when `simp` would close the goal in
  principle ‚ÄĒ `simp`'s failure mode here is silent (it just doesn't
  fire) and is easy to mistake for a missing lemma. Codified.

- **`PresheafOfModules.pullback` is OPAQUE on `.obj`/`.map`; bypass via the adjunction transpose to the transparent `.pushforward`**
  *(NEW iter-137 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by the iter-137 PARTIAL
  prover lane on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:500`)*:
  Mathlib defines `PresheafOfModules.pullback` as
  `(pushforward ŌÜ).leftAdjoint` (file
  `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback:44`). The
  `leftAdjoint` definition is **opaque on `.obj`/`.map`** ‚ÄĒ given
  `M : PresheafOfModules S` and an open `V : DŠĶíŠĶĖ`, the chart-wise
  module `((PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ).obj M).obj V` does NOT
  reduce or unfold to a recognisable tensor-product form. This blocks
  every chart-by-chart route that needs to construct a derivation
  `D : RHS.Derivation' ŌÜ'` or a section iso `app V : LHS.obj V ‚ČÖ RHS.obj V`
  on the RHS. **Two escape routes**:
  1. **Build a `pullbackObjEquivTensor` chart-unfolding helper**
     (~30‚Äď60 LOC infrastructure): goes through
     `PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`'s unit/counit
     to express the chart-wise sections as
     `TensorProduct ((F.op ‚čô S).obj V) (R.obj V) (M.obj (F.op.obj V))`.
     Once landed, the full chart-by-chart recipe (Step 1 = chart-level
     `Algebra.IsPushout`-from-affine-product, Step 3 = chart-wise
     derivation `D_V`, Step 4 = `KaehlerDifferential.lift`, Step 5 =
     `PresheafOfModules.isoMk` assembly) becomes tractable.
  2. **Adjunction-transpose to the TRANSPARENT side**: a morphism
     `(pullback ŌÜ).obj M ‚Üí N` corresponds bijectively (via
     `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.homEquiv.symm`) to a morphism
     `M ‚Üí (pushforward ŌÜ).obj N`. The right-hand side
     `(pushforward ŌÜ).obj N` IS transparent on `.obj`/`.map`
     (`pushforward = pushforward‚āÄ ‚čô restrictScalars`, with both
     pieces carrying `@[simps]` at
     `ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:39, 86`), so a derivation /
     iso construction targeting this side proceeds without
     chart-unfolding. **Tradeoff**: this route only gives an
     `inverse` direction (RHS ‚Üí LHS) directly; the forward direction
     (LHS ‚Üí RHS) needs a separate step (e.g.,
     `isIso_of_isInverse` + a constructed forward morphism, OR
     `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_locally_surjective`-style local-iso
     argument).
  **Concrete skeleton (validated as compiling-typeable via
  `lean_run_code` iter-137)** for Route 2's inverse direction:
  ```lean
  noncomputable def basechange_inv ... := by
    let Ōą : S ‚ü∂ F.op ‚čô R := <compatibility>
    let LHS := <left-hand presheaf-of-modules>
    let MG : PresheafOfModules S := <source>
    let D : ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward Ōą).obj LHS).Derivation' ŌÜ_MG := sorry
    let univ_step := (DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' ŌÜ_MG).desc D
    let adj := PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction Ōą
    exact (adj.homEquiv MG LHS).symm univ_step
  ```
  The body reduces to a single sub-goal: construct the derivation `D`
  on the transparent `(pushforward Ōą).obj LHS` (~100‚Äď200 LOC =
  pointwise pre-composition + Leibniz + `d_app` verifications).
  **Recommended use**: prefer Route 1 (chart-unfolding helper) when
  the helper is also useful as reusable infrastructure beyond the
  immediate consumer; prefer Route 2 when the immediate iter wants
  to ship under a tight sorry-count budget and the consumer doesn't
  need the bidirectional simp lemmas `isoMk` provides.

- **`change` over `show` for definitional-equality carrier reshapes
  through `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp`**
  *(NEW iter-136 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced + codified by the iter-136
  prover lane closing
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:508`)*:
  When `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp.app ő©` merges two nested
  `PresheafOfModules.pullback` along scheme-morphism compatibility
  morphisms (built via `Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom`), the combined
  compatibility morphism it produces ‚ÄĒ
  ```
  (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f.left).hom ‚Čę
    (Opens.map f.left.base).op.whiskerLeft
      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g.left).hom
  ```
  ‚ÄĒ is **definitionally equal** to
  `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (g.left ‚Čę f.left)).hom`, by
  `Scheme.Hom.c` of a composition (`LocallyRingedSpace.comp_c`) +
  `whiskerRight` distributing over `whiskerLeft`. Lean's `change` tactic
  accepts this reshape without any rewriting; `show` does NOT (the Lean
  linter warns "Tactic 'show' has changed the goal" for genuine shape
  changes ‚ÄĒ `show` is for surface-syntax aliasing only, `change` is for
  definitional reshapes). Recipe:
  ```lean
  refine iso1 ‚Č™‚Čę ?_ ‚Č™‚Čę iso2.symm   -- iso1, iso2 from pullbackComp.app
  refine eqToIso ?_
  change (PresheafOfModules.pullback (toRingCatSheafHom (lift_lhs)).hom).obj ő© =
         (PresheafOfModules.pullback (toRingCatSheafHom (lift_rhs)).hom).obj ő©
  rw [<scheme-morphism-equality-lemma>]                                -- closes
  ```
  Net cost: ~22 LOC body + ~5 LOC for the named scheme-morphism-equality
  helper. Reusable for any "pullback-of-pullback ‚ČÖ pullback-of-pullback"
  iso whose backbone is a categorical identity at the underlying scheme
  level.

- **`refine iso1 ‚Č™‚Čę ?_ ‚Č™‚Čę iso2.symm`** for iso chains with a hole
  *(NEW iter-136 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ same prover lane as above)*:
  The `Iso.trans` API does **not** accept the literal `sorry` token in
  chained position. Writing `iso1.trans (sorry.trans iso2.symm)` triggers
  `Unknown identifier '¬ęsorry¬Ľ.trans'` (`sorry` is not a valid name in
  the dotted-method position, despite being a term elsewhere). The clean
  Mathlib alternative is the `‚Č™‚Čę`-notation form combined with `refine`:
  ```lean
  refine iso1 ‚Č™‚Čę ?_ ‚Č™‚Čę iso2.symm
  ```
  produces a clean intermediate iso goal for the hole `?_`. Use this
  whenever you need a hole in the middle of an iso composition (the
  same pattern works with `‚Čę` for morphisms via
  `refine f ‚Čę ?_ ‚Čę g`).

- **Canonical-section identity in `Over (Spec k)` for a `GrpObj G`**
  *(NEW iter-136 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `section_snd_eq_identity_struct`
  helper at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:452`)*:
  The categorical identity `s ‚Čę pr_2 = ő∑_G ‚ąė ŌÄ_G` in `Over (Spec k)`,
  where `s := lift (ūĚüô G) (toUnit G ‚Čę ő∑[G])` is the canonical section of
  the first projection of the binary product, lifts to the underlying
  scheme level as
  `s.left ‚Čę pr_2.left = G.hom ‚Čę ő∑[G].left`.
  Closable in ~5 LOC via `Mathlib`'s rfl-level identities:
  ```lean
  private lemma section_snd_eq_identity_struct
      (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] :
      (lift (ūĚüô G) (toUnit G ‚Čę ő∑[G])).left ‚Čę (snd G G).left =
        G.hom ‚Čę (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left ő∑[G]) := by
    rw [‚Üź Over.comp_left, lift_snd, Over.comp_left, Over.toUnit_left]
    rfl
  ```
  **The trailing `rfl` is load-bearing** ‚ÄĒ it bridges the human-named
  `CommaMorphism.left ő∑[G]` (which appears in docstrings) with Lean's
  auto-elaborated `ő∑.left`. These are equal but NOT syntactically
  identical to `rw`. Minimal binders (only `(G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
  [GrpObj G]`; no `n` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension` / `IsProper` /
  `GeometricallyIrreducible` ‚ÄĒ these are not needed for the categorical
  identity). The helper is the load-bearing close of any "Step 3"-shape
  argument that needs to identify the two ways of composing pullbacks
  implementing the section identity `pr_2 ‚ąė s = ő∑_G ‚ąė ŌÄ_G` at the
  presheaf-of-modules level (combined with the
  `change`-over-`pullbackComp` pattern above).

- **Binary-product shear-iso closure recipe**
  *(NEW iter-134 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced + codified by the iter-134
  prover lane on `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight` at
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:349`)*:
  When closing the inverse-pair `hom_inv_id` / `inv_hom_id` proofs of a
  binary-product shear iso `ŌÉ : G ‚äó G ‚ČÖ G ‚äó G` in a Cartesian monoidal
  category `C` over `GrpObj G` data (with `ŌÉ.hom := lift (fst G G) őľ`,
  `ŌÉ.inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ‚Čę őĻ) (snd G G) ‚Čę őľ)`), the
  Mathlib template `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight`'s bulk `simp_only`
  chain DOES NOT transfer ‚ÄĒ the binary-product version has 2 free
  inputs (`fst`, `snd`) where `mulRight` has 1, so `comp_lift_assoc`
  matches too eagerly and the goal stays out of `lift_fst_snd`-reducible
  form. Recipe that works:
  ```lean
  apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
  ¬∑ simp                                                 -- fst coord
  ¬∑ rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
    rw [‚Üź Category.assoc, comp_lift]
    rw [‚Üź Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
    -- Goal: `lift (fst G G ‚Čę őĻ) őľ ‚Čę őľ = snd G G`
    -- (or symmetric `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ‚Čę őĻ) (snd G G) ‚Čę őľ) ‚Čę őľ = snd G G`
    --  for inv_hom_id; positions of `őľ` and `őĻ` flip)
    rw [show (lift (fst G G ‚Čę őĻ) őľ ‚Čę őľ : G ‚äó G ‚ü∂ G) =
        lift (lift (fst G G ‚Čę őĻ) (fst G G) ‚Čę őľ) (snd G G) ‚Čę őľ from by
          rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
      CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left,           -- or `lift_comp_inv_right`
      MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
  ```
  **Key insight**: structural rewrites (`Category.id_comp`, `lift_snd`,
  `comp_lift`, `lift_fst`) must precede the `lift_lift_assoc` rewrite,
  AND the inner `őľ`-expansion to `lift (fst G G) (snd G G) ‚Čę őľ` must
  happen at exactly the right pattern position via a single `show`-
  equation (NOT `conv_lhs`, which matches both occurrences of `őľ`).
  This NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL contribution candidate (Mathlib has no
  packaged binary-product shear iso) can be upstreamed once stable. ~30
  LOC per inverse-pair, kernel-only axioms.

- **The `Nonempty (X ‚ČÖ X)` tautological-placeholder anti-pattern (DO NOT REPEAT)**
  *(NEW iter-134 Archon canonical NEGATIVE finding ‚ÄĒ surfaced by both
  `lean-auditor-review134` and
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review134` as
  must-fix-this-iter on 3 load-bearing declarations in
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476/508/566`)*:
  When the prover lane needs to land a named declaration for a
  multi-iter Mathlib-gap target (e.g., a deferred sheaf-level base-change
  iso), **DO NOT** ship the body as
  `theorem foo : Nonempty (X ‚ČÖ X) := ‚ü®Iso.refl _‚ü©` with the intended
  substantive type recorded only in the docstring. This pattern:
  - Compiles cleanly with kernel-only axioms (because `Iso.refl _` is
    a real term, not `sorry`).
  - Emits **zero machine-readable incompleteness signal** ‚ÄĒ
    `sorry_analyzer`, `lean_diagnostic_messages`, and `sync_leanok` all
    classify the declaration as complete; the deterministic
    `\leanok`-sync phase will add `\leanok` to the blueprint's proof
    block because the Lean compiles.
  - Triggers the strict rubric of `lean-auditor` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`
    as "weakened-wrong-definition + signature mismatch + excuse-comment"
    on the load-bearing declaration. `\notready` on the blueprint side
    + intended-type prose in the docstring **does not** clear the
    rubric.
  - Leaves the dependency surface broken: parameters like
    `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, `[IsProper G.hom]`,
    `[GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]` declared on the placeholder are
    **unused** by `Iso.refl _`, a Lean-side smoke signal of the
    body/type mismatch (Lean's unused-variable hints fire under strict
    settings).
  **PREFERRED PATTERN** when a multi-iter Mathlib-gap target needs a
  scaffold: write the **intended** type in the signature and ship the
  body as `sorry`. Mirrors `Jacobian.lean:194-215`'s
  `positiveGenusWitness` honest scaffold (audited iter-134 by
  `lean-auditor-review134` and classified as honest). Example, taking
  `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` from iter-134:
  ```lean
  -- BAD (iter-134 anti-pattern):
  theorem mulRight_globalises_cotangent (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) ... :
      Nonempty (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ‚ČÖ
                Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom) :=
    ‚ü®Iso.refl _‚ü©

  -- GOOD (honest scaffold):
  noncomputable def mulRight_globalises_cotangent
      (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) ... :
      Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ‚ČÖ
        (PresheafOfModules.pullback (ŌÜ_str G)).obj
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (ŌÜ_ő∑ G)).obj
            (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)) :=
    sorry
  ```
  The honest scaffold:
  - Emits a downstream-visible `sorry` (sorry counter increments).
  - Pins the intended signature for the iter-135+ prover lane to fill in.
  - Uses the declared instance parameters (because the intended type
    references them through `PresheafOfModules.pullback (ŌÜ_str G)`).
  - Aligns with the project's iter-127-onward scaffold convention
    (`genusZeroWitness` iter-127, `nonempty_jacobianWitness` Phase-C,
    `rigidity_over_kbar` iter-126, `positiveGenusWitness` iter-134).
  **Codified rule**: NEVER ship `Nonempty (X ‚ČÖ X)` as a stand-in for a
  substantive iso. If the iter cannot produce the intended type
  signature (because the supporting infrastructure ‚ÄĒ e.g. `ŌÜ_*`
  compatibility morphisms ‚ÄĒ does not yet exist), then either (a)
  produce the supporting infrastructure in the same iter, or (b) DEFER
  the declaration entirely until the iter that can produce it. Do not
  introduce a hollow placeholder declaration into the environment.

- **Honest sorry-bodied scaffold refactor: the canonical fix for a `Nonempty (X ‚ČÖ X) := ‚ü®Iso.refl _‚ü©` placeholder pattern**
  *(NEW iter-135 Archon canonical POSITIVE finding ‚ÄĒ the workflow
  that resolved 3 must-fix-this-iter findings from iter-134 review
  audits cleanly in 1 plan-only iter)*:
  When a prior iter has shipped a load-bearing target as
  `theorem foo : Nonempty (X ‚ČÖ X) := ‚ü®Iso.refl _‚ü©` (the iter-134
  anti-pattern; see the related Knowledge Base entry above), the
  canonical iter-N+1 remediation is a refactor lane that:
  1. **Dispatches a `mathlib-analogist` first** to verify (via
     `lean_run_code` elaboration check) that the intended substantive
     RHS type elaborates cleanly against the current project state.
     This is decision (D) of a 4-part analogist verdict structure
     ((A) which Mathlib idiom to use; (B) literal Lean text for the
     intended types; (C) whether to introduce new project `def`s for
     the compatibility morphisms; (D) `lean_run_code` elaboration
     check). Produces a persistent file at `analogies/<slug>.md`.
  2. **Lands a refactor lane** swapping each placeholder
     `theorem ... : Nonempty (X ‚ČÖ X) := ‚ü®Iso.refl _‚ü©` to a
     `noncomputable def ... : <intended-substantive-RHS> := sorry`.
     The signature is now what the math claims; the `sorry` body
     emits a downstream-visible incompleteness signal that
     `sorry_analyzer`, `lean_diagnostic_messages`, and `sync_leanok`
     can all see.
  3. **Has no prover dispatch in the refactor iter**
     (`planValidate.status: ok_intentional_skip`,
     `prover.durationSecs: 0`). The next iter dispatches the prover
     lane against the corrected signatures.
  4. **Codifies the convention** in a section docstring at the
     refactor's edit site naming the canonical compatibility-morphism
     idiom (in the iter-135 instance: `Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom`
     for `PresheafOfModules.pullback`, contrasted with the
     adjunction-transpose helper) so future iters cannot accidentally
     re-introduce the placeholder pattern.
  **Iter-135 instance**: closed 3 must-fix-this-iter findings (3
  weakened-wrong placeholders at `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476/508/566`
  ‚Üí 3 honest sorry-bodied scaffolds at L468/L496/L560) in 1
  plan-only refactor + writer iter. Sorry count 4 ‚Üí 6 ‚ÄĒ but BOTH
  review-phase audits (`lean-auditor-review135`,
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review135`)
  independently classified the refactor as a "legitimate honesty
  improvement" (`lean-auditor` verbatim verdict). The 4 ‚Üí 6 increase
  reflects 3 placeholder-pattern declarations becoming machine-
  readable as incomplete (which they always were ‚ÄĒ `Iso.refl _`
  hides the incompleteness from `sorry_analyzer`). **Net semantic-
  health delta: materially positive.** **Documented at
  `iter/iter-135/plan.md`, `iter/iter-135/review.md`, and
  `analogies/phi-compatibility-morphisms.md`.**

- **Plan-phase deepening over low-quality prover dispatch when a HARD GATE fires**
  *(NEW iter-133 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ the workflow pattern that produced
  a clean iter-133 close despite no prover lane)*:
  When the iter's mandatory `blueprint-reviewer` returns a HARD GATE
  DEFER on a planned prover target (because the corresponding blueprint
  section is under-spec'd ‚ÄĒ missing Lean signature stub, name-vs-
  construction ambiguity, missing named Mathlib lemma for a helper),
  the correct iter shape is **plan-phase deepening** (parallel
  blueprint-writer to harden the section + parallel mathlib-analogist
  if a design decision is needed + parallel refactor for any
  stale-framing cleanup the iter-N-1 review-phase audit flagged) and
  an `ok_intentional_skip` recorded in `meta.json`'s
  `planValidate.status`. The 1-iter latency cost of staging the prover
  lane is small compared to the cost of formalizing a broken blueprint
  the next iter throws away. Iter-133 instance: blueprint-reviewer's
  HARD GATE on `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` triggered a parallel
  blueprint-writer (chapter 324 ‚Üí 511 LOC) + mathlib-analogist (PROCEED
