  + ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB on sheaf-level RHS, persistent file at
  `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`) + refactor (5 docstring
  refreshes + 1 style nit on `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`); iter-134
  prover lane is now staged with bills-of-materials decomposed into
  4 sub-pieces (~30‚Äď60 + ~150‚Äď300 + ~30‚Äď80 + ~30‚Äď60 LOC = 210‚Äď440 LOC
  envelope). **Rule**: when a blueprint-reviewer's HARD GATE DEFER
  fires, do NOT silently dispatch the prover anyway "to make progress";
  the iter should pivot to plan-phase deepening + record the skip in
  PROGRESS.md's `## Current Objectives` with `(no prover dispatch this
  iter ‚ÄĒ see iter/iter-NNN/plan.md for rationale)`. **Documented at
  `iter/iter-133/plan.md` and `iter/iter-133/review.md`.**

- **Sheaf-level RHS phrasing avoids chart-non-canonicity cocycle issues for `mulRight`-equivariance of cotangent constructions**
  *(NEW iter-133 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by
  `mathlib-analogist-mulright-globalises-iter133` Decision 4 +
  documented at `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`)*:
  When the project needs to express `mulRight`-equivariance of a
  cotangent-like construction on a smooth group scheme `G/k`, the
  iter-133 mathlib-analogist recommends phrasing the RHS at the
  **`PresheafOfModules` (sheaf) level** ‚ÄĒ `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf
  G.hom ‚ČÖ (PresheafOfModules.pullback (ŌÜ_str G)).obj ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (ŌÜ_ő∑ G)).obj (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom))`
  ‚ÄĒ rather than at the **value level** `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G`.
  Reason: the iter-131 chart-base-changed body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
  depends on a chart `V` extracted via `Classical.choose` from the
  `smooth_locally_free_omega` existential. Under right-translation
  `mulRight_g`, the chart set `mulRight_g(V)` need not equal `V`,
  creating a chart-set cocycle concern at the value level. Phrasing
  the RHS at the sheaf-of-modules level puts the ŌÉ-iso composition
  entirely at the presheaf-of-modules level where the chart non-
  canonicity does not enter, AND decouples the (chart-localisation
  identification) bridge to `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` into a separate
  ~100‚Äď200 LOC lemma consumed in piece (i.c), NOT inside piece (i.b)'s
  body. The 4-step closure chain (shear iso ‚Üí base-change of
  differentials ‚Üí restriction along identity section ‚Üí main
  composition) all lives at the presheaf level. Iter-133 instance:
  the recommendation pinned piece (i.b)'s envelope at 210‚Äď440 LOC
  (NOT the 300‚Äď800 LOC value-level variant). The companion side-
  effect: iter-130 `strategy-critic` Q2 worry about a deferred
  `(B)‚Üí(A)` bridge resurfacing is REFUTED ‚ÄĒ the sheaf-level RHS
  bypasses it entirely. **Documented at
  `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md` Decision 4 and
  `iter/iter-133/plan.md` ¬ß "Response to critics".**

- **`Classical.choose`-chain parallel extraction for rank lemmas against opaque body shapes**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ the technique that closed
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` in ~40 LOC after 4 iters of
  body-shape work)*:
  When the body of a `noncomputable def` is built from a chain of
  `Classical.choose` / `Classical.choose_spec` extractions on an
  existential `h : ‚ąÉ X, P X`, a downstream rank-style lemma whose goal
  targets the body's underlying carrier (e.g. `Module.finrank k (someDef G) = n`)
  can be proven by **reconstructing the same chain locally inside the
  lemma's proof**. Because the same `Classical.choose` call (with the
  same arguments to `h`) produces the *same* term (Lean's definitional
  equality is reflexive on `Classical.choose`-applied-to-the-same-
  proof), the goal's `someDef G` and the proof's
  `(ModuleCat.extendScalars Ōą).obj (ModuleCat.of ‚Ä¶ ő©[‚Ä¶])` form share the
  same underlying carrier ‚ÄĒ exposable via `change` to the explicit
  `TensorProduct ‚Ä¶` form via `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'`'s
  definitional unfolding. Recipe:
  ```lean
  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x‚āÄ
  set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose with hU_def
  set V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose with hV_def
  set e : V ‚Č§ G.hom ‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose with he_def
  have hxV : x‚āÄ ‚ąą V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
  have hfree : Module.Free ‚Ä¶ := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
  have hrank : Module.rank ‚Ä¶ = ‚ÜĎn := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
  -- (install algebra structures via letI, ŌąV via let, Nontrivial via haveI ‚Ä¶)
  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ‚Ä¶ k ő©[‚Ä¶]) = n
  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ‚Ä¶) (M' := ő©[‚Ä¶])]
  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
  ```
  This avoids the cost of building a `_eq_extendScalars`-style strong
  acceptance lemma + `obtain ‚ü®U, V, e, htop, heq‚ü© := acceptance` +
  `rw [heq]` chain (which iter-131 also installs in
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:198` as a defensive
  alternative). **Key insight**: `Classical.choose` is opaque but
  *deterministic on equal proofs*; two `Classical.choose`-chains on
  the same existential reduce to definitionally-equal terms. This is
  precisely the structural property the iter-131 body refactor
  delivered (per iter-131 mathlib-analogist
  `analogies/cotangent-body-shape.md`); iter-132 is the closure
  test that proved the design works for downstream rank lemmas.

- **`RingHom.domain_nontrivial` discharges `Nontrivial` instance on the source of a ring map into a field**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ closed the `StrongRankCondition`
  instance hypothesis chain for `Module.finrank_baseChange`)*:
  When `Module.finrank_baseChange` (or similar Mathlib finrank-base-
  change lemmas) requires `[Nontrivial S]` on the source ring `S` of a
  base-change `S ‚Üí R`, and the project context provides only a ring
  map `(someRingMap : S ‚Üí+* R)` into a (nontrivial) field `R`, the
  one-liner discharge is:
  ```lean
  haveI : Nontrivial S := someRingMap.domain_nontrivial
  ```
  Combined with Mathlib's `commRing_strongRankCondition` instance
  (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.FreeModule.StrongRankCondition`), this
  completes the `StrongRankCondition S` hypothesis chain that
  `Module.finrank_baseChange` requires. Much cleaner than going
  through `IsAffineOpen V`-of-a-nonempty-affine reasoning. Iter-132
  instance: discharged `Nontrivial őď(G.left, V)` via
  `ŌąV.hom.domain_nontrivial` where `ŌąV : őď(G.left, V) ‚ü∂ CommRingCat.of k`.

- **`change` vs `show` for definitional-equality carrier shifts**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lean linter warning surfaced during
  the iter-132 rank-lemma close, resolved by swapping to `change`)*:
  When a goal's carrier (e.g. `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G : ModuleCat k`)
  is *definitionally equal* but not syntactically equal to a target
  form (e.g. `TensorProduct őď(G,V) k ő©[‚Ä¶]`), use `change` rather than
  `show`. The Lean linter actively warns on `show` when it observes
  the tactic actually shifting the goal:
  > The `show` tactic should only be used to indicate intermediate
  > goal states for readability. However, this tactic invocation
  > changed the goal.
  `change` is the right primitive for genuine def-eq carrier shifts;
  `show` is reserved for verbatim restatement to aid readability.
  Both tactics share the same semantic effect (def-eq goal swap) but
  Lean treats them as structurally different. Iter-132 instance:
  `show Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ‚Ä¶) = n` ‚Üí warning;
  `change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ‚Ä¶) = n` ‚Üí silent. Both
  closed the proof; only `change` is linter-clean.

- **`Classical.choice (őĪ := X) ?_` is the standard escape for "Type-valued `def` body depending on a `Prop`-level existential", but it discards content**
  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced as the critical iter-130
  finding flagged by `lean-auditor-review130` must-fix #1)*:
  When a `def` returning a `Type` (e.g. `ModuleCat k`) needs to use data
  from a `Prop`-level existential `‚ąÉ U V, P U V`, the elaborator
  forbids `obtain ‚ü®U, V, h‚ü© := proof` directly with
  `recursor Exists.casesOn can only eliminate into Prop`. The standard
  workaround:
  ```lean
  refine Classical.choice (őĪ := X) ?_
  obtain ‚ü®U, V, h‚ü© := proof  -- now legal: Prop goal context Nonempty X
  exact ‚ü®theTermYouWant U V h‚ü©
  ```
  **The result is NOT definitionally equal to `theTermYouWant U V h`** ‚ÄĒ
  it is some unspecified inhabitant of `X` chosen by `Classical.choice`.
  Only `Nonempty X` is structurally recoverable; any downstream lemma
  about the structure of the inhabitant CANNOT be proved by unfolding
  the `def` and applying a fact about `theTermYouWant U V h`. **Use
  `Classical.indefiniteDescription` / chained `Classical.choose` /
  `Classical.choose_spec`** if any downstream proof needs to access
  the destructured data. Iter-130 instance:
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` body wrapped the
  explicit chart-base-changed K√§hler module
  `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ŌąV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of ‚Ä¶ ő©[‚Ä¶‚ĀĄ‚Ä¶])` in
  `Classical.choice (őĪ := ModuleCat k)`. The body type-checks, passes
  `lean_verify` with kernel-only axioms, AND satisfies the
  progress-critic's vacuity-rule-out acceptance test (references
  `smooth_locally_free_omega`, 40 LOC, not simp-only) ‚ÄĒ yet the
  iter-129+ rank lemma `finrank cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G = n` is
  **unprovable against this body** for the same structural reason
  iter-128's was. **Lesson**: in addition to "kernel-clean ‚Č†
  mathematically correct" (iter-129 lesson #2), kernel-clean +
  acceptance-test-pass also do not guarantee structural-content-
  delivery to downstream consumers. A `Type`-valued `def` whose body
  uses an outer `Classical.choice` wrapper is opaque to definitional
  unfolding; rank/free/finite/dimension claims do not transport
  through it. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_130/summary.md` and ¬ß
  recommendations.md CRITICAL block.**

- **Lattice symbols (`‚ä§`, `‚ä•`, `‚äď`, `‚äĒ`) cannot be part of identifiers in tactic blocks**
  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced as a parse-error during
  the iter-130 body swap)*:
  Hypothesis names like `h‚ä§` parse partially in tactic blocks ‚ÄĒ Lean
  tokenises the `‚ä§` as the lattice top symbol and aborts the parse
  with `unexpected token '‚ä§'; expected ':=' or '|'`. Use ASCII-only
  identifiers (`htop`, `hbot`, `hsup`, `hinf`) for hypothesis names in
  tactic blocks. This is distinct from term-mode definitions (where
  `‚ä§` survives, with surrounding whitespace), and from operator
  notations (where the symbol is a valid token). **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_130/summary.md`.**

- **The unique-point collapse for `Spec` of a field: `(s : ‚Ü•(Spec (.of k)))` is always `default` via `Subsingleton.elim`**
  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced during the iter-130 body
  swap when establishing `(‚ä§ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ‚Č§ ő∑left ‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ V`)*:
  For `k` a field, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`
  provides `Unique ‚Ü•(Spec (.of k))`, so any two terms of
  `‚Ü•(Spec (.of k))` are equal via `Subsingleton.elim s default`. This
  turns any obligation of the form `s ‚ąą U` for a point `s :
  ‚Ü•(Spec (.of k))` and an open `U ‚äÜ Spec (.of k)` containing the
  unique point into a 1‚Äď2 line proof:
  ```lean
  intro s _
  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
  exact <proof that default ‚ąą U>
  ```
  Companion: `PrimeSpectrum.instUnique` in
  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.Basic` gives the corresponding
  `Unique (PrimeSpectrum R)` instance for `[Field R]`. Reusable
  anywhere a proof needs to dispatch a `Spec k`-side point obligation.
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_130/summary.md`.**

- **Presheaf-pullback at the top open of `Spec k` collapses to `k`; evaluating a presheaf-of-modules pullback along a `Spec k ‚Üí X` morphism at `‚ä§_X` discards the `X`-side structure**
  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced as a critical CORRECTION
  to iter-128 pattern #2; recorded by `mathlib-analogist-lieAlgebra-rank-bridge-iter129`)*:
  For `f : X ‚ü∂ Spec k` (so `f.base : X.topology ‚Üí Spec k`),
  `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (op ‚ä§)`
  is by definition the pointwise left Kan extension colimit over the
  indexing diagram `{V : Opens(Spec k) | f.base(‚ä§_X) ‚äÜ V}`. Since
  `Spec k` is single-point (only opens are `‚ąÖ` and `‚ä§`), the indexing
  diagram has a unique element `V = ‚ä§_{Spec k}`, and the colimit
  collapses to `őď(Spec k, ‚ä§) ‚ČÖ k` via `Scheme.őďSpecIso`. **Consequence
  for the iter-128 `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` construction**:
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom` evaluated at `op ‚ä§` is, via the
  `rfl` identification `relativeDifferentials'_obj`, the K√§hler module
  of `ŌÜ'.app (op ‚ä§) : k ‚Üí őď(G, ‚ä§)`. For smooth proper geometrically
  irreducible `G/k`, smoothness ‚áí geometrically reduced ‚áí geometrically
  integral ‚áí `őď(G, ‚ä§) = k` (Stacks 0BUG; Mathlib's
  `AlgebraicGeometry.isField_of_universallyClosed`). So
  `ŌÜ'.app (op ‚ä§) : k ‚Üí k` is essentially the identity, and
  `KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective` gives `ő©[k/k] = 0`;
  the iter-128 body `(ModuleCat.extendScalars Ōą.hom).obj 0 = 0` (the
  zero `k`-module) for the entire target class. **The iter-128 pattern
  #2** (`ModuleCat.extendScalars` of `presheaf.obj (op ‚ä§)` is the
  global-sections form of a presheaf-of-modules pullback) is
  **correct as an algebraic construction but mathematically degenerate**
  for the cotangent-at-identity application because the proper geom-irr
  hypothesis forces all global sections to land in `k`. Anyone needing
  a non-zero pullback-of-modules along a section over a proper
  geom-irr scheme must work either (a) on an affine chart `V ‚ąč ő∑(pt)`
  via `smooth_locally_free_omega` (Replacement B in
  `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`); (b) stalk-side via
  `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` (Replacement A; 500-1000 LOC);
  or (c) by sheafifying first (Replacement C; 800-2000 LOC). **Documented
  at `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md` (full 5-step diagnostic +
  three-replacement comparison) and `proof-journal/sessions/session_129/summary.md`
  ¬ß "Critical iter-129 discovery".**

- **Soundness lesson: kernel-clean does not imply mathematically correct**
  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced when the iter-128
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` body, certified kernel-only by `lean_verify`
  with `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, was discovered to
  compute the zero `k`-module for the target class)*:
  `lean_verify <decl>` returning only kernel axioms certifies that the
  **proof** uses only kernel-allowed axioms (no `sorryAx`, no project
  axiom, no named-axiom escalation). It does **not** certify that the
  **statement** is mathematical content as intended. Definitions
  whose bodies type-check can encode the zero object, the empty type,
  or any other vacuous construction when the math claims something
  substantive. **Rule for future scaffolding rounds**: when a new
  declaration's Lean construction differs structurally from the
  blueprint's proof sketch (here: evaluate-then-extend-scalars on a
  *presheaf* vs the `ūĚĒ™/ūĚĒ™¬≤` *stalk* presentation the blueprint
  diagrams), dispatch a `mathlib-analogist` consult **before** locking
  the construction into a refactor ‚ÄĒ even if `lake build` and
  `lean_verify` are both clean. The iter-128 review-phase
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review128` flagged the
  bridge gap abstractly; the iter-129 analogist consult sharpened it
  to a concrete vacuity claim. **Lesson**: file-vs-blueprint bridges
  that exist in prose ("the bridge will be a future lemma") must be
  treated as load-bearing requirements that may not exist in Mathlib;
  if a `mathlib-analogist` cannot name the bridge, the construction
  needs revision. **Documented at `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
  Decision 1 + `proof-journal/sessions/session_129/summary.md` ¬ß Key findings.**

- **`Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` is the canonical Mathlib name** (not `IsRegularLocalRing.cotangentSpace`)
  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ minor; surfaced when the iter-129
  strategy-critic + blueprint-reviewer flagged the phantom name)*:
  The local-ring cotangent space `ūĚĒ™/ūĚĒ™¬≤` is `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R`
  in Mathlib `b80f227`, defined as `(maximalIdeal R).Cotangent` in
  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent`. The names
  `IsRegularLocalRing.cotangentSpace` and `LocalRing.cotangentSpace`
  do **not** exist ‚ÄĒ `lean_local_search "cotangentSpace"` returns
  only `Algebra.Generators.cotangentSpaceBasis`,
  `Algebra.Extension.CotangentSpace`, and
  `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace`. For the regular-local-ring
  finrank claim, the bridge is `IsRegularLocalRing` ‚Üí
  `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` (not via `IsRegularLocalRing`'s
  own namespace). **Replace any future blueprint or strategy prose
  citing the phantom name.** Caught + corrected in iter-129's
  `RigidityKbar.tex` rewrite by `blueprint-writer-rigiditykbar-iter129`.

- **`Over.left` of the cartesian terminal object is definitionally the base; `CommaMorphism.left ő∑[G]` typechecks as a scheme morphism for free**
  *(NEW iter-128 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced during `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.lieAlgebra` body closure)*:
  In `Over (Spec (.of k))` with the cartesian monoidal structure, the
  unit object `(ūĚüô_ (Over (Spec (.of k))))` has `.left = Spec (.of k)`
  by definition (via the `Over` terminal-object construction). This
  means that for any `[CategoryTheory.GrpObj G]` over `Spec k`, the
  scheme-level identity section is recoverable by ONE LINE:
  ```lean
  let ő∑left : Spec (.of k) ‚ü∂ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left ő∑[G]
  ```
  No explicit coercion, no `Iso.hom`, no `letI`-typeclass jiggling.
  Reusable across the project for any `Over (Spec R)` `MonObj` /
  `GrpObj` whose scheme-level structure needs extracting. The `ő∑[G]`
  notation is the `MonObj.one G : ūĚüô_ ‚ü∂ G` notation from
  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Mon_`. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_128/summary.md` ¬ß Per-target
  detail and ¬ß Key findings.**

- **`ModuleCat.extendScalars` of `presheaf.obj (op ‚ä§)` is the global-sections form of a presheaf-of-modules pullback along a scheme section**
  *(NEW iter-128 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced during `lieAlgebra` body closure as the cheaper alternative to `PresheafOfModules.pullback`)*:
  When the project needs to pull back a `PresheafOfModules` `M` on a
  scheme `X` along a section `ő∑ : Spec k ‚ü∂ X` and only the global
  sections are needed, the cheap form is:
  ```lean
  let Ōą : őď(X, ‚ä§) ‚ü∂ CommRingCat.of k :=
    ő∑.appTop ‚Čę (Scheme.őďSpecIso (.of k)).hom
  (ModuleCat.extendScalars Ōą.hom).obj (M.obj (op ‚ä§))
  ```
  This evaluates the presheaf at the top open (a
  `ModuleCat (őď(X, ‚ä§))`), then extends scalars along `Ōą` (tensoring
  with `k` over `őď(X, ‚ä§)`) to land in `ModuleCat k`. **Crucially,
  this bypasses the full `PresheafOfModules.pullback` functor**,
  whose `(pushforward ŌÜ).IsRightAdjoint` instance is non-trivial in
  the scheme setting. Reusable any time the project needs a presheaf
  of modules' value at a section's image at the base level. Caveat:
  this is the global-sections form, NOT the canonical
  `colim`-over-open-neighborhoods pullback; the two agree for
  proper geometrically irreducible schemes (where `őď(X, O_X) = k`)
  but disagree on general presheaves. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_128/summary.md` ¬ß Per-target
  detail and ¬ß Key findings.**

- **Soundness lesson: "smallest signature surface" framing in a refactor directive can produce over-specialised signatures the prover then locks in**
  *(NEW iter-128 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced post-prover via
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review128` MUST-FIX
  S-1 on `lieAlgebra`'s `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 G.hom]`
  hardcoding)*:
  When a refactor lane scaffolds a new declaration whose blueprint
  prose is dimension/property-agnostic (e.g. "smooth group scheme"
  with NO dimension constraint), the directive must pin a CONCRETE
  Lean signature stub (not just prose). The iter-128 directive said
  piece (i.a) has "smallest signature surface" but left dimension
  unspecified; the prover landed `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 G.hom]`,
  which is over-specialised relative to the blueprint AND makes the
  declaration unusable for the downstream consumer
  (`rigidity_over_kbar` applied to an abelian variety of arbitrary
  relative dimension). **Rule for future scaffolding refactors**:
  the directive's "reference body" code block MUST exactly specify
  the desired signature (e.g. `{n : ‚Ąē} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n
  G.hom]` to share the dimension parameter with the iter-129+ rank
  lemma, or `[Smooth G.hom]` for the most-general form). Encoding
  notes that grant "prover discretion" should be paired with a
  concrete stub example, not just prose. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_128/recommendations.md`
  CRITICAL #1 and ¬ß Strategic.**

- **`AlgEquiv.ofBijective` + `IsLocalization.lift_{injective,surjective}_iff` is the canonical narrowing step for "construct an AlgEquiv from an `IsLocalization.lift`"**
  *(NEW iter-124 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced during M1.b `commutes'`-closure + bijectivity narrowing on `appLE_isLocalization`)*:
  When the forward map is already an `IsLocalization.lift hg` to a
  target `B` whose algebra structure is `g.toAlgebra`, the
  cleanest way to produce an `AlgEquiv` is to promote `forward` to
  an explicit `AlgHom` (`forwardAlg`) and reduce the residual to
  `Function.Bijective ‚áĎforwardAlg` via `AlgEquiv.ofBijective`. The
  bijectivity then decomposes via
  `IsLocalization.lift_injective_iff` (gives a precise injectivity
  criterion in terms of `algebraMap` equality vs `g` equality) and
  `IsLocalization.lift_surjective_iff` (gives a precise surjectivity
  criterion in terms of `v * g x.2 = g x.1` representability). This
  avoids the alternative route via `RingEquiv.ofRingHom backward +
  AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv` that requires constructing the backward
  map directly and verifying two inverse identities. Iter-124
  instance: `Differentials.lean:345-398` reduces the iter-123
  `Localization M ‚ČÉ‚āź[őď(S, U)] A_colim` AlgEquiv residual to
  `Function.Bijective ‚áĎforwardAlg` in 1 substantive Edit, keeping
  the per-file sorry count at exactly 1 and packaging Steps 2 + 3
  of the M1.b chain into a single named bijectivity claim. **Pairs
  naturally with the next pattern**: `commutes'` closes in 1 LOC
  via `RingHom.congr_fun`.
  ```lean
  let forwardAlg : Localization M ‚Üí‚āź[A] B :=
    { forward with
      commutes' := by
        intro r
        have h := RingHom.congr_fun h_fwd_comp r
        exact h }
  exact AlgEquiv.ofBijective forwardAlg ?_
  -- residual: Function.Bijective ‚áĎforwardAlg
  ```
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_124/summary.md`
  ¬ß Per-target detail and ¬ß Key findings**.

- **`RingHom.congr_fun h_fwd_comp r` closes the `commutes'` field of an AlgHom when target algebra is `someRingHom.toAlgebra`**
  *(NEW iter-124 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced during the iter-124 `forwardAlg` promotion)*:
  When (i) the target algebra structure is constructed from a ring
  map via `letI : Algebra A B := ringHom.toAlgebra` (or a top-level
  `@[reducible] noncomputable def`), and (ii) you have a fact
  `f.comp (algebraMap A C) = ringHom`, then the `commutes'` field
  of `{ f with commutes' := ... } : ... ‚Üí‚āź[A] B` closes in 1 LOC:
  ```lean
  commutes' := by
    intro r
    have h := RingHom.congr_fun h_fwd_comp r
    exact h
  ```
  The `RingHom.congr_fun` produces `f (algebraMap r) = ringHom r`;
  the goal needs `f (algebraMap r) = algebraMap r` on the RHS; the
  two RHS forms are defeq via the unfolding of
  `RingHom.toAlgebra`'s `algebraMap`, so `exact h` succeeds without
  any `show`/`change` rewrite. Companion to the iter-122 helper-
  extraction pattern (lifting `appLE_colimAlgebra` to a top-level
  `@[reducible] noncomputable def` makes the defeq available
  wherever needed). Iter-124 instance: `Differentials.lean:349-354`.
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_124/summary.md`
  ¬ß Key findings**.

- **`suffices` reduction to consolidate multi-step inverse-construction PARTIALs into a single residual sorry**
  *(NEW iter-123 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced during M1.b Step 1 + Step 4 closure on `appLE_isLocalization`)*:
  When a multi-step proof has steps 1 + N immediately constructible
  and intermediate steps jointly required to build a single object
  (an iso, an `AlgEquiv`, a `LinearEquiv`), use `suffices` to reduce
  to that object. This keeps the file's sorry count at exactly 1
  (vs. splitting the residual into separate sorries for each
  remaining step), AND makes the iter-N+1 prover's target explicitly
  the intermediate object. Companion to iter-119's "structurally-
  minimal residual-sorry cascade" (`constructor / all_goals first | exact _ | sorry`).
  ```lean
  -- Step 1: build the forward direction (concrete code).
  have forward_data : ... := ...
  -- Step N: reduce to the existential via a Mathlib constructor.
  suffices intermediate : SomeIsoType from
    Mathlib.someClosingLemma forward_data intermediate
  -- Steps 2 ‚Ä¶ N-1: the residual (single sorry here).
  sorry
  ```
  Iter-123 instance: `Differentials.lean:328‚Äď331` reduces
  `IsLocalization M A_colim` via
  `IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv M AE`
  to the single residual `AlgEquiv` `Localization M ‚ČÉ‚āź[őď(S, U)] A_colim`.
  Companion: `IsLocalization.lift_comp` captures the compatibility
  witness `(IsLocalization.lift hg).comp algebraMap = g` for free
  ‚ÄĒ use it to package the forward-map-composed-with-algebra-map
  fact for downstream Step-3 inverse-identity verification and
  Step-4 algebra-map compatibility. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_123/summary.md` ¬ß Key findings**.

- **`IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv` (not `_of_le` or `_of_ringEquiv`) is the canonical Step-4 closer for "colim of localizations" ‚Üí `IsLocalization`**
  *(NEW iter-123 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ verified by iter-123 M1.b tactical mathlib-analogist)*:
  When proving `IsLocalization M B` via a constructed AlgEquiv
  `Localization M ‚ČÉ‚āź[A] B` (the cleanest output of the two-direction
  forward/backward construction documented in the iter-121 Knowledge
  Base entry), the right Mathlib closer is
  `IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv M : (Localization M ‚ČÉ‚āź[A] B) ‚Üí IsLocalization M B`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic:304`).
  The iter-122 plan-phase recommendation listed `IsLocalization.of_le` ‚ÄĒ
  that lemma is for the *different* shape "extend an existing `IsLocalization
  M S` to `IsLocalization N S` for `M ‚Č§ N`" ‚ÄĒ and is NOT directly applicable
  when only an iso is in hand. The "`isLocalization_iff_of_ringEquiv`"
  alternative is iff-shaped (less direct). The constructor requires an
  `AlgEquiv` (not a `RingEquiv`); package via `AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv` from
  a `RingEquiv` + algebra-map compatibility (the iter-123
  `Differentials.lean:348‚Äď361` recipe sketch documents the assembly).
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_123/summary.md` ¬ß Key findings**.

- **`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fails on `Lan`-defined functors; pre-prove `hmc` with `Functor.map_comp _ _ _` and use `erw [hmc]`**
  *(NEW iter-122 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced during M1.b Step 0 closure)*:
  Goals shaped
  `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).map (g‚āĀ ‚Čę g‚āā) = ...`
  cannot be advanced by `rw [Functor.map_comp]` (the rewriter's
  pattern match fails despite the printed-pattern-vs-target equality
  being literal). Diagnosis: the `Lan`-defined functor instance
  carries category-instance metadata that the rewriter's unification
  doesn't see through. **Workaround**: pre-prove the rewrite as a
  `have`, then use `erw` instead of `rw`:
  ```
  have hmc : Pf.map (g‚āĀ ‚Čę g‚āā) = Pf.map g‚āĀ ‚Čę Pf.map g‚āā :=
    Functor.map_comp _ _ _
  erw [hmc]
  ```
  Companion lessons:
  - `rw [Category.assoc]` similarly fails on the same goals;
    use `exact Category.assoc _ _ _` directly when the goal
    is exactly the associativity equation.
  - `adj.unit.naturality` produces an equation involving the identity
    functor `((ūĚü≠ _).obj _)`; use `simpa using
    ((adj.unit.app S.presheaf)).naturality _` to clean it up.
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_122/summary.md`
  ¬ß Key findings.**

- **`change`/`show` on `algebraMap g` fails even when the algebra is `ŌÜ.hom.toAlgebra`; route via `IsUnit` + `exact` instead**
  *(NEW iter-122 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced during M1.b Step 0 closure)*:
  When `letI : Algebra A B := ŌÜ.hom.toAlgebra` is in scope and the
  goal involves `algebraMap A B g`, the `change` and `show` tactics
  fail to unify the goal with a form involving `ŌÜ.hom g` directly,
  even though they ARE definitionally equal (`'change' tactic
  failed, pattern ... is not definitionally equal to target`).
  **Workaround**: route the conclusion via the equation form
  ```
  have h_alg_unfold : (algebraMap A B) g = ŌÜ.hom g := rfl
  rw [h_alg_unfold]
  ```
  or via `RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra` when applicable. The `rfl`
  succeeds at the `have`-level even though `change` fails at the
  goal-level. For `IsUnit (algebraMap A B g)`-shaped goals,
  alternatively route through `IsUnit (ŌÜ.hom g)` first via a
  helper `have h_factor`, then `exact` the result with the
  defeq absorbed. Reusable across the project.
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_122/summary.md`
  ¬ß Key findings.**

- **Helper-lemma extraction pattern: name top-level decls instead of inlining `letI`-stubs to make consumers re-usable**
  *(NEW iter-122 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ pattern paid off in M1 closure)*:
  When the plan-phase refactor introduces a sorry-bearing declaration
  whose body contains a `letI : Algebra A B := sorry` or similar
  typeclass instance, the temptation is to close the `letI` inline
  in the same iter. The better pattern is to lift the algebra
  structure out into a top-level `noncomputable def` (e.g.
  `appLE_colimAlgebra := (appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom.toAlgebra`),
  which makes the algebra re-usable across every consumer's scope.
  Companion: when the proof itself decomposes into "step 0 sub-claim
  + steps 1‚ÄďN main", extract step 0 as a named helper theorem
  (e.g. `isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim`) before the main proof
  body; the named helper is then directly invokable as a hypothesis
  to `IsLocalization.lift` (Step 1) and similar consumers in the
  same iter or downstream iters. The iter-122 M1 closure used this
  pattern with `appLE_colimRingHom`, `appLE_colimAlgebra`,
  `appLE_colimRingHom_comp_ŌÜV`, `isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim`
  ‚ÄĒ making the bridge body `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE`
  close in a single Edit after the helpers landed. Reusable across
  the project (cf. the iter-098 split-slot pattern with
  `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`).

- **`Functor.Final` is the WRONG framing for "directed colimit of localizations = localization at the union submonoid"; use `IsLocalization.of_le` with cocone universality**
  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by `mathlib-analogist-bridge-iter121`)*:
  the natural-looking colim-comparison shape
  ```
  colim_{g ‚ąą M, in the basic-open-cocone} A_g  ‚ČÉ  Localization M A
  ```
  has no off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma in `b80f227` (verified via
  `lean_leansearch`, `lean_loogle`, `lean_local_search` on multiple
  variants). Trying to use `CategoryTheory.Functor.Final` to reduce
  to a cofinality-of-indexing-category argument leads into Mathlib
  territory that doesn't exist. The cleanly Mathlib-aligned path is
  to **bypass the colim-comparison entirely** and prove
  `IsLocalization M A_colim` directly:
  (a) Construct `A_M ‚Üí A_colim` via `Localization M`'s universal
      property (after showing each `g ‚ąą M` is a unit in `A_colim`).
  (b) Construct `A_colim ‚Üí A_M` via the colim cocone's universal
      property, picking basic-open refinements `D(g) ‚äÜ W` on each
      open and mapping `őď(S, W) ‚Üí őď(S, D(g)) = A_g ‚Üí A_M`.
  (c) Verify the composites are identity via
      `IsLocalization.ringHom_ext`.
  (d) Conclude `IsLocalization M A_colim` via `IsLocalization.of_le`
      (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Defs`:
      `[IsLocalization M S] ‚Üí (h : ‚ąÄ n ‚ąą N, IsUnit (algebraMap n)) ‚Üí IsLocalization N S`).
  Reusable for any "directed colimit of localizations = localization
  at union" claim in Lean. **Documented at
  `analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md` and
  `STRATEGY.md ¬ß Roadmap M1` (iter-121).**

- **`KaehlerDifferential` of a localization is `Subsingleton` via `FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization` ‚ÄĒ NOT a Mathlib gap**
  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ surfaced by `mathlib-analogist-bridge-iter121`)*:
  the claim "`ő©[L‚ĀĄA]` is subsingleton when `A ‚Üí L` is a localization"
  is NOT a Mathlib gap, contrary to the iter-121 first-draft blueprint.
  Mathlib supplies it as the 2-line composition:
  ```
  [IsLocalization M L]
    ‚Üí Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization M
         (Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic:303)
    ‚Üí @Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential _ _ _ _ _ _
         (Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic:57-59, instance)
  ```
  Use this whenever a proof needs `ő©[L/A] = 0` from a localization
  hypothesis; do NOT carry it around as a standalone `\lean{...}`-tagged
  declaration. The corollary "K√§hler module of a quotient-localization
  iso" (`tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale`-style: `ő©[B/A] ‚ČÉ‚āó[B] ő©[B/L]`
  for `L = Localization M A`) is ~10‚Äď30 LOC via
  `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` +
  `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`, and is the most extractable
  Mathlib contribution candidate from M1 (generalises
  `tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale` to "only the lower step is
  formally unramified"). **Documented at
  `analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md` and
  `STRATEGY.md ¬ß Roadmap M1` (iter-121).**

- **Proactive `mathlib-analogist` dispatch BEFORE the bridge ships pays for itself in saved refactor cost**
  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical)*: dispatching `mathlib-analogist`
  proactively against a not-yet-shipped infrastructure declaration
  (the M1 bridge `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE`)
  returned 5 `ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB` + 1 `NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL`
  findings (LinearEquiv shape with `@[simps]`; bare-theorem
  `IsLocalization` packaging; `_iso_` ‚Üí `_equiv_` rename; namespace
  `IsAffineOpen.‚Ä¶` over `Scheme.‚Ä¶`; M1.c is not a Mathlib gap; M1.b
  cofinality re-framing). Each of these would have cost a refactor +
  blueprint-writer + prover-rework round if discovered post-ship;
  caught pre-ship, they cost only a directive update. **Pattern**:
  any time the loop is about to introduce a new sorry-bearing
  declaration tied to a Mathlib namespace the project hasn't authored
  before, dispatch `mathlib-analogist` BEFORE the declaration enters
  the Lean tree. Persistent file
  `analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md` is the
  artefact for future iters. Caveat surfaced this iter: do NOT
  parallel-dispatch the analogist with a `blueprint-writer` on the
  SAME chapter ‚ÄĒ the writer can't see the analogist's findings yet,
  so it lands sub-optimal vocabulary that the next iter has to
  correct.

- **`by_cases h : genus C = 0` is a well-formed Lean decomposition for `nonempty_jacobianWitness`-style protected signatures with no genus parameter**
  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ strategy-critic-iter121 alternative #1 adopted)*:
  when a protected signature quantifies over a `Nat`-genus-implicit
  object (here: `C : Over (Spec (.of k))` with smoothness +
  irreducibility + properness instances but no `g : ‚Ąē` parameter),
  the body may still be a `Nat`-valued case split because
  `AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ‚Ąē` is `DecidableEq`. The shape
  ```
  theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness ... := by
    by_cases h : AlgebraicGeometry.genus (k := k) C.left = 0
    ¬∑ exact ‚ü®genusZeroWitness C h‚ü©      -- closed by M2
    ¬∑ exact ‚ü®positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)‚ü©  -- closed by M3
  ```
  converts a "genus-stratified" prose proof into an explicit
  Lean-level decomposition, which the autonomous loop can then close
  one arm at a time. Reusable for any all-quantified protected
  signature whose informal proof case-splits on a decidable scalar.
  **Documented at `STRATEGY.md ¬ß Decomposition: genus-stratified
  body of nonempty_jacobianWitness` (iter-121).**

- **Genus-0 over non-algebraically-closed `k` is NOT `C ‚ČÖ ‚Ąô¬Ļ_k`; use base change to `kŐĄ` + Galois descent**
  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ strategy-critic-iter121 alternative #2 adopted)*:
  the iter-121 first-draft strategy claimed M2.c was the
  identification `C ‚ČÖ ‚Ąô¬Ļ_k` for genus-0 curves. This is
  **mathematically false** on the protected
  `nonempty_jacobianWitness` signature, which permits curves
  without `k`-rational points: Brauer‚ÄďSeveri conics over `‚Ąö`
  (`x¬≤ + y¬≤ + z¬≤ = 0` is a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
  curve of genus 0 with no `‚Ąö`-point and is NOT isomorphic to
  `‚Ąô¬Ļ_‚Ąö`). The corrected route is: base change to `kŐĄ` (where
  `C_{kŐĄ}` is automatically nonempty geometrically integral, hence
  has a `kŐĄ`-point and IS isomorphic to `‚Ąô¬Ļ_{kŐĄ}`); apply rigidity
  for `‚Ąô¬Ļ_{kŐĄ} ‚Üí A_{kŐĄ}`; Galois-descend constancy back to `k`.
  Reusable for any "X ‚ČÖ ‚Ąô¬Ļ_k under genus-0 + smooth + proper +
  geometrically irreducible" claim ‚ÄĒ the `k`-rational-point
  hypothesis is silently required and is NOT implied by the named
  hypotheses. **Documented at `STRATEGY.md ¬ß Roadmap M2` and
  `Jacobian.tex ¬ß (C.2)` 7-sub-step expansion (iter-121).**

- **Presheaf-of-modules section at a single open is NOT defeq to the chart-local Mathlib ring-theoretic output ‚ÄĒ unfold before claiming `rfl`**
  *(NEW iter-119 Archon canonical)*: a blueprint claim that
  `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V‚āÄ)` is
  "definitionally equal" to `ő©[őď(X,V‚āÄ)‚ĀĄőď(S,U‚āÄ)]` via the project-local
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler` (whose body is `rfl`) is
  **WRONG**. The `rfl` body identifies the section module with
  `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (ŌÜ'.app (op V‚āÄ))` where the source
  ring of `ŌÜ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
  CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c` is
  `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj
  (.op V‚āÄ)` ‚ÄĒ i.e. the inverse-image presheaf at `V‚āÄ`, which by the
  left Kan extension formula computes to
  `colim_{f V‚āÄ ‚äÜ W ‚äÜ S} S.presheaf.obj W`. This is a colimit ring
  strictly larger than `őď(S, U‚āÄ)` in general; the two K√§hler
  differential modules are not definitionally the same type, and the
  bridge requires a real `LinearEquiv` constructed via cofinality of
  `U‚āÄ` in the colimit cone of neighbourhoods of `f V‚āÄ` conditional on
  `IsAffineOpen U‚āÄ ‚ąß IsAffineOpen V‚āÄ ‚ąß V‚āÄ ‚Č§ f‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ U‚āÄ`. **Pattern**: when
  a blueprint claims "definitional equality" between a presheaf-of-
  modules construction and a ring-theoretic Mathlib output, *unfold the
  construction at the Lean level before accepting the claim*. The left
  Kan extension at a single open is generically not `rfl`-equal to the
  ring-of-sections-on-a-containing-open. **Documented at
  `Differentials.lean:130-134` (iter-119 prover Step-5 comment block)
  and `Differentials.tex:91-104` (`% NOTE (iter-119 prover finding)`
  block added this review iter); iter-120 corrective is a new helper
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE`.**

- **Chart-level algebraization via the `algebraize` tactic on `@[algebraize]`-tagged Mathlib bridges**
  *(NEW iter-119 Archon canonical)*: `algebraize
  [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Hom.appLE f U‚āÄ V‚āÄ e)]` installs both the
  `Algebra` structure (via `RingHom.toAlgebra`) and any structural class
  the RingHom carries ‚ÄĒ here `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n
  őď(S, U‚āÄ) őď(X, V‚āÄ)` from the `RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`
  hypothesis ‚ÄĒ in **one shot**, because Mathlib annotates the bridge
  lemma `RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.toAlgebra` with
  `@[algebraize ...]`. No manual `letI`/`haveI` ring-algebra dance
  required. Applies whenever a chart hypothesis is one of Mathlib's
  `@[algebraize ...]`-tagged predicates (`Finite`, `IsStandardSmooth`,
  `IsEtale`, etc.). **Documented at `Differentials.lean:101` (iter-119
  prover Step 2).**

- **`Scheme.component_nontrivial X V` for `[Nontrivial őď(X, V)]` synthesis from a point**
  *(NEW iter-119 Archon canonical)*: when a Mathlib lemma requires
  `[Nontrivial B]` where `B = őď(X, V)` and the goal context contains a
  point `x ‚ąą V`, the canonical two-line synthesis is
  ```
  haveI : Nonempty V := ‚ü®‚ü®x, hxV‚ü©‚ü©
  haveI : Nontrivial (őď(X, V) : Type _) := Scheme.component_nontrivial X V
  ```
  `Scheme.component_nontrivial` is the Mathlib instance
  `[Nonempty ‚Ü•U] ‚Üí Nontrivial (X.presheaf.obj (.op U))`. Reusable
  for any K√§hler-rank pin / dimension formula that carries a
  `[Nontrivial B]` hypothesis. **Documented at `Differentials.lean:106-107`
  (iter-119 prover Step 4).**

- **Structurally-minimal residual-sorry cascade: `constructor / all_goals first | <exact_a> | <exact_b> | sorry`**
  *(NEW iter-119 Archon canonical)*: when the body of a multi-conjunct
  goal has been built (e.g. `have hfree : ...`, `have hrank : ...`) but a
  type-bridge between the lemma outputs and the goal's components is
  still pending, the right shape is
  ```
  constructor
  all_goals first
    | exact hfree
    | exact hrank
    | sorry
  ```
  This keeps the file's sorry count at exactly 1 (vs. `refine ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©;
  ¬∑ sorry; ¬∑ sorry` which emits 2) AND will auto-close both goals once
  a future bridge supplies the type identification (e.g. the
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE` helper). Reusable
  for any "lemmas built, type-bridge still pending" PARTIAL outcome on
  a conjunction goal. **Documented at `Differentials.lean:132-136`
  (iter-119 prover Step 7).**

- **Iff-shape correctness audit when refactoring quantified statements**
  *(NEW iter-118 Archon canonical)*: when a refactor changes the
  quantifier shape of an iff (sheaf-form ‚Üí presheaf-form, global ‚Üí basis-
  localised, etc.) **re-derive each direction independently**; do NOT
  preserve the iff shape by transport. The iter-117 refactor of
  `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega` preserved an iff that had been valid
  against the *sheafified* K√§hler module but became false in the
  converse direction once restated against the presheaf form on each
  affine open (concrete counterexample: `Spec k ‚Üí Spec k[t]` via
  `t ‚Ü¶ 0`; locally of finite presentation, `ő©_{k/k[t]} = 0` locally
  free of rank 0, but not flat hence not smooth ‚ÄĒ the converse requires
  `Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent A B)` which Mathlib's
  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of_basis_kaehlerDifferential` carries as an
  explicit hypothesis). The iter-118 demotion to a forward-only
  implication restored correctness; the converse is honestly disclosed
  as an out-of-autonomous-loop Mathlib gap in the chapter's
  `\sec{Converse direction --- out of autonomous-loop scope}`.
  **Documented at `Differentials.lean:66-86` (new docstring),
  `Differentials.tex` `rem:converse_counterexample` /
  `rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses` / `rem:stacks_02G1`, and
  `STRATEGY.md` Phase C correctness-fix paragraph.**

- **The blueprint-reviewer hard-gate deferring a prover lane by one iter
  is a positive workflow signal, not a stall** *(NEW iter-118 Archon
  canonical)*: when the blueprint-reviewer flags a must-fix-this-iter
  finding on a chapter that gates an active prover lane, the right move
  is **never** to dispatch the prover against the broken material to
  "see how it goes." Iter-118 deferred the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
  prover lane by one iter to allow the iter-117 iff-is-false issue to
  be corrected via refactor + blueprint-writer; the cost was a 1-iter
  latency on closure, and the benefit was that the prover (iter-119)
  reads a correct statement and a correctly-named verified Mathlib
  chain. Without the deferral, the prover would burn iters on the
  false converse direction. **Pattern applies whenever the
  blueprint-reviewer's per-chapter checklist marks a chapter
  `correct: false` or `complete: false` and a prover lane targets a
  declaration in that chapter.**

- **No-axiom hard rule beats no-deferral user directive at the
  declaration boundary** *(NEW iter-118 Archon canonical)*: the user's
  iter-116 "nothing deferred" directive applies to scope of *content*
  (orphan-to-protected-chain declarations the loop cannot close), not
  the *form* of declaration (`sorry` vs `axiom`). When a strategy-critic
  proposes converting an `:= sorry` foundational hypothesis to an
  `axiom` (citing "the user directive forbids any deferral"), the plan
  agent's rebuttal must invoke `.archon/prompts/plan.md` L43 ("**NEVER**
  propose adding new axioms") and observe that axiomization is a
  different *form* of deferral ‚ÄĒ it hides the gap from `sorry_analyzer`
  rather than removing it, and breaks the per-iter audit
  infrastructure that depends on transparent sorry tracking. **The
  rebuttal goes in the iter's `plan.md` sidecar per strategy-critic
  dispatcher_note (which explicitly forbids silent ignore).**

- **Aggressive TRIM as a response to STUCK routes with no autonomous corrective**
  *(NEW iter-117 Archon canonical)*: when (a) a route has been STUCK
  across ‚Č•5 prior iters with a recurring blocker phrase naming a
  concrete missing Mathlib bridge, AND (b) the route's load-bearing
  declaration is *orphan* to the project's protected chain (i.e.
  deleting the declaration does not break any protected signature),
  the right move is to **delete the declaration outright** rather
  than spawn another helper round. The cost saved is multi-iter
  prover churn; the cost paid is one chapter-rewrite to demote the
  trimmed material to an "Out of autonomous-loop scope" disclosure.
  Iter-117 applied this pattern to: `Differentials.lean` L191
  unique-gluing route (5 prior STUCK iters; missing `Scheme.PresheafOfModules`-
  sheaf-on-affine-basis bridge), `BasicOpenCech.lean` L1120
  `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` (7 prior PARTIALs; `Pi.lift` discrimination-
  tree blocker), `Modules/Monoidal.lean` `instIsMonoidal_W`
  (varying-ring `PresheafOfModules.stalk_tensorObj` Mathlib gap), and
  the Picard chain (FGA representability gap). **Net effect: 16 ‚Üí 2
  sorries in one iter, with the surviving 2 on (i) a route with a
  pre-verified Mathlib closure plan and (ii) the single foundational
  existence hypothesis.** Pattern applies whenever the orphan-to-
  protected verdict + STUCK-with-no-corrective verdict both fire.

- **Signature-form refactor (presheaf ‚Üí algebra-K√§hler) to dissolve a colimit-source bridge gap**
  *(NEW iter-120 Archon canonical; second instance of the broader
  "signature-refactor over auxiliary-infrastructure" pattern; the
  iter-117 sheaf‚Üípresheaf refactor below is the first)*: iter-119
  surfaced a real bridge gap ‚ÄĒ the iter-117 presheaf-form
  `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega` had its section module identifying
  (via `rfl`) with the K√§hler module over the inverse-image-presheaf
  *colimit* ring `A_colim`, not over `őď(S, U)` (the source of the
  appLE algebra structure). Closing the bridge as a helper-lemma
  required ~200‚Äď400 LOC of cofinality / left-Kan-extension
  manipulation. iter-120 instead **refactored the (non-protected)
  signature** to conclude on the appLE algebra K√§hler module
  `ő©[őď(X, V) ‚ĀĄ őď(S, U)]` directly, eliminating the bridge from the
  theorem's content. Proof body landed in **11 LOC, single Edit**.
  The bridge is documented as a Mathlib gap in
  `Differentials.tex ¬ß sec:bridge-out-of-scope` (with full
  mathematical content: `A ‚Üí A_colim` is a localization of `A` at
  `M := {g ‚ąą A : appLE(g) ‚ąą B^√ó}`; `ő©[A_colim/A] = 0`; the iso is via
  `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`). **Pattern**: when a
  sorry's residual is a real Mathlib bridge gap AND the declaration
  is a non-protected leaf with no downstream consumers, prefer the
  signature refactor over building the bridge. Persistent design
  rationale at `analogies/cotangent-presheaf-design.md`. **Documented
  at `Differentials.lean:66‚Äď110` (new algebra-K√§hler signature +
  honest gap docstring) + `STRATEGY.md` Phase C + `Differentials.tex`
  thm:smooth_locally_free_omega proof block.**

- **One-attempt closure on a strategy-critic-endorsed signature
  refactor as CONVERGING ratification**
  *(NEW iter-120 Archon canonical)*: when iter-N's plan-phase
  `strategy-critic` + `mathlib-analogist` jointly recommend a
  signature refactor over a helper-lemma construction (overriding an
  iter-N‚Äď1 session_NNN recommendation), and the iter-N prover then
  closes the body in a single Edit event vs. the iter-N‚Äď1 prover's
  ~15+ edits before PARTIAL, this is the cleanest CONVERGING signal
  the project produces. Treat it as ratifying the iter-N plan's
  strategic call, not as luck. Concrete iter-120 instance: the
  session_119 CRITICAL #1 recommendation (build a 200‚Äď400 LOC bridge
  helper) was overridden by iter-120 plan-phase Option (iii)
  convergence; the prover closed `smooth_locally_free_omega` in one
  Edit (11 LOC body) vs. iter-119's ~45 LOC of partial body and one
  residual sorry. **Pattern**: when overriding a session_NNN
  recommendation per fresh-context critic convergence, the
  iter/iter-NNN/plan.md rebuttal section must record *why*; if the
  next iter's prover ratifies the override via one-attempt closure,
  the rebuttal is upgraded to "documented best practice" in the
  Knowledge Base.

- **Signature-form refactor (sheaf ‚Üí presheaf) to sidestep a missing Mathlib bridge**
  *(NEW iter-117 Archon canonical)*: `Differentials.lean`
  `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega` was originally stated against the
  sheafified `relativeDifferentials f` (a `SheafOfModules`); closing
  it required the missing "sheaf-on-affine-basis ‚áí sheaf-on-X" bridge.
  The iter-117 refactor restates the same mathematical content
  against the presheaf form `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op U)`
  on each affine open `U`, sidestepping the sheaf-condition machinery
  entirely. The new statement is logically equivalent (the universal
  property of sheafification preserves the local-freeness predicate
  on a basis), but its proof routes through 5 [verified] Mathlib
  bridges entirely on the algebra side, with no scheme-level sheaf-
  condition step. **Documented at `Differentials.lean:74‚Äď81` (new
  signature) + `STRATEGY.md` Phase C (forward + converse decomposition
  with verified-name list) + `Differentials.tex` thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega
  proof block. SUPERSEDED iter-118 by the demotion to a forward-only
  implication, and the resulting iter-119 PARTIAL surfaced the
  colimit-source bridge gap which iter-120's algebra-K√§hler refactor
  then dissolved per the entry above.**

- **`JacobianWitness` bundle uniform over marked point ‚ÄĒ projection
  pattern for protected instances**
  *(NEW iter-117 Archon canonical)*: when a single existence
  hypothesis must back multiple protected typeclass instances on a
  derived scheme, the right shape is a `structure JacobianWitness`
  with one `J : Scheme` field plus one field per typeclass instance
  + one `‚ąÄ P, IsAlbanese C P J` quantification field (encoding that
  the universal-property data is uniform over marked points). Each
  protected instance on the derived scheme is then a one-line
  projection `(jacobianWitness C).<field>` from the witness extracted
  via `Classical.choice` from the `Nonempty` existence theorem. The
  pattern keeps `nonempty_jacobianWitness` as the *only* `sorry` and
  every protected projection closed. **Documented at
  `Jacobian.lean:143‚Äď222` and `AbelJacobi.lean:51‚Äď82`. Lean-auditor
  iter-117 review-phase: "honest single projections ‚Ä¶ no fake
  content."**

- **Unique-gluing reformulation route for sheaf-of-types conditions on schemes**
  *(NEW iter-113 Archon canonical; SUPERSEDED iter-117 ‚ÄĒ see "Aggressive TRIM" pattern
  above; the unique-gluing route was deleted along with the rest of `Differentials.lean`'s
  sheaf-condition machinery)*: when a basis-to-opens descent is
  needed and Mathlib does NOT package an off-the-shelf
  "sheaf-on-affine-basis-of-Scheme ‚áí sheaf" lemma, the unique-gluing
  form provides an alternative reformulation route via the two-link
  Mathlib chain
  `TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_of_isSheafUniqueGluing_types` +
  `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafOpensLeCover`. Both arrows are
  framework Mathlib equivalences with no mathematical content; the
  unique-gluing form replaces categorical-cone limit machinery
  (`mapCone`, `(opensLeCoverCocone U).op`) with a single existential
  `‚ąÉ! s, IsGluing F U sf s` about compatible families gluing to a
  section on `iSup U`. **The unique-gluing form is the right shape
  when the universal property of the underlying module-construction
  is available** (here: `KaehlerDifferential` +
  `KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation` for uniqueness +
  `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D` + `ModuleCat.Derivation.desc`
  for the lift-back). Note the directionality: the chapter prose may
  warn against `IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` as a basis-to-opens hook
  (taking a finished `IsSheaf` and converting to `IsSheafUniqueGluing`)
  ‚ÄĒ that is the *wrong* direction; the unique-gluing-as-source-of-math
  direction is the correct one and uses a different Mathlib name
  (`isSheaf_of_isSheafUniqueGluing_types`). **Documented at
  `Differentials.lean:168‚Äď175` (new sub-helper
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheafUniqueGluing_type`) +
  `:209‚Äď234` (helper #1's body).**

- **`Opens.IsBasis X.affineOpens` direct form (no `Set.range` wrapping)**
  *(NEW iter-113 Archon canonical)*: `Scheme.isBasis_affineOpens X`
  produces a hypothesis of type `Opens.IsBasis X.affineOpens` directly,
  because `X.affineOpens : Set X.Opens`. Do NOT wrap with
  `Set.range (fun V : X.affineOpens => (V : X.Opens))` ‚ÄĒ that
  wrapping forces a `convert+rfl` bridge that fails with
  `Type mismatch ... has type ?m = ?m but is expected to have type
  (Set.range fun V ‚Ü¶ ‚ÜĎV) = X.affineOpens`. Use the direct form.
  **Documented at `Differentials.lean:209` (Attempt 1.b in iter-113
  prover task).**

- **Bar-B scaffolding for single-sorry main theorems through multi-step recipes**
  *(NEW iter-112 Archon canonical)*: when a single-sorry "main theorem" needs
  to enact a multi-step recipe but the load-bearing content is one
  consolidated sub-claim, the right structural-advance shape is:
    1. **One load-bearing helper** with the load-bearing sub-claim, whose
       conclusion already uses the *characterisation namespace* that the
       multi-step recipe lands in (e.g. `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheafOpensLeCover`
       after Step 1 forgetful reduction, not `Presheaf.IsSheaf`); body
       `sorry`.
    2. **One derived helper**, fully closed, bridging from the load-bearing
       helper back to the recipe's intermediate goal-shape via the
       characterisation lemma (e.g. `isSheaf_iff_isSheafOpensLeCover.mpr`).
    3. **Main theorem body fully closed**: performs the leading reduction
       (e.g. `rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget _)]`) and
       delegates to the derived helper.
  This shape keeps the file's sorry count flat at the current
  (project-tracked) value while exposing the single residual mathematical
  content as one named helper. Avoids two regressive anti-patterns:
  (v1) two identical-signature helpers (boilerplate); (v2) two
  distinct-signature helpers each with a sorry body (regresses sorry count).
  **Documented at `Differentials.lean:98‚Äď227` (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` +
  helpers `_isSheafOpensLeCover_type` / `_isSheaf_type`).**

- **`CategoryTheory.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat)` for the Ab ‚Üí Type Step-1 forgetful reduction in the `Opens.grothendieckTopology X` setting**
  *(NEW iter-112 Archon canonical)*: when the goal is
  `Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) F`, the right
  rewrite to forgetfully reduce to a sheaf-of-types statement is the
  **bare-namespace** `Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (...)`, NOT
  the `TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp ...` variant. The TopCat
  variant rewrites the alias `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf` rather than the raw
  `Presheaf.IsSheaf`; the two are defeq but the syntactic `rw` match
  fails. The `_ _` placeholders give the two category metavariables; the
  explicit argument is the forgetful functor. **Documented at
  `Differentials.lean:225`.**

- **`forget AddCommGrpCat` (without `CategoryTheory.` prefix) anti-parse**
  *(NEW iter-112 Archon canonical)*: under the file's `open AlgebraicGeometry`
  + `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, the bare `forget AddCommGrpCat`
  is parsed as `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.forget`, not `CategoryTheory.forget`.
  Always use the fully-qualified `CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat`
  inside the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` namespace. **Documented at
  `Differentials.lean:225`.**

- **`TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheafOpensLeCover (...)` is a qualified standalone declaration, not a field projection**
  *(NEW iter-112 Archon canonical)*: do NOT write
  `(F ‚čô forget _).IsSheafOpensLeCover` in a lemma signature ‚ÄĒ this fails
  with `Invalid field IsSheafOpensLeCover: The environment does not contain
  CategoryTheory.Functor.IsSheafOpensLeCover`. `IsSheafOpensLeCover` is a
  standalone `def` under the `TopCat.Presheaf` namespace, not a `Functor`
  field. Use `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheafOpensLeCover (F ‚čô forget _)` in the
  signature. **Documented at `Differentials.lean:160`.**

- **Skeleton-quotient goals via double-`change` + `congr_toSkeleton_of_iso`**
  *(NEW iter-109 Archon canonical)*: when a `mapSkeleton.obj` goal must be
  reduced to a `toSkeleton` equality, two `change` invocations in sequence ‚ÄĒ
  first to unfold `mapSkeleton.obj`, second to expose `toSkeleton` ‚ÄĒ let the
  iso-rewrite fire cleanly. Pair with `Quotient.out_eq _` to discharge the
  trailing `toSkeleton u.val.out = u.val`. **Beats explicit
  `rw [show ... from rfl]`**, which is brittle once the first `change`
  has already unfolded the term. Reusable for any skeleton-quotient proof
  routed through `(Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapSkeleton.obj`. **Documented
  at `Picard/LineBundle.lean:L131-L142` (`Pic.pullback_id`).**

- **`apply Units.ext` over `show ((MonoidHom_apply) u).val = ...`**
  *(NEW iter-109 Archon canonical)*: when proving equality of `Units`-valued
  MonoidHom applications where the hom is shaped `Units.map ‚ü®‚ü®_,_‚ü©,_‚ü©` (the
  anonymous-constructor form), the `.val`-projection equality is NOT
  `rfl`-equal to the goal's syntactic form. `show ((h) u).val = (g) u .val`
  fails with `pattern ... is not definitionally equal to the goal`.
  Replace with `apply Units.ext` after `ext u` to cleanly project to the
  underlying `Units`-equality. **Documented at `Picard/LineBundle.lean:L131,L150`.**

- **Pullback-functoriality on `Skeleton` decomposes through `Functor.mapIso (fromSkeletonToSkeletonIso _).symm`**
  *(NEW iter-109 Archon canonical)*: the iso required for
  `(pullback (f ‚Čę g)).obj u.val.out ‚ČÖ (pullback f).obj ((toSkeleton ((pullback g).obj u.val.out)).out)`
  decomposes into two halves:
  (i) `(pullbackComp f g).symm.app u.val.out` (the natural-iso application
  bridging `pullback g ‚čô pullback f` to `pullback (f ‚Čę g)`), then
  (ii) `(Scheme.Modules.pullback f).mapIso (fromSkeletonToSkeletonIso _).symm`
  (the `Functor.mapIso` step bridging the skeleton-out gap).
  Composed with `.trans`, they yield the desired iso, which then closes
  via `congr_toSkeleton_of_iso`. Reusable for any pullback-functoriality
  proof on Skeleton. **Documented at `Picard/LineBundle.lean:L156-L162`
  (`Pic.pullback_comp`).**

- **Sister-gap pattern for protected named-deferred sorries**
  *(NEW iter-109 Archon canonical)*: when a downstream consumer needs
  *two* outputs of an unstated Mathlib instance (e.g. őľIso + őĶIso of
  `Functor.Monoidal F`), and the existing named-gap declaration is
  signature-protected, introduce sister `noncomputable def`-bodied sorries
  with parallel docstrings rather than trying to widen the protected
  signature. Both collapse together upon the same Mathlib refresh ‚ÄĒ
  they share a single conceptual gap, two syntactic sorries. **Documented
  at `Picard/LineBundle.lean:L82-L98`**
  (`SheafOfModules.pullback_tensorObj` őľIso + `SheafOfModules.pullback_oneIso`
  őĶIso for the missing `Functor.Monoidal (Scheme.Modules.pullback f)`).

- **Plan-step recipe simplification: anonymous-constructor on `MonoidHom` fields over `Quotient.lift` when Skeleton unfoldings are `rfl`**
  *(NEW iter-109 Archon canonical)*: when `Skeleton`'s `mul_eq` /
  `one_eq` and `mapSkeleton.obj` unfold definitionally (true for all
  current usages in this project), the plan-step's sketched
  `refine Units.map ‚ü®‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©, ?_‚ü©` with `Quotient.lift` machinery is
  overkill. Use anonymous-constructor on `MonoidHom`'s `toFun`/`map_one'`/
  `map_mul'` fields directly with `change` to advertise the unfolded shape.
  No `Quotient.lift` congruence obligation needed. **Documented at
  `Picard/LineBundle.lean:L108-L127` (`Pic.pullback`).**

- **Route-pivot via `-- DEFERRED (budget): ...` annotation when CHURNING + Mathlib infrastructure exists**
  *(NEW iter-108 Archon / iter-110 narrative)*: when progress-critic returns
  CHURNING on a route where (a) Mathlib provides the conceptual closure
  pieces and (b) consecutive iters of inline scaffolding fail to eliminate
  the residual sorry due to *elaboration friction* (e.g. `letI`-in-goal-type
  binder propagation), the correct closure-shape is a 10-line annotation
  attached to the `sorry` token:
  ```
  sorry -- DEFERRED (budget): provable from Mathlib's <X> + <Y>;
        -- mechanization deferred at iter-NNN due to <specific obstruction>.
        -- The inline scaffolding at L<A>-L<B> (<helper names>) is preserved
        -- as inert infrastructure for a future re-attempt. NOT a Mathlib gap.
  ```
  Distinct from `-- MATHLIB GAP:` (reserved for genuinely-absent Mathlib
  infrastructure). The classification preserves the project's named-gap
  count distinct from elaboration-deferral count in the end-state framing.
  **Citation precision rule**: when naming anonymous Mathlib instances in
  the annotation, cite the *file path* (`Mathlib.X.Y.Z:NN`) rather than an
  invented dot-name. The iter-108 annotation at `BasicOpenCech.lean:L1846`
  was flagged MAJOR by lean-auditor for citing `IsLocalizedModule.pi`,
  `IsLocalizedModule.prodMap`, and `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid`
  ‚ÄĒ these are *anonymous instances* at `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi:82,97`
  and `Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.IsLocalization:49`, not
  named declarations. **Bundle a chapter-side `\begin{remark}[Implementation
  status (iter-NNN escape-valve)]` mirror** in the same iter to keep the
  blueprint coherent (cf. `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex:rem:basicOpenCover_step2_status`).
- **`‚ąŹŠ∂ú` over `Fin (n+1)` ‚ÜĒ `(Finset.image x univ).inf'` bridge**
  *(NEW iter-109 narrative / iter-107 Archon)*: when `‚ąŹŠ∂ú` of an `Opens`-
  valued family indexed by `Fin (n+1)` needs to be rewritten as
  `Finset.inf'` to match a project helper expecting `Finset (‚ÜĎs‚āÄ)`
  (e.g. `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen`), a direct
  `rw [CompleteLattice.finite_product_eq_finset_inf]` FAILS by
  universe mismatch ‚ÄĒ Mathlib's lemma requires `őĪ, őĻ : Type u` same
  universe, but `Fin (n+1) : Type 0` vs `Opens : Type u` violates this.
  The 14-LOC workaround chain is:
  ```
  rw [show (‚ąŹŠ∂ú fun a => basicOpenCover ‚ÜĎs‚āÄ (x a)) =
      ‚®Ö a, basicOpenCover ‚ÜĎs‚āÄ (x a) from
    le_antisymm (le_iInf fun a => leOfHom (Pi.ŌÄ _ a))
      (leOfHom (Pi.lift fun a => homOfLE (iInf_le _ a)))]
  rw [‚Üź Finset.inf_univ_eq_iInf, ‚Üź Finset.inf'_eq_inf]
  exact (Finset.inf'_image (s := Finset.univ) (f := x)
      (hs := Finset.image_nonempty.mpr Finset.univ_nonempty)
      (g := basicOpenCover ‚ÜĎs‚āÄ)).symm
  ```
  Reusable for any future Fin-indexed `‚ąŹŠ∂ú ‚Üí Finset.inf'` bridging
  inside a ńĆech-style proof. **Documented at L1799-L1812 in
  BasicOpenCech.lean (`h_pi_eq_inf'` in `h_loc_exact`).**
- **`letI ... in <goal-type>` does NOT propagate to the proof body**
  *(NEW iter-109 narrative / iter-107 Archon, anti-pattern)*: when an
  inline `have` has the form
  ```
  have foo : letI alg1 := ... ; letI alg2 := ... ; <goal-type>
    := by intro alg1 alg2; ...
  ```
  the `letI` elaborates eagerly during goal type-checking, leaving NO
  `intro` targets in the body and the local typeclass instances are
  invisible to `infer_instance`. This is a recurring trap encountered
  at the Step 2 algebra-threading micro-step of the analogist Q1
  recipe. **Workarounds**: (i) hoist the `letI` to outer scope (loses
  the per-x bound-variable shape); (ii) term-mode `IsLocalizedModule.mk`
  construction (manually supply LinearMap + surj/mk_eq_mk_iff); (iii)
  top-level helper outside the body (the iter-107 plan forbade this;
  iter-108 may need to lift it). **Documented in `.archon/.debug-feedback/
  debug_feedback.md` and iter-107 task_results.**
- **Explicit `@`-form on `IsLocalizedModule` is too brittle to scale**
  *(NEW iter-109 narrative / iter-107 Archon, anti-pattern)*: argument-
  position 2 is misparsed as `Submonoid` instead of `CommSemiring R`;
  `RingHom.toLinearMap` does NOT exist on bare `RingHom` (needs Algebra
  structure first); ~60 LOC of explicit-argument bookkeeping is the
  minimum cost. Prefer the implicit-instance route via
  `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid`
  with the algebras threaded via outer-scope `letI`.
- **The `@`-form `IsLocalization.Away` typing pattern from iter-059**
  *(RECONFIRMED iter-109 narrative)*: for a conclusion of shape
  `IsLocalization.Away ((presheafMap _).hom f.1) őď(V_x ‚äď D(f.1))`
  where the algebra must be supplied explicitly, mirror iter-059's
  `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization` shape exactly. The
  default `algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance only fires
  for the syntactic `őď(X, X.basicOpen f)` form; for `őď(X, V ‚äď D(f))`
  the algebra must be inlined on the conclusion type. Works at the
  single-step `h_isLoc` use case (L1822-L1834 in BasicOpenCech.lean);
  does NOT generalise to `IsLocalizedModule` (see anti-pattern above).
- **2-line geometric setup for localised slice-cover identification**
  *(NEW iter-108)*: when proving exactness of a localised differential
  at a coord `f ‚ąą s‚āÄ` inside an `exact_of_isLocalized_span` outer
  scaffold (the `h_K‚āÄ_exact` shape at `BasicOpenCech.lean:1781-1802`),
  the boilerplate first two lemmas are:
  (i) `have h_V_le_U (x) : (‚ąŹŠ∂ú fun a => basicOpenCover ‚ÜĎs‚āÄ (x a)) ‚Č§ U
      := by refine (Pi.ŌÄ _ ‚ü®0, by omega‚ü©).le.trans ?_; exact basicOpen_le _ _`,
  (ii) `have h_slice_eq (x) : (‚ąŹŠ∂ú ...) ‚äď C.left.basicOpen f.1 =
      C.left.basicOpen ((presheaf.map (homOfLE (h_V_le_U x)).op).hom f.1)
      := (Scheme.basicOpen_res C.left f.1 _).symm`.
  These are one-liners modulo coercion bookkeeping and should be the
  first two `have`s of any future Mathlib-aligned localised-ńĆech proof.
  **Documented at L1786-L1795 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **`IsLocalizedModule.pi` is the canonical Mathlib idiom for
  finite-product localisation** *(NEW iter-108 ‚ÄĒ RECONFIRMS analogist
  Q1a)*: the typeclass instance at
  `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi:93` provides
  `IsLocalizedModule S (LinearMap.pi fun i ‚Ü¶ f i ‚ąė‚āó LinearMap.proj i)`
  for finite `őĻ`. Always consume via `IsLocalizedModule.iso` + the
  Mathlib instance; never re-derive the product-localisation
  commutation. Bridge to `Limits.Pi.lift`-form via `ModuleCat.piIsoPi`.
  **Persistent rationale at
  `analogies/finite-product-localisation-and-cech-r-linearity.md`.**
- **`Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` is the closing tool for
  localised-exactness transport (not `LocalizedModule.map_exact`)**
  *(NEW iter-108 ‚ÄĒ anti-pattern + correct route)*: in an
  `exact_of_isLocalized_span` outer scaffold the localised exactness
  goal is `Function.Exact ‚áĎ(LocalizedModule.map S f_R)
  ‚áĎ(LocalizedModule.map S g_R)`. The Mathlib lemma
  `LocalizedModule.map_exact` would close this from `Function.Exact f_R g_R`,
  but that is exactly the `h_K‚āÄ_exact` we are constructing ‚ÄĒ **circular**.
  Correct route: use `IsLocalizedModule.iso` (from `IsLocalizedModule.pi`)
  to transport slice-cover function-level exactness (already in scope
  as `h_a‚āÄ_fun f`) across a `LinearEquiv` ladder via
  `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` (or the one-iso variant
  `LinearEquiv.conj_exact_iff_exact`). **Documented at iter-108
  `task_results/Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.lean.md` + the analogies file.**
- **Body-local `have h_iter104 := <iter-104 R-linearity lemma> hU s‚āÄ n hn i r' y'`
  as Route B staging** *(iter-107)*: at the post-S5 LinearMap-chain frame
  of `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`, the iter-104 binder-level R-linearity (typed at
  `Fin ((prev n) + 2) ‚Üí s‚āÄ`) can be staged in tactic-state as a `have`. From
  there iter-108 can derive per-coord version at `j_int := j' ‚ąė Fin.cast hRel'`
  via `congrFun` + `Pi.smul_apply`, then bridge from `(Pi.ŌÄ Z_int j_int).hom`
  to `(Pi.ŌÄ Z‚āā j').hom (eqToHom_outer.hom ...)` via manual `Pi.hom_ext` + `eqToHom`
  lemma chain OR `rcases n with _ | n_` to symbolically eliminate `(prev n)`.
  **Documented at L1115-L1119 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **`generalize hŌÉ : ((-1 : ‚Ą§) ^ (‚ÜĎi : ‚Ąē)) = ŌÉ` is harmless but ineffective**
  *(NEW iter-107)*: generalizing a polymorphic scalar to a fresh ‚Ą§ variable
  succeeds (no obstacle) but does NOT bypass the whnf timeout on subsequent
  `rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]`. Confirms iter-100's finding: the whnf cost is in
  the Pi.lift body, not in the scalar. Useful as a no-op diagnostic; not a
  workaround. Reconfirms iter-100/iter-103 dead-end class in the post-S5 frame.
- **`Pi.ŌÄ_comp_eqToHom` Mathlib gap: object-equality case is absent**
  *(NEW iter-106)*: Mathlib exposes `Limits.Pi.ŌÄ_comp_eqToHom` for
  **per-index** equality (eqToHom from `i = j` index renaming), but the
  `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` blocker needs the **object-equality** case
  (`‚ąŹŠ∂ú Z‚āĀ = ‚ąŹŠ∂ú Z‚āā` from `Fin a = Fin b`). The two structurally distinct
  cases require a project-local helper proved via `Limits.Pi.hom_ext`
  + per-coord `Pi.lift_ŌÄ_apply` + `Fin.cast_cast`. **Iter-106 Route 1
  lemma `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_comp_eqToHom_eq_summand_family'`
  at L732 is precisely this helper, with body still sorry.** Future
  designs should AVOID introducing object-equality eqToHom when an
  alternative `Fin.cast`-direct re-parameterisation exists.
- **`rcases n with _ | n'; ¬∑ omega; ¬∑ have hPrev := simp [...]` does
  NOT propagate into type-level Fin indices** *(NEW iter-106, anti-
  pattern)*: when the residual goal has `‚ąŹŠ∂ú fun j : Fin a ‚Üí s‚āÄ ‚Ü¶ ...`
  with `a = (ComplexShape.up ‚Ąē).prev (n'+1) + 2`, the auxiliary
  `hPrev : (ComplexShape.up ‚Ąē).prev (n'+1) = n'` proves but does NOT
  rewrite the type-position Fin index. `simp only [hPrev] at hCod`
  reports "no progress" because simp does not fire on type-level
  occurrences inside `‚ąŹŠ∂ú`'s index family. **Workaround**: use
  type-level substitution upstream (e.g. `simp only [show
  (ComplexShape.up ‚Ąē).prev (n+1) = n by simp [...]] at *` BEFORE
  introducing the eqToHom), or re-design the lemma to use `Fin.cast`
  directly without the rcases scaffold.
- **Wrapper-via-direct-Pi.lift for Fin-index mismatch bridging**
  *(iter-105, NEW)*: when bridging a Fin-index mismatch between a
  named top-level family (e.g.
  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family : Fin ((prev n) + 2) ‚Üí _`) and a
  client site (e.g. `Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí _`) via a wrapper def, define the
  wrapper as a **direct `Pi.lift`** with `Fin.cast` applied at each
  coordinate ‚ÄĒ NOT as `named_family ‚Čę eqToHom`. The direct form keeps
  the wrapper's binder-level R-linearity proof discrim-tree-clean
  (reusing the iter-104 pattern below) at the cost of deferring the
  eqToHom-vs-Pi.ŌÄ transport burden into a single client proof. The
  `named_family ‚Čę eqToHom` alternative re-introduces the iter-099/
  100/101/103 discrim-tree blocker at the R-linearity proof.
  **Documented at L604-L630 in BasicOpenCech.lean (Helper 1
  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'`).**
- **iter-104 R-linearity pattern as production template ‚ÄĒ reusable
  across multiple bound-index families** *(iter-105, REINFORCED ‚ÄĒ second
  invocation)*: `RingHom.toModule_smul` +
  `piIsoPi_inv_kernel_őĻ_apply` + term-level `Eq.trans` + `congrArg`
  + `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` mirrored byte-for-byte from
  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` (L494, iter-104) to
  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear` (L634, iter-105) with only
  the Fin.cast index translation differing. **The pattern is now
  confirmed across 2 invocations on different but structurally similar
  targets** and is the project's standard template for R-linearity
  transport in Pi-codomain settings. Reference the iter-104 block
  before designing any future similar proof. **Documented at L494
  and L634 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **Refactor-then-prove production cadence ‚ÄĒ two-iter pair**
  *(iter-104, ESTABLISHED PATTERN)*: when a target is HOU- or
  discrim-tree-blocked at a call site whose literal morphism family
  is an anonymous Pi.lift closure, the durable closure technique is
  to (a) refactor the closure into a named top-level def in iter N
  via the refactor subagent, then (b) close the binder-level
  R-linearity / structural body in iter N+1 via the prover. Iter-098
  split-slot + iter-099 bridge was the first instance; iter-102
  named-family + iter-104 R-linearity is the second. The technique is
  now the project's primary technique for HOU-blocked targets in
  Phase A. **Documented at L454/L494/L536 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **`piIsoPi_inv_kernel_őĻ_apply` for the `e‚āĀ.symm` direction**
  *(iter-104, NEW)*: when converting `(Pi.ŌÄ Z‚āĀ a).hom (e‚āĀ.symm Z) = Z a`
  where `e‚āĀ = (piIsoPi Z‚āĀ).toLinearEquiv`, use the `inv` form
  `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_őĻ_apply` (NOT the `hom` form
  `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply`). The wrapper proof is 2 lines:
  `intro Z a; exact ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_őĻ_apply Z‚āĀ a Z`.
  Project-local helper `hSym` packs this as a `have` in the proof
  body. **Documented at L573-L578 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **`RingHom.toModule_smul` as simp lemma for r ‚ÄĘ expansion in Pi
  codomains** *(iter-104, NEW)*: when the goal has `r ‚ÄĘ _` on a Pi
  codomain whose R-module structure was built via
  `RingHom.toModule (presheaf.map _).hom`, fire
  `simp only [RingHom.toModule_smul]` to convert `r ‚ÄĘ _` to
  `f(r) * _` multiplication. The rewrite is rfl. Exposes the ring
  structure for downstream `map_mul`-style reasoning. **Documented
  at L583 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **Term-level `Eq.trans + congrArg` to bypass HMul-synth blockers**
  *(iter-104, NEW)*: when `rw [(presheaf.map _).hom.map_mul]` fails
  because of HMul typeclass synthesis issues on the output ring
  (and variants `change`/`generalize_proofs`/`set`/`show ... from`
  also fail), switch to term level:
  `((presheaf.map _).hom.map_mul a b).trans (congrArg (¬∑ * f y_val) eq_for_f)`
  composes the ring-hom-multiplicativity + a per-summand equality
  cleanly. Requires `set Pl := Pi.lift ... with hPl_def` first to give
  implicit-arg metas a target type. **Documented at L590-L598 in
  BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **`letI` body-local reconstruction for r ‚ÄĘ y HSMul re-synthesis**
  *(iter-104, REINFORCED ‚ÄĒ first introduced iter-092)*: signature-level
  `letI perI‚āĀ/perI_int/h_mod_pi_*` bindings do NOT survive `intro` into
  the goal frame in a way that the goal's `r ‚ÄĘ y` HSMul re-synthesises
  against the elaborated `Pi.module + RingHom.toModule` structure.
  Reconstruct the same letI bindings inside the tactic body immediately
  after `intro R Z‚āĀ Z_int e‚āĀ e_int`. Mirrors the pattern at L781-L805
  of `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`. **Documented at L538-L562 in
  BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **Free-standing `map_zsmul` for ZSMul-preservation through a
  LinearEquiv** *(iter-103, NEW)*: when proving R/‚Ą§-linearity through
  a `LinearEquiv` like `e‚āā := (piIsoPi Z).toLinearEquiv`, use the
  free-standing `map_zsmul` from `Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Hom.Defs`
  (`AddMonoidHomClass.map_zsmul`), NOT the dot-projected
  `(e‚āā : _ ‚Üí‚āó[k] _).map_zsmul`. The dot form fails because
  `LinearMap.map_zsmul` is not a projectable field; the free-standing
  form fires through `e‚āā`'s `AddMonoidHomClass` instance.
  Same applies to `map_smul`, `map_nsmul`, etc. **Documented at L607
  in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **Binder-level Path B reduction of ŌÉ-sign-wrapped sums to sign-free
  abstract structural lemmas** *(iter-103, NEW)*: when an inert
  structural lemma `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` is HOU-stuck
  at the call site because the literal `F i = (-1)^‚ÜĎi ‚ÄĘ Pi.lift_thing`
  has anonymous closure, introduce a *sign-extended* sister lemma
  `alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` with `ŌÉ : őĻ' ‚Üí ‚Ą§` and
  `G : őĻ' ‚Üí (A ‚ü∂ B)` as separate binders. The new lemma's body
  closes in ~8 lines: `intro r y; rw [Preadditive.sum_comp _ _ _];
  simp_rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]; exact
  alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux ... (fun i hi r y => by show _; rw
  [map_zsmul e‚āā ..., map_zsmul e‚āā ..., hG i hi r y, smul_comm ...])`.
  HOU-free because all binders are abstract.
  **Documented at L590-L609 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **S4 constant-level rfl pivot
  `ConcreteCategory.hom ‚Üí ModuleCat.Hom.hom`** *(iter-103, NEW)*:
  when the goal head is `ConcreteCategory.hom f x` with `f`
  containing Pi.lift anonymous-closure, the rewrite
  `rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom : _ ‚Üí _) = ModuleCat.Hom.hom from
  rfl]` always lands. The rewrite head is a constant (no Pi.lift
  pattern), so it sidesteps the discrim-tree class. Useful as a
  preparatory step before `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp,
  LinearMap.comp_apply]` to decompose categorical compositions.
  **Documented at L823 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **S5 LinearMap.comp_apply composition decomposition for moving
  smul to innermost layer** *(iter-103, NEW)*: after S4, applying
  `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]` decomposes
  the categorical `‚Čę` into nested `LinearMap.comp` and splits via
  `comp_apply`, bringing the leading `(-1)^‚ÜĎi ‚ÄĘ` directly onto the
  innermost single-morphism (e.g., `Pi.lift_thing`). This is the
  *cleanest possible form* for subsequent extraction, though it
  does NOT itself dissolve the discrim-tree blocker (which lives
  at the smul-Pi.lift interface, not the surrounding composition).
  Useful as a structural diagnostic and as a setup for refactor
  exit. **Documented at L826 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
