- **`show` def-eq pivot for `e‚āā _ j' ‚Üí (Pi.ŌÄ Z j).hom _`**
  *(iter-099, NEW)*: when the LHS uses a LinearEquiv coercion
  (e.g. `e‚āā` constructed from `(piIsoPi Z).toLinearEquiv`), the
  equivalent `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` lemma does NOT fire
  via `rw` directly ‚ÄĒ discrimination-tree key differs because the
  LinearEquiv coercion masks the underlying `piIsoPi.hom` head.
  Pivot the goal by `show (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.ŌÄ Z j)) _ = ‚Ä¶`
  to put `Pi.ŌÄ Z j` explicitly into both sides ‚ÄĒ this works by
  def-eq and unblocks subsequent `‚Üź ConcreteCategory.comp_apply`
  chains. **Documented at L770‚ÄďL771 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **4-layer `‚Üź ConcreteCategory.comp_apply` chain for absorbing
  nested ConcreteCategory.hom applications** *(iter-099, NEW)*:
  when a post-funext goal has shape `(Pi.ŌÄ Z j).hom (eqToHom_hom
  (smul_thing.hom (e_inv x)))` (4 layered hom-applications), the
  chain `rw [show ModuleCat.Hom.hom = ConcreteCategory.hom (C :=
  ModuleCat.{u} k) from rfl, ..., ‚Üź ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
  ‚Üź ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ‚Üź ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
  ‚Üź ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]` fuses into a single categorical
  morphism composition. **Documented at L776‚ÄďL779 in
  BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **`funext j'` per-coordinate pivot for Pi.lift-anonymous-closure
  discrimination-tree failures** *(iter-100, ESTABLISHED, qualified
  iter-099)*: when a smul- or
  composition-rewrite fails because the discrimination tree cannot
  locate the occurrence through Pi.lift's anonymous-closure codomain
  (e.g. `‚ąŹŠ∂ú (fun i_1 ‚Ü¶ Pi.ŌÄ Z_unfolded (i_1 ‚ąė őī i) ‚Čę presheaf.map ...)`),
  pivot to per-coordinate via `funext`. Works when the equation is
  already at `‚ąÄ j, Z j` level after a `LinearEquiv` like `e‚āā`.
  Reduces the goal to a single concrete `Z j'` where `Pi.ŌÄ Z j'`
  projections fuse via `‚Üź ModuleCat.hom_comp` + eqToHom-naturality
  + `Pi.lift_ŌÄ_apply`. The original polymorphic-scalar blocker
  dissolves because each output coordinate carries its own concrete
  R-action via `RingHom.toModule (presheaf.map _).hom`.
  **Documented at L726‚ÄďL767 in BasicOpenCech.lean.** *Caveat (iter-099,
  NEW)*: the per-coordinate frame does NOT dissolve the
  discrimination-tree class when the morphism composition retains
  the anonymous-closure Pi.lift in its source (post-S3 goal
  `(((-1)^‚ÜĎi ‚ÄĘ Pi.lift_thing) ‚Čę eqToHom ‚Čę Pi.ŌÄ Z j').hom`). Use
  `funext` for goal-shape simplification only; pair with refactor
  (named-T helper) for full closure.
- **`set : T` as a scalar-type diagnostic** *(iter-100, NEW)*: when
  uncertain whether a polymorphic `n` elaborates to type `T‚āĀ` or `T‚āā`,
  try both `set h : T‚āĀ := n` and `set h : T‚āā := n` at LSP. The one
  that folds (substitutes in the goal) is the elaborated type.
  Useful for resolving a plan-directive's ascription assumption
  BEFORE committing it. *Caveat: the `set` diagnostic identifies the
  scalar's type, but does NOT guarantee that smul-aware lemmas (e.g.
  `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` for the ‚Ą§ case) will pattern-match ‚ÄĒ those
  can fail independently due to discrimination-tree issues.*
- **Refactor split-slot pattern for HOU-blocked single-slot lemmas**
  *(iter-098/099, NEW + VINDICATED)*: when an abstract structural
  lemma's single morphism slot `F : őĻ' ‚Üí (A ‚ü∂ B)` is HOU-blocked at
  a call site whose literal F-body has nested non-Miller binder
  references, refactor to a two-slot signature
  `(G : őĻ' ‚Üí (A ‚ü∂ C), E : C ‚ü∂ B)` with `F i = G i ‚Čę E`. The
  two-slot version's Miller-pattern unification at the call site
  puts the literal "blocker" (`eqToHom`, fixed post-composition,
  etc.) into the independent `E` slot. Body provable from the
  single-slot version via `Preadditive.sum_comp` + family-level
  rewrite (3 tactic lines). **Confirmed working at iter-099 for
  `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` body (L532) + call site
  (L700‚ÄďL712).**
- **`rw [‚Üź Pi.smul_apply (i := j)]; refine congrFun ‚Ä¶ j` as j-eval
  lifter** *(iter-099, NEW)*: when a goal has shape `f z j = r ‚ÄĘ
  g z j` where `f` and `g` agree as functions of `z`, reverse the
  smul-eval on the RHS to `(r ‚ÄĘ _) j` form, then strip `j`
  symmetrically with `congrFun`. The remaining sub-goal is the
  family-level statement, on which a family-level structural lemma
  applies via Miller unification.
- **`Finset.cons_induction` on an abstract Finset binder of a
  structural lemma + `revert hF`** *(iter-097, NEW)*: for proofs of
  "sum of P-satisfying maps satisfies P" structural lemmas, induct on
  the binder `s`, `revert hF` to keep the inductive `ih` general,
  discharge cons step via
  `simp only [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply, map_add]
   ; rw [hF i (Finset.mem_cons_self i s'), ih (‚ąÄ j hj => hF j ‚Ä¶) r y, smul_add]`,
  discharge empty case via `simp [Finset.sum_empty, ModuleCat.hom_zero, ‚Ä¶]`.
  HOU-free because all binders are abstract.
- **`simp_rw [‚Üź ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z‚āā j]` as a
  `(Pi.ŌÄ Z‚āā j).hom z` ‚Üí `(piIsoPi Z‚āā).hom.hom z j` bridge**
  *(iter-097, NEW)*: when a goal has `(Pi.ŌÄ Z‚āā j).hom z` on both LHS
  and RHS, this single `simp_rw` lifts the outer `j`-eval to expose
  the underlying `e‚āā` (= `piIsoPi Z‚āā .toLinearEquiv`) structure.
- **Plan-recipe transcription with one-call name-correction**

  before transcribing. Iter-097: `ModuleCat.zero_hom` ‚Üí
  `ModuleCat.hom_zero`. Iter-099: `Preadditive.smul_comp` ‚Üí
  `CategoryTheory.Linear.smul_comp` or
  `CategoryTheory.Preadditive.zsmul_comp`.

The embedding-dimension reroute reuses the already-built conormal bridge and needs NO new dimension-theory infra.)*

That goal is blocked: the intermediate `őď(Spec R,‚ä§)`/`őď(Spec R',‚ä§)` actions are buried inside `Module.compHom`/`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` and are **not synthesizable `SMul`/`Module` instances on the final carrier**, so none of `show ŌÜ.hom r ‚ÄĘ s = r ‚ÄĘ s` / `change ‚Ä¶ globalSectionsIso ‚Ä¶ ‚ÄĘ s` / `rfl` / `IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul` / `erw [restrictScalars.smul_def]` can name the common `őď(Spec R',‚ä§)`-action to `congr` down to the scalar equality (`őďSpecIso_inv_naturality`). **RECOMMENDED LIVE ROUTE:** element-free ‚ÄĒ `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` + `restrictScalarsId'App` + `eqToIso`(`őďSpecIso_inv_naturality` ring equality), all identity-carrier (no element smul). Step (a) `rfl`-confirmed; step (b) blocked only on plumbing `Ōą : őď(Spec R,‚ä§)‚Üíőď(Spec R',‚ä§)` out of `PresheafOfModules.pushforward = pushforward‚āÄ ‚čô restrictScalars` internals (`(Spec.map ŌÜ).toRingCatSheafHom` is an `InducedCategory.Hom`; `.val` does not project). **Scope:** closing this őď-fragment does NOT close `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (still needs QC-of-pushforward [Mathlib-absent] + pullback dict + fibre-product id + `cancelBaseChange` match ‚ÄĒ multi-iter).

Then `algebraMap _ _ t ‚Č† 0` and `order_{{u=0}} t = 1` (so `hlp` holds), yet `positivePart(principal t) = [{u=0}] + [{u=1}]` has degree 2 while `Module.finrank K K(C) = 1`. Then `principal 1 halg`: every prime divisor `Y` has `order Y 1 = WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = WithZero.log 1 = 0`, so `principal 1 halg = 0`. `positivePart 0 = 0`. `degree 0 = 0`. So LHS = 0. RHS = `Module.finrank K(C) K(C) = 1` (any field is rank-1 over itself). `0 ‚Č† 1`. The theorem CANNOT be closed by any tactic at the current signature. The blueprint chapter prose at `lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` correctly encodes the missing constraint (`t_‚ąě` MUST be a local parameter at `‚ąě`); the Lean signature does not. **Three corrective options** for iter-193+ plan-phase: (1) **Concrete `Ring.ordFrac` hypothesis** (recommended; most precise; matches chapter prose verbatim): `(hlp : ‚ąÉ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Y.point) (algebraMap K C.left.functionField t) = WithZero.coe (Multiplicative.ofAdd (-1 : ‚Ą§)))`. (2) **`IsLocalParameter` typeclass** (Mathlib upstream PR). (3) **Existential bundle** matching the consumer pattern (`(localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`). `% NOTE (iter-192 review)` annotation landed on the chapter block. **Do NOT re-dispatch Lane I prover on the body** until iter-193 plan-phase picks a corrective + updates the consumer at `RationalCurveIso.lean:560-562`. The route iii sketch has a logical gap. **The successful closure used prime-avoidance instead** (see Proof Patterns above): pick a *fresh* witness `x' ‚ąą ūĚĒ™ \ (ūĚĒ™¬≤ ‚ą™ ‚čÉ minimalPrimes R)` via `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`, then apply `hIH` (universally quantified IH) to `R/(x')`. **Reusable lesson**: when an induction's IH is universally quantified in the ring `R'`, the "obvious" closure that fights the specific `x` in the goal is often the wrong instinct ‚ÄĒ pick a fresh witness that avoids the problem set and let the IH handle the heavy lifting.

- **`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` (RationalCurveIso.lean) IS MATHEMATICALLY FALSE AS STATED ‚ÄĒ iter-187 body of `Hom.poleDivisor` is a principal divisor (degree 0); RHS is positive** *(NEW iter-189 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane I structural-conflict diagnosis, surfaced by iter-189 prover during Pin 2 closure attempt.)* `Hom.poleDivisor ŌÜ = Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (algebraMap _ _ (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val) halg` (iter-187 body) is a *principal* divisor; principal-divisor degree is 0 by Hartshorne II.6.10 (`principal_degree_zero`). Theorem RHS = `Module.finrank K(P^1) K(C)` is strictly positive for any non-constant `ŌÜ`. Hence `degree LHS = 0 ‚Č† finrank > 0 = RHS`. **The theorem cannot be closed by any tactic** until plan-phase picks a corrective: (a) refactor `Hom.poleDivisor` to be the positive part `(div(\varphi^* t_\infty))_0 = \varphi^*[\infty]` (new project-bespoke `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` infrastructure, ~50-100 LOC), OR (b) rename the theorem to operate on `positivePart (Hom.poleDivisor \varphi)`. Both require coordinated edits to (i) the Lean body, (ii) the blueprint `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` pin, (iii) callers (notably `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` in the same file). `% NOTE (iter-189 review)` block landed on `RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex` `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor`. **Do NOT re-dispatch Lane I prover on Pin 2** without the corrective. Distinct from the iter-186 "circular dep" blocker (since resolved iter-187 via typeclass binder) ‚ÄĒ this is a *body-vs-statement* unprovability discovered iter-189.

- **Mathlib's default `Module B (TensorProduct A B M)` instance synthesizes only when B is the LEFT factor ‚ÄĒ `TensorProduct A M B` (M left, B right) fails typeclass synthesis** *(NEW iter-187 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane F `pullback_tildeIso` signature trap in `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`.)* First signature attempt with `TensorProduct A M B` failed Lean's `Module B (TensorProduct A M B)` synthesis. - **Parallel-lane signature-change race: when one prover lane edits a shared declaration's signature, every other co-dispatched lane that consumes the declaration sees the PRE-edit shape; no per-lane build catches the resulting mismatch** *(NEW iter-176 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ Lane D + Lane K; RECURRED iter-177 Lane WD + Lane 1; recurrence-prevention obligation now ESCALATED.)* **iter-177 recurrence**: Lane WD (`WeilDivisor.lean`) introduced a NEW typeclass `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` and made it required on `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal` / `principal_hom` / `principal_degree_zero` / `LinearEquivalence`; Lane 1 (`OCofP.lean` FIX-BUILD) was specifically dispatched to repair the iter-176 race on `Scheme.RationalMap.order` and threaded `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` + per-Q `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 _]` as instructed, but could not anticipate Lane WD's NEW class addition. Lane WD's task_result explicitly disclosed the signature change as "Signature changes (heads-up for downstream files)" ‚ÄĒ BUT incorrectly claimed "No iter-177 downstream file consumes `principal_degree_zero`, so no breakage this iter"; the breakage was on `principal` (consumed at OCofP.lean L335 inside `exists_nonconstant_genusZero`), not on `principal_degree_zero`. Result: `lake build AlgebraicJacobian` ‚Üí 1 error at L335 `failed to synthesize C.left.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`. **Original iter-176 example**: Lane D (`WeilDivisor.lean`) added `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` and `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]` instance binders to `Scheme.RationalMap.order` at 13:56:23Z; Lane K (`OCofP.lean`) ‚ÄĒ committed 4 minutes EARLIER at 13:51:54Z ‚ÄĒ calls `Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f` in `globalSections_iff` (L194-195) and `exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (L327-328) without those instances. Each lane's local `lake env lean <file>` returned green; the merged `lake build AlgebraicJacobian` returned 4 errors. **Mitigations** (planner-side, escalated after iter-177 recurrence ‚ÄĒ choose at least one): (a) **serialize signature-changing lanes** ‚ÄĒ do NOT co-dispatch a lane whose primary objective edits a shared signature alongside a lane that authors a new file consuming that signature; (b) **post-merge build gate inside the loop** ‚ÄĒ after all lanes commit, run `lake build` once and surface failures as a same-iter mini-repair pass before sync_leanok runs; (c) **directive contract on the consuming lane's side** ‚ÄĒ when a file-skeleton lane consumes a name owned by another in-flight lane, encode "thread `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]` IF present in the consumed signature" as a defensive shim; (d) **iter-178+ MANDATE**: any prover lane that adds OR removes an instance binder on a shared lemma signature MUST audit + enumerate every downstream consumer file in its task_result; the disclosure must name SPECIFIC call sites (not "no downstream file consumes X" ‚ÄĒ that's not enough granularity to catch sibling consumers). **Detection rule**: when sorry counts go up and `lake build` goes red but no per-lane task_result reports errors, suspect a parallel-lane signature race. Now 2 consecutive iters ‚ÄĒ SYSTEMIC loop issue, not lane-author negligence.

- **Placeholder-body laundering on weak-type encodings ‚ÄĒ closing kernel-clean proofs against a trivial body moves the problem one layer deeper without making it easier** *(NEW iter-176 Archon canonical ‚ÄĒ `RelativeSpec` / `structureMorphism` / their 3 dependents; do-not-treat-as-genuine-closure.)* iter-176 example: Lane B closed all 5 file sorries in `Picard/RelativeSpec.lean` axiom-clean, BUT the bodies of `RelativeSpec` and `structureMorphism` are placeholders: `RelativeSpec _ūĚíú := X` (the base scheme itself; does not depend on `ūĚíú`) and `structureMorphism _ūĚíú := ūĚüô X` (the identity). The three downstream theorems (`UniversalProperty`, `affine_base_iff`, `base_change`) discharge trivially against these placeholders via routine instance + iso construction (`infer_instance` on `IsAffineHom (ūĚüô X)`; `change IsAffine (Spec R)`; `asIso (pullback.fst g (ūĚüô X))`). The proofs are HONEST given the body; the body is NOT "the relative spectrum." Existing chapter `% NOTE:` flags from iter-173 already warned that the encoded types were weaker than the prose; iter-176 closed those weakened types against an even-weaker body. **Do not treat axiom-clean closures against placeholder bodies as genuine route progress**: the next planner reading "RelativeSpec 5/5 closed kernel-clean" must check the body shape before claiming the route advanced. **Recurrence-prevention**: (a) prover task_results should disclose body-vs-type-vs-prose gaps explicitly (Lane B did this ‚ÄĒ emulate); (b) review must add `% NOTE (iter-N review)` to the proof block when the proof discharges against a placeholder body, naming the upgrade trigger; (c) downstream consumers of placeholder-closed theorems must be explicitly marked "do not rely on iter-N closure" until the genuine body lands. Mirror pattern: strategy-critic's "pivots that move the same hard problem one layer deeper" framing on Route C ‚ÄĒ Lane B is the same shape applied to types instead of routes.

Both `lean-auditor-review134` and `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review134` classified this as must-fix-this-iter under the strict rubric. **Do not retry**: future iters needing a multi-iter scaffold MUST either (a) write the intended type signature with a `sorry` body (mirroring `Jacobian.lean:194-215`'s `positiveGenusWitness`), or (b) defer the declaration entirely until the supporting infrastructure (e.g., `ŌÜ_*` compatibility morphisms) is in place. See the `Nonempty (X ‚ČÖ X)` anti-pattern Proof Pattern entry above for the rationale and the recommended refactor. The iter-134 placeholders themselves are recommended for iter-135 refactor to the honest scaffold pattern (project sorry count 4 ‚Üí 7 transiently).

- **`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheafUniqueGluing_type` (Differentials.lean L175 ‚Üí L191) substantive closure** *(SUPERSEDED iter-117 ‚ÄĒ entire declaration deleted as part of the aggressive trim; orphan to the 9 protected declarations)*:
  the route was correctly classified as gate-fired-stuck after 5 audited

- **`MonoidalCategory.Invertible (X.Modules)` as a C1 promotion target name**

- **Concurrent firing of orthogonal Phase actions without analogist consult**
  *(NEW iter-108 Archon, anti-pattern)*: strategy-critic-iter108 must-fix #2

  The iter-108 plan agent rebutted: same critic flagged `MonoidalCategory.Invertible`
  as NOT FULLY VERIFIED ‚Üí verification this iter found the strategy text
  wrong (name does not exist) ‚Üí analogist returned three additional critical

  could not have anticipated. Lesson: **defer high-stakes refactors one iter
  to consume the analogist's persistent-file recipe**; 1-iter latency cost
  beats landing-then-cleaning a flawed refactor.
- **`LocalizedModule.map_exact` direct application in
  `exact_of_isLocalized_span` outer scaffolds** *(NEW iter-108,
  anti-pattern)*: the lemma takes `Function.Exact f_R g_R` and
  produces `Function.Exact (LocalizedModule.map S f_R)
  (LocalizedModule.map S g_R)`. In the `h_K‚āÄ_exact` scaffold the
  unlocalised premise is exactly the conclusion being constructed ‚ÄĒ
  feeding it would be circular. Verified iter-108 via `lean_run_code`
  inspection of the signature. **Never use this lemma as the closing
  tool inside an `exact_of_isLocalized_span` outer scaffold.** Use
  `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` + `IsLocalizedModule.pi`
  + `IsLocalizedModule.iso` instead (see Proof Patterns above).
- **Reuse of `h_loc_X_i f` directly without slice-cover bridge for
  `h_loc_exact`** *(NEW iter-108)*: `h_loc_X_i f` is
  `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1 (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap ...)` which
  is `localizedModuleIsLocalizedModule _` ‚ÄĒ trivial; contributes no
  slice-cover identification. The slice-cover bridge requires
  geometric `h_V_le_U` + `h_slice_eq` + the algebra adapter chain.
- **Heartbeat-budget escalation on `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`** *(NEW iter-107
  ‚ÄĒ FORBIDDEN by iter-108 abort policy)*: the iter-106 review's recommended
  workaround "bump maxHeartbeats 1600000 ‚Üí 3200000+" is now FORBIDDEN per
  STRATEGY.md iter-108 abort policy. Six consecutive iters with whnf-timeout
  blockers (4 of 6 audited) confirm the cost is in the Pi.lift body itself
  (anonymous-closure codomain), not in the scalar or in the chain depth.
  Higher heartbeat budgets are a workflow accelerant for a polynomial-cost
  problem; this one is exponential in the closure depth. Use refactor /
  mathlib-analogist / strategy-critic pivot instead.
- **`generalize hŌÉ : ((-1 : ‚Ą§) ^ (‚ÜĎi : ‚Ąē)) = ŌÉ` followed by

  subsequent rw still hits whnf-timeout at 1600000 heartbeats because the

  attempt on this combination.
- **`rw [‚Üź LinearMap.comp_apply, ‚Üź ModuleCat.hom_comp, ‚Üź LinearMap.comp_apply,
  ‚Üź ModuleCat.hom_comp]` chain to reverse L1114's simp** *(NEW iter-107)*:
  whnf-timeout at 1600000 heartbeats. Confirms the blocker is at the smul-Pi.lift
  INTERFACE, robust to forward AND backward composition reasoning. Don't
  attempt simp-reversal as an escape route.
- **`change` / `show` with named family + explicit `eqToHom` proof object
  on the post-S5 frame** *(NEW iter-107 dead-end + RECONFIRMS iter-100/103
  for new frame)*: the type-equality `Fin ((prev n) + 2) ‚Üí s‚āÄ = Fin (n+1) ‚Üí s‚āÄ`
  requires `Fin.cast`-based construction; `omega` alone cannot prove the
  family-equality. Application type mismatch on the constructed `eqToHom ‚čĮ`
  metavariable. Reconfirms iter-100/iter-103 `_`-codomain ascription failure
  class in the post-S5 frame.
- **`rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` at maxHeartbeats=1600000 after
  ‚Üź ConcreteCategory.comp_apply (√ó4) fuse on cechCofaceMap_pi_smul**
  *(NEW iter-106 + RECONFIRMED iter-107)*: after the iter-099 S1-S3 chain
  fuses the 4-layer function-application stack into a single categorical
  composition `((-1)^‚ÜĎi ‚ÄĘ F_at_i) ‚Čę eqToHom_outer ‚Čę Pi.ŌÄ Z‚āā j'`, the
  subsequent `rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` triggers a deterministic whnf
  timeout at 1600000 heartbeats. The iter-106 review recommended bumping
  the budget; the iter-108 abort policy now FORBIDS that workaround. Use
  refactor of the anonymous-closure Pi.lift form instead.
- **`rcases n with _ | n'` + `have hPrev := simp [...]` body
  scaffold for object-equality eqToHom lemma proofs** *(NEW iter-106
  dead-end + lean-auditor major flag)*: hPrev does not propagate
  into the eqToHom's hCod-level types. Cleanest is to either
  complete the proof (via type-level substitution upstream) or
  revert to a single `sorry` until the proof is ready.
- **`simp only [eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp]` to collapse
  propositional-equality hCod** *(NEW iter-106 dead-end)*:
  `eqToHom_refl` requires LITERAL rfl syntactic match on the proof
  object, not propositional equality on hCod. Use `subst` (if the
  equation has a variable on one side) or `congr` (if the two
  sides are def-equal under context substitution).
- **`subst hPrev` to convert (prev (n'+1)) ‚Ü¶ n' inside hCod**
  *(NEW iter-106 dead-end)*: subst direction needs a variable on
  the LHS; hPrev's LHS `(prev (n'+1))` is computational, not a
  variable. Cannot subst from def-form.
- **`simp only [hPrev] at hCod`** *(NEW iter-106 dead-end)*: simp
  does not fire on type-level Fin index occurrences inside `‚ąŹŠ∂ú`'s
  index family.
- **`aesop_cat` on the Route 1 eqToHom-vs-Pi.ŌÄ succ-case body**
  *(NEW iter-106 dead-end)*: no aesop rule for eqToHom-vs-Pi.ŌÄ
  across products with different Fin index types.
- **`rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z‚āā j']` (forward
  direction) on the wrapper R-linearity hypothesis `h_wrap_pt` in
  `e‚āā x j'` form** *(iter-105 ‚ÄĒ RECONFIRMED across the wrapper)*:
  iter-099 first documented that this `rw` fails because of the
  LinearEquiv coercion `e‚āā = (piIsoPi Z‚āā).toLinearEquiv` masking the
  discrimination key. Iter-105 attempt 6 re-probed on the wrapper's
  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear` output ‚ÄĒ same failure.
  **Project-wide rule**: when stating a Pi-output transport theorem
  intended for `rw` at the client site, prefer `(Pi.ŌÄ _ _).hom _`
  directly in the conclusion (avoiding `e‚āā` = LinearEquiv form), or
  supply a `show` def-eq pivot at the client site.
- **`simp only [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul, LinearMap.smul_apply, map_zsmul,

  in context as a hypothesis, the LHS pattern `((-1)^‚ÜĎi ‚ÄĘ F_at_i).hom z`
  under eqToHom + multiple ConcreteCategory.hom layers prevents
  `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` from firing by simp. **Same discrim-tree class
  as iter-099/100/101 attempts; eqToHom wrap does not dissolve it.

- **`rcases n with _ | m; case succ => simp only [show prev (m+1) = m
  from by simp [...]] at *` to collapse eqToHom for L1147**

  fully collapse eqToHom because `Fin (m+2) = Fin (m+1)` still drives
  the eqToHom proof object. **Workaround for iter-106**: stronger simp
  set `[eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id,
  Fin.cast_refl]` + post-simp `convert` / `rfl`.
- **eqToHom-vs-Pi.ŌÄ transport for *object* equality is a Mathlib gap**
  *(NEW iter-105, project-relevant note)*: Mathlib exposes
  `Limits.Pi.ŌÄ_comp_eqToHom` for **index** equality (eqToHom from
  `i = j`), but `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` needs the **object** equality
  case (`‚ąŹŠ∂ú Z‚āĀ = ‚ąŹŠ∂ú Z‚āā` from `Fin a = Fin b`). The workaround is a
  project-local helper proved via `Limits.Pi.hom_ext` + per-coord
  `Pi.lift_ŌÄ_apply` + `Fin.cast_cast`. Not suitable for Mathlib
  upstream without universe-polymorphic generalization. **See
  session_103/recommendations.md Route 1 for the suggested helper
  shape.**
- **Body-local `have h_smul_hom : ‚ąÄ {M N : ModuleCat.{u} k} (n : ‚Ą§)
  (f : M ‚ü∂ N) (x), (n ‚ÄĘ f).hom x = n ‚ÄĘ f.hom x := by intros; rfl`
  then `simp only [h_smul_hom]` / `rw [h_smul_hom]` on
  `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` post-S3 goal** *(NEW iter-099 ‚ÄĒ KILLS
  E1 ESCAPE ROUTE)*: helper TYPECHECKS (the equation is `rfl`) but
  `simp only` and `rw` both report "no progress" because the
  discrimination tree's pattern-match runs **before** rfl-evaluation.
  Same structural class as Mathlib `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul`. **The
  body-local helper escape route (E1 in iter-100/iter-101 plans)
  is CONFIRMED DEAD for the Pi.lift-anonymous-closure blocker.**
  Use top-level refactor with named binders instead.
- **In-place `set Pi_lift_thing : (‚ąŹŠ∂ú Z‚āĀ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ‚ü∂ _ :=
  Pi.lift fun i_1 ‚Ü¶ ...`** *(NEW iter-099 ‚ÄĒ KILLS in-place E2)*:
  `set` substitutes by syntactic match; the `_`-codomain ascription
  does NOT fold the actual `‚ąŹŠ∂ú (fun i_1 ‚Ü¶ ...)` codomain in the
  goal. Pi.lift's anonymous-closure codomain CANNOT be hidden
  behind a name without rewriting the full inferred type. Need
  top-level helper with named per-coordinate codomain `Z‚āā j`
  instead.
- **`rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z‚āā j']` (forward
  direction) on `e‚āā x j'` post-funext** *(NEW iter-099)*: the
  LinearEquiv coercion `e‚āā = (piIsoPi Z‚āā).toLinearEquiv` masks the
  discrimination key; `rw` reports "no occurrence". Use `show`
  def-eq pivot instead (works because the equation is rfl on the
  underlying head).
- **`rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` / `simp_rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]`

  on `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` POST-S3 goal** *(NEW iter-099)*:
  pattern `(?n ‚ÄĘ ?f) ‚Čę ?g` not found ‚ÄĒ the smul's RHS `Pi.lift fun
  i_1 ‚Ü¶ ...` with anonymous-closure codomain blocks pattern
  unification. Same root cause as pre-funext iter-100 failures;
  the per-coordinate frame does NOT dissolve this class because
  the composition is still inside `ConcreteCategory.hom (_ ‚Čę _ ‚Čę _)`.
- **`set h_sgn : k := (-1)^(‚ÜĎi : ‚Ąē)` on the `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`
  pre-funext goal** *(NEW iter-100)*: the scalar is ‚Ą§-typed (via
  Preadditive ZSMul on the categorical morphism), not k-typed; the
  `set : k` does not fold. Use `set : ‚Ą§` if needed at all.
- **`rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]` / `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]`
  / `dsimp only [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul]` on the `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`
  pre-`funext j'` goal** *(NEW iter-100)*: `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` IS
  rfl-applicable in vacuum (verified via `lean_run_code` on
  `(n ‚ÄĘ f).hom x = n ‚ÄĘ f.hom x` for concrete `Z : őĻ ‚Üí ModuleCat`),
  but FAILS to fire on the in-context goal through the Pi.lift's
  anonymous-closure codomain `‚ąŹŠ∂ú (fun i_1 ‚Ü¶ Pi.ŌÄ Z‚āĀ_unfolded (i_1 ‚ąė
  őī i) ‚Čę (toModuleKPresheaf C).map ...)`. Discrimination tree
  pattern-unification issue, NOT a missing-lemma issue. **Workaround:
  pivot to `funext j'` BEFORE attempting smul-extraction.**
- **`change e‚āā (((-1 : ‚Ą§)^‚ÜĎi) ‚ÄĘ ModuleCat.Hom.hom (Pi.lift fun i_1 ‚Ü¶ _)) ... = _`**
  *(NEW iter-100)*: inner `_` placeholders can't be filled because

- **`apply LinearEquiv.injective e‚āā.symm; funext j'`** *(NEW iter-100)*:

### User policy directives (iter-116 response in `USER_HINTS.md`)

After the iter-116 user-escalation pause, the user responded with five
policy-level rules that supersede any prior loop-internal framing:

1. **Self-decide best strategy** ‚ÄĒ the user does not pick among
   planner-surfaced options. The loop must autonomously decide and
   commit, then explain the reasoning in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`.
2. **Strict correctness ‚ÄĒ no temporarily-wrong statements** ‚ÄĒ any wrong
   mathematical statement (definition, signature, proof) must be removed,
   and the gap filled (either with a correct closure or an honest
   disclosure that is itself mathematically clean). "Temporarily wrong"
   is no longer an acceptable state.
3. **Blueprints must be detailed enough for provers** ‚ÄĒ chapters need
   enough mathematical material that a prover can formalize without
   re-deriving the content.
4. **`STRATEGY.md` must be cleanly organized ‚ÄĒ strategy-only, no history**
   ‚ÄĒ cumulative iter-by-iter achievement enumeration is policy-banned in
   STRATEGY.md. History belongs in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` sidecars and
   here in PROJECT_STATUS.md's iteration log.
5. **Nothing should be deferred** ‚ÄĒ every active sorry needs a concrete
   closure plan with a budget. "Permanent named Mathlib gap" framing is
   no longer policy-acceptable. Named gaps remain valid as scaffolding,
   but each must have an active multi-iter closure route.

**These rules apply to the entire iter-117+ planning surface**, not only
the L175 route. Plan agents must verify every dispatched lane and every
STRATEGY.md edit against rules (2) and (5) before committing.

### Sandbox issue (RESOLVED iter-092)

The "`.lake/packages/mathlib` missing / `lake` not on PATH" condition
was **resolved structurally** in iter-092 by symlinking
`~/.elan/bin/{lake,lean,...}` into `~/.local/bin/`. LSP/lake/build
working as of iter-092 and confirmed through iter-099.

- **iter-221 ‚ÄĒ `PresheafOfModules.dual` built (funded Decision-1 internal-hom block, sub-step 3 PARTIAL).** The contravariant dual presheaf `M ‚Ü¶ ‚Ąčom(M, R)` (`def:presheaf_dual`, `PresheafOfModules.dual` ~L1349 of `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`) is built axiom-clean as `dual M := InternalHom.internalHom M (ūĚüô_)` (the iter-220 `internalHom` specialised at the monoidal unit; `ūĚüô_ = PresheafOfModules.unit` by `rfl`) ‚ÄĒ refuting, for the dual specifically, the iter-219 analogist's "absent at every level" pessimism. Per-object eval `internalHomEvalApp` (~L1398) + helpers `evalLin`/`evalLin_add`/`evalLin_smul`/`termRingMap_terminal` also axiom-clean (6 decls, `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, re-verified by lean-auditor ts221). sorry flat 3‚Üí3 (untouched residuals `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` ~L602, `exists_tensorObj_inverse` ~L1839, `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` ~L1889). The FULL natural eval morphism `internalHomEval` is NOT assembled ‚ÄĒ naturality reduces (via `tensor_ext`) to `PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply ŌÜ (Over.homMk f.unop).op s`, the SAME `Over.map` pseudofunctor coherence obstacle iter-220 cracked for `restrictionMap` (helper `hom_app_heq`: `subst h; rfl` + `eq_of_heq`) ‚ÄĒ so reuse that, do not re-derive. `sync_leanok` iter-221 sha `b263bbf6` added 2 markers (the axiom-clean dual block), removed 0; `lem:internal_hom_eval` correctly stays UNMARKED (the `internalHomEval` decl does not yet exist ‚ÄĒ only the per-object `internalHomEvalApp`).
- **Diamond-bridge tricks for the eval map (iter-221, reusable):** (1) over-ring CAST via `evalLin`'s type ascription (`ŌÜ.app term` is linear over `((Over.forget X.unop).op ‚čô R‚āÄ).obj _`, defeq-but-not-syntactic to `R‚āÄ.obj X`); (2) keep the eval value at its NATURAL over-ring type in `change` proofs (never cast), then `rw [termRingMap_terminal]` + `rfl` discharges the smul defeq; (3) `show ModuleCat.of (R‚āÄ.obj X) _ ‚ü∂ ModuleCat.of (R‚āÄ.obj X) (R‚āÄ.obj X) from ofHom ...` at the `ofHom` boundary. NEVER land eval in `(ūĚüô_).obj X` ‚ÄĒ it reduces to the bare `RingCat` carrier and loses the `CommRingCat` module. `termRingMap` needs its ring presheaf passed explicitly (`termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ‚čô R‚āÄ) ...`).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-230 addition)

- **C-bridge `dual_restrict_iso` via the iter-229 shared root `overSliceSheafEquiv` (iter-230, DOES NOT CLOSE ‚ÄĒ do not retry).** `overSliceSheafEquiv : Sheaf((gt X).over U) A ‚ČĆ Sheaf(Opens U) A` is **sheaf-level** and parametric in a **fixed value category** `A`. The C-bridge residual after Steps 1‚Äď3 + H1 is `(PresheafOfModules.pushforward ő≤).obj (dual M.val) ‚ČÖ dual ((pushforward ő≤).obj M.val)` ‚ÄĒ **presheaf-level and varying-ring-module** (ModuleCat over the varying `ūĚí™_Y(V)`). Three independent mismatches confirmed live (lean_goal/lean_multi_attempt: "Unknown constant" at the site + type-incompat): (1) sheaf vs presheaf; (2) fixed value-cat vs varying-ring module action (`internalHomObjModule`); (3) whole-`U` slice site vs per-open `restr W = pushforward‚āÄ(Over.forget W)` slices. **General lesson: a value-category-FIXED site/sheaf equivalence cannot transport a varying-ring module iso.** The A-engine `homOfLocalCompat` (value cat `Type`) IS served by the root, but `exists_tensorObj_inverse` needs C too. Real missing ingredient: a presheaf+module per-`V` slice-internal-hom comparison (`f^* ‚Ąčom(A,B) ‚ČÖ ‚Ąčom(f^*A,f^*B)` for an open immersion), ~150‚Äď300 LOC with genuine `Over.map` coherence risk (thinness does NOT collapse it here). Decomposition: `informal/dual_restrict_iso.md`. As of iter-230 the whole tensorObj-substrate route is escalated to the USER (divisor `Pic‚Āį` vs. the slice-comparison sub-build); 11 iters (217‚Üí230) flat at sorry 80.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-231 addition)

- **`dual_restrict_iso` as a single monolithic axiom-clean lemma in ONE iter (iter-227‚Üí231, do not retry this shape).** Five iters (227 helpers, 228 H2‚Ä≤ hard-block, 229 shared-root, 230 probe-falsifies-root, 231 no-edit stall) failed to land it in one shot. **Root cause, precisely stated (iter-231 prover):** for the CLOSED `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the Step-4 residual "pushforward commutes with `‚äó‚āö`" was discharged in ~5 lines by Mathlib-adjacent machinery ‚ÄĒ `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective` + `pushforward‚āÄOfCommRingCat.Monoidal` supply the tensorator `őľIso` for free. **There is NO analogous packaged "dual commutes with pushforward."** `PresheafOfModules.dual` is the project's bespoke slice/end internal-hom (`internalHom(-, ūĚüô_)`, value over `Over W`) with no Mathlib base-change API, so the residual `(pushforward ő≤).obj (dual M.val) ‚ČÖ dual ((pushforward ő≤).obj M.val)` is a genuine new ~150‚Äď300 LOC build: a presheaf-level, `ūĚí™_Y(V)`-linear slice comparison `Hom_{Over_X(fV)}(restr A, restr ūĚüô) ‚ČÖ Hom_{Over_Y V}(restr (pushforward A), restr ūĚüô)`, induced by the per-`V` slice equivalence `Over_Y V ‚ČĆ Over_X (f.opensFunctor V)` + ring-iso transport `ő≤ = f.appIso`, with REAL `Over.map` module-coherence risk (thinness does NOT collapse it here ‚ÄĒ the slice presheaves carry the module structure). **Only attempt as an EXPLICIT decomposed multi-iter sub-build:** first iter lands ONLY the per-`V` slice equivalence as a standalone axiom-clean `def` (the one coherence-risk-free piece); the `Over.map` module-coherence transport is its own later iter.
- **Route II (object-glue `exists_tensorObj_inverse` off the dual) does NOT dodge the C-bridge.** Gluing local contractions `(L ‚äó dual L)|_{UŠĶĘ} ‚ČÖ ūĚí™_{UŠĶĘ}` still transitively needs `dual_isLocallyTrivial` ‚Üí `dual_restrict_iso`. It sidesteps the global associator/whiskering machinery but inherits the same C-bridge dependency. Partial escape, not a whole-block break.
- **Environment (iter-230‚Üí231): one-batch-behind harness tool-result latency.** Tool results (Read/Bash/`lean_diagnostic_messages`/Grep) arriving one batch late in delayed bursts degraded BOTH phase agents (plan: confabulation near-misses; prover: read‚Üíedit‚Üíverify loop infeasible, budget spent reading the 2375-line file). Mitigation: file-split `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` to shrink the per-iter read surface. Not an agent-fixable condition.
- **General lesson (carried, iter-231 sharpening):** before committing a multi-iter categorical sub-build whose size is contested between subagents (analyst said near-definitional; blueprint-clean said ~150‚Äď300 LOC), run a CHEAP scratch probe of just the value-level defeq (`lean_multi_attempt` on the objectwise map) to get a live datapoint first. iter-231's no-edit stall produced no such signal because the gate was all-or-nothing.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-232 addition)

- **Adjoint-mate base-change comparison map (`Scheme.Modules`).** To build a canonical comparison `g^*(f_*F) ‚ü∂ f'_*(g'^*F)` for a commutative square (`comm : g' ‚Čę f = f' ‚Čę g`), take `((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm` of the composite `(pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ‚Čę (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ‚Čę (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ‚Čę (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _`. The pseudofunctor coherences `pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` carry the square's commutativity. Confirmed axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` and type-sound at every intermediate step (lean-auditor iter-232). Used in `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean:pushforwardBaseChangeMap`.
- **`Scheme.Modules` namespace gotcha:** under `open Limits`, bare `pullback` is ambiguous with `Limits.pullback`. Always qualify `Scheme.Modules.pullback` in signatures.
- **Sectionwise iso reduction for `SheafOfModules` maps:** `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` reduces `IsIso ŌÜ` to `‚ąÄ U, IsIso (Hom.app ŌÜ U)` ‚ÄĒ a real, live reduction (Mathlib `AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean`).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-232 addition)

- **Dual / `dual_restrict_iso` / `exists_tensorObj_inverse` route DEMOTED to a deferred bridge (iter-232 carrier pivot ‚ÄĒ do not re-prioritize).** The 14-iter stall was a **carrier-choice** problem. Carrying `Pic X` on the locally-trivial predicate forces *manufacturing* an inverse object (the dual or cocycle gluing). Carrying it on tensor-invertibility `IsInvertible M := ‚ąÉN, M‚äóN‚ČÖūĚí™` (Stacks 0B8M) makes the inverse the membership **witness** ‚ÄĒ free. Both predicates define the SAME relative Picard functor (Stacks 0B8M: invertible ‚üļ locally free rank 1), so the pivot is faithful, not a weakening. The dual bridge (`IsInvertible ‚üļ IsLocallyTrivial`) is retained only for a contingency (a downstream consumer that provably needs the locally-trivial carrier ‚ÄĒ none identified; A.3 Poincar√© bundle named as candidate). All the iter-227‚Üí231 dual blockers above remain valid as "why the dual is hard" but are now **off the group's critical path.**
- **Affine pushforward/pullback dictionary for tilde-modules is ABSENT from Mathlib (iter-232).** `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean:affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` reduces cleanly via `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` but its residual section-iso needs: (1) `Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map ŌÜ)` ‚ČÉ `ModuleCat.restrictScalars`; (2) `Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ŌÜ)` ‚ČÉ `- ‚äó B`; (3) "iso local on an affine cover" for `SheafOfModules` maps. None exist (confirmed by source grep over `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/` + `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/`). The closing algebra `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (`(R'‚äó[R]A)‚äó[A]M ‚ČÉ R'‚äó[R]M`) DOES exist. Build the dictionary as a separate `mathlib-build` sub-lane; decomposition in `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Process gotcha (iter-232 addition)

- **A new module under `AlgebraicJacobian/` does not enter the canonical build until imported by `AlgebraicJacobian.lean`.** iter-232's `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` was created + built standalone (GREEN, axiom-clean def + 2 documented sorries) but NOT imported by the aggregator ‚ÄĒ so its work is invisible to the project sorry count and package build until the plan/refactor phase adds `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange`. Provers (single-file write domain) cannot wire this themselves; they must flag it and the next plan phase must land the import. Watch for orphan engine modules when opening new parallel lanes.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-233 addition)

- **`colimit.desc` into `AddCommGrpCat` ‚ÄĒ cocone naturality recipe (iter-233, validated in `StalkTensor.lean`).** To discharge the cocone-compatibility square when descending sectionwise maps to a presheaf stalk: bare `ext z` (categorical), then `induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on`. The `tmul` case closes with `simp only [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, Functor.const_obj_map]` then `erw [tensorObj_map_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul]; erw [germ_res_apply, germ_res_apply]; rfl`. **`erw` is essential** ‚ÄĒ `R` vs `R ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat`, and PoM-`map` vs presheaf-`map`, are defeq but not syntactic. The `zero`/`add` cases need **term-mode** `exact (AddMonoidHom.map_zero _).trans (AddMonoidHom.map_zero _).symm` / `exact (AddMonoidHom.map_add _ p q).trans ((congrArg‚āā HAdd.hAdd hp hq).trans (AddMonoidHom.map_add _ p q).symm)` ‚ÄĒ generic `map_zero`/`map_add`/`rw` do NOT match the `AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom` wrapper. `.presheaf` of a `PresheafOfModules` is valued in `AddCommGrpCat` (constructors `AddCommGrpCat.of`/`.ofHom`); `(tensorObj A B).presheaf.obj (op U)` is defeq to `TensorProduct (R.obj (op U)) (A.obj (op U)) (B.obj (op U))`.
- **`Scheme.Modules` iso-locality criteria (iter-233, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** Mathlib has the per-open `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` and the `TopCat.Sheaf`-level `isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`, but NOT stalk/basis-local criteria at the `Scheme.Modules` morphism level. Build them: stalk route packages the underlying `Ab`-presheaves as `TopCat.Sheaf`, applies `isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`, then `toPresheaf` reflects isos via `isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso`; basis route reduces to the stalk one via `stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis` + `germ_exist_of_isBasis`. GOTCHA: `((toPresheaf X).map ŌÜ).app (op U)` is defeq-not-syntactic to `ŌÜ.app U`, so `stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply` needs `erw` + a trailing `rfl`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-233 addition)

- **d.2 forward map built, ISO still open (iter-233, the named critical path).** `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorDesc : (A‚äóŠĶĖB).stalk x ‚ü∂ A_x‚äó_{R_x}B_x` (the FORWARD comparison map) is axiom-clean in `StalkTensor.lean` (+ germ chars `germ_stalkTensorDesc`, `stalkTensorDesc_germ_tmul`). The blueprint-pinned ISO `stalkTensorIso` (`lem:stalk_tensor_commutation`) does NOT yet exist. Remaining: `stalkTensorDescU_smul` (blocked by the CommRingCat/RingCat **carrier duality** ‚ÄĒ with `r : ‚ÜĎ(R.obj (op U))` the tensor smul lemmas fail to synthesize `DistribMulAction`/`SMulCommClass`; with the RingCat carrier they fire but `germ R r` mistypes; needs a small `RingEquiv`/`eqToHom` bridge, ~20‚Äď40 LOC) ‚Üí `R_x`-linear map (mirror `Vestigial.lean::stalkLinearMap`) ‚Üí reverse map (nested colimit descent, ~150‚Äď250 LOC) ‚Üí iso bundling on generators. Closing `stalkTensorIso` unblocks `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` ‚Üí unconditional associator ‚Üí `thm:pic_commgroup`.
- **`TopCat.Sheaf.isIso_iff_isIso_basis` is ABSENT in the pinned Mathlib (iter-233).** `lean_loogle` reports it but it is loogle-stale; do not `rw`/`exact` it. Use the stalk route (`isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` + germ-of-basis lifts).
- **`EnoughInjectives`/`IsGrothendieckAbelian` for `SheafOfModules` (`X.Modules`) is ABSENT from Mathlib (iter-233).** `Abelian X.Modules` exists (`Scheme.Modules.instAbelian`) but the Grothendieck-abelian instance `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.instIsGrothendieckAbelian` is only for `Sheaf J A` (fixed value category), not modules over a sheaf of rings. So `Functor.rightDerived` (hence `higherDirectImage`) requires carrying `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` as an explicit hypothesis. Do NOT sorry an `EnoughInjectives` instance ‚ÄĒ it would silently contaminate every consumer. A `mathlib-build` lane proving `IsGrothendieckAbelian X.Modules` would drop the hypothesis.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-235 addition)

- **Reverse-map double-colimit descent (iter-235, `StalkTensor.lean`, stage iv of d.2).** Building the additive bilinear comparison `A_x ‚Üí+ B_x ‚Üí+ (A‚äóŠĶĖB).stalk x` by hand (no Mathlib "tensor commutes with filtered colimit"): inner leg `revInnerLeg : B(V) ‚Üí St`, `b ‚Ü¶ germ_{U‚äďV}((a|)‚äó(b|))` for fixed `a` (annotate the `TensorProduct.mk` with the **plain-module** carriers `‚ÜĎ(A.obj (op (U‚äďV)))`/`‚ÜĎ(B.obj (op (U‚äďV)))` over `R.obj (op (U‚äďV))` + explicit type ascriptions on both factors ‚ÄĒ the na√Įve annotation triggers a `restrictScalars` Module-synth failure); descend via `colimit.desc` over `(OpenNhds.inclusion x).op ‚čô B.presheaf`; repeat for the `A`-variable to get `revBihom`. Germ characterisations via `colimit.őĻ_desc`.
- **Cocone-naturality plumbing for `colimit.desc` into `AddCommGrpCat` (iter-235 refinement).** `apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext; ext b; simp only [comp_apply, Functor.const_obj_map]; erw [CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.id_apply]`. The `((Functor.const _).obj _).map f = ūĚüô` survives `rw [Category.comp_id]`/`simp`; the `(ūĚüô).hom` wrapper must be killed at the **element** level by `erw [ConcreteCategory.id_apply]` (plain `rw`/`simp` on it times out in `whnf`). To expand a `private` `rfl`-leg-eval lemma under a `ūĚüô`/`ofHom` wrapper without a `whnf` timeout, narrow scope with `conv_rhs => erw [leg_apply]`.
- **Section-equality from presheaf functoriality + poset thinness (iter-235, the germ_res residual).** `rw [‚Üź presheaf_map_apply_coe, ‚Ä¶]` to uniformise `M.map` ‚Üí `M.presheaf.map`, then `erw [‚Üź CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ‚Üź Functor.map_comp]` to merge composites (`comp_apply` merges ANY two composable `AddCommGrpCat` morphisms regardless of producing functor; after `Functor.comp_map` it merges `((incl).op ‚čô M.presheaf).map f` with `M.presheaf.map _`), then `congr 1` discharges the morphism equality by thinness (proof irrelevance on `homOfLE` `‚Č§`-proofs). `map_add`/`map_zero` on a bundled `ConcreteCategory.hom` need `erw [_root_.map_add, ‚Ä¶]` (plain `rw`/`simp` fail the `AddMonoidHomClass` synth during matching).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-235 addition)

- **The CommRingCat/RingCat carrier-duality is NOT fully "retired" ‚ÄĒ it resurfaces at the d.2 balancing with an extra `restrictScalars` wrapper (iter-235).** iter-234's review recorded the carrier duality as retired by the stage-(iii) `stalkTensorDescU_smul` `erw`/`smul_tmul'` recipe. That was narrowly true for stage (iii). The stage-(iv) balancing `revBihom_balanced` (`revBihom (r‚ÄĘa) b = revBihom a (r‚ÄĘb)`, feeding `TensorProduct.liftAddHom` ‚Üí `stalkTensorRev`) reduces (via `germ_smul` + `revBihom_germ_tmul`) to a section-level identity, but `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` then introduces a **`restrictScalars` wrapper** absent in `stalkTensorDescU_smul`: the smul lands over the `RingCat` carrier `(R ‚čô forget‚āā).obj (op (W‚äďW))` on a `restrictScalars`-module, while the section tensor is annotated over the `CommRingCat` carrier on the plain module ‚ÄĒ so `TensorProduct.smul_tmul` cannot synthesize a `DistribMulAction` defeq to the inferred `ModuleCat.instModuleCarrierObjRestrictScalars`. **DO NOT retry the section-level `smul_tmul` route** (all of `exact smul_tmul`, `smul_tmul (R:=‚Ä¶)` both carriers, `haveI := ModuleCat.isModule ‚Ä¶; smul_tmul`, `rw smul_tmul/smul_tmul'` fail). **Use route 2:** prove the balancing at the **stalk level** `revBihom (r‚ÄĘa) b = r ‚ÄĘ revBihom a b` (St carries the `R_x`-action from `ModuleCat.Stalk`) via the `T`-presheaf `germ_smul`, keeping the scalar at `R_x` so no `RingCat`/`restrictScalars` carrier appears.
- **d.2 progress vs. counter (iter-235).** The d.2 lane has landed a named stage axiom-clean for THREE consecutive iters (233 forward map, 234 `R_x`-linear packaging, 235 reverse-map descent ‚ÄĒ 10 axiom-clean `private` decls). This is convergence, not churn. But the critical-path counter (`thm:pic_commgroup`) is flat for 18 iters (since iter-217): `stalkTensorIso` is still unbuilt ‚ÄĒ only `revBihom_balanced` (route 2) + the stage-(v) mutual-inversion bundle remain.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-236 addition)

- **d.2 balancing carrier wall RESOLVED ‚ÄĒ prove the section identity at the W level, not W‚äďW (iter-236, `StalkTensor.lean`, supersedes the iter-235 known-blocker).** The iter-235 wall (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul` adds a `restrictScalars` wrapper ‚Üí `TensorProduct.smul_tmul` cannot synthesize the `DistribMulAction` defeq at `W‚äďW` over the `RingCat` carrier) is REAL but AVOIDABLE. **Recipe:** prove `(r‚āÄ‚ÄĘa)‚äób = a‚äó(r‚āÄ‚ÄĘb)` at the **W-section level over the `CommRingCat` carrier `R(W)`** ‚ÄĒ where `R' = R`, so `smul_tmul` synthesises cleanly with no carrier mixing ‚ÄĒ then transport down to the `W‚äďW` identity via `congrArg ((tensorObj A B).map j.op)` + `erw [tensorObj_map_tmul]`. The route-2 stalk-level `germ_smul` idea (iter-235 handoff) feeds the reduction, but the actual close is the W-level section identity. **CRITICAL gotcha:** a STANDALONE bare-`‚äó‚āú[R.obj (op Z)]` lemma of `A.presheaf.map` outputs FAILS `Module` synth (`A.presheaf.obj` vs `A.obj`); WRAP the equality in `germ` (`revBihom_balanced_germ` is germ-wrapped, not a bare section identity) ‚ÄĒ the germ's domain supplies the expected module type. This closed `revBihom_balanced` ‚Üí `stalkTensorRev` ‚Üí `stalkTensorIso`.
- **`TensorProduct.induction_on` carrier bridge in iso inversion proofs (iter-236).** When proving `left_inv`/`right_inv` of a stalk-tensor `LinearEquiv` by `TensorProduct.induction_on`, the `tmul` cases come out over the `RingCat` carrier `(R ‚čô forget‚āā).obj` while the germ-characterisation lemmas (`stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul`, `stalkTensorRev_germ_tmul`) use `R.obj` (CommRingCat). Bridge with `erw` (defeq), NOT `rw`. The `zero`/`add` cases: `simp only [map_zero/map_add]` makes NO progress on `ConcreteCategory.hom`-applied terms ‚ÄĒ use `erw [map_zero, map_zero, map_zero]` / `erw [map_add, map_add, map_add, hp, hq]`. Folding `A.map`/`B.map` via `‚Üź tensorObj_map_tmul` needs a SINGLE restriction map (`germ_tensorObj_map_tmul` takes one `j` applied with `inf_le_left`; the `B`-side `inf_le_right` unifies by proof-irrelevance defeq) plus the `presheaf.map`‚Üí`map` bridge `‚Üź presheaf_map_apply_coe`.
- **Element-free ModuleCat iso across a propositional ring-naturality equation (iter-236, `FlatBaseChange.lean`, the őď-fragment keystone).** When two `ModuleCat` carriers are definitionally equal but their `Module` instances differ by a ring-hom-naturality equation (e.g. `őď((Spec ŌÜ)_* N)` vs `restrictScalars ŌÜ (őď N)`), do NOT attempt the element-level `map_smul'` (see Known Blockers below). Build the iso at the functor level: both sides peel by `rfl` to nested `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` towers over the common section module; reconcile via `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` √ó 2 (the trivially-named `rfl` composition equalities) + `eqToIso (congrArg (fun h => (restrictScalars h).obj SecN) hcomp)` for the ring equation. The ring equation `hcomp : pushTop.comp gsRhom = gsR'hom.comp ŌÜ.hom` comes from `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop` (proved by `globalSectionsIso S .hom = (őďSpecIso S).inv` (rfl) + `Scheme.őďSpecIso_inv_naturality`) via `RingHom.ext` + `congr($_.hom x)`. NO element-level `smul` touched, so the carrier-instance wall never arises. `restrictScalarsComp'App` lives in `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Resolved blockers / Known Blockers (iter-236 addition)

- **d.2 critical path CLOSED ‚ÄĒ `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorIso` is BUILT, axiom-clean (iter-236).** The varying-ring stalk‚Äďtensor commutation `(A ‚äóŠĶĖ B).stalk x ‚ČÉ‚āó[R.stalk x] A_x ‚äó_{R_x} B_x` (`lem:stalk_tensor_commutation`, Stacks `lemma-stalk-tensor-product`) is complete and `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (verified first-hand + lean-auditor NON-VACUOUS + lean-vs-blueprint PASS). This is the single Mathlib-absent ingredient that gated the unconditional associator `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` ‚áí `thm:pic_commgroup` for ~19 iters. **It is the INGREDIENT, not the consumer:** `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` in `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` is still sorry-bearing and must be WIRED to consume `stalkTensorIso` (next critical-path unit ‚ÄĒ a bounded wiring task, no remaining Mathlib gap). Supersedes the iter-233/235 "d.2 still open" known-blockers.
- **őď-fragment iso `gammaPushforwardIso` BUILT ‚ÄĒ route (a) section-level `map_smul'` is DEAD (iter-236, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** The keystone `őď((Spec ŌÜ)_* N) ‚ČÖ restrictScalars ŌÜ (őď N)` that iter-234/235 could not build is now axiom-clean via the element-free route (b) above. **Route (a) confirmed dead ‚ÄĒ do NOT retry any section-level finisher:** the `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso`/`AddEquiv.refl` approach typechecks once annotated `‚ČÉ‚āó[(R : Type u)]` (NO `respectTransparency` ‚ÄĒ supersedes the iter-234 claim) and `map_smul'` reduces EXACTLY to `A ‚ÄĘ m = B ‚ÄĘ m` with `A,B : őď(Spec R',‚ä§)` equal by `őďSpecIso_inv_naturality`, but every element-level finisher hits the alias-vs-reduced-type `HSMul`-synth wall (`congr 1` ‚Üí `whnf` timeout; `congrArg (¬∑ ‚ÄĘ m)` / type-forced binder / `change _ ‚ÄĘ (m : reduced) = _` ‚Üí `failed to synthesize HSMul (alias) (carrier)`; `congr 2` ‚Üí `RingHom`-type + `HEq`).
- **FlatBaseChange object iso `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` reduced to ONE obligation (iter-236).** With `gammaPushforwardTildeIso` built, the object iso `pushforward (Spec ŌÜ)_*(tilde M) ‚ČÖ tilde (restrictScalars ŌÜ M)` factors through `fromTildeőď`, an iso **iff** `(Spec ŌÜ)_*(tilde M)` is quasi-coherent. QC of the pushforward is the sole remaining obligation ‚ÄĒ Mathlib-absent and circular with the counit route (`moduleSpecőďFunctor` reflects isos only between QC objects; the tilde-adjunction UNIT is the iso, not the counit). **Recommended attack = route (iii):** direct basic-open `Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis` (already in the file) + `IsLocalizedModule` (`(restrictScalars ŌÜ M)` localised at `a` = `M` localised at `ŌÜ a`) ‚ÄĒ yields the object iso AND QC simultaneously. Fallbacks: `SheafOfModules.Presentation`; `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop`. **Blueprint hazard (lean-vs-blueprint iter-236):** the `lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` proof sketch has a CIRCULAR QC dependency (QC presented as a corollary of the object iso when it is a prerequisite) ‚ÄĒ must be fixed by a blueprint-writer before the next FBC object-iso prover round.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-237 addition)

- **+/0 carrier-duality distribution wall ‚ÄĒ distribute via `map_add`/`map_zero` TERMS, never `rw`/`simp` on the concrete hom (iter-237, `Vestigial.lean`, the whiskering closure).** When a stalk/germ goal mixes the `AddCommGrpCat`/`ConcreteCategory` stalk hom `ŌÜ` (or `eN` whose argument came from `ŌÜ`) with `LinearEquiv` legs, the additive `+`/`0` instances on the defeq carriers `(toPresheaf _).obj (F ‚äó M)` vs `(Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf` (and `stalkFunctor.obj` vs `stalk ‚Ä¶ .presheaf`) differ **syntactically**, so `rw [map_add]` / `simp only [map_add]` will NOT fire on `ŌÜ`/`eN`. **Recipe:** in the `add` case distribute additivity via `have h := map_add _ _ _` *terms* (defeq-coerced to the goal's syntactic `+`), then `rw [h]`; in the `zero` case `erw [map_zero (ConcreteCategory.hom ŌÜ), map_zero eN, ‚Ä¶]` (defeq matching). `simp only [map_add]` DOES fire on the `LinearEquiv` legs (`eM`, `E`) whose arguments are already in canonical type. Also: `LinearEquiv` coe = underlying linear map by **defeq**, so `have hRM : eM (germ ‚Ä¶) = ‚Ä¶ := stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul ‚Ä¶` typechecks directly (no coe lemma).
- **d.1-bridge for the topological site `J.W g ‚Üí ‚ąÄx, IsIso (stalk map)` (iter-237, `Vestigial.lean`, `isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W`).** Mathlib supplies the presheaf *surjective* stalk bridge (`locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks`) and the *injective* one only at the SHEAF level (`app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`). The presheaf-level injective bridge `injective_stalk_of_isLocallyInjective` had to be built directly via `germ_eq` + the equalizer-sieve cover. Combine: `rw [W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg; intro z; rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]; constructor` then injective via the new bridge, surjective via Mathlib. `WEqualsLocallyBijective.W` unfolds to locally-injective AND locally-surjective.
- **Two-localization `LinearEquiv` when `.iso`/`.linearEquiv`/`.trans` fail instance synthesis (iter-237, `FlatBaseChange.lean`, `fromTildeőď_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule`).** `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S j ŌĀ` and inline `(iso S j).symm.trans (iso S ŌĀ)` BOTH fail to synthesize `ŌĀ`'s `IsLocalizedModule` instance even though `inferInstance` succeeds standalone ‚ÄĒ the `.trans`/`linearEquiv` expected-type propagation reinterprets `ŌĀ`'s domain and the local instance key no longer matches. **Fix:** elaborate each `iso` in a separate `set ej := ‚Ä¶ with`/`set eŌĀ := ‚Ä¶ with` (each resolves independently with its own instance), then `let e := ej.symm.trans eŌĀ`. Also `j` must be defined over the SAME unfolded domain expression as `ŌĀ` (`(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj (op ‚ä§)`), not via a `set őďN`, or the domain types diverge. `clear_value` the localization map before building the instance so unification does not unfold it.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-238 addition)

- **By-hand Picard `CommGroup` over a `Quotient` of iso-classes needs NO monoidal coherence (iter-238, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, `picCommGroup`).** Carry `Pic X := Quotient (picSetoid X)` where `picSetoid` (an `instance`) is `r M M' := Nonempty ((M:X.Modules) ‚ČÖ M')` on `{M // IsInvertible M}`. Every `CommGroup` field discharges identically: `rintro a (b c)‚Ä¶; induction ‚Ä¶ using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_; exact Quotient.sound ‚ü®‚Ä¶iso‚Ä¶‚ü©`. `Quotient.sound` reduces through `Quotient.lift‚āā`/`lift` **definitionally** (no `simp`/`erw`). The isos are: `mul_assoc`‚Üź`tensorObj_assoc_iso`, `one_mul`/`mul_one`‚Üźunitors, `mul_comm`‚Üź`tensorObj_braiding`, `inv_mul_cancel`‚Üź`tensorObj_braiding _ _ ‚Č™‚Čę (Classical.choose_spec _).some`. **No pentagon/triangle/hexagon, no `MonoidalCategory` instance** ‚ÄĒ group axioms are *existence* of isos, not coherence of a chosen one. `inv := picInv` via `Quotient.lift` over `Classical.choose` (the `IsInvertible` membership witness), well-def by `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`. `picMul` via `Quotient.lift‚āā`, well-def by `tensorObjIsoOfIso ea eb`. **This is the Stacks 0B8M carrier-pivot** ‚ÄĒ inverse is the membership witness, free.
- **Namespace shadowing inside `Foo.bar`-named decls (iter-238).** Inside a decl named `IsInvertible.tensorObj`, the bare identifier `tensorObj` resolves to the decl ITSELF (the `IsInvertible` namespace is in scope while elaborating) ‚Üí `Application type mismatch: ‚Ä¶ expected IsInvertible ?m ‚Ä¶`. **Fix:** fully-qualify (`Scheme.Modules.tensorObj`) in BOTH statement and body. Dot-notation (`hM.tensorObj hM'`) elsewhere is unaffected (it *means* `IsInvertible.tensorObj`). Same applied in `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`.
- **Class-type `def` ‚Üí `instance` for the reducible lint (iter-238).** A `noncomputable def picCommGroup : CommGroup ‚Ä¶` trips *"Definition of class type must be marked `@[reducible]`/`@[implicit_reducible]`"*. Declaring it `noncomputable instance` clears the lint cleanly (when no competing `CommGroup (PicGroup X)` instance exists ‚Üí no diamond). (Contrast: `PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` is kept a `@[implicit_reducible] def` to avoid a diamond with the existing typed-`sorry` instance.)
- **Dropping vestigial hypotheses to match an unconditional blueprint (iter-238).** When a decl carries hyps the body never consumes (here `tensorObj_assoc_iso`'s three `IsLocallyTrivial`), confirm soundness by `lean_verify` (axioms unchanged = kernel-clean, hyps genuinely unused) AND grep that no caller argument-applies them (only comment refs are fine) before dropping. The hyp-drop then enables the specialisation chain (`tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`).

Both review subagents confirmed genuine + faithful, 0 must-fix. **The canonical counter did NOT drop:** the group law is the *ingredient*; its consumers ‚ÄĒ the two deferred sorries `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L693) and `PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (L891), and Lane RPF's dishonest `PicSharp := const PUnit` / `functorial := 0` ‚ÄĒ are the next, now-reachable WIRING targets (re-base RPF onto `IsInvertible`/`PicGroup`). - **Route changes outran their prose on `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (iter-238) ‚ÄĒ NON-blocking but actively misleading.** Both reviewers flagged stale descriptions of the SUPERSEDED flatness route: Lean docstring `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:302‚Äď340` still describes `W_whisker*_of_flat` + sectionwise flatness and claims sorry-transitivity through `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` (the body uses `_of_W`, axiom-clean); module header L43‚Äď45 lists wrong residuals + omits ¬ß5; ¬ß5 (L730) sits BEFORE ¬ß4 (L865). ## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-239 addition)

- **Rewrite the EQUATION, not pointwise, for CommRingCat-coerced `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` legs (iter-239, `FlatBaseChange.lean`, closed `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`).** When a leg is a `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` over a `CommRingCat` coercion, `‚ąė‚āó` does NOT unify the `‚ąė‚āõ‚āó` pattern, so `LinearMap.comp_apply`/`comp_assoc`/`simp [LinearMap.coe_comp]` all no-fire on `(f ‚ąė‚āó ‚ÜĎe) x`. **Recipe:** state the triangle as a linear-map equation `restriction ‚ąė‚āó e.toLinearMap = target`, get it via `congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom htri` + `rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp]`, then rewrite to the `‚ąė‚āó e.symm` form via `(LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm _ _).mpr`, and finish with `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (powers b) (toOpen ‚Ä¶).hom e.symm`.
- **`basicOpen 1 = ‚ä§` inside a dependent type (iter-239).** `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one ‚Ėł inferInstance` and `simpa only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]` do NOT rewrite `basicOpen 1 ‚Üí ‚ä§` inside the dependent `tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen 1)` (rewrite-pattern-not-found / typeclass-stuck). Use `have h := inferInstance; rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] at h; exact h`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-239 addition)

- **`IsInvertible.pullback` via sectionwise `extendScalars` is STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE ‚ÄĒ do NOT re-dispatch the recipe (iter-239, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** `Scheme.Modules.pullback f = SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`, and the underlying `PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ.hom`, are BOTH `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint` ‚ÄĒ abstract left adjoints with **NO sectionwise and NO stalkwise formula** in the pinned Mathlib (grep of `ModuleCat/{Presheaf,Sheaf}/` finds no `pullback_obj`/`pullbackObjIso`/`pullback‚Ä¶Monoidal`). The plan-agent recipe ("presheaf pullback is strong-monoidal sectionwise via `(extendScalars f).Monoidal`; assemble the tensorators `őľ`") cannot typecheck ‚ÄĒ there is no sectionwise `(pullback ŌÜ).obj` to attach the tensorator to. `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` gives the comparison map `őī : f^*(A‚äóB) ‚ü∂ f^*A‚äóf^*B` for free, but only at PRESHEAF level (no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules ‚Ä¶)`); proving `IsIso őī` reduces back to the same missing sectionwise identification. `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso only under `F.Final` (open immersions), false for general `f`. **PIVOT:** (1) local-chart-finality via the axiom-clean `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` + local FINAL map `g = f.resLE U V` (how `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` does general `f`) ‚ÄĒ first build the Mathlib-absent `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackComp`/`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` naturality cluster; OR (2) a FLAT-restricted `IsInvertible.pullback` (the RPF structure maps `ŌÄ_T`/base-changes are flat). `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` (landed this iter) is the RHS bridge route (1) will consume for the tensor part. Run a mathlib-analogist + strategy-critic pass BEFORE committing prover effort.
- **FlatBaseChange `hloc` / `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: the Module-R carrier wall is in its 4th recurrence ‚ÄĒ do NOT re-dispatch a verbatim `hloc` round (iter-239).** The unconditional brick reduces (via `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule`) to `hloc(a) = IsLocalizedModule (powers a)` on the pushforward-side restriction `őď(N,‚ä§)‚Üíőď(N,D a)`. The discharge needs an `R`-action on the `R'`-side sections via `Module.compHom _ ŌÜ.hom`, but `letI` it is NOT consumed by `LinearMap.restrictScalars R` (clashes with the `modulesSpecToSheaf`-supplied `R`-action); and the naturality square `e‚āā‚ąėŌĀ = (restrictScalars ŌÜ ŌÉ)‚ąėe‚āĀ` needs Mathlib-absent naturality of `restrictScalarsComp'App` in its module arg. **Two new axiom-clean bricks landed** (`gammaPushforwardIsoAt`, `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`) ‚ÄĒ KEEP them and the `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` skeleton. **Next attempt MUST be one of:** (a) prove naturality of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` in its open argument ‚Üí `of_linearEquiv` closes `hloc`; (b) the tower route (peel `ŌĀ = restrictScalars gsR ‚ąė restrictScalars appTop ŌÉ‚āĀ` + `powers_restrictScalars` √ó2 + `of_restrictScalars`); (c) if the wall resists a 5th time, the planner's armed pivot ‚ÄĒ a direct scheme-level `IsQuasicoherent`-of-affine-pushforward criterion. NOT the by-hand `letI Module.compHom`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-240 addition)

- **Pushforward/pullback composition-coherence of the unit comparison via adjunction-mate transport (iter-240, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, axiom-clean).** To prove the *pullback-side* coherence `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` ‚ÄĒ `pbu(h‚Čęf) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ‚Čę (pullback h).map(pbu f) ‚Čę pbu h` (the blueprint's "genuinely-new ingredient" for `lem:pullback_unit_iso`; Mathlib-absent at the pin) ‚ÄĒ do NOT attack it directly. First prove the EASY *pushforward-side* `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp` sectionwise (`SheafOfModules.Hom.ext ‚Üí PresheafOfModules.hom_ext ‚Üí ModuleCat.hom_ext ‚Üí LinearMap.ext ‚Üí rfl`; the pushforward unit comparison is sectionwise just the structure-sheaf ring map). Then transport across the adjunction: `apply (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h‚Čęf)).homEquiv.injective`, rewrite the LHS adjunct to `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h‚Čęf)` (`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit`) then to the composite via the pushforward-side lemma; package the mate identity (`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`) relating `(pullbackComp h f).inv` ‚ÜĒ `(pushforwardComp h f).hom`; collapse the composite-adjunction unit with `Adjunction.comp_unit_app` + `homEquiv_naturality_{left,right}` sub-lemmas; slide `pushforwardComp.hom` past via `NatTrans.naturality`. ~87 lines, `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
- **`SheafOfModules R` compositions are defeq-but-NOT-syntactic ‚áí use `erw`, never `rw`, for coherence rewrites (iter-240, reusable).** Category compositions over `SheafOfModules R` appear in two defeq forms (`Scheme.Modules.pullback f` vs `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`), so plain `rw [Category.assoc / Functor.map_comp / <coherence lemma> / <local have>]` **silently fails** ("pattern not found" / "no progress") even when the goal visibly matches. **Use `erw` for every assoc / functoriality / sub-lemma rewrite.** Bridge the `Adjunction.homEquiv_unit ‚ÜĒ G.map` higher-order match with an explicit `e := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj:=‚Ä¶) (X:=‚Ä¶) (Y:=‚Ä¶) (f:=‚Ä¶)` then `e.symm.trans` (defeq-tolerant `exact`) ‚ÄĒ never `rw [‚Üź Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]` (its `?adj`/`G.map` HO pattern does not fire). Restate `SheafOfModules.‚Ä¶` lemmas in `Scheme.Modules.‚Ä¶` short-namespace form via `have h : ‚Ä¶ := by exact <lemma>` so later `rw`s match the goal's printed namespace.
- **`algebraize [ŌÜ.hom]` is the carrier-wall fix for `restrictScalars`-of-section localizations (iter-240, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** The recurring `Module.compHom _ ŌÜ.hom` `letI` mechanism for putting an `R`-action on `R'`-side sections is DEAD (not consumed by `LinearMap.restrictScalars R`). The right mechanism is `algebraize [ŌÜ.hom]` (run AT the goal), which installs honest `Algebra ‚ÜĎR ‚ÜĎR'` (= `ŌÜ.hom.toAlgebra`) + `IsScalarTower`. Then discharge `IsLocalizedModule (powers a) (ŌÉ.restrictScalars R)` via `@IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` with **explicit** instance args ‚ÄĒ ordinary TC does NOT auto-pick the `Module R` instances on the bare section carriers, so pass `mTop`/`mDa` (= `inferInstanceAs` of the `(restrictScalars ŌÜ.hom).obj` bundled instances) and `tTop`/`tDa` (= `IsScalarTower`) positionally; reconcile `powers (ŌÜ a) ‚ÜĒ algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a)` with `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid_powers`. NOTE: the pushforward/`modulesSpecToSheaf` peeling through `restrictScalars` over *preimage* opens is DEFINITIONAL (`SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen` is `rfl`) ‚ÄĒ the structural exposures hold by `rfl`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-240 addition)

- **`gammaPushforwardIsoAt` open-naturality square (`hsq`) won't rewrite-unify ‚ÄĒ repackage as a `NatIso` (iter-240, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** The sole residual sorry of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` is `hsq : ŌĀ ‚Čę e‚āā.hom = e‚āĀ.hom ‚Čę Gmor` (naturality-in-the-open of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`), a composite of 3 squares. `nat1` (the exact inv-naturality first piece) typechecks, but ~15 distinct rewrite forms (`rw [nat1]`, `reassoc_of% nat1`, `simp [..._inv_naturality_assoc]`, `slice_lhs`, `conv_lhs`, `set X ‚Ä¶ ; rw [‚Üź Category.assoc X, nat1]`) ALL fail to unify the `restrictScalarsComp'App _ _ _ _ _ .inv` subterm against `nat1`'s LHS **despite identical printing** ‚ÄĒ a representational/universe discrepancy between the `App` baked into `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` (built inside a `by`-block with `set`s) and a freshly-applied `restrictScalarsComp'App`. **DO NOT retry the same rewrite forms.** FIX (ranked): (1) refactor `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` (lives in THIS file; consumers use only `.app U`) into a genuine `NatIso` via `NatIso.ofComponents` ‚Üí naturality-in-open becomes the `.naturality` field, definitional; (2) Mathlib bump #37189 (`isIso_fromTildeőď_pushforward`) collapses the def to ~3 lines (the recorded HARD reversing-signal ‚ÄĒ take it if the NatIso refactor stalls); (3) element-wise (blocked on an `eqToHom`-applied-to-element identity lemma). NOTE: `lean_diagnostic_messages` over a line *range* can be STALE/partial (reported "no errors" for a range excluding the failing `rw`); always confirm with `lake env lean`.
- **`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` instance-synthesis non-canonicity blocks `pullbackUnitIso` assembly (iter-240, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`) ‚ÄĒ pure plumbing, NOT math.** With the linchpin `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` landed, the per-chart `isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` recipe is complete and its `‚Ėł`/`of_isIso_comp_*`/`choose`/globalizer parts typecheck, BUT inside the rich per-chart context `infer_instance` FAILS to synthesise `IsIso (pbu U.őĻ)`, `IsIso (pbu g)`, `IsIso ((pullbackComp g U.őĻ).inv.app _)` ‚ÄĒ all of which synthesise FINE STANDALONE (verified `lean_run_code`). Cause: `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit ŌÜ` carries non-canonical implicit instance args (`[(pushforward ŌÜ).IsRightAdjoint]`, `[F.IsContinuous]`); after `rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp]` the produced subterms bind those implicits to forms the in-scope `(Opens.map _).Final` haveIs no longer match. `set`/`clear_value`/`asIso`-`mapIso`/`refine @IsIso.comp_isIso ?_ ?_ ?_` all fail identically. FIX (ranked): (1) build each component iso as a fully type-ascribed named `Iso` BEFORE the `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite, then `convert`/transport `IsIso` across the coherence eq (pins implicits, no re-synthesis); (2) add `@[instance] lemma isIso_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_of_final` with the canonical implicit shape, apply `@`-explicitly; (3) mathlib-analogist consult. CAVEAT (lean-auditor): the name `instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` cited in the HANDOFF/blueprint is **absent** from Mathlib and all project `.lean` ‚ÄĒ verify the real name via `lean_local_search` before the next attempt.
