- **FlatBaseChange `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` CLOSED axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ the 4-iter `hsq`/carrier wall is fully resolved (iter-241).** The sole residual (the open-naturality square `hsq : ŌĀ ‚Čę e‚āā.hom = e‚āĀ.hom ‚Čę Gmor` of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`) that stalled iters 237‚Äď240 closed by **`ext x; rfl`** after a one-line structural refactor (see Proof patterns below). `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (review re-verified first-hand); file sorry 3‚Üí2. The iter-240 reversing trip-wire ("flat sorry ‚Üí Mathlib bump #37189") is SATISFIED by success ‚ÄĒ **the #37189 bump is NOT needed.** Supersedes the iter-240 `hsq` known-blocker. Still open in the file: `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (own sub-lane, see below) and `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` (deep ńĆech, out of scope).
- **`pullbackUnitIso` (Phase 1, `lem:pullback_unit_iso`) LANDED axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ and the blueprint chart-chase is UNNECESSARY (iter-241, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** MAJOR FINDING: `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an iso for **every** scheme morphism `f`, not only `Final`-chart ones ‚ÄĒ `(Opens.map f.base).Final` holds *unconditionally* (preimage-on-opens preserves finite limits ‚áí representably flat ‚áí `CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat`). So `pullbackUnitIso f := pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f` after `haveI : (Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _` ‚ÄĒ a one-liner. The elaborate affine chart-chase the blueprint demands (and even the iter-240 linchpin `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`) is NOT needed for the unit iso; the prover built the full chart-chase `isIso_restrict_pbu` axiom-clean but did NOT commit it (redundant). `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` is RETAINED (genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence; most plausible Phase-2 ingredient). `lean_verify pullbackUnitIso = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (review re-verified). Phase 2/3 still open (see below). Resolves the iter-240 `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` instance-synthesis known-blocker (the TC accident was real but only a proof-tactic issue ‚ÄĒ fixed by the `@asIso`-explicit-witness idiom below).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-241 addition)

- **Stuck `restrictScalars`-iso naturality square ‚Üí delete the `eqToIso`, then `ext x; rfl` (iter-241, `FlatBaseChange.lean`, the keystone fix).** When a chain of `restrictScalars`-based isos has a naturality square that resists ALL rewrite forms (`rw`/`simp`/`slice`/`conv`/`reassoc_of%`, ~15 tried over iters 237‚Äď240, all failing to unify a `restrictScalarsComp'App ‚Ä¶ .inv` subterm despite identical printing): the blocker is a single `eqToIso`/`eqToHom` cast breaking definitional equality. **Recipe:** replace the `eqToIso (congrArg (fun h => (restrictScalars h).obj M) hcomp)` with the identity-on-element NatIso component `(ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app M` (built from `AddEquiv.refl`, clean `rfl` apply-lemma, SAME type, consumer-safe). All `restrictScalars*` repackaging maps (`restrictScalarsComp'App_{hom,inv}_apply`, `restrictScalarsCongr` app, `restrictScalars.map_apply`) are `rfl`-identity on the underlying carrier (`restrictScalars` never changes the carrier type); once the one `eqToHom` is gone, both legs are defeq and `ext x; rfl` closes the square with no rewrite-matching at all. This is the element-level realization of a "repackage as `NatIso` and use `.naturality`" directive ‚ÄĒ cheaper than the full `NatIso.ofComponents` refactor.
- **`@asIso ‚Ä¶ (explicit witness)` to bundle an `Iso` when `asIso`/`infer_instance` fails at reducible transparency (iter-241, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** `pullbackObjUnitToUnit ŌÜ` carries buried implicit instance args (`[F.IsContinuous]`, `[(pushforward ŌÜ).IsRightAdjoint]`) that are defeq-but-not-syntactic when several `(Opens.map _).Final` are in scope, so `asIso (pbu g)` / `Functor.map_isIso _ _ (pbu g)` FAIL to synthesise `IsIso (pbu g)` (reducible transparency) even with a type-ascribed `haveI` in scope. **Fix (the `Functor.Monoidal.őľIso` bundled-iso idiom):** (1) `private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final g [(Opens.map g.base).Final] : IsIso (pbu g) := inferInstance` isolates the lone `Final` so synthesis runs at a clean site; (2) `def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g := @asIso _ _ _ _ (pbu g) (isIso_pbu_of_final g)` passes the witness EXPLICITLY ‚ÄĒ the `@asIso` typecheck is `isDefEq` at DEFAULT transparency, which succeeds where reducible synthesis does not. Asymmetry note: `have t : IsIso (pbu g) := inferInstance` (type-ascribed) WORKS because the expected type pins the implicits before synthesis; `asIso (pbu g)` does not. Always pass instances explicitly through `@asIso`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Process gotcha (iter-241 addition)

- **`sync_leanok` mis-inserts `\leanok` INSIDE a multi-line `\uses{...}` brace when `\uses{` opens immediately after `\begin{proof}` (iter-241, `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex` L500/L551).** When a proof block is `\begin{proof}` then a `\uses{label1, label2,` continued on the next line, `sync_leanok` puts the proof-block `\leanok` on its own line *between* the `\uses{` opener and the rest of the label list, e.g. `\uses{lem:a, lem:b,\n  \leanok\n  lem:c}`. This (a) breaks the trailing `\uses` dependency edges (`lem:c` is read as part of a malformed label `\leanok lem:c`) ‚ÄĒ surfaced by blueprint-doctor as "broken cross-references" even though the labels DO exist ‚ÄĒ and (b) mis-places the marker. **Root cause is a single misplaced `\leanok`; fixing its position fixes both doctor findings.** This is NOT the review agent's to edit (`\leanok` = sync domain; `\uses{}` prose = plan-agent domain). MITIGATION for the plan agent: move the `\leanok` to directly follow `\begin{proof}` (its own line, before the `\uses{`), or put the whole `\uses{...}` on one line so the sync's insertion point is unambiguous.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-241 addition)

- **Phase 2 `pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M‚äóN) ‚ČÖ f^*M ‚äó f^*N`) is a genuine Mathlib-absent build ‚ÄĒ no comparison map exists yet (iter-241, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** Confirmed by Mathlib grep: there is NO tensor-pullback comparison and NO `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules ‚Ä¶)` at the pinned commit (only `PresheafOfModules/Monoidal.lean`; `PullbackFree.lean` ships only `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (unit) + `pullbackObjFreeIso` (free), no tensor). `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` is an abstract left adjoint with no sectionwise/stalkwise value, so there is no canonical map to even *state* the iso, and the `OfFinal` unit trick (which gave Phase 1 for free) does NOT generalise to tensor. The prover correctly left it ABSENT (no sorry pin). **Decomposition (multi-hundred-LOC sub-lane):** (1) build `pullbackObjTensorToTensor` via `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` + presheaf-level monoidality of the presheaf pullback + the landed `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` (RHS reconciliation); (2) prove it iso (extension of scalars is STRONG monoidal ‚ÄĒ the genuine content; likely needs a sectionwise/stalkwise identification of the abstract presheaf pullback with `extendScalars`, itself Mathlib-absent at the pin, OR a finality chart-chase mirroring `isIso_restrict_pbu` once a comparison map exists). **Check first whether a post-2026-05-31 Mathlib bump adds a monoidal `(Presheaf|Sheaf)OfModules` pullback ‚ÄĒ that would collapse Phase 2.** Phase 3 `IsInvertible.pullback` is gated on Phase 2 (route: `f^*N` as inverse; `tensorObj (f^*M)(f^*N) ‚ČÖ f^*(tensorObj M N) ‚ČÖ f^*ūĚí™_X ‚ČÖ[pullbackUnitIso] ūĚí™_Y`).
- **`affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` still needs its own pullback-of-tilde sub-lane ‚ÄĒ do NOT re-dispatch as a single-iter close (iter-241).** With `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` now closed, the affine close has its locality reduction and pushforward-of-tilde half in place, but STILL needs (3) the Mathlib-absent pullback-of-tilde dictionary `pullback (Spec.map ŌÜ) (tilde M) ‚ČÖ tilde (R' ‚äó_R M)` and (4) recognising the abstract `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` adjoint-mate as `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange`. The blueprint itself (L980‚Äď985) calls this "a multi-lane engine build, not a single-iter target." Scope the sub-lane in the blueprint before any prover round; the documented partial (`rw [Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens]; intro U; <comments> sorry`) is preserved (no bare sorry).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Resolved blockers (iter-242 addition)

- **`pullback_spec_tilde_iso` LANDED axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ the affine pullback-of-tilde dictionary now exists (iter-242, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** `(Spec ŌÜ)^*(MŐÉ) ‚ČÖ (extendScalars ŌÜ M)~` (Stacks 01I9 part 1), the pullback companion of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`. Built by **uniqueness of left adjoints**, NOT a sectionwise comparison: compose `tilde.adjunction (R)` ‚ąė `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec ŌÜ)` and `extendRestrictScalarsAdj ŌÜ.hom` ‚ąė `tilde.adjunction (R')`, identify the two right adjoints by the new `gammaPushforwardNatIso ŌÜ`, then `((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso ŌÜ)).symm.app M`. `lean_verify = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (review re-verified first-hand). This RESOLVES iter-241 known-blocker item (3) (the pullback-of-tilde dictionary) for `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`. Both affine dictionaries (push + pull) are now in place.
- **The presheaf-level pullback comparison map `őī` EXISTS ‚ÄĒ correcting the iter-241 "no comparison map exists yet" claim (iter-242, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** Two axiom-clean instances landed: `presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal` (`(PresheafOfModules.pushforward ŌÜ).LaxMonoidal`, the analogist-named `őľ_G`) and `presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal` (`(PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ).OplaxMonoidal` via `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction ŌÜ).leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`). The iter-241 KB claim that there is "no canonical map to even state the iso" and that the oplax route "requires `MonoidalCategory(SheafOfModules)`" is **WRONG at the presheaf level**: `PresheafOfModules` IS monoidal, the pullback IS a genuine left adjoint, `pushforward` IS lax, so the canonical `őī : f^*(A‚äóB) ‚ü∂ f^*A ‚äó f^*B` exists abstractly with NO `SheafOfModules` monoidal instance. `őī` is only a MAP (no sectionwise value); **iso-ness is the sole remaining gap** (not statability).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-242 addition)

- **`CategoryTheory.conjugateIsoEquiv` (non-deprecated) for "iso of right adjoints ‚áí iso of left adjoints" (iter-242, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** `Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso` is DEPRECATED ‚Üí use `conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚āĀ adj‚āā : (G ‚ČÖ G') ‚ČÉ (F ‚ČÖ F')` (from `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Mates.lean`). Direction: it conjugates an iso of RIGHT adjoints into an iso of LEFT adjoints; a given nat iso of right adjoints `r : G ‚ČÖ G'` typically needs `(conjugateIsoEquiv adj‚āĀ adj‚āā).symm r` and then an outer `.symm` to match the target orientation. Compose the two-step adjunctions with `Adjunction.comp` before feeding them in.
- **`forget‚āā`-association diamond ‚ÄĒ defeq-cast the morphism, do NOT bridge with `letI : MonoidalCategory` (iter-242, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** When a composite-functor instance (e.g. `LaxMonoidal`) won't synthesize because the hypothesis morphism `ŌÜ` is typed in the *inner* association `F.op ‚čô (R ‚čô forget‚āā)` while `MonoidalCategory`/`pushforward‚āÄOfCommRingCat` are keyed on the *outer* `(F.op ‚čô R) ‚čô forget‚āā`: keep `ŌÜ` in the inner form (so `pushforward ŌÜ` typechecks), then `let ŌÜ' : (S ‚čô forget‚āā) ‚ü∂ (F.op ‚čô R) ‚čô forget‚āā := ŌÜ` (a pure defeq cast), synthesize the composite instance on the outer form natively, and `exact` it back (defeq to the inner form). A `letI : MonoidalCategory (...inner...)` bridge produces a kernel-rejected diamond (the `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp` re-synthesizes a different middle monoidal structure than the `letI` carried).
- **(Reconfirmed)** The iter-241 `eqToIso ‚Üí restrictScalarsCongr` lesson applied AGAIN to make `gammaPushforwardIso` naturality-friendly so `gammaPushforwardNatIso` closes by `ext x; rfl`. Any `restrictScalars`-iso leg that must survive a pointwise `rfl` through a colimit/component map must be an identity-on-carrier `restrictScalarsCongr`/`restrictScalarsComp'App` component, never `eqToIso (congrArg ‚Ä¶)`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-242 addition)

- **`pullbackTensorIso` for GENERAL `f` via the concrete-`P` route is Mathlib-SCALE ‚ÄĒ NOT closable by a prover round (iter-242, supersedes iter-241 Phase-2 known-blocker on the "build a comparison map" half).** The comparison MAP now exists (presheaf `őī`, see Resolved above); what's Mathlib-scale is the planner's concrete-`P` recipe ("strong-monoidal `P ‚ČÖ pullback f`, transport via `leftAdjointUniq`"): `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` and `PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ.hom` are BOTH `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint` (abstract). For a general `f` the concrete left adjoint is `(inverse-image presheaf) ‚čô extendScalars`, and BOTH are Mathlib-absent at the pin ‚ÄĒ (a) NO `PresheafOfModules.extendScalars` (only `ModuleCat.extendScalars`); (b) the underlying-presheaf inverse image is an un-built left Kan extension (a colimit, not sectionwise). **DO NOT re-dispatch the concrete-`P` route.** The critical-path workaround (avoids the concrete inverse image): transport presheaf `őī` to the sheaf level as a MAP (via `sheafificationCompPullback` + `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`), then upgrade to iso on the INVERTIBLE pair via local trivialization + `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`; needs the forward bridge `IsInvertible ‚áí IsLocallyTrivial` built first. Full general `pullbackTensorIso` is only needed for a non-invertible consumer (none on the A.2.c path) ‚ÄĒ defer or bump.
- **`affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` is blocked on TWO distinct Mathlib-absent obligations, not one (iter-242, refines iter-241).** With both affine dictionaries now proved, the residual is: (1) the **affine reduction** ‚ÄĒ base-change compatibility of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` under restriction to affine opens of `S'` (arbitrary `S,S',X,X'`; not packaged in Mathlib); and (2) the **adjoint-mate ‚ÜĒ `cancelBaseChange`** identification (the abstract mate transported through the four dictionary isos must equal `tilde(cancelBaseChange)`). `cancelBaseChange` itself is present (no flatness); the gap is the mate identification. Blueprint the two as named sub-lanes before any prover dispatch; `#37189` (`isIso_fromTildeőď_pushforward`) remains the documented fallback. The documented partial (`rw [Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens]; intro U; <comments> sorry`) is preserved (no bare sorry).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Resolved blockers (iter-243 addition)

- **`pullbackTensorMap` (= őī_sheaf) LANDED axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ the SHEAF-level pullback‚Äďtensor comparison map now exists for GENERAL `M,N` (iter-243, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, `lem:pullback_tensor_map`).** `(pullback f).obj (M‚äó_X N) ‚ü∂ (pullback f).obj M ‚äó_Y (pullback f).obj N`, the sheaf-level transport of the presheaf `őī` (iter-242). Built as a 4-step composite (3 isos + `a_Y.map őī`): `(sheafificationCompPullback ŌÜ).hom` ‚Čę `a_Y.map (OplaxMonoidal.őī (pullback ŌÜ') M.val N.val)` ‚Čę `(sheafifyTensorUnitIso ‚Ä¶).hom` ‚Čę `a_Y.map (tensorHom (forget.map (pullbackValIso f M).hom) (forget.map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))`. Helper `pullbackValIso` (`a_Y((pullback ŌÜ.hom).obj M.val) ‚ČÖ (pullback f).obj M`) via the reflective-counit idiom. `lean_verify = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (review re-verified). This is the critical-path workaround the iter-242 KB named (transport őī to sheaf level as a MAP); iso-ness on the invertible pair remains gated on deliverable 2.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-243 addition)

- **`IsInvertible.isLocallyTrivial` (deliverable 2, `lem:isinvertible_implies_locallytrivial`) is Mathlib-SCALE ‚ÄĒ confirmed first-hand (iter-243, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`). The A.1.c critical path passes ENTIRELY through this.** Two scheme-level ingredients are absent at the pin: (1) **stalk-invertibility plumbing** ‚ÄĒ no in-tree bridge `(sheaf iso M‚äóN‚ČÖūĚí™_X) ‚áí Module.Invertible ūĚí™_{X,x} (M.val.stalk x)`; needs stalk-of-sheafification at the PresheafOfModules level + d.2 `stalkTensorIso` + `ūĚí™_X.val.stalk x ‚ČÖ ūĚí™_{X,x}` + deriving the `Module.Invertible` instance (~150 LOC, no single lemma); (2) **finite-presentation spreading-out for `SheafOfModules` on a scheme** (the hard blocker) ‚ÄĒ "M f.p. + M_x free ‚áí M‚ČÖūĚí™ on an affine nbhd", present in Mathlib only at the CommRing/LocalizedModule level (`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`, `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`); moreover M's finite presentation is NOT part of the SheafOfModules data and must first be derived from invertibility (needs QC/finite-type infra the bare carrier lacks). **DO NOT** have a prover write the ~150-LOC stalk fragment alone (reaches no pin, leaves blocker (2) intact). Next move = dedicated mathlib-build sub-lane for (2) OR a Mathlib bump for AG-level invertible‚áílocally-free, gated on a `mathlib-analogist` absence-check. The iter-242 "local-trivialization pivot" routes THROUGH this build; it does not sidestep it. `IsInvertible.pullback` (deliverable 3) is blocked on this by construction.
- **The SheafOfModules functor `.map`-of-composite is NOT syntactically rewritable ‚ÄĒ now a recorded dead end for `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (iter-243, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** The aux brick `pushforwardBaseChangeMap_naturality` reached the final naturality square (both `‚Üź Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left/right_symm` fired; unit step closed via `‚Üź Functor.map_comp_assoc` + `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.naturality ő∑`, with `show‚Ä¶from‚Ä¶` bridging `(ūĚü≠).map ő∑ = ő∑`) but DIED on the three component naturalities (`pushforwardComp.hom.naturality_assoc`, `pushforwardCongr.hom.naturality_assoc`, `pushforwardComp.inv.naturality`): plain `rw` of them / of `‚Üź Functor.comp_map` gives "did not find an occurrence of the pattern" (functor `.map` of a composite is defeq-not-syntactic for SheafOfModules functors); `erw` whnf-explodes (`timeout at whnf, 200000 heartbeats`). The section-wise dodge (`ext U`+`simp`) reaches a clean section goal but `pushforwardComp_*_app_app = id` still won't rewrite (same defeq-display wall; `simp` "unused", `rw` no-match, `aesop_cat` stalls). Use `erw [Functor.map_comp_assoc]` ONLY for the single unit-composite split that works. **progress-critic ts243 watch-signal FIRED** (both obligations blocked, no in-tree reduction) ‚Üí take the **`#37189` Mathlib bump** (`isIso_fromTildeőď_pushforward`); do NOT re-dispatch another in-tree `rw`/`erw` round on `pushforwardComp` `.map`-of-composite terms. The `cancelBaseChange` concrete-iso wrapper was assessed and DROPPED (its orientation `M‚äó[A](A‚äó[R]N)‚ČÉ[B]M‚äó[R]N` won't orient to the blueprint's affine objects standalone).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Resolved blockers (iter-244 addition)

- **D1 of the general strong-monoidal pullback build LANDED axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ `pullbackLanDecomposition` (iter-244, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).** The functorial decomposition `pullback ŌÜ ‚ČÖ extendScalars ŌÜ ‚čô pullback0` for general `PresheafOfModules` (any `F : C ‚•§ D`, ring presheaves `R,S`), plus the two named carriers `extendScalars := (restrictScalars ŌÜ).leftAdjoint`, `pullback0 := (pushforward‚āÄ F R).leftAdjoint`, their adjunctions, and two private `IsRightAdjoint` lemmas ‚ÄĒ 7 decls, each `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. Built from the *definitional* factorization `pushforward ŌÜ = pushforward‚āÄ F R ‚čô restrictScalars ŌÜ` (Mathlib `Pushforward.lean:86`) via `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` fed `Iso.refl`. This is the EASY (purely functorial) leg of A.1.c; the canonical Picard sorries do NOT move on it.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-244 addition)

- **`restrictScalars (ūĚüô R) ‚Č° ūĚü≠` defeq is the lever that makes `pushforward‚āÄ`/`restrictScalars` recognizable as right adjoints (iter-244; memory `restrictscalars-id-defeq`).** Witnessed by Mathlib's `instance : (restrictScalars (ūĚüô R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (ūĚü≠ _).Full`. Consequence: `pushforward‚āÄ F R` is defeq `pushforward (ūĚüô (F.op ‚čô R))` and `restrictScalars ŌÜ` is defeq `pushforward (F := ūĚü≠ C) ŌÜ`, so the general `instance : (pushforward ŌÜ).IsRightAdjoint` (`Pullback.lean:97`) supplies both via `inferInstanceAs (pushforward ‚Ä¶ ).IsRightAdjoint` ‚ÄĒ and their `.leftAdjoint`s (`pullback0`/`extendScalars`) then exist. GOTCHA: pin the ring presheaf explicitly (`(R := F.op ‚čô R)`) in the restrictScalars bridge or the `F` metavariable won't resolve. Pattern: **`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` fed `Iso.refl` of a definitional composite** turns a right-adjoint composite identity into a left-adjoint decomposition iso ‚ÄĒ reusable for any `G = G‚āÄ ‚čô G‚āĀ` definitional factorization of right adjoints.
- **Namespace shadowing inside `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (iter-244):** the bare type `PresheafOfModules` resolves to a Scheme-indexed thing; use `_root_.PresheafOfModules` in *type position* (member projection `PresheafOfModules.pushforward‚āÄ` is unaffected).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-244 addition)

- **D2 (`extendScalars` strong monoidal) and D3 (`pullback0_tensor_iso`) require a CONCRETE POINTWISE MODEL of the abstract `.leftAdjoint` functors ‚ÄĒ Mathlib-scale, confirmed first-hand (iter-244, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** `extendScalars ŌÜ`/`pullback0` are abstract `(restrictScalars ŌÜ).leftAdjoint`/`(pushforward‚āÄ F R).leftAdjoint` exposing NO sectionwise/pointwise value, so neither Mathlib's `AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange` (D2 strong tensorator) nor a pointwise Lan colimit + filtered-colimit/‚äó interchange (D3) can be invoked directly. D2 needs `extendScalarsConcrete ŌÜ` over CommRingCat bases (value at `U` = `ModuleCat.extendScalars (ŌÜ.app U).hom`) identified with the abstract one via `leftAdjointUniq`; D3 needs `pullback0 ‚ČÖ Lan F.op` + pointwise colimit over the up-directed comma category `(F‚ÜďV)` + ‚äó-interchange for `ModuleCat`-valued presheaves (anchor: `pullbackObjFreeIso`). strategy-critic ts244 est ~20‚Äď38 iters / ~400‚Äď750 LOC. **DO NOT re-dispatch D2/D3 verbatim as a single-session close** ‚ÄĒ only as a committed multi-iter lane, OR re-weigh the iter-243 local-trivialization route (needs the iso only on the invertible pair; never rebutted by evidence). The canonical critical-path counter has been FLAT 6 iters (239‚Äď244) precisely because all reachable bricks land while this Mathlib-scale leg remains.

Fix on next prover-touch (comment-only). Also: 34√ó deprecated `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.val` (‚Üí `ObjectProperty.obj`) in `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` ‚ÄĒ schedule a mechanical sweep before any Mathlib bump.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Route pivot + Resolved blockers (iter-245 addition)

- **The general strong-monoidal pullback build (iter-244 D1/D2/D3) is ABANDONED ‚ÄĒ NOT necessary; do NOT revive (iter-245 plan pivot, adversarially confirmed).** Supersedes the iter-244 D2/D3 Mathlib-scale known-blocker as the *active route* (the D1 decls `pullbackLanDecomposition`/`pullback0`/`extendScalars`/adjunctions are RETAINED off-path, axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ don't delete, don't build D2/D3 on them). Two analyst passes (the second adversarially scoped) established: (a) the only consumer of `f^*(M‚äóN) ‚ČÖ f^*M ‚äó f^*N` is the relative Picard functor, whose carrier `OnProduct` is `{M // IsLocallyTrivial M}` ‚ÄĒ it needs the iso on LINE-BUNDLE pairs only, never general modules; (b) the "őī-not-iso / `őď(‚Ąô¬Ļ,ūĚí™(1))=0`" obstruction was MISATTRIBUTED ‚ÄĒ it concerns the LAX tensorator of PUSHFORWARD (right adjoint / global sections), NOT the OPLAX őī of PULLBACK (left adjoint); pullback provably preserves local triviality (`IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`), so őī IS an iso on locally-trivial objects; (c) the blocker forward bridge `IsInvertible ‚áí IsLocallyTrivial` is Mathlib-scale AND off-path (the consumer carries `IsLocallyTrivial` directly; only the easy reverse `exists_tensorObj_inverse` is used). New route: loc-triv chart-chase D1'‚ÄďD4' (~8‚Äď16 iters vs 20‚Äď38). Do NOT pursue the forward bridge for this route.
- **`isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` LANDED axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ the loc-triv reduction brick (iter-245, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** Reduces iso-ness of the 4-fold composite `pullbackTensorMap f M N` to iso-ness of the SINGLE sheafified presheaf comparison `a_Y.map (őī (pullback ŌÜ') M.val N.val)` (`ŌÜ' = f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom`, `a_Y = sheafification (ūĚüô Y.ringCatSheaf.val)`). Pieces 1/3 (`sheafificationCompPullback`, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`) and piece 4 (the sheafified `‚äó‚āė` of the two `pullbackValIso`, via the new private helper `isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso`) are isos unconditionally; only piece 2 (the sheafified őī) is conditional, supplied by the hypothesis. `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (review re-verified first-hand). Every downstream target (D2'/D3'/D4'/`IsInvertible.pullback`) now funnels through one `IsIso (a_Y.map őī ‚Ä¶)` goal. NOTE: this landed decl has NO blueprint block yet (lean-vs-blueprint-checker ts245 MAJOR thinness gap) ‚ÄĒ needs a blueprint-writer block + `\lean{}` pin so `sync_leanok` can track it.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-245 addition)

- **Build the explicit `Iso` and take `.isIso_hom`; do NOT synthesize `IsIso` when carrier forms differ syntactically (iter-245, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** When a `‚äó‚āė` lives in the def's pinned `_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat)` form while its `forget`-mapIso arguments key on the defeq-but-distinct `Y.ringCatSheaf.obj` form, `inferInstance` / `Functor.map_isIso` / `MonoidalCategory.tensor_isIso` ALL miss on discrimination-tree keying. Fix: construct the iso directly ‚ÄĒ `(a_Y.mapIso (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat)) (forget.mapIso e‚āĀ) (forget.mapIso e‚āā))).isIso_hom` ‚ÄĒ which performs NO `IsIso` search. MUST be a top-level lemma (mirrors the proven `tensorObjIsoOfIso`); the `tensorIso (C := ‚Ä¶)` does NOT elaborate inside a tactic block carrying `extract_lets` locals. Then chain the composite's factors explicitly with `IsIso.comp_isIso'` (not a whole-composite `infer_instance`).
- **Pin `ŌÜ'` via a `letI` IN THE HYPOTHESIS TYPE to unstick a stuck `OplaxMonoidal` accessor (iter-245).** Extends the iter-243 `ŌÜ'` let-coercion pattern: when a lemma's hypothesis mentions `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.őī (PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ') ‚Ä¶`, write the hypothesis as `(h : letI ŌÜ' : (X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā ‚Ä¶) ‚ü∂ (Opens.map f.base).op ‚čô (Y.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā ‚Ä¶) := f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom; IsIso (‚Ä¶ őī (pullback ŌÜ') ‚Ä¶))` ‚ÄĒ mirroring the def's own `let ŌÜ' := ŌÜ.hom`. Without it the codomain `MonoidalCategory` is a stuck metavar and `Functor.OplaxMonoidal (PresheafOfModules.pullback ‚Ä¶)` cannot resolve.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-246 addition)

- **`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` CANNOT cite `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` ‚ÄĒ import cycle (iter-246, first-hand).** The DAG is `Mathlib ‚Üí LineBundlePullback ‚Üí RelPicFunctor ‚Üí {StalkTensor, Vestigial, PresheafInternalHom} ‚Üí TensorObjSubstrate`, i.e. **`TensorObjSubstrate.lean:9` imports `RelPicFunctor`**. So the whole tensor substrate (`tensorObj`, `tensorObjOnProduct`, `exists_tensorObj_inverse`, unitors/braiding/`tensorObj_assoc_iso`/`tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`) is strictly DOWNSTREAM of RPF and unreachable from it. The iter-246 objectives' recipe ("build `PicSharp.addCommGroup` by citing those decls") was infeasible; the sibling `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` in TensorObjSubstrate (whose comment says "the RPF instance closes against it") is likewise unreachable from RPF. **FIX IS ARCHITECTURAL (plan-agent, refactor subagent):** (a) PREFERRED ‚ÄĒ relocate the tensor substrate to a file upstream of `RelPicFunctor`; or (b) move `PicSharp.addCommGroup` downstream into TensorObjSubstrate. Do NOT have a prover paper over it by copying ~200 LOC of substrate into RPF (iter-246 did a *local private* version modulo 4 bridges as a stopgap ‚ÄĒ the prover itself flags it "the wrong fix"). Memory: `rpf-import-cycle-blocks-tensorobj`.
- **Carrier reality check (iter-246):** `RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` (`LineBundlePullback.lean:349`) is the *iso-class* relation `Nonempty (L ‚ČÖ L')`, so `Quotient (preimage_subgroup ŌÄC ŌÄT)` is `Pic(C√ó_S T)` itself ‚ÄĒ the honest group on it is the tensor-product Picard group (additive mirror of `picCommGroup`), NOT the quotient-by-`H_T` the RPF chapter's Step 2‚Äď4 prose describes (that is a different future carrier). Annotated in `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex` (`% NOTE:` iter-246 review).
- **Undiagnosed Mathlib `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit ŌÜ)` synthesis quirk (iter-246).** For `ŌÜ = W.őĻ.toRingCatSheafHom` the instance `[F.Final] : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit ŌÜ)` (`PullbackFree.lean:105`) refuses to synthesize even though `Final`, `(pushforward ŌÜ).IsRightAdjoint`, `J/K.HasSheafCompose` all resolve via `inferInstance`; yet the IDENTICAL `asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)` compiles inside `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (`LineBundlePullback.lean:192`). Pinning `.{u}` and naming the instance both failed. Diagnose with `set_option trace.Meta.synthInstance true` before any blind retry of `isLocallyTrivial_unit`; relocating the substrate upstream (so the proven `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` path is reused) may sidestep it.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-246 addition)

- **D2' őī-on-the-unit-pair ‚Üí ő∑-bridge, axiom-clean (iter-246, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** To show `IsIso (a_Y.map (őī (pullback ŌÜ') ūĚüô_ ūĚüô_))`: use `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left_unitality_hom F ūĚüô_` (gives `őī F ūĚüô_ ūĚüô_ ‚Čę (ő∑ F ‚Ė∑ F.obj ūĚüô_) ‚Čę (őĽ_).hom = F.map (őĽ_).hom`), sheafify, cancel the unitor + `F.map (őĽ_)` iso factors, and upgrade the whiskered ő∑ factor via the new converse `W_of_isIso_sheafification` ‚Üí `W_whiskerRight_of_W` ‚Üí `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (flatness-free, NO stalkwise őī). Then chain into the iter-245 reduction brick (`isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`) to land `IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f ūĚí™ ūĚí™)`. Decls: `isIso_sheafifyDelta_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta`, `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta`.
- **Converse iso-localizer by reading a morphism-property EQUALITY backwards (iter-246).** `W_of_isIso_sheafification` (`IsIso (sheafification.map f) ‚Üí J.W (toPresheaf.map f)`) is just `inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` (sheafification IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage toPresheaf`) read in the reverse direction. Generic technique for any "X is the localization at W" equality.

- **ő∑-bridge transpose recipe (iter-246, residual not yet closed):** `IsIso (a_Y.map (ő∑ (pullback ŌÜ')))` ‚Üź compose `pullbackValIso.inv`, `apply (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction ŌÜ).homEquiv.injective`, `rw [pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_ő∑, Adjunction.homEquiv_counit]` ‚Üí one concrete pushforward-side mate identity. The two-level `leftAdjointUniq` chase of `sheafificationCompPullback` does NOT close by `simp`; glue: `Adjunction.{homEquiv_,unit_,}leftAdjointUniq_hom_app*`, plus `őĶ (pushforward ŌÜ) = unitToPushforwardObjUnit ŌÜ` by `rfl`. ~60‚Äď120 LOC manual chase; unit-side analog of the proven `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-250 addition)

- **Strip `restrictScalars (ūĚüô R)` wrappers SYNTACTICALLY via a propositional `:= rfl` lemma, NEVER by `show`/`whnf` (iter-250, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** New helper `restrictScalarsId_map : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (ūĚüô R)).map g = g := rfl`, stated propositionally over an ABSTRACT `g`, is essentially free and lets `rw [restrictScalarsId_map]` strip the wrapper landing in the syntactic presheaf category. DEAD END (do NOT repeat): stripping via `show ‚Ä¶`/`rfl` (the analogist eps250 headline idiom ‚ÄĒ "restrictScalars(ūĚüô) is absorbed defeq cheaply") triggers a whole-term `whnf` on sheafification-laden composites that blows >6.4M heartbeats. The "cheap defeq" premise is FALSE at this term scale. Also DEAD: extracting the (‚ąó‚ąó) goal to a standalone lemma ‚ÄĒ the statement-type elaboration itself is catastrophic; assemble INLINE on the telescope's reduced metavariable goal.
- **`erw` is load-bearing for merging two defeq-but-differently-spelled `pushforward`-images (iter-250).** In `pullbackEtaUnitSquare`'s close, the leading image is `pushforward (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` (from `leftAdjointUniqUnitEta`) and the trailing is `pushforward ŌÜ.hom` (the `set`-local `ŌÜ`). `rw [Category.assoc]`/`simp only [Category.assoc]` fail to match at the connecting object; `rw [hŌÜ]` gives a dependent-motive error and `simp only [hŌÜ]` makes no progress (`ŌÜ` is a stuck `set`-local); a `show` unifying `ŌÜ` blows the heartbeat budget. `erw [Category.assoc, ‚Üź Functor.map_comp, pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle f, presheafUnit_comp_map_eta f, epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit f]` reassociates/merges via keyed-defeq tolerance and fits within `maxHeartbeats 3200000`.
- **Sectionwise `őĶ(pushforward ŌÜ') = (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ŌÜ).val` needs a `letI` CommRing on the `(restrictScalars f).obj ūĚüô_` carrier (iter-250, `epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit`).** `apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext; intro X‚āÄ; apply ModuleCat.hom_ext; ext r; erw [unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_ő∑]; rfl`, but `synthInstance` does NOT reduce `(restrictScalars f).obj ūĚüô_` to the canonical `forget‚āā`-carrier `CommRing` instance (a diamond) ‚ÄĒ supply it with a `letI` and wrap in `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`.
- **Read `ő∑ F` off the goal to avoid `OplaxMonoidal` re-synthesis (iter-250, `pullbackSheafifyUnitEtaTriangle`).** The Y-side right-triangle close: `letI : (pushforward ŌÜ').IsRightAdjoint := ‚Ä¶; rw [‚Üź Category.assoc]; erw [‚Üź unit.naturality, Category.assoc, Functor.id_map]; have htri := ‚Ä¶right_triangle_components (unit Y); refine Eq.trans ?_ (Category.comp_id _); congr 1`. The `IsRightAdjoint` instance is REQUIRED for the naturality `erw`; reading `ő∑ F` off the goal (rather than naming it) sidesteps `OplaxMonoidal` re-synthesis. `rw`/`simp [htri]` cannot fire (codomain `ūĚüô_Yp` vs `(unit Y).val` defeq only at non-reducible transparency).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Process gotcha (iter-250 addition)

- **The `\leanok`-in-`\uses{}` sync defect RECURRED at 4 NEW locations this iter (iter-250, `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`).** `sync_leanok` (+28/‚ąí0, sha `78061c14`, chapters_touched = TS only) inserted `\leanok` proof markers INTO open multi-line `\uses{...}` braces at four decls: `lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk` (proof `\uses{` L1465‚Äď1467), `lem:eta_bridge_unit_square` (proof `\uses{` L3487‚Äď3489 ‚Üí breaks `lem:leftadjointuniq_app_unit_eta`, `lem:epsilon_presheaf_to_sheaf_unit`), `lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`/`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso` block (L4244‚Äď4246), and `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso_invertible`/`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso` block (L4353‚Äď4354). All target labels EXIST; the breakage is the misplaced `\leanok`. Note the iter-249 "relocation held" was decl-specific ‚ÄĒ this is the same ROOT defect re-firing on different decls, NOT a regression of the prior fix. The transitive-taint false-positive `\leanok` on `lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit` (flagged iter-248) NOW RESOLVES on its own ‚ÄĒ the decl is genuinely sorry-free this iter. ## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-251 addition)

- **Carrier-normalisation `:= rfl`/characterisation lemma is THE proven corrective for `.val`/forget‚āā-carrier friction (iter-251, generalised).** When a Mathlib rewrite (`Functor.map_comp`, `whisker_exchange`, `Category.assoc`) silently fails to fire because the morphism is spelled on a local instance carrier (`MonoidalCategoryStruct` / `Sheaf.val` / `restrictScalars (ūĚüô)`) that is defeq-but-not-syntactic to the canonical one, the fix is a small lemma restating the morphism on the canonical carrier, then plain `rw`/`erw` fires. THREE instances this iter: (a) `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq := rfl` strips the `letI instMS` cast onto the `‚čô forget‚āā` carrier (CLOSED); (b) `rw [show (SheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ).map g = (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).map g from rfl]` ‚ÄĒ morphism-level, NOT functor-level (functor-level breaks the motive) (CLOSED, in `pullbackValIso_hom_natural`); (c) a whisker-carrier restatement re-stating local `‚Ė∑`/`‚óĀ` via the canonical `MonoidalCategory` instance so `whisker_exchange`/`comp_whiskerRight`/`whiskerLeft_comp` fire (OPEN ‚ÄĒ the residual blocker of `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural`, square 3 of D1‚Ä≤).
- **`erw` idiom kit for `SheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ` vs `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` naturality (iter-251, `pullbackValIso_hom_natural`, axiom-clean).** `simp only [pullbackValIso, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, Category.assoc]; rw [‚Üź Category.assoc]; erw [(sheafificationCompPullback ŌÜ).inv.naturality u.val]; rw [show (pullback ŌÜ).map g = (pullback f).map g from rfl]; erw [Category.assoc]; erw [‚Üź Functor.map_comp F g1 g2]; congr 1; congr 1; exact (sheafificationAdjunction ..).counit.naturality u`. `erw` matches `(őĪ.app M').inv ‚Č° őĪ.inv.app M'` and `(F‚čôa).map ‚Č° a.map(F.map)` up to defeq; `‚Üź Functor.map_comp` must be FULLY explicit (a metavar functor whnf-bombs). CORRECTION to a prior diagnosis: the "`exact` bombs" symptom was a stray `rw [‚Üź restrictScalarsId_map]`, not `exact` itself.
- **Eval-at-1 presheaf-dual-of-unit iso (iter-251, `presheafDualUnitIso`/`dualUnitIsoGen`/`unitDualSectionEquiv`, axiom-clean).** `dual ūĚüô_ ‚ČÖ ūĚüô_` (= `‚Ąčom(ūĚüô_,ūĚüô_) ‚ČÖ ūĚüô_`) via a section equiv `(restr X ūĚüô_ ‚ü∂ restr X ūĚüô_) ‚ČÉ‚āó R‚āÄ(X)`, forward `ŌÜ ‚Ü¶ evalLin ūĚüô_ X ŌÜ 1`, inverse `r ‚Ü¶ globalSMul (..) r`. Substantive `left_inv` = "every unit-endomorphism is mult by its value at 1" via `ŌÜ`-naturality toward the terminal + `unit_map_one`. Idioms: `evalLin_add` is `rfl`-level so `map_add' := rfl` (the CommRingCat/RingCat carrier blocks generic `LinearMap.add_apply`); `erw` (not `rw`) for `globalSMul_hom_apply`/`unit_map_one`; ascribe `(1 : ((R‚āÄ‚čôforget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))` to dodge `OfNat` synthesis through the ModuleCat coercion; pin `internalHomObjModule` with `letI`. Build generally over `{D}[Category D]{R‚āÄ : DŠĶíŠĶĖ ‚•§ CommRingCat}` then instantiate at `R‚āÄ := Y.presheaf`.
- **Scratch-verify-then-port for axiom-clean infra under carrier friction (iter-251).** Develop a heavy new infra block in an isolated scratch importing ONLY its true dependency (here `PresheafInternalHom.lean`), verify 0 warnings/errors/sorry, port verbatim, delete the scratch. Insulates from a parallel-lane race that leaves the shared import broken mid-session.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Resolved blockers (iter-252 addition)

This was the blueprint's documented "genuine coherence risk." Built directly as a `NatTrans` value of `presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf` over `U i`: `app W` = `M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom h.symm) ‚Čę ((toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).őĻ ‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ W.left)) ‚Čę N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom h)` with `h : (U i).őĻ ''ŠĶĀ ((U i).őĻ ‚ĀĽ¬ĻŠĶĀ W.left) = W.left` (from `Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf` + `inf_eq_right.mpr W.hom.le`); the `naturality` field reduces to the naturality `hm` of `(toPresheaf _).map (f i).val` conjugated by thin-poset `Subsingleton.elim` edge equalities. Crucial defeq exploited: `restrict_obj`/`restrict_map` are `rfl`, so `(M.restrict (U i).őĻ).val.presheaf.{obj,map}` IS `M.val.presheaf.{obj,map}` at the image open.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-252 addition)

- **The general dissolution of `.val`/forget‚āā-carrier friction at the SECTION level is "descend to elements" (iter-252, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_natural`).** When a whisker/monoidal goal carries the `Sheaf.val` vs `_ ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat` split (`monoidalCategoryStruct` vs `monoidalCategory.toMonoidalCategoryStruct`), the robust route is NOT a carrier-restatement of the whiskers (that is the OPEN square-3 residual that resisted 5 iters) but: `refine PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext (funext fun U => ?_)` ‚Üí `simp only [MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft, whiskerRight, comp_app, Monoidal.tensorHom_app, tensorHom_def, Category.assoc]` (distributes the forget‚āā-carrier legs) ‚Üí `erw [comp_app, tensorHom_app]` (the ő∑-group legs live over `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf`; head-keyed `simp` MISSES them, keyed-defeq `erw` FIRES) ‚Üí `apply ModuleCat.hom_ext; ext x`. No monoidal instance survives at the element level. Residual = a concrete `TensorProduct` identity closed by `TensorProduct.induction_on` + sectionwise ő∑-naturality. GOTCHA: `simp`/`simp only [map_add]` does NOT auto-fire on the bundled `ModuleCat.Hom.hom (‚Ä¶) 0` / `(‚Ä¶) (a+b)` ‚ÄĒ feed `map_zero`/`map_add` explicitly.
- **Restrict/image carrier-friction recipe for `presheafHom` naturality (iter-252, `homLocalSection`, axiom-clean).** Composites mixing `M.val.presheaf` (image form) with `(M.restrict).val.presheaf` (restrict form) at the `((toPresheaf).map (f i).val).app` boundary are defeq-but-not-syntactic, breaking `rw`/`simp`/`slice` on `Functor.map_comp`/`Category.assoc` ASYMMETRICALLY (one side merges, the other "pattern not found"; `Category.assoc` silently rewrites the wrong side). WORKING: (1) state the M-side bridge `hML` with RHS in **restrict form**, prove via `rw [(F.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]; exact F.map_comp _ _` (NOT `‚Üź Functor.map_comp` twice ‚ÄĒ the 2nd fails to match); (2) state the N-side bridge `hNR` in **pure `N.val.presheaf` form**, apply with **`erw`**; (3) main chain `rw [‚Üź Category.assoc, hML]; erw [Category.assoc, reassoc_of% hm, hNR]; simp only [Category.assoc]; rfl`. DEAD: plain `rw [Functor.map_comp]` forward-split (never matches the opensFunctor-image factor); `slice_lhs` (treats a defeq-boundary 2-comp as one atom); term-mode `congrArg F.map (Subsingleton.elim _ _)` inside a `.trans` (whnf timeout ‚ÄĒ Subsingleton args underdetermined).

After `rw [sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq, ‚Üź Functor.map_comp √ó4]; congr 1` the goal IS the presheaf identity `(p ‚äó‚āė q) ‚Čę (ő∑_{P'}‚Ė∑Q' ‚Čę (aP').val‚óĀő∑_{Q'}) = (ő∑_P‚Ė∑Q ‚Čę (aP).val‚óĀő∑_Q) ‚Čę ((a.map p).val ‚äó‚āė (a.map q).val)` ‚ÄĒ but it carries two defeq-but-distinct monoidal `‚Čę` instances (`monoidalCategoryStruct` from the `:= rfl` lemma `sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq` vs `monoidalCategory.toMonoidalCategoryStruct` from `tensorHom_def`). DEAD routes: (1) **element-descent** `apply ModuleCat.hom_ext; ext x; induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on` (the iter-252 pivot) ‚ÄĒ the `tmul` case reduces to an instance-free `TensorProduct` identity but closing it re-introduces the sectionwise ő∑-naturality and the instance split; LSP said clean, real build did not close. (2) **whisker calculus** `rw [tensorHom_def p q, tensorHom_def]; erw [whisker_exchange, ‚Üź comp_whiskerRight_assoc, ‚Üź whiskerLeft_comp, hp, hq, ‚Ä¶]` ‚ÄĒ hand-derivation complete, but `erw`'s keyed-defeq does `whnf` on `restrictScalars (ūĚüô)(aP').val` over sheafification ‚Üí **(deterministic) timeout at whnf even at maxHeartbeats 6400000**; plain `rw [Category.assoc]`/`whisker_exchange` fail to match ("pattern not found", the instance split). (3) **uniform-instance helper** `sheafifyUnitWhiskerPaste` (restate over `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj` so `whisker_exchange` runs by plain `rw`) ‚ÄĒ **4√ó `synthInstanceFailed`: `MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj)` is not synthesizable** (the ring-functor spelling lacks the monoidal instance; only the defeq-massaged in-context term has it). Also DEAD from iter-252: the whisker252 `letI` route. Next action = the pre-armed pc253b SECONDARY (mathlib-analogist consult); given 3 iters / 4 disproved approaches all rooted in the SAME `restrictScalars (ūĚüô)`-over-sheafification carrier, strongly prefer a **cross-domain-inspiration** consult on the carrier shape, NOT a 5th api-alignment retry.
- **`homOfLocalCompat`'s `hf` hypothesis (HEq between `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images) is unconsumable ‚ÄĒ a SIGNATURE-RESTRUCTURE need, not proof-filling (iter-253, `DualInverse.lean`).** Sub-step (a) IsCompatible reduces (verified in-context) to the explicit sectionwise core equation `M.map(eqToHom) ‚Čę (f i).val.app ‚Čę N.map(eqToHom) = (j-analog)`, whose agreement must come from `hf := ‚ąÄ i j, (pullback (resLE (ūĚüô X)(U i)(U i‚äďU j))).map (f i) ‚Čć (pullback (resLE ‚Ä¶)).map (f j)`. To turn this HEq-of-morphisms into an `Eq` one needs the source/target OBJECT equalities, but **each object is a sheafification of a pullback presheaf ‚ÄĒ only isomorphic, not propositionally equal** (scratch-verified): `rfl` fails, `exact?` fails, `congr 1` exposes the false residual `(‚ÜĎU i).Modules = (‚ÜĎU j).Modules` (different source categories), and no `SheafOfModules`/`PresheafOfModules` HEq-extensionality exists (`leansearch` returns only the homogeneous `Hom.ext`). `Subsingleton.elim` collapses only the thin `Opens` route-difference, not the non-subsingleton `Ab` hom-set agreement of `f i` vs `f j`. So `hf` as phrased is unconsumable and arguably unsatisfiable by a caller. **`homOfLocalCompat` is NOT in `archon-protected.yaml`** ‚ÄĒ re-phrasing `hf` is the loop's to do (planner/blueprint/refactor), NOT a user block. Two fixes (both make (a) short): (1) **sectionwise** ‚ÄĒ `hf` = "for all `i j`, opens `V ‚Č§ U i ‚äď U j`, the conjugated section maps `(f i).val.app (op ((U i).őĻ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ V))` and `(f j).val.app ‚Ä¶` agree" (exactly the core eqn; bridge = `rfl`/direct); (2) via **`restrictFunctor` + `restrictFunctorComp`/`eqToHom` transport** of `f i`, `f j` into the common object `M.restrict (U i ‚äď U j).őĻ` (`restrict_obj`/`restrict_map` are `rfl`). Do NOT re-dispatch `homOfLocalCompat` against the current `hf`.

GOTCHAS: `topSectionToHom`'s `F G` must be typed `Ab` (NOT `AddCommGrp` ‚ÄĒ unknown identifier with `autoImplicit false`) and `X : TopCat` (NOT `Scheme`); `presheafHom_map_app` rewrites require **`erw`** (the `Over.mk W.hom` keying is defeq-not-syntactic); state `hcompat`'s type with the explicit `presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf` (NOT via the sheaf `H.obj` abbreviation ‚ÄĒ the `rw` pattern won't match the abbreviated form).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-254 addition)

- **The `(C := ‚Ä¶)` term-level monoidal-lemma device dissolves non-canonical-`MonoidalCategory`-instance poisoning (iter-254, reusable across the D1‚Ä≤ squares).** When a goal's `‚äó‚āė`/whiskers carry a non-canonical `MonoidalCategory` instance (leaked from `Monoidal.tensorObj`/`instMS` through `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`), `rw`/`erw` of EVERY monoidal lemma fails ("pattern not found" even for terms clearly present). Remedy: apply the monoidal lemmas **as TERMs** ‚ÄĒ `refine (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_comp_tensorHom (C := ‚Ä¶) _ _ _ _).trans ?_; rw [hp, hq]; exact (‚Ä¶ (C := ‚Ä¶) _ _ _ _).symm` ‚ÄĒ with an explicit **`(C := X.presheaf ‚čô forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat)`** (the `_ _` form leaves `MonoidalCategory ?C` a stuck metavariable). `‚Üź Functor.map_comp` similarly fails to `rw` but `erw` bridges it. NAME TRAP: `MonoidalCategory.tensor_comp` does NOT exist (Unknown constant); the lemma is `MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_comp_tensorHom` (`(f‚āĀ‚äóf‚āā)‚Čę(g‚āĀ‚äóg‚āā)=(f‚āĀ‚Čęg‚āĀ)‚äó(f‚āā‚Čęg‚āā)`). TOOLING TRAP: `lean_multi_attempt` reported a false `goals:[]` for `tensor_comp` ‚ÄĒ trust the full-file diagnostic, not `multi_attempt`'s empty-goals.
- **Right-associated `a.map` composites merge with the REASSOC form `‚Üź Functor.map_comp_assoc`, not `‚Üź Functor.map_comp` (iter-254, `pullbackTensorMap_natural` Square-2).** The iter-253 "two `a_Y.map`s don't unify" BLOCKER was misdiagnosed: the maps are right-associated (`a.map A ‚Čę (a.map B ‚Čę rest)`), so `A`,`B` are not the operands of one `‚Čę`. `erw [‚Üź Functor.map_comp_assoc]` merges `a.map A ‚Čę a.map B ‚Čę rest ‚Üí a.map (A ‚Čę B) ‚Čę rest`. (`erw` needed for the non-canonical-instance bridge, as STEP A.)
- **`erw [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]` (√ó4 for a triple composite, both sides) decomposes a `homLocalSection`-style composite at the element level ‚ÄĒ `rw`/`simp only [comp_apply]` do NOT fire on the wrapped `AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom` (iter-254).** Then push the scalar with `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` on the M-leg.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-254 addition)

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-255 addition)

- **The `.val`/`.obj` connecting-object boundary recipe (iter-255, `pullbackTensorMap_natural` Sq3/Sq4, reusable).** When an iso typed on `‚Ä¶ringCatSheaf.val` meets a `.obj`-normalised helper, the connecting object is defeq-but-not-syntactic and blocks ALL kabstract tactics (`rw [Category.assoc]` / `rw/erw [‚Üź Functor.map_comp]` / `congr_arg‚āā (¬∑‚Čę¬∑)` ‚Üí "pattern not found"; `simp only [Category.assoc]` ‚Üí "no progress"; plain `congr 1` ‚Üí `HEq`). Bridge: (1) `erw [Category.assoc]` (NOT `rw`/`simp`) right-associates across the gap; (2) `erw [Iso.cancel_iso_hom_left]` peels a common iso prefix across the gap; (3) `refine ((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans ?_).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _)` merges BOTH `a.map`s into `a.map (_ ‚Čę _) = a.map (_ ‚Čę _)` via `isDefEq` ‚ÄĒ `refine`'s isDefEq is strictly stronger than `erw`'s keyed matching for the merge (erw FAILS it); (4) `congr 1` descends to the presheaf level; (5) `MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_comp_tensorHom (C := ‚Ä¶‚čôforget‚āā‚Ä¶)` applied as a `(C:=‚Ä¶)`-pinned TERM for bifunctoriality, with `erw [hleg a, hleg b]` again bridging the gap. Per-leg `hleg`: `rw [‚Üź Functor.map_comp]; erw [pullbackValIso_hom_natural]; rw [Functor.map_comp]; rfl`.
- **Carrier-correct section linearity over varying rings: use őď-level `Scheme.Modules.map_smul`, NOT `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` (iter-255, `DualInverse.lean` `homOfLocalCompat` sub-step (c)).** `PresheafOfModules.map_smul M.val e‚āĀ` produces a smul over the *semilinear codomain* `(ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.ringCatSheaf.map e‚āĀ)).obj (M.val.obj (op image))` ‚ÄĒ an `X(W)`-module ‚ÄĒ which is over a DIFFERENT base ring than the f-leg `(f i).val.app P`'s native `X(image)`-action; genuinely not defeq, rejected even under `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`. The fix is `Scheme.Modules.map_smul M` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean`, native őď-module level, NO `restrictScalars` artifact): `erw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul M]` closes the M-leg cleanly. This narrows the iter-254 carrier-duality wall from a different-base-ring mismatch (`restrictScalars e‚āĀ`) to an identity-ring-map mismatch (`restrictScalars ūĚüô`, via `Scheme.Opens.őĻ_appIso = Iso.refl`), bridgeable by `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def` / `restrictScalarsId'App`.

THREE statement-level frictions block even stating Sq2b: (1) `őī (pullback ŌÜ')` leaves `MonoidalCategory` metavars stuck ‚Üí needs the D1‚Ä≤ CommRingCat/`forget‚āā` canonical-spelling pin (`show ‚Ä¶ from` / `let ŌÜ' : ‚Ä¶ ‚čô forget‚āā`); (2) unifying őī against `pullbackComp`'s codomain needs explicit `(F := _ ‚čô _)` + an associativity defeq (HO stall); (3) the reconcile defeq does NOT fire at the `.app` connecting-object even under `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` ‚Üí needs an explicit `eqToHom` bridge from `toRingCatSheafHom_comp_hom_reconcile`. Sq1/Sq4 (`sheafificationCompPullback`/`pullbackValIso` comp coherences) are also Mathlib-absent `leftAdjointUniq`-across-composite args, deferred behind Sq2b. pc257 read this lane CONVERGING; the iter's only "close" was the trivial rfl helper while the genuine content is Mathlib-scale ‚ÄĒ flag for the next progress-critic.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Process gotcha (iter-257 addition)

- **Do NOT co-schedule a prover on a `.lean` file and a prover on a file that IMPORTS it (iter-257).** `DualInverse.lean` imports `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`; running the TS-inv and TS-cmp provers concurrently left the import non-compiling for most of the session, so the TS-inv LSP returned empty goals (`failed_dependencies` whenever the dep source ‚Č† its `.olean`), making iterative development of the ~200-LOC `sliceDualTransport` body impractical (the prover cited this as decisive in its non-close). When both files have live prover work, SERIALIZE them (or freeze the importee's signature for the iter).
- **The `\uses{\leanok ‚Ä¶}` corruption at `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex:144-146` is RE-INTRODUCED BY `sync_leanok` ITSELF each iter (iter-257).** This iter's sync (sha `a60632e1`, +1/‚ąí0) inserted a proof-block `\leanok` on its own line INSIDE the multiline `\uses{‚Ä¶}` argument (opened L144, label list continues L146), splitting the cross-ref list ‚Üí blueprint-doctor reports the trailing label as a broken ref. The planner's plan-phase manual fix is REVERTED by sync every iter, so a plan-side prose reflow is not durable ‚ÄĒ this needs a fix to the `sync_leanok` SCRIPT (maintainer) so the proof-block `\leanok` lands directly after `\begin{proof}`, never between `\uses{` braces. NOT the review agent's domain (`\leanok` ownership).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-258 addition)

- **`‚Ü•‚ÜĎU` vs `‚Ü•U` discrimination-tree mismatch ‚ÄĒ the `change`-to-subtype fix (iter-258, `SheafOverEquivalence.lean`).** The site carrier `‚Ü•(‚ÜĎU : Scheme)` (reached via `Scheme ‚Üí TopCat ‚Üí Type`) is *definitionally* the subtype `‚Ü•U` but **keys differently** in the instance discrimination tree, so a project instance stated for the bare topological space `‚Ü•U` (e.g. `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`) is NOT found by typeclass search, AND the priority-900 `IsDenseSubsite ‚Üí IsContinuous` chain silently fails. **FIX**: state the instance on the scheme-carrier form, then `change ‚Ä¶(Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•U)‚Ä¶; infer_instance` (defeq-convert to the subtype key); `exact <project_instance>` also typechecks by defeq even when `infer_instance` alone fails.
- **`Functor.map_comp` on a `forget‚āā`-composite functor (iter-258, reusable).** `rw [‚Üź Functor.map_comp]` (even functor-pinned) REFUSES to combine `F.map a ‚Čę F.map b` when `F = X.ringCatSheaf.obj` (a `forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat`-composite) ‚ÄĒ "did not find pattern". Recipes: (a) `erw [‚Üź Functor.map_comp]` works when the `X.ringCatSheaf` leg is FIRST in each combine; (b) when it would overreach (the `(‚ÜĎU).ringCatSheaf` leg first ‚Üí spawns a metavar through `opensFunctor`), peel `forget‚āā` first (`change (forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat).map _ ‚Čę ‚Ä¶ = ‚Ä¶; rw [‚Üź (forget‚āā _ _).map_comp]`) then combine the inner maps with `Functor.map_comp` applied as a TERM: `(Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans ((congrArg _ (Subsingleton.elim _ _)).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))` (thinness of `(Opens ‚Ü•X)ŠĶíŠĶĖ` closes via `Subsingleton.elim`). ALWAYS write `.obj.map`, never `.val.map` (records as `Sheaf.val`, syntactically distinct from the `.obj` `simp` produces, blocking combines).
- **`pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` is the canonical Mathlib idiom** for lifting a site equivalence to a `SheafOfModules` equivalence (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:305`); only the ring morphism `ŌÜ` is genuine content ‚ÄĒ `Ōą`/`H‚āĀ`/`H‚āā` are mutual-inverse coherences and the `[IsContinuous]` legs infer.

FIX on the consuming def: `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` + supply the legs explicitly as `haveI hF1/hF2` (by `inferInstance`) and the composite via `@Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ ‚Ä¶ hF1 hF2` with topology `K` pinned to `Opens.grothendieckTopology ‚Ü•(‚ÜĎU:Scheme)`. - **D3‚Ä≤ Sq2b mate-calculus recipe ‚ÄĒ PROVEN `pullbackComp_őī` (iter-259, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** ő∑‚Üíőī analog of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`: transpose under `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction Ōá).homEquiv.injective`; LHS őī via `Adjunction.unit_app_tensor_comp_map_őī`; MATE step `conjugateEquiv (pullbackComp ŌÜ Ōą).inv = ūĚüô` (since `pushforwardComp = Iso.refl`, via `conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv` + `unit_conjugateEquiv`); őľ-COH (`pushforwardComp_lax_őľ`); őľ-NAT (`Functor.LaxMonoidal.őľ_natural`); TRI; tensorHom merge. PINS: state LHS pullback as `pullback (F:=F‚čôG) (R:=T‚āÄ‚čôforget‚āā) (ŌÜ ‚Čę F.op.whiskerLeft Ōą)` (both `F` and `R` pinned, else oplax instance won't resolve). őī/őľ rewrites need `erw` (registered oplax instance defeq-not-syntactic to `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`). `conjugateEquiv`/`unit_conjugateEquiv` are BARE (no `Adjunction.`). Duplicate monoidal-vs-base Category instance ‚áí generic `rw` of `Iso.inv_hom_id_app`/`tensorHom_comp_tensorHom`/`Category.assoc` fail; use `erw [(iso).inv_hom_id_app P]`, pin `tensorHom_comp_tensorHom (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (‚Ä¶‚čôforget‚āā))`, close trailing assoc with `exact Category.assoc _ _ _`. `CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id` (NOT bare `Functor.map_id` = the `<$>` lemma). See [[ts259-d3-sq2b-reduced]].

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-260 addition)

- **Opaque lax-őľ of a `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` reduces DEFINITIONALLY to the lighter `restrictScalars` őľ (iter-260).** `őľ (pushforward ŌÜ) A B = őľ (restrictScalars ŌÜ') (P‚āÄ.obj A) (P‚āÄ.obj B)` is `rfl` (`pushforward_őľ_eq`), where `P‚āÄ = pushforward‚āÄOfCommRingCat F R‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ because `pushforward = pushforward‚āÄ ‚čô restrictScalars` and `pushforward‚āÄ` is strongly monoidal with `őľIso = refl`. Sectionwise, `(őľ (restrictScalars őĪ) M‚āĀ M‚āā).app W = őľ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (őĪ.app W).hom) (M‚āĀ.obj W) (M‚āā.obj W)` is also `rfl` (`restrictScalars_őľ_app`, under `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`). Then on a pure tensor every `restrictScalars` őľ is the identity (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_őľ_tmul`) and both sides collapse to `m ‚äó‚āú n`.
- **whnf-wall pattern for heavy sheafification/pushforward‚āÄ section objects (iter-260).** A direct `rw`/`erw`/`simp [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_őľ_tmul]` whnf-EXPLODES (>200000 heartbeats even at 2M) because the section objects are huge. Robust pattern: instantiate an ATOM-stated helper with the goal's concrete heavy objects as EXPLICIT arguments into a `have`, then `erw [that_have]` ‚ÄĒ `erw` matches only the residual defeq (instance / `forget‚āā`-association), never whnf-s the heavy objects. Silent HO no-match fix: pin `forget‚āā`-association implicits, e.g. `restrictScalars_őľ_app (R := S‚āÄ) (S := F.op ‚čô R‚āÄ)` (goal carries `ŌÜ` at the *inner* `F.op ‚čô (R‚āÄ ‚čô forget‚āā)`, lemma needs the *outer* `(F.op ‚čô R‚āÄ) ‚čô forget‚āā`).
- **`hom_ext` + `ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m n => ?_)` beats `induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on` (iter-260).** The latter leaves zero/add cases that `map_zero`/`map_add` repeatedly fail to fire on (the +/0 structure on the defeq carriers is syntactically distinct); `tensor_ext` after `hom_ext` gives ONLY the pure-tensor case. Also: a morphism-level reduction helper (`őľ (pushforward ŌÜ).app W = ModuleCat őľ`) WON'T typecheck ‚ÄĒ `(presheaf-tensor).obj W` is not defeq to the `ModuleCat` tensor of the `.obj W`s, even with `eqToHom (by rfl)`; stay at `PresheafOfModules` morphism level or go fully element-level.

After `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso ?_`, the reduced `‚ČÉ‚āó` goal is between presheaf internal-hom section modules over DIFFERENT slice categories (`Over_X fV'` vs `Over_Y V`); its content is that the dual COMMUTES WITH SLICE REINDEXING along `f.opensFunctor`, which needs `overEquivalence`'s functor (a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`) to be strong-monoidal CLOSED ‚ÄĒ `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R‚āÄ)`, the structure the project deliberately avoids (`rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`). Confirmed by BOTH the prover and lvb-di260. Genuine close = **route-(2)** (~150‚Äď250 LOC sectionwise: leg A `homLocalSection`-style `eqToHom`-conjugation across `f.opensFunctor` ‚ąė leg B `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` along `(f.appIso V).inv`), NOT decomposable into independent compiling sub-pieces (leg B does not type-apply before leg A). ## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-261 addition)

- **Enter a `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso` whose carriers reduce away the needed `Module` instance (iter-261, `sliceDualTransport`).** When `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso ?_; refine { toFun := ‚Ä¶ }` fails with "failed to synthesize `Module ūĚí™_Y(V) (carrier)`" because the structure literal re-synthesizes the instance on a `pushforward‚āÄ`-REDUCED carrier: (1) `set ő≤ := Functor.whiskerRight {app := ‚Ä¶} (forget‚āā ‚Ä¶) with hő≤` to re-fold the goal to the un-reduced `(pushforward ő≤ ‚Ä¶)` form; (2) `letI lhsMod := inferInstance` and `letI rhsMod := InternalHom.internalHomObjModule (R := ‚Ä¶) V.unop (pushforward ő≤ M.val) (ūĚüô_ _)` ‚ÄĒ the RHS (a dual's section module) is NOT `inferInstance`, it is `internalHomObjModule`; (3) `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (m‚āĀ := lhsMod) (m‚āā := rhsMod) ?_` ‚ÄĒ `m‚āĀ`/`m‚āā` MUST be passed explicitly, else they are deferred metavars re-synthesized (and failing) at the struct literal. Then build each component map **categorically via `.map`** (`(ModuleCat.restrictScalars ŌÜ).map (‚Ä¶)`), NEVER `ModuleCat.ofHom { toFun := ‚Ä¶ }` ‚ÄĒ the latter reduces the carrier and loses the `restrictScalars`/`pushforward‚āÄ` module instance (verified failure).
- **`erw` (not `rw`) for `Adjunction.homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` (iter-261, Sq1).** When the composite left adjoint of `B.comp` is spelled `pullback ŌÜ' ‚čô sheafification` rather than syntactically `F'.obj P`, plain `rw` fails the HO-match; `erw` fires. Opening recipe for a `leftAdjointUniq`-of-composite-adjunctions identity: `apply (A).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective` ‚Üí `rw [sheafificationCompPullback_eq_leftAdjointUniq]` ‚Üí `erw [Adjunction.homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app]` (collapses LHS to `B.unit.app P`) ‚Üí `rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, Adjunction.comp_unit_app, ‚Ä¶]` ‚Üí concrete unit identity.
- **`leftAdjointUniq_trans_app` does NOT apply when the three adjunctions have different right adjoints (iter-261).** `Mathlib.leftAdjointUniq_trans_app` requires the three adjunctions to share the SAME right adjoint `G`; the three `sheafificationCompPullback` isos (composite vs single pushforward right adjoints) do not. The coherence is the genuine pseudofunctor-tensorator interchange, proved as the őī-free twin of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`'s mate calculus, NOT via `_trans`.
- **D3‚Ä≤ four-square paste INTERLEAVES (iter-261).** The `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` 4-square decomposition cannot be pasted row-by-row: `S1_h` on the RHS acts on `tensorObj ((pullback f).obj M).val ((pullback f).obj N).val`, NOT on `PresheafOfModules.pullback ŌÜ'_f (M‚äóN)`. Factors must slide past each other via `őī_natural`/NatTrans naturality (the D1‚Ä≤ merge of `a.map őī ‚Čę S3 ‚Čę S4` into one `a.map ő®`) before assembly. Sq1 (`sheafificationCompPullback_comp`) + Sq4 (`pullbackValIso` composition coherence) must land first.
- **Mathlib `Scheme.OpenCover` migrated to `ZeroHypercover` (iter-261).** `X.OpenCover` is now `(Scheme.precoverage IsOpenImmersion).ZeroHypercover X`: index type `ūĚíį.I‚āÄ`, the `j`-th scheme `ūĚíį.X j`, the map `ūĚíį.f j`, finiteness `[Finite ūĚíį.I‚āÄ]`. The old `ūĚíį.J`/`ūĚíį.obj`/`ūĚíį.map` are GONE. `Scheme.Modules` is `Abelian` (`Scheme.Modules.instAbelian`, noncomputable) ‚áí `CochainComplex S.Modules ‚Ąē` has `.homology i : S.Modules`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-261 addition)

- **`sliceDualTransport` codomainMap (leg-B unit ring-iso swap) ‚ÄĒ two frictions, do NOT retry `exact inv (őĶ ‚Ä¶)` blind (iter-261).** The intended term is `CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.őĶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (ő≤.app (op W')).hom))`; `IsIso (ő≤.app (op W'))` (via `forget‚āā.map` of `(f.appIso W').inv`) and its `Function.Bijective` (`ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`) are dischargeable, but TWO genuine obstacles block the term: **(a)** `restrictScalars_isIso_őĶ_of_bijective` (PresheafInternalHom L212, top-level, no namespace) needs `[CommRing R][CommRing S]`, but the section rings appear as `forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat`-images `‚ÜĎ(Y.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (op W'))` whose `CommRing` instance is not synthesized at the `RingCat` spelling (need a CommRing recovery via `Y.presheaf.obj`'s instance + defeq `change`, OR a CommRing-free `őĶ`-iso lemma); **(b)** `őĶ`'s type carries `ūĚüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Ä¶)` but the goal carries the defeq-but-not-syntactic `(restr V ūĚüô_Y).obj W` unit-section forms ‚ÄĒ `exact inv (őĶ ‚Ä¶)` will not unify without a `change`/`eqToHom` bridge.

- **A net-zero-close iter can still be genuine structural progress ‚ÄĒ but the STUCK clock is now running (iter-261).** Three lanes all produced compiling code (instance walls resolved, leg-A built, Sq1 isolated + LHS-reduced, a clean engine scaffold) yet ZERO sorries were eliminated. This is NOT helper-churn (each addition advances a named goal and compiles), but the progress-critic's pc261 WATCH ("no sorry-reduction ‚áí STUCK") is now live for the dual lane. Distinguish "structural progress with documented next-step" (acceptable for 1 iter) from "churn" (helpers multiply, residual flat) ‚ÄĒ but require iter-262 to convert at least one of {DualInverse codomainMap, Sq1 unit reassembly} into a real close, else escalate (mathlib-analogist on the `restrictScalars`/`őĶ`-CommRing wall) before a third grind.

The fix (ma-legb262, both predictions held EXACTLY): **friction (a)** ‚ÄĒ phrase the őĶ-iso at the **CommRingCat carrier** `(f.appIso W').inv.hom` (where `CommRing` is native), NOT the `forget‚āā`'d RingCat carrier; `restrictScalars` of the `forget‚āā`'d hom is `rfl`-equal so leg-A's domain still matches ‚Üí `isIso_őĶ_restrictScalars_appIso := restrictScalars_isIso_őĶ_of_bijective _ (ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _)`. **friction (b)** ‚ÄĒ the `ūĚüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Ä¶)` vs `(restr V ūĚüô_Y).obj W` unit-section endpoints reconcile by **pure defeq, NO `change`/`eqToHom` bridge** (the predicted "rfl" held). `dualUnitRingSwap := inv (őĶ (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom))` fills the hole; `(ő≤.app (op W')).hom = (f.appIso W').inv.hom` by `rfl`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-262 addition)

- **Change-of-rings őĶ-iso at the CommRingCat carrier (iter-262).** A lax-monoidal unit `őĶ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g)` is an iso for a ring iso `g`; ALWAYS phrase it at the `CommRingCat` carrier where `CommRing` is native, never at the `forget‚āā CommRingCat RingCat`-image carrier (whose `CommRing` instance is not synthesized at the RingCat spelling). Downstream domains/endpoints reconcile by `rfl`/defeq. Reusable for any future `restrictScalars`-unit-iso obligation.
- **`erw`-splice a proven `key` past `comp_unit_app`-glued factors (iter-262, Sq1 R0-peel).** To splice `key : A ‚Čę B' = ‚Ä¶` into a goal where `A ‚Čę B'` is NOT a syntactic subterm (factors glued by `comp_unit_app`; `‚Čę`s and intermediate objects at defeq-but-not-syntactic `Functor.obj` apps): (1) `simp only [Functor.comp_map] at key ‚äĘ` to align both to the same `restrictScalars.map (forget.map _)` normal form; (2) `erw [Category.assoc]` (NOT `rw`/`simp` ‚ÄĒ only `erw`'s defeq-implicit matching flattens the glued `(unit ‚Čę A)`); (3) `erw [reassoc_of% key]` (again `erw`, NOT `rw` ‚ÄĒ same instance-defeq issue). Plain `rw [Category.assoc]` reports no `(f‚Čęg)‚Čęh` head; `slice_rhs 2 3` keeps the glued factor as one; `rw [reassoc_of% key]` normalizes the spelling away.
- **`conv_rhs` mandatory for one-sided `Functor.map_comp` distribution (iter-262).** Distributing `Functor.map_comp` on the RHS of a unit identity: a bare `erw [Functor.map_comp]` on the whole goal CONTAMINATES the LHS (rewrites `sheafAdj.unit` into `toSheafify ‚Čę restrictHomEquiv`); `rw [Functor.map_comp]` does not fire (defeq-but-not-syntactic composite functor); an unconfined `erw` chain whnf-times-out. `conv_rhs => erw [Functor.map_comp]; erw [Functor.comp_map F]` fixes all three.
- **ńĆech nerve‚Üícomplex is coherence-free; isolate the cost to the push-pull functor (iter-262).** Split the relative ńĆech construction into (i) the geometric backbone `coverArrow := Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc ūĚíį.f)` ‚Üí `coverCechNerve := (coverArrow ūĚíį).augmentedCechNerve` (UNCONDITIONAL ‚ÄĒ `Scheme` has coproducts + `HasFiniteLimits`), and (ii) `relativeCechComplexOfNerve` = `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` + `CosimplicialObject.whiskering (pushforward f)` + `Augmented.drop` (preadditivity of `S.Modules` ONLY, no `pushforwardComp`/`pullbackComp` coherence). `CechComplex` then has a genuine body. The ENTIRE coherence cost concentrates in the single push-pull functor `G : (Over X)ŠĶíŠĶĖ ‚•§ X.Modules`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Resolved blockers (iter-263 addition)

The `internalHomObjModule`-add IS the ambient `Preadditive` add (single shared add); the bridge is `rfl`. Close with: `intro x y; apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext; intro W; change (ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).map ((x+y).app _) ‚Čę _ = _; rw [show (x+y).app _ = x.app _ + y.app _ from rfl, Functor.map_add, Preadditive.add_comp]` + trailing `rfl`. `PresheafOfModules.add_app` does NOT fire (defeq-not-syntactic), but the whole equation is defeq so `rfl` lands. (`map_smul'` of the same struct reduced to the exposed crux `d.hom (s‚ÄĘu) = c‚ÄĘd.hom u` but NOT yet closed ‚ÄĒ tactic friction, not a missing ingredient.)
- **D3‚Ä≤ Sq1 main lemma `sheafificationCompPullback_comp` ‚ÄĒ CLOSED sorry-free (iter-263), residual relocated.** The main lemma now closes via `erw [‚Üź Functor.map_comp, ‚Üź Functor.map_comp]; exact sheafificationCompPullback_comp_tail h f P`. Spell the whole term and give each `eqToHom` an EXPLICIT `congrArg` proof so its object equality is determined, not inferred from neighbours (`Over.w g : g.left ‚Čę Y‚āĀ.hom = Y‚āā.hom`).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Known Blockers (iter-263 addition)

- **D3‚Ä≤ Sq1 tail ‚ÄĒ do NOT transpose the whole equation (iter-263, circular).** Re-expressing `B.unit` via `homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq` and stripping the sheafification wrapper (`compHomEquivFactor` + `homEquiv_unit` + `congr`) produces a goal `homEquiv.injective`-EQUIVALENT to the original statement (re-folding `sheaf_unit_comp_pushforward_pullbackComp_inv` backwards + `Functor.map_comp` collapses it right back). The prover verified this by hand and REVERTED. `rw [‚Üź sheafificationCompPullback_eq_leftAdjointUniq]` also FAILS (motive/homEquiv wrapper). Correct direction: keep the LHS as concrete `B.unit` and ASSEMBLE the RHS via `homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` at the two `f`/`h` layers + `pushforwardComp`/`pullbackComp` unit-naturality.
- **`sliceDualTransport` map_smul' crux ‚ÄĒ defeq-not-syntactic RHS projection (iter-263).** After exposing both `globalSMul` actions, the goal `d.hom (s‚ÄĘu) = c‚ÄĘd.hom u` cannot be closed by `rw`/`show ‚Ä¶ from rfl`/`conv_rhs` because the RHS `(toFun-section).hom z` is a `{app:=‚Ä¶}.app W` projection defeq-but-not-syntactic to `d.hom u`, and `rw [hring]` fails because the scalar carries the `{toFun := (ő≤.app V).hom.toFun, ‚Ä¶} m` (MonoidWithZeroHom-coe) form, not `(RingCat.Hom.hom (ő≤.app V)) m`. Finish with a projection-tolerant `exact hcrux` after normalizing the scalar form; ingredients (`termRingMap_naturality`, `ő≤.naturality`, `‚Üź ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def'`, `map_smul`) all verified to fire in isolation.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-264 addition)

- **Mate-calculus identity functor law** (engine `pushPullMap_id`, axiom-clean): for a push‚Äďpull
  functor `G` built from `pullback ‚ä£ pushforward`, prove `G(ūĚüô) = ūĚüô` by:
  `star := unit_conjugateEquiv (Adjunction.id) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (ūĚüô _)) (pullbackId _).hom _`
  then `rw [conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom]` (identity-adjunction unit triangle) ‚Üí
  `hru := pseudofunctor_right_unitality` applied at the object ‚Üí `hpf` pushforward-coercion collapse via
  `apply hom_ext; intro U; rfl` (`pushforwardComp_hom_app_app`/`pushforwardId_hom_app_app` are `rfl`) ‚Üí
  `hzig` unit zig-zag via `(pushforwardId).hom.naturality` + `star` + `Iso.inv_hom_id_app` ‚Üí assemble
  with `erw [reassoc_of% hpf, ‚Ä¶, ‚Üź Functor.map_comp]; erw [hzig, Functor.map_id]; rfl`. **The `(ūĚü≠).obj`
  defeq wall needs `erw` + `reassoc_of% star`** (plain `rw` silently fails). **OMIT `Category.id_comp`**
  from the `star`-cleanup `simp` (reported unused AND it un-collapses the form `reassoc_of% star` needs).
- **Projection-tolerant `map_smul'` reduction** (`sliceDualTransport.map_smul'`, axiom-clean ‚ÄĒ SUPERSEDES
  the iter-263 "map_smul' crux unresolved" note above): when `smul_def`/`smul_def'` BOTH fail because the
  restrictScalars action is the **`compHom` instance** (not `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.obj`, so neither
  keys), reduce the RHS value with **`conv_rhs => arg 2; change ‚Ä¶`** (conv-navigation + `change` see
  through the projections). Pull the codomain scalar through a `ModuleCat` hom with **`d.hom.map_smul _ _`
  as a TERM** inside `refine (congrArg d.hom ?_).trans ‚Ä¶` ‚ÄĒ bare `rw [‚Üź map_smul]` mis-resolves to the
  PresheafOfModules overload. Ring-map reconcile: `congr 1; simp only [termRingMap, ‚Ä¶]; exact
  (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (appIso_inv_naturality f _) m).symm`.
- **P-general recovery brick** (`leftAdjointUniqUnitEta_app`): generalize a `ūĚüô_`-specialized mate lemma
  to all objects `P` by an object-generic copy of the body when nothing in the original used `ūĚüô_`. Proof
  = `Adjunction.homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` + `comp_unit_app`; axiom-clean immediately.
- **`conv_rhs` confinement for `Functor.map_comp`** (D3‚Ä≤ Sq1 step-0): when distributing a `Functor.map`
  of a composite, confine the rewrite to `conv_rhs` if the LHS carries an adjunction unit (here
  `sheafAdj_Z.unit`) that a whole-goal `rw/erw [Functor.map_comp]` would unfold to
  `toSheafify ‚Čę restrictHomEquiv`.

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Resolved blockers (iter-265 addition)

- **`pushPull_unit_mate` (engine mate-core, axiom-clean).** Splits a composite adjunction unit:
  `ő∑^p ‚Čę p_*(ő∑^f) ‚Čę pushforwardComp f p .hom = ő∑^{f‚Čęp} ‚Čę (f‚Čęp)_*((pullbackComp f p).inv)`. Proof =
  `unit_conjugateEquiv (adj_p.comp adj_f) adj_{f‚Čęp} (pullbackComp f p).inv N`, then
  `rw [conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv, Adjunction.comp_unit_app]`, `simpa only [Category.assoc]`. 3
  lines, kernel-cheap. Reusable for the push‚Äďpull pentagon AND the ńĆech-nerve augmentation.
- **`forget_map_pushforward_map` (D3‚Ä≤ Sq1 STEP-1 bridge, `rfl`, axiom-clean).** `forget.map
  ((SheafOfModules.pushforward ŌÜ).map g) = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward ŌÜ.hom).map (forget.map g)` ‚ÄĒ
  definitional (`SheafOfModules.pushforward` sectionwise from `PresheafOfModules.pushforward`; `forget`
  = `.val`). Fires on a goal only via **`erw`** (composite is defeq-not-syntactic).
- **DUAL leg-B `.hom`-direction swaps (axiom-clean).** `dualUnitRingSwapHom = inv(őĶ(restrictScalars
  (appIso f W').hom.hom))` (the codomain swap `invFun` needs), `isIso_őĶ_restrictScalars_appIso_hom`,
  `dualUnitRingSwapInv` (= `őĶ` itself, `.inv` dir, for toFun round-trips), + 2 `@[simp]` cancellation
  lemmas (`őĶ ‚Čę inv őĶ = ūĚüô`, `inv őĶ ‚Čę őĶ = ūĚüô`).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-265 addition)

  `ő∑^{f‚Čęp}` into iterated `ő∑^p`/`ő∑^f` + pushforward comparison.

  the final `eqToHom` cancellation forces the KERNEL to check defeq of `pushforward`/`pullback`-applied
  comparison objects, which `whnf`-explodes. **`maxHeartbeats 1e6`, `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency
  false`, `subst`, and a `subst`-telescope helper applied by `exact` ALL fail** (kernel timeout is not
  heartbeat-bounded for the proof term). FIX = make `pushPullMap` transport-light (absorb the
  over-triangle substitution without `eqToHom`, or a kernel-cheap `eqToHom`-cancellation lemma for the
  `(pushforward q).map őĻ ‚Čę eqToHom ‚Čę (pushforward p).map ŌÄ ‚Čę eqToHom` shape). This is a **refactor**,
  not a prove pass. Dead ends (do NOT retry): sectionwise `hom_ext`; `rw [Over.w k]` (motive not
  type-correct); `subst (Over.w k)` (circular); raising `maxHeartbeats`.

  produced compiling axiom-clean motion every lane, but eliminated ZERO file/decl sorries on
  `sliceDualTransport` / `sheafificationCompPullback_comp_tail` / `pushPullMap_comp`. The iter-264

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Resolved blockers (iter-271 addition)

- **The 5-iter kernel `whnf` wall on `pushPullMap_comp` (CechHigherDirectImage.lean) is BYPASSED.**
  The blocker (iter-265) was a kernel `whnf` blow-up when cancelling `pushPullMap`'s two over-triangle
  `eqToHom` transports *in situ* at concrete `pushforward`/`pullback` fibers. Resolution: state the
  cancellation as a lemma `pushPull_transport_cancel` with a **free** equality hypothesis (not the
  specific `Over.w`), proved `subst h; simp` ‚ÄĒ `subst` collapses both transports to `eqToHom rfl = ūĚüô`
  on **abstract** objects, kernel-cheap. In the real goal, `erw [pushPull_transport_cancel ‚Ä¶]` fires on
  all three `pushPullMap` occurrences with no blow-up (`rw` never fires ‚ÄĒ defeq-not-syntactic). Option-(a)
  (refactor `pushPullMap` transport-light) is NOT needed. `pushPullMap_comp` is now a documented
  ~60‚Äď100 LOC whiskered-pentagon `erw` grind (`pushPullMap_comp_aux` ‚Üí `pushPull_unit_mate` +
  `pseudofunctor_associativity (f:=kl,g:=gl‚āā,h:=p‚āĀ)`).
- **Blueprint invFun őĶ-direction math error (iter-265 watch, `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` ~L5859) is
  FIXED.** The chapter now states the `.hom`-direction `inv(őĶ(restrictScalars (f.appIso W').hom.hom))` =
  `dualUnitRingSwapHom`, matching the Lean (confirmed by lean-vs-blueprint-checker dualinverse-iter271).
- **DUAL `sliceDualTransportInv` extracted top-level + TYPECHECKS** ‚ÄĒ the iter-265 binder-metavar-under-
  binder blocker is gone; `invFun` of `sliceDualTransport` is wired (internal holes 4‚Üí3).

## Knowledge Base ‚ÄĒ Proof patterns (iter-271 addition)

- **Free-hypothesis `subst` to dodge kernel `whnf` on `eqToHom` transports.** When a transport-cancel
  identity blows up the kernel at concrete fibers, restate it with a *free* equality hypothesis and
  `subst h; simp` ‚ÄĒ it collapses the transports on abstract objects. Reusable beyond push-pull.
- **Cross-fiber ModuleCat transport needs a `restrictScalars`-őĶ conjugation, NOT a plain `eqToHom`.**
  When two `ModuleCat`-fiber objects differ by a *propositional* (not syntactic) ring equality `he`
  (e.g. `X.presheaf.obj (op A) ‚Č† X.presheaf.obj (op B)` with `A = B` provable), `eqToHom` cannot bridge
  them. Conjugate by the őĶ-iso along `X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (op he))` (the machinery `dualUnitRingSwapHom`
  uses for `f.appIso`). (DUAL `sliceDualTransportInv.app`, ~20‚Äď40 LOC.)
- **`conjugateEquiv_whiskerRight` vs `whiskerLeft` discriminant.** For a goal factor of the form
  `(pullback h).map (g.app P) = (whiskerRight g (pullback h)).app P`, the correct mate device is
  `CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_whiskerRight` (Mathlib `Mates.lean:536`), NOT `conjugateEquiv_whiskerLeft`
  (which gives `g.app ((pullback h).obj P)`). Namespace is bare `CategoryTheory.‚Ä¶`, not `‚Ä¶Adjunction.‚Ä¶`.
